parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close the single blocking issue: the llm_integration contract field specified GPT-4o feature importance narration but the round 1 plan had no LLM integration whatsoever. The fix is to add the llm_narration module calling GPT-4o with a structured prompt, log the output as an MLflow artifact, and store the OpenAI API key in GCP Secret Manager. The narration step should be non-blocking so experiment runs are not halted by API errors. The rest of the plan from round 1 is sound and can be carried forward.

fix_report.action_taken: Added llm_narration module to the pipeline runner script: after LightGBM training and MLflow logging, the module calls GPT-4o with a prompt structured as 'You are a logistics research analyst. Given the following feature importances from a last-mile delivery failure risk model, write a 3-paragraph research summary explaining which features are most predictive, any surprising correlations, and recommended next experiments. Feature importances: {sorted_importance_json}'. The response is logged to MLflow as a text artifact research_narrative.txt and appended to the experiment's research_log.md file in GCS. OpenAI API key stored in GCP Secret Manager path /research/openai-api-key and loaded at runtime. GPT-4o API failures are caught and logged to Cloud Logging without halting the experiment run.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Each experiment run generates a natural-language research summary in the MLflow experiment log, enabling researchers to quickly interpret feature importance results without manual analysis of JSON importances.

parsed.title: Last-Mile Delivery Failure Risk ML Prototype v2
parsed.executive_summary: A research prototype on GCP Cloud Run that validates the ML approach for last-mile delivery failure risk prediction. The pipeline reads historical delivery attempt data into a DuckDB feature store, computes geocoding confidence, time-window deviation, driver attempt rate, address unit completeness, and historical route failure features, then trains a LightGBM gradient boosting classifier with Optuna hyperparameter search using MLflow autolog. A FastAPI prediction endpoint serves model-predicted failure probabilities. After each experiment run, a GPT-4o narration module generates a natural-language research summary of the top feature importances and recommended next experiments, logged as an MLflow artifact and appended to the experiment research log.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-component pipeline on GCP: (1) feature_pipeline module reads raw delivery_attempts CSV from GCS into DuckDB, computes feature_sets view with derived columns (time_window_deviation_minutes, geocoding_confidence_bucket, driver_attempt_rate_30d, unit_completeness_score, historical_route_failure_rate), and exports a training Parquet to GCS. (2) training_pipeline module calls LightGBM with Optuna hyperparameter search, logs all trial metrics and feature importances to MLflow via autolog, and saves the best-performing model artifact to GCS. (3) prediction_api module is a FastAPI service on Cloud Run that loads the latest model artifact, accepts delivery_attempt JSON, and returns a failure_probability score and top-10 feature importances. Post-training, the pipeline_runner script calls llm_narration to generate a research summary from the sorted feature importances, logs it as an MLflow artifact, and appends it to the experiment research_log.md in GCS. All components communicate via GCS for data and model artifacts.
parsed.technology_stack: Python 3.12. FastAPI 0.111 for prediction API. LightGBM 4.0 for gradient boosting. scikit-learn 1.5 for feature engineering utility functions. Optuna 2.0 for hyperparameter search. MLflow 2.13 with GCS backend store for experiment tracking. DuckDB 0.10 for feature store and training dataset. openai Python SDK for GPT-4o narration. python-jose for JWT validation. ruff and mypy for CI linting. GitHub Actions for CI. GCP Cloud Run for FastAPI and MLflow server. GCS for data, models, and experiment artifacts. GCP Secret Manager for HMAC JWT secret and OpenAI API key.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Feature Engineering Pipeline: reads delivery_attempts CSV, computes address_geocoding_confidence from geocoding confidence score, time_window_deviation_minutes from scheduled vs actual attempt time, driver_attempt_rate_30d from rolling 30-day attempt count per driver_id, unit_completeness_score from address_unit_present flag, historical_route_failure_rate from route_id failure history. Training Pipeline: LightGBM classifier with Optuna search over n_estimators (10-500), learning_rate (0.01-0.1), max_depth (3-15), feature_subset_tag from config.yaml. Prediction API: POST /predict accepts delivery_attempt JSON, returns {failure_probability: float, top_features: [{feature_name, importance: float}]}. LLM Narration: GPT-4o post-training step generating 3-paragraph research summary from sorted feature importances.
parsed.system_components: feature_pipeline module: DuckDB connection, delivery_attempts CSV reader, feature_sets view computation, Parquet export to GCS. training_pipeline module: LightGBM classifier, Optuna hyperparameter search, MLflow autolog, model artifact save to GCS. prediction_api module: FastAPI app with JWT validation, model loader from GCS, prediction endpoint. llm_narration module: OpenAI GPT-4o client, prompt construction from sorted feature importances, MLflow artifact logging, GCS research_log append. pipeline_runner script: orchestrates feature_pipeline then training_pipeline then llm_narration. Cloud Run service for FastAPI and MLflow server. GCS bucket research-delivery-risk/ with subdirectories data, models, experiments, research_logs. GCP Secret Manager for HMAC JWT secret and OpenAI API key.
parsed.workflows: Experiment Run: Researcher runs the pipeline_runner script with --experiment-tag feature_subset_v3 and --config config.yaml. Feature pipeline reads delivery_attempts CSV from GCS, computes feature_sets view, exports training Parquet to GCS. Training pipeline calls LightGBM with Optuna search, logs all trial metrics and feature importances to MLflow via autolog, saves best model artifact to GCS. Post-training, llm_narration module calls GPT-4o with sorted feature importances, logs response as research_narrative.txt MLflow artifact, appends to GCS research_log_v{run_id}.md. Model Evaluation: Researcher calls POST /predict with a delivery_attempt JSON, receives {failure_probability: 0.42, top_features: [{feature_name: 'time_window_deviation_minutes', importance: 0.23}, {feature_name: 'historical_route_failure_rate', importance: 0.18}]}. Researcher views MLflow experiment run in the MLflow UI to see feature importance chart and research_narrative.txt summary.
parsed.data_model: delivery_attempts DuckDB table: attempt_id VARCHAR PK, tracking_number VARCHAR, route_id VARCHAR, driver_id VARCHAR, scheduled_window_start TIMESTAMP, scheduled_window_end TIMESTAMP, actual_attempt_ts TIMESTAMP, outcome ENUM success/failed/rescheduled, failure_reason TEXT, address_geocoding_confidence FLOAT, address_unit_present BOOLEAN, historical_route_failure_rate FLOAT. feature_sets DuckDB view: computed per delivery_attempt from delivery_attempts table, columns: time_window_deviation_minutes FLOAT, geocoding_confidence_bucket ENUM high/medium/low, driver_attempt_rate_30d FLOAT, unit_completeness_score INT, historical_route_failure_rate FLOAT. model_experiments MLflow tracking: run_id, experiment_tag, n_estimators INT, learning_rate FLOAT, auc_roc FLOAT, log_loss FLOAT, precision_at_10pct FLOAT, recall_at_10pct FLOAT, top_feature_name STRING, feature_importances JSON. research_logs GCS: research_log_v{run_id}.md per experiment with timestamp, experiment_tag, model_version, auc_roc, top_features, research_narrative text.
parsed.api_design: FastAPI routes on Cloud Run: POST /predict (body: {tracking_number, route_id, driver_id, scheduled_window_start, scheduled_window_end, actual_attempt_ts, outcome, address_geocoding_confidence, address_unit_present, historical_route_failure_rate}; returns {failure_probability: float, top_features: [{feature_name: str, importance: float}]}; custom JWT required). GET /model-info (returns {model_version, training_date, feature_list, auc_roc}; custom JWT required). GET /health (returns {status: 'ok'}; no auth). All routes internal-only; Cloud Run ingress restricted to VPC. No external HTTP endpoints.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT: research team generates tokens via a helper script using a shared HMAC-SHA256 secret stored in GCP Secret Manager path /research/jwt-secret. FastAPI validates JWT on /predict and /model-info routes using python-jose. OpenAI API key stored in GCP Secret Manager path /research/openai-api-key, loaded at Cloud Run startup via Secret Manager environment variable injection. Cloud Run service account: roles/storage.objectAdmin on the research GCS bucket only; no broader IAM permissions. VPC Service Controls: research GCS bucket and Cloud Run service in a dedicated VPC-scoped project. No PII or financial data in feature set; historical delivery data is internal operational data.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest unit tests for feature engineering functions and prediction endpoint, Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy via gcloud CLI. No production approval gate; research environment only. Cloud Run services: prediction-api (FastAPI, 1 instance min 0 max 3, 1 vCPU/2GB, ingress internal-only) and mlflow-server (MLflow tracking server, 1 instance min 0 max 1, 1 vCPU/2GB). GCS bucket research-delivery-risk/ with uniform bucket-level access. GCP Secret Manager: /research/jwt-secret and /research/openai-api-key. All infrastructure managed via Terraform in the research project.
parsed.observability: MLflow: all experiment runs tracked with run_id, experiment_tag, auc_roc, log_loss, precision_at_10pct, recall_at_10pct, top_feature_name, and feature_importances JSON. MLflow experiment dashboard shows feature importance bar charts per run. Cloud Logging: structured JSON pipeline execution logs with run_id, feature_count, training_duration_s, narration_status (success/failure), and error_reason. No APM; research prototype does not require SLA monitoring. Researchers monitor MLflow experiment runs and Cloud Logging for pipeline execution status.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GCP costs: Cloud Run prediction API ~$0.01 per prediction at 1 prediction per minute (research pace); Cloud Run MLflow server ~$0.01 per hour; GCS storage ~$0.02 per GB per month; Secret Manager ~$0.01 per month. Total estimated ~$5-10 per month at research pace. Scaling: Cloud Run scales to 3 instances automatically; MLflow server scales to 1 instance. No auto-scaling needed for research prototype.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core Pipeline (2 weeks): feature_pipeline with DuckDB feature engineering, training_pipeline with LightGBM and Optuna, prediction_api with FastAPI and JWT validation, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 - LLM Narration (1 week): llm_narration module with GPT-4o prompt construction and MLflow artifact logging, GCP Secret Manager for OpenAI API key, research_log append to GCS. Phase 3 - Experiment Framework (1 week): MLflow experiment tags for feature_subset_v1-v5, precision_at_10pct and recall_at_10pct metrics, feature importance chart artifact, research_narrative.txt summary in MLflow UI.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting. pytest with 80% coverage for feature engineering functions and prediction endpoint. All MLflow experiment parameters and metrics must be logged via MLflow autolog; no manual logging of metrics to Cloud Logging. LLM narration prompt must be version-controlled in a narrations/prompts/ directory; prompt changes require a new prompt version tag. OpenAI API key must never appear in config.yaml or requirements.txt; always loaded from GCP Secret Manager.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o narration API errors halt experiment runs. Mitigation: llm_narration module catches openai.APIError and logs to Cloud Logging without halting the experiment; narration is non-blocking. Risk: Shared HMAC JWT secret is insufficient for production authentication. Mitigation: documented in prototype scope limitations; per-user JWT tokens should be implemented before productionization. Tradeoff: DuckDB over a dedicated feature store like Feast for research prototype simplicity; acceptable for this scale. Tradeoff: LightGBM over XGBoost for faster training on smaller research dataset.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed GPT-4o over GPT-4o-32 for narration to get full 4K context window for feature importance explanations. Confirmed MLflow GCS backend over local SQLite for experiment persistence across pipeline_runner runs. Confirmed shared HMAC JWT over per-user tokens for research prototype simplicity.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 has one focus issue: ISS-001, the missing LLM feature importance narration step. The llm_integration contract field explicitly requires GPT-4o to generate a natural language research summary after each training run. I will add a llm_narration module to the post-training pipeline that calls GPT-4o with the sorted feature importance JSON, logs the response as a research_narrative.txt MLflow artifact, and appends it to the GCS experiment research_log.md. The step is non-blocking so API failures do not halt experiment runs. OpenAI API key stored in GCP Secret Manager.

fix_report.action_taken: Added llm_narration module to the post-training pipeline. After LightGBM training and MLflow metric logging, the pipeline calls llm_narration.generate(run_id, sorted_importances) which: loads OpenAI API key from GCP Secret Manager at runtime, calls GPT-4o with a structured logistics-domain prompt containing the sorted feature importance JSON, logs the response as research_narrative.txt MLflow artifact, appends the narrative with experiment metadata to the GCS research_log.md file for the experiment. GPT-4o API errors are caught and logged to Cloud Logging as non-fatal; the pipeline continues and marks narration_status='failed' in MLflow tags.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Each completed experiment run produces an LLM-generated natural language feature importance summary in MLflow and GCS, satisfying the llm_integration contract requirement and accelerating researcher interpretation of model outputs

reference.title: Last-Mile Delivery Failure Risk ML Prototype v2
reference.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 research prototype predicting last-mile delivery failure risk using a LightGBM gradient boosting classifier trained on features derived from historical delivery attempt outcomes, address quality signals, time-window adherence patterns, and driver behavior metrics. The feature engineering pipeline uses DuckDB to compute derived features from raw delivery_attempts data and export training Parquet files to GCS. LightGBM training uses Optuna hyperparameter search with MLflow autolog for experiment tracking. After each training run, a GPT-4o narration step generates a natural language research summary of feature importances logged as an MLflow artifact. A FastAPI prediction endpoint on GCP Cloud Run allows researchers to score individual delivery attempts for model evaluation.
reference.architecture_overview: The prototype runs as three sequential pipeline stages orchestrated by a Python runner script. The feature_pipeline stage reads delivery_attempts CSV from GCS into DuckDB, computes derived features in the feature_sets view (time_window_deviation_minutes, geocoding_confidence_bucket, driver_attempt_rate_30d, unit_completeness_score, historical_route_failure_rate), and exports a training Parquet file back to GCS. The training_pipeline stage loads the Parquet into LightGBM, runs an Optuna study for hyperparameter optimization (n_estimators 100-500, learning_rate 0.01-0.2, num_leaves 20-100), logs all trial metrics via MLflow autolog, and saves the best model as a .pkl artifact to GCS. The llm_narration stage calls GPT-4o via the openai Python SDK with the sorted feature importance dict and a structured logistics domain prompt, receiving a 3-paragraph research narrative logged as research_narrative.txt in MLflow and appended to the GCS research_log.md. The prediction_api is a separately deployed FastAPI Cloud Run service that loads the latest model artifact from GCS at startup and serves failure_probability scores.
reference.technology_stack: ML: Python 3.12, LightGBM 4.3, scikit-learn 1.5, Optuna 3.6 for hyperparameter search. Feature store: DuckDB 0.10. Experiment tracking: MLflow 2.13 with GCS artifact store. LLM: OpenAI GPT-4o via openai Python SDK 1.30. API: FastAPI 0.111, Uvicorn, python-jose for JWT validation. GCP: Cloud Run for prediction API and MLflow server, GCS for data and artifacts, Secret Manager for API keys, Cloud Logging for structured pipeline logs, Artifact Registry for Docker images. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with ruff, mypy, pytest.
reference.functional_feature_map: Feature Engineering: DuckDB computes 5 delivery attempt features from raw data: time_window_deviation_minutes (actual_attempt_ts minus scheduled_window_end), geocoding_confidence_bucket (high/medium/low bins on address_geocoding_confidence float), driver_attempt_rate_30d (rolling 30-day attempt count per driver_id), unit_completeness_score (1 if address_unit_present else 0), historical_route_failure_rate (30-day failure count / total attempts for route_id). Model Training: LightGBM binary classifier (outcome=failed as positive class) with Optuna study, MLflow autolog capturing per-trial AUC-ROC, log-loss, and feature importances. LLM Narration: GPT-4o generates 3-paragraph research summary of feature importances after each completed training run. Prediction API: FastAPI endpoint scoring individual delivery attempts using loaded model artifact. Experiment Comparison: MLflow UI for comparing AUC-ROC and feature importances across feature subset experiments.
reference.system_components: Feature Pipeline: Python script executing DuckDB SQL feature engineering and GCS Parquet export. Training Pipeline: LightGBM + Optuna training loop with MLflow autolog and model artifact save. llm_narration module: openai SDK caller with Secret Manager key loading, MLflow artifact logger, and GCS research_log appender. FastAPI Prediction API: Cloud Run service loading model from GCS on startup, JWT-protected prediction endpoint. MLflow Tracking Server: Cloud Run service with GCS backend store at gs://research-delivery-risk/mlflow. GCS bucket research-delivery-risk/: /data for raw CSVs, /models for .pkl artifacts, /experiments for Parquet exports and research_log.md files. GCP Secret Manager: /research/openai-api-key and /research/jwt-secret paths.
reference.workflows: Experiment Run Workflow: researcher runs python run_experiment.py --config config.yaml --feature-subset all|core5|no-driver. Feature pipeline executes DuckDB SQL, exports training.parquet to GCS. Training pipeline loads Parquet, runs Optuna study (50 trials by default), MLflow autolog captures per-trial metrics, best model saved as model_{run_id}.pkl to GCS. llm_narration.generate(run_id, importances) called: Secret Manager loads OPENAI_API_KEY, openai.ChatCompletion.create called with prompt 'You are a logistics research analyst reviewing a last-mile delivery failure risk model. The following feature importances were computed from a LightGBM classifier trained on {n_samples} delivery attempts (failure rate {failure_rate}%). Write a 3-paragraph summary: (1) which features are most predictive and why, (2) any surprising patterns or low-importance features, (3) recommended next feature engineering experiments. Feature importances (sorted): {importances_json}'. Response logged as MLflow artifact research_narrative.txt and appended to gs://research-delivery-risk/experiments/{run_id}/research_log.md. On GPT-4o failure: Cloud Logging error with narration_status=failed tag, pipeline continues. Prediction Evaluation Workflow: researcher POSTs a delivery_attempt JSON to FastAPI /predict, receives failure_probability float and top_3_features list, uses response to validate model behavior on specific scenarios.
reference.data_model: delivery_attempts DuckDB table: attempt_id VARCHAR PK, tracking_number VARCHAR, route_id VARCHAR, driver_id VARCHAR, scheduled_window_start TIMESTAMP, scheduled_window_end TIMESTAMP, actual_attempt_ts TIMESTAMP, outcome VARCHAR CHECK('success','failed','rescheduled'), failure_reason TEXT, address_geocoding_confidence FLOAT, address_unit_present BOOLEAN, historical_route_failure_rate FLOAT -- loaded from GCS CSV. feature_sets DuckDB view: attempt_id, time_window_deviation_minutes FLOAT (actual_attempt_ts - scheduled_window_end in minutes), geocoding_confidence_bucket VARCHAR, driver_attempt_rate_30d FLOAT, unit_completeness_score INT, historical_route_failure_rate FLOAT, failure_label INT (1 if outcome='failed' else 0). model_experiments MLflow tracking: run_id, experiment_name, feature_subset_tag, n_estimators, learning_rate, num_leaves, auc_roc, log_loss, precision_at_10pct, top_feature (tag). Artifacts per run: plotly_feature_importance.html, model_{run_id}.pkl, research_narrative.txt (LLM output), training.parquet reference.
reference.api_design: FastAPI prediction API on Cloud Run internal ingress. POST /predict: request {attempt_id: string, address_geocoding_confidence: float, address_unit_present: bool, time_window_deviation_minutes: float, driver_attempt_rate_30d: float, historical_route_failure_rate: float}, response {failure_probability: float, failure_label_predicted: bool, top_features: [{feature: string, importance: float}] top 3, model_version: string}. GET /model-info: response {model_version: string, trained_at: datetime, feature_count: int, training_auc_roc: float}. GET /health: response {status: 'ok', model_loaded: bool}. JWT required on /predict and /model-info via Authorization Bearer header. Custom JWT validated with python-jose using HMAC-SHA256 secret from GCP Secret Manager.
reference.security_and_compliance: Cloud Run service account: roles/storage.objectAdmin on research-delivery-risk GCS bucket; roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor on /research/* Secret Manager paths. No public Cloud Run ingress; internal-only ingress setting. OpenAI API key loaded at container startup from Secret Manager via Secret Manager environment variable injection (OPENAI_API_KEY env var). JWT secret loaded similarly (JWT_SECRET env var). No API keys in code, config.yaml, or Docker image. VPC Service Controls perimeter around research GCP project preventing data exfiltration. Research data contains no PII; delivery_attempts uses internal route_id and driver_id codes without personal names.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run services: prediction-api (0-3 instances, 1 vCPU/2GB, internal ingress, min-instances=0) and mlflow-server (1 instance, 0.5 vCPU/1GB, internal ingress). GCS bucket created via Terraform or gcloud. GitHub Actions CI: (1) ruff and mypy on PR, (2) pytest for feature engineering functions and FastAPI endpoint with mocked LightGBM model and mocked openai responses, (3) Docker build with Artifact Registry push, (4) Cloud Run deploy via gcloud run deploy on main merge. MLflow tracking URI configured in config.yaml as https://mlflow-server-{hash}-uc.a.run.app. Reproducibility: full pipeline reproduced by installing requirements.txt (pinned versions) and running python run_experiment.py --config config.yaml with the same GCS data path.
reference.observability: MLflow experiment tracking: per-run metrics (auc_roc, log_loss, precision_at_10pct, recall_at_10pct), parameters (n_estimators, learning_rate, feature_subset), tags (top_feature, narration_status), artifacts (feature importance chart, model pkl, research_narrative.txt). Cloud Logging structured logs: pipeline stage start/end with run_id, feature_count, training_duration_s, optuna_best_trial, narration_status (success/failed/skipped). Cloud Run request logs for prediction API. Researchers monitor MLflow UI for experiment comparison; no PagerDuty or SLA alerting for research prototype.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run prediction API: 0 instances at rest (scale-to-zero), ~$5/month for intermittent research usage. MLflow server Cloud Run: ~$10/month. GCS storage for data and model artifacts: ~$5/month for expected research data volume. OpenAI GPT-4o narration: ~$0.015 per experiment run (1K input tokens feature importances + 500 output tokens). Total: ~$25/month. No database infrastructure cost; DuckDB runs in-process. Scales to larger historical datasets by increasing DuckDB memory limit in config.yaml.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (completed round 1): feature_pipeline with 5 core derived features, baseline LightGBM training with MLflow autolog, FastAPI prediction endpoint, GCS artifact storage, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (this round): llm_narration module with GPT-4o integration, Secret Manager API key management, narration artifact logging to MLflow and GCS research_log.md, non-blocking error handling for API failures. Phase 3 (research roadmap): Optuna hyperparameter search expanded to include feature selection (XGBoost SHAP-based feature pruning), additional feature experiments (weather API integration for delivery window conditions), feature drift detection comparing training data distribution against recent dispatch data, model card generation for productionization handoff.
reference.development_guidelines: Python type hints on all public functions; mypy strict mode. Feature engineering functions have unit tests with synthetic delivery_attempts fixtures covering boundary cases (attempt exactly at window end, null geocoding confidence). openai SDK calls mocked in pytest via unittest.mock for CI reproducibility without API key. MLflow runs in pytest via mlflow.set_tracking_uri('sqlite:///test.db') for isolated test tracking. config.yaml schema validated with pydantic settings on pipeline startup. DuckDB queries in dedicated .sql files under src/queries/ not inline strings for readability. LightGBM model pkl saved with model version tag in filename for artifact traceability.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o output quality for feature importance narration depends on prompt quality; initial prompts may produce generic summaries not tailored to delivery domain. Mitigation: include delivery domain context (failure rate, n_samples, feature definitions) in the system prompt and iterate based on researcher feedback. Risk: DuckDB in-memory processing may run out of memory for historical datasets exceeding available Cloud Run RAM. Mitigation: configure DuckDB memory_limit in config.yaml; large datasets processed in chunks via DuckDB streaming. Tradeoff: LightGBM gradient boosting over neural network approaches enables interpretable feature importances at the cost of modeling complex non-linear interactions; acceptable for research validation phase. Tradeoff: shared HMAC JWT secret over per-researcher tokens simplifies prototype auth at the cost of individual auditability; documented as upgrade requirement for productionization.
reference.open_questions_resolved: LLM integration gap resolved: GPT-4o narration module added to post-training pipeline. OpenAI API key management via GCP Secret Manager confirmed. Feature drift detection deferred to Phase 3 roadmap; not blocking current research validation. MLflow GCS backend confirmed over SQLite for multi-researcher access.

raw:
{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a federated learning research prototype for cross-pharma toxicity model training. Key architectural decisions: Flower framework for federated coordination, mTLS with Azure Key Vault for partner certificate management, differential privacy Gaussian noise at epsilon=1.0 for gradient protection, and PostgreSQL at the aggregator for round performance logging. The prototype is intentionally stateless for the aggregator (ACI) to match the Azure-first constraint. Partner nodes run independently in their own Azure subscriptions.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "Cross-Pharma Federated Toxicity Model Training Prototype",
 "executive_summary": "A research prototype evaluating federated learning for training toxicity prediction models across multiple pharma company data

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a federated learning research prototype for cross-pharma toxicity model training. Key architectural decisions: Flower framework for federated coordination, mTLS with Azure Key Vault for partner certificate management, differential privacy Gaussian noise at epsilon=1.0 for gradient protection, and PostgreSQL at the aggregator for round performance logging. The prototype is intentionally stateless for the aggregator (ACI) to match the Azure-first constraint. Partner nodes run independently in their own Azure subscriptions.
parsed.title: Cross-Pharma Federated Toxicity Model Training Prototype
parsed.executive_summary: A research prototype evaluating federated learning for training toxicity prediction models across multiple pharma company data silos using Azure Container Instances and the Flower federated learning framework. Partner nodes run PyTorch GCN on their local hERG toxicity datasets, submit gradient updates to the aggregator via mTLS-secured gRPC channels, and receive a globally updated model without sharing raw compound structures. Aggregator applies FedAvg with differential privacy Gaussian noise and evaluates the global model on the Tox21 hERG benchmark after each round.
parsed.architecture_overview: Stateless aggregator (Azure Container Instance) runs a Flower gRPC server that coordinates federated rounds with registered partner nodes. Each partner node runs a Flower NumPyClient container that trains a PyTorch GCN on its local hERG dataset for N local epochs, computes gradient updates, and submits them to the aggregator via mTLS-secured gRPC. Aggregator applies FedAvg aggregation, injects Gaussian differential privacy noise at epsilon=1.0, and stores round metrics in PostgreSQL. Global model artifacts are checkpointed to Azure Blob Storage. Partner nodes are deployed independently in each partner's Azure subscription; no raw compound data crosses company boundaries.
parsed.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Flower 0.12, PyTorch 2, PyTorch Opacus for DP noise, grpc, azure-keyvault-secrets. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server for round metrics. Storage: Azure Blob Storage for global model checkpoint artifacts. Hosting: Azure Container Instances for aggregator, partner nodes in partner-owned Azure subscriptions. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, PostgreSQL federation_rounds table.
parsed.functional_feature_map: FedAvg Gradient Aggregation: aggregator receives gradient updates from all participating nodes, computes weighted average, applies Gaussian DP noise, updates global model weights. Local Model Training: partner node trains PyTorch GCN on hERG dataset for N local epochs, computes gradient update, submits to aggregator. mTLS Authentication: aggregator validates partner node client certificate CN against approved_partners list before accepting gradient update. Global Model Evaluation: Tox21 hERG benchmark evaluation after each round; AUC logged to federation_rounds. Partner Node Management: certificate issuance, CRL management, status tracking in participating_nodes PostgreSQL table.
parsed.system_components: Aggregator ACI (Flower gRPC server, certificate_manager, fl_aggregator Strategy subclass, model_evaluator). Partner Node Docker Container (Flower NumPyClient, PyTorch GCN trainer, local hERG dataset loader). Azure Key Vault (aggregator server certificate and CA private key, partner node client certificate public keys). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (federation_rounds, participating_nodes, global_model_artifacts). Azure Blob Storage (global model checkpoint artifacts). Azure Container Registry (aggregator and partner node Docker images).
parsed.workflows: Federated Training Round: Aggregator initiates round via gRPC broadcast to all registered participating_nodes; each node responds with local gradient update; aggregator applies FedAvg, injects DP noise, updates global model weights; stores round metrics in federation_rounds; evaluates global model on Tox21 hERG benchmark; logs AUC and loss. Partner Node Onboarding: Partner engineer requests certificate; CA issues client certificate; partner installs cert in local cert store; partner engineer runs Docker pull fl-client:v{tag} and installs; registers partner_id with aggregator via gRPC; aggregator adds to participating_nodes with status active. Certificate Revocation: Partner engineer notifies administrator; administrator removes partner_id from approved_partners; CRL issued; next round aggregator rejects any gradient from revoked partner_id.
parsed.data_model: PostgreSQL aggregator: federation_rounds (id UUID PK, round_number INT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, participating_nodes INT, global_model_auc FLOAT, global_model_loss FLOAT, dp_epsilon_applied FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). participating_nodes (id UUID PK, partner_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, certificate_cn VARCHAR, last_seen_round INT, status ENUM active/revoked, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). global_model_artifacts (id UUID PK, round_id UUID FK, model_state_dict_blob_path VARCHAR, checkpoint_hash VARCHAR(64), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Local partner data (never transmitted): herg_assay_data (compound_smiles VARCHAR, label ENUM active/inactive, assay_protocol VARCHAR, source_dataset VARCHAR).
parsed.api_design: gRPC: Flower protocol. Aggregator server: FlowerServer.GetServer(). AddFlowerStrategy(fl_aggregator.Strategy()). Partner node client: FlowerClient.connect('aggregator_endpoint:50051', cert_reqs='CERT_REQUIRED'). Aggregator REST (internal): GET /api/v1/rounds?round_number= (list round metrics), GET /api/v1/rounds/{id}/evaluation (Tox21 AUC result), POST /api/v1/participants (register partner node). Partner node REST (internal): POST /api/v1/certificates (request certificate), GET /api/v1/certificates/{id} (check status). All REST endpoints require mTLS client certificate.
parsed.security_and_compliance: mTLS: all aggregator-node gRPC channels use TLS 1.3 with server certificate issued by internal CA stored in Azure Key Vault; each partner node presents a unique client certificate also issued by internal CA. Certificate CN encodes partner_id for audit logging. GDPR: differential privacy Gaussian noise at epsilon=1.0, delta=1e-5 satisfies GDPR Article 25 data minimization for gradient sharing. Inter-company data sharing agreements: no raw SMILES or fingerprint vectors transmitted; only gradient weight tensors exchanged. Gradient update poisoning detection: L2-norm bounds enforced; updates > 3 standard deviations from round mean rejected. Certificate revocation: CRL issued on revocation; next round aggregator rejects any gradient from revoked partner_id.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Aggregator: Azure Container Instance with custom image fl-aggregator:v{tag} pulled from Azure Container Registry on startup. Environment variables: AGGREGATOR_KEY_VAULT_URL, AGGREGATOR_CERTIFICATE_ID, AGGREGATOR_DB_URL, AGGREGATOR_BLOB_URL. Partner node: Docker container fl-client:v{tag} deployed in partner's own Azure subscription. Partner onboarding: partner engineer runs cert issuance script; cert stored in partner's local cert store; partner engineer runs Docker pull and Docker run. Azure DevOps Pipelines: pytest for FedAvg aggregation unit tests, mTLS handshake integration test, DP noise calibration test, Docker build and push to ACR on Git tag. Partner deployment: documented in partner onboarding guide.
parsed.observability: Azure Monitor container metrics for aggregator ACI: CPU utilization, memory, restart count. PostgreSQL federation_rounds table as primary observability artifact: round_number, participating_nodes, global_model_auc, global_model_loss, dp_epsilon_applied. Azure Monitor alert if aggregator ACI exits unexpectedly. Partner node logs remain local; no log shipping to aggregator. Custom metric: dp_epsilon_consumed per round tracked in federation_rounds.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Aggregator ACI: ~$0.05 per hour at low CPU utilization. PostgreSQL Flexible Server: ~$50 per month at prototype scale. Azure Blob Storage: ~$5 per month for checkpoint artifacts. Total: ~$60 per month at prototype scale. Scaling: ACI scales to 0 between rounds; PostgreSQL scales vertically if round frequency increases.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): FedAvg with DP noise, mTLS with Azure Key Vault, 2-3 simulated partner nodes, hERG GCN model, Tox21 evaluation, round logging to PostgreSQL, partner onboarding guide. Phase 2 Evaluation (4 weeks): Live partner node deployment with 2-3 partner companies, per-class AUC monitoring for non-IID detection, DP epsilon tuning, model inversion attack evaluation. Phase 3 Production (4 weeks): Secure aggregation (cryptographic gradient protection), web management UI for partner onboarding and round monitoring, production multi-partner deployment across 3-5 pharma companies.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations. pytest for all FedAvg aggregation logic, mTLS handshake, DP noise, and CRL validation. Docker images versioned with Git tag; ACR tag policy requires semver. Partner onboarding guide documented in /docs/partner_onboarding.md. All certificate management logic in certificate_manager module; no hardcoded cert paths. DP epsilon tuning results tracked in experiment_results CSV.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Non-IID compound distributions across partner nodes cause FedAvg to diverge or produce biased global model. Mitigation: monitor per-class AUC across rounds; if AUC drops > 5% for any class, trigger early evaluation. Risk: Azure Key Vault throttling during rapid ACI restarts causes aggregator startup failure. Mitigation: retry certificate_manager startup with exponential backoff. Tradeoff: FedAvg over FedProx; FedAvg is simpler but may converge slower for non-IID data; FedProx can be evaluated in Phase 2 if convergence issues arise. Tradeoff: epsilon=1.0 for DP noise; this is a conservative choice for small hERG datasets; empirical evaluation in Phase 2 may show higher epsilon is sufficient.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Flower chosen over TensorFlow Federated for Python-native API and better PyTorch integration. FedAvg chosen over FedProx for Phase 1 simplicity; FedProx deferred to Phase 2. DP epsilon set to 1.0 with delta=1e-5 as starting point; empirical evaluation in Phase 2 will determine if higher epsilon is sufficient for hERG gradient updates.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a federated learning prototype with partner_facing exposure and confidential IP data, requiring mTLS for all inter-node communication and differential privacy on gradient updates. Key decisions: Flower for FL coordination, PyTorch GCN for molecular graph representation of compounds, Azure Container Instances for the aggregator, PostgreSQL at aggregator only (never partner data), Azure Key Vault for certificate management. The GDPR and inter-company data sharing compliance context drives the no-raw-data constraint and DP noise requirement.
reference.title: Federated Toxicity Prediction Prototype - Cross-Pharma FL Evaluation
reference.executive_summary: A research prototype on Azure implementing federated learning across simulated pharma partner nodes to train a hERG toxicity prediction GCN model without sharing raw compound structures. The Flower-based aggregator runs in Azure Container Instances, uses FedAvg with Gaussian differential privacy noise on aggregated gradients, and communicates with partner nodes via mTLS gRPC channels. Global model performance is evaluated on the public Tox21 hERG benchmark after each round; round metrics and model artifacts are stored at the aggregator in PostgreSQL and Azure Blob Storage. No compound SMILES or assay data ever leaves partner boundaries.
reference.architecture_overview: Hub-and-spoke federated architecture: a central Flower aggregator in Azure Container Instances coordinates federation rounds with partner Flower client containers running in each company's own Azure subscription. Communication uses gRPC with mTLS mutual authentication (unique per-partner client certificates validated against approved_partners list). FedAvg aggregation applies Gaussian DP noise (epsilon=1.0) to the aggregated gradient before global model update. Each partner node trains a PyTorch GCN on its local hERG assay dataset for a fixed number of local epochs, returns gradient weight updates (not raw data) to the aggregator, and receives the updated global model. Aggregator PostgreSQL stores only round performance metrics and model artifact references; no partner compound data reaches the aggregator.
reference.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Federated learning: Flower 1.8 (gRPC server/client). Model: PyTorch 2.3, PyTorch Geometric 2.5 for GCN layers, RDKit 2024.3 for SMILES-to-molecular-graph conversion. Differential privacy: PyTorch Opacus for Gaussian noise injection on gradients. Communication: gRPC with grpc-tools, mTLS via Python ssl module and Azure Key Vault. Databases: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (aggregator metrics). Storage: Azure Blob Storage for model checkpoints. Infrastructure: Azure Container Instances, Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault, Azure Monitor. CI: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Benchmark data: Tox21 hERG from DeepChem datasets.
reference.functional_feature_map: Federation Round Coordinator: Flower server implements custom FedAvgWithDP strategy; broadcasts global model to available partner clients at round start; collects gradient updates; applies FedAvg; injects Gaussian noise; updates global model; evaluates on Tox21 hERG benchmark; logs round metrics to PostgreSQL. Partner Node Client: Flower NumPyClient loads local hERG assay data; converts SMILES to molecular graphs via RDKit; trains PyTorch GCN for 5 local epochs; returns gradient update as NumPy arrays via gRPC. Certificate Manager: loads aggregator server cert from Azure Key Vault; validates partner client certificate CN against approved_partners on each connection. Model Evaluation: after each aggregation round, evaluates global model on Tox21 hERG held-out test set, computing AUC-ROC, AP, and accuracy; results stored in federation_rounds.
reference.system_components: Flower gRPC Server (aggregator, ACI container, port 50051 mTLS): FedAvgWithDP strategy, certificate validation, round state management. Flower gRPC Client (partner node, local Docker container): GCNTrainer, local dataset loader, gradient preparation. GCNTrainer: PyTorch Geometric GCN with 3 message-passing layers, global mean pooling, binary classification head for hERG active/inactive. DPNoiseInjector: PyTorch Opacus GradSampleModule wrapping aggregated gradients; Gaussian noise at epsilon=1.0, delta=1e-5. CertificateManager: Azure Key Vault SDK for aggregator cert; approved_partners table in PostgreSQL for CN validation. ModelEvaluator: Tox21 hERG benchmark loader from DeepChem; AUC-ROC computation per round. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (federation_rounds, participating_nodes, global_model_artifacts). Azure Blob Storage (model checkpoint state_dicts). Azure Container Registry (fl-aggregator and fl-client versioned images).
reference.workflows: Federation Round Execution: aggregator Flower server waits for min_available_clients=2 partner connections on gRPC port 50051; validates each connecting client certificate CN against approved_partners; broadcasts current global model weights as NumPy arrays; each partner client receives global model, loads into local PyTorch GCN, trains on local hERG dataset for 5 epochs with Adam optimizer (lr=0.001), computes gradient delta (trained_weights - global_weights), returns gradient as NumPy arrays via gRPC; aggregator computes FedAvg weighted by partner dataset size; DPNoiseInjector applies Gaussian noise to aggregated gradient; global model updated; ModelEvaluator computes Tox21 AUC-ROC; federation_rounds row inserted with participating_nodes count, global AUC, loss, dp_epsilon_applied; round repeated for N_rounds=50. Partner Onboarding: aggregator admin generates unique client certificate for partner (signed by internal CA); certificate CN set to partner_id; participating_nodes row inserted with status=active; partner receives Docker pull command for fl-client image and cert installation script; partner deploys container in their Azure subscription and connects to aggregator gRPC endpoint.
reference.data_model: PostgreSQL aggregator: federation_rounds (id UUID PK, round_number INT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, participating_nodes INT, global_model_auc_roc FLOAT, global_model_avg_precision FLOAT, global_model_loss FLOAT, dp_epsilon_applied FLOAT, fedavg_weights_json JSONB). participating_nodes (id UUID PK, partner_id VARCHAR unique, certificate_cn VARCHAR, onboarded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_round INT, status ENUM active/revoked, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). global_model_artifacts (id UUID PK, round_id FK, blob_storage_path VARCHAR, checkpoint_hash_sha256 VARCHAR, model_arch_version VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Local partner data (never transmitted to aggregator): herg_compounds (smiles VARCHAR, label TINYINT 0/1, assay_protocol VARCHAR, source_dataset VARCHAR); stored as CSV or SQLite at partner site.
reference.api_design: No REST API. Flower gRPC protocol surfaces: aggregator gRPC endpoint fl.aggregator.internal:50051 (mTLS required). Flower server methods called by client: get_parameters() -> model weights NumPy, fit(parameters, config) -> updated weights NumPy + metrics dict, evaluate(parameters, config) -> loss FLOAT + metrics dict. Aggregator admin CLI: python fl_admin.py add-partner --partner-id PHARMA_B --cert-cn pharma-b-node-1 (inserts participating_nodes row). python fl_admin.py revoke-partner --partner-id PHARMA_B (updates status=revoked, adds CN to CRL). python fl_admin.py run-federation --rounds 50 --min-clients 2 (launches Flower server).
reference.security_and_compliance: mTLS on all Flower gRPC channels: aggregator presents certificate signed by internal CA; partner clients present unique certificates with CN=partner_id; aggregator validates client cert CN against approved_partners before accepting any gradient update. Certificate revocation: remove from approved_partners and regenerate CRL; CRL reloaded at each round startup. Gradient poisoning defense: gradient L2-norm computed per partner; updates with norm > mean + 3*std rejected with participating_node alert. Differential privacy: Gaussian noise with epsilon=1.0, delta=1e-5 applied to aggregated gradient using Opacus; DP budget tracked per round in federation_rounds. No raw compound SMILES transmitted: only PyTorch gradient NumPy arrays cross company boundaries; gRPC binary format provides no human-readable compound data. Azure Key Vault for aggregator certificate storage. GDPR compliance: no personal data processed; compound toxicity data is not personal data under GDPR Article 4(1); gradient updates treated as confidential IP under inter-company data sharing agreements.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Aggregator deployed as Azure Container Instance in resource group fl-prototype-rg (East US). ACI started on-demand per federation session via az container create with gRPC port 50051 exposed via internal VNET; not exposed to public internet. Azure Container Registry stores fl-aggregator:v{tag} and fl-client:v{tag} images built by Azure DevOps Pipelines on Git tags. Partner nodes: partner teams run docker pull {acr}/fl-client:v{tag} in their own Azure subscription; cert installation script provided in onboarding package. Azure DevOps Pipeline: pytest unit tests (FedAvg aggregation, DP noise calibration, mTLS handshake mock), Docker build and push to ACR, integration test with 2 simulated partner containers on CI runner. No persistent aggregator service between federation sessions; ACI deleted after each experiment run.
reference.observability: Azure Monitor container metrics for aggregator ACI: CPU, memory, network. PostgreSQL federation_rounds table as the primary observability record: per-round global AUC-ROC, loss, participating_nodes count, DP epsilon consumed, round duration. Python logging in fl_aggregator to stdout (captured by ACI log stream): connection events, certificate validation failures, gradient norm outlier rejections, round completion events. Azure Monitor alert on unexpected ACI container exit. No observability data transmitted from partner nodes to aggregator; partner node stdout logs captured locally by partner's own infrastructure.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $50-100/month for prototype: ACI on-demand per session (~$0.0012/vCPU-second, 4 vCPU = ~$1.20/hour per run), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server Burstable B1ms (~$15/month), Azure Blob Storage for model checkpoints (~$1/month for 50 round checkpoints at 10MB each), Azure Container Registry Basic (~$5/month). Federation round runtime: 50 rounds x 3 minutes/round (local training + gRPC transfer) = ~2.5 hours/session. Scaling to 10 partner nodes requires no aggregator infrastructure change; Flower scales to 100s of clients on ACI with minimal CPU increase.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Simulated FL Prototype (8 weeks): aggregator and partner node containers, FedAvg with DP noise, mTLS authentication, 2-3 simulated partner nodes (same company, partitioned hERG dataset), Tox21 benchmark evaluation, PostgreSQL round metrics. Phase 2 - Privacy Evaluation (4 weeks): model inversion attack simulation against aggregated gradients, DP epsilon sensitivity analysis (epsilon 0.1-10 vs AUC-ROC), gradient poisoning defense validation, non-IID compound distribution impact assessment. Phase 3 - Live Partner Deployment (4 weeks): onboard 1-2 real pharma partner nodes with live hERG assay data, partner onboarding runbook, certificate lifecycle procedures, inter-company data sharing agreement review.
reference.development_guidelines: FedAvgWithDP strategy must include unit tests verifying: gradient aggregation produces weighted average by dataset size, Gaussian noise with given epsilon/delta is applied, gradient norm outliers are rejected. mTLS integration test simulates valid and revoked client certificate connections and asserts correct accept/reject behavior. Partner client container must be tested in an isolated Docker network to confirm it cannot reach any aggregator infrastructure other than gRPC port 50051. Opacus DP noise parameters (epsilon, delta, max_grad_norm) must be logged to federation_rounds and never hardcoded in model training code; injected via config file. All gradient exchange serialization uses NumPy float32 to bound transmission size.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Gaussian DP noise at epsilon=1.0 significantly degrades GCN model accuracy on small hERG partner datasets (500-2,000 compounds); mitigated by empirical epsilon sweep in Phase 2 and accepting higher epsilon if accuracy loss exceeds 5% AUC-ROC. Risk: Non-IID compound distributions (e.g., one partner has only congeneric series) cause FedAvg to produce biased global model; mitigated by monitoring per-partner local AUC after applying global model and switching to FedProx if heterogeneity is severe. Risk: ACI restart re-establishes Key Vault connection; Key Vault throttling could delay round start; mitigated by retry logic with exponential backoff. Tradeoff: FedAvg over FedProx sacrifices convergence on heterogeneous data for implementation simplicity; appropriate for prototype evaluation phase. Risk: partner certificate compromise enables adversarial gradient submission; mitigated by gradient norm anomaly detection and revocation procedure.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Flower over PySyft for cleaner gRPC abstractions and active maintenance. Chose PyTorch GCN over fingerprint-based MLP because molecular graph representation captures structural features relevant to hERG channel binding without requiring predefined fingerprint bit strings. Confirmed FedAvg as starting aggregation strategy; FedProx deferred to Phase 2 based on non-IID analysis. Accepted epsilon=1.0 as initial DP budget; sensitivity analysis scheduled for Phase 2. mTLS chosen over API keys for partner authentication to avoid shared-secret management across companies.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the final medium issue: the Regulations.gov comment link must be conditionally hidden after the comment deadline passes, and a nightly GitHub Actions scheduled workflow must be added to trigger a production incremental build on days when comment periods close. The overall architecture is sound from round 2 — Gatsby 5 on GovCloud CloudFront with USWDS, incremental builds, axe-core and Flesch-Kincaid CI checks, and per-build CSP nonce. The fix is a template conditional and a scheduled workflow addition.

fix_report.action_taken: Added comment_deadline conditional in ProposedRuleDetail Gatsby template: if comment_deadline is in the past, renders a 'Comment period closed' notice with a link to the final rule page instead of the Regulations.gov comment button. Added a GitHub Actions scheduled workflow that triggers on cron schedule '0 0 23 * *' (11 PM UTC) to run a Gatsby incremental build and deploy to production if any proposed_rules content node has a comment_deadline within the next 24 hours. This ensures the production site reflects closed comment periods before the business day begins.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Regulations.gov comment link correctly hidden after comment period closes; public notice accuracy consistent with APA publication requirements; comment period status reflected in production within 24 hours of closure

parsed.title: Federal Regulatory Agency Public Website
parsed.executive_summary: A compliance-hardened Gatsby 5 static website on AWS GovCloud CloudFront + S3, publishing proposed and final regulatory rules, public comment dockets, enforcement actions, and agency guidance documents for a federal regulatory agency. The site meets Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA, Plain Writing Act Flesch-Kincaid grade 12, and FedRAMP Moderate hosting requirements. Proposed rule detail pages conditionally hide the Regulations.gov comment link after the comment deadline passes, and a nightly GitHub Actions scheduled workflow ensures production reflects closed comment periods within 24 hours.
parsed.architecture_overview: Gatsby 5 TypeScript monorepo built on GitHub Actions, producing fully static HTML/CSS/JS output. Content is sourced from Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in a Git repository, with PDF regulatory documents stored in an S3 GovCloud public bucket. CloudFront distribution serves the static output with 1-hour TTL for stable rule documents and 10-minute TTL for active docket pages. A CloudFront WAF managed rule group blocks common web exploits. HSTS preloading and per-build CSP nonce via gatsby-plugin-csp ensure third-party script security. Dynatrace RUM provides real user performance monitoring. GitHub Actions scheduled workflow triggers a nightly incremental build on comment closure days.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Gatsby 5, TypeScript, React 18, USWDS 3.x, gatsby-plugin-csp, gatsby-plugin-image, gatsby-source-filesystem. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter, JSON content files. Hosting: AWS GovCloud CloudFront + S3 us-gov-west-1. Analytics: Dynatrace RUM (FedRAMP-authorized GovCloud instance). CI: GitHub Actions with axe-core, Flesch-Kincaid readability check, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. WAF: AWS CloudFront WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Proposed Rules Index: active comment period countdowns, Regulations.gov comment link visible only when comment_deadline is in the future, Federal Register citation and URL. Proposed Rule Detail: comment_deadline conditional — 'Comment period closed' notice with link to final rule when deadline passes; Regulations.gov comment link visible only when deadline is in the future. Final Rules Archive: year/subject-matter filters, Federal Register citation, PDF download. Enforcement Actions: case status badges (open/settled/dismissed), settlement amount, order date. Public Dockets: comment count, comment period open/closed status. Agency Guidance: abstract, issued date, PDF download. Press Releases: headline, published date, PDF download. RSS feeds for each regulatory category.
parsed.system_components: GitHub Actions runner (Gatsby build, axe scan, Flesch-Kincaid check, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation). S3 GovCloud bucket (static output, regulatory PDFs, CloudFront origin). CloudFront distribution (static delivery, WAF, HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options). CloudFront WAF (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, rate limiting 5,000/5min per IP). Dynatrace RUM (GovCloud instance, real user monitoring). GitHub Actions scheduled workflow (nightly comment closure build trigger).
parsed.workflows: Proposed Rule Publication: content editor merges Markdown with comment_deadline date; Gatsby incremental build runs on push to main; axe-core scan and Flesch-Kincaid check run; if comment_deadline is in the future, Regulations.gov comment button visible; if comment_deadline is in the past, ProposedRuleDetail template renders 'Comment period closed' notice with link to final rule. Comment Period Closure: GitHub Actions scheduled workflow triggers at 11 PM UTC; checks proposed_rules content nodes for comment_deadlines within the next 24 hours; for each node with a deadline within 24 hours, runs a Gatsby incremental build and deploys to production; CloudFront invalidation issued for affected paths; production site reflects closed comment periods before business day begins.
parsed.data_model: proposed_rules (slug, docket_id, title, summary_plainlanguage, comment_deadline, status ENUM open/closed/withdrawn, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, published_at). final_rules (slug, docket_id, title, effective_date, cfr_citation, federal_register_citation, pdf_s3_key, related_proposed_rule_slug). enforcement_actions (slug, case_number, respondent_name, violation_type, case_status ENUM open/settled/dismissed, settlement_amount, order_date). public_dockets (docket_id, title, comment_count, comment_period_open BOOLEAN, last_updated). agency_guidance (slug, title, guidance_type, issued_date, pdf_s3_key). press_releases (slug, headline, published_at, pdf_s3_key). All entities stored as Markdown YAML frontmatter in Git; PDFs in S3 GovCloud public bucket.
parsed.api_design: No server-side API. Client-side Gatsby GraphQL queries: proposedRules({status: 'open', limit: 10}) -> [{slug, docket_id, title, comment_deadline, status, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation}]. proposedRuleDetail(slug: string) -> {proposed_rules: {slug, docket_id, title, summary_plainlanguage, comment_deadline, status, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation}, comment_deadline_in_past: boolean}. enforcementActions({status: 'open', limit: 10}) -> [{slug, case_number, respondent_name, violation_type, case_status, settlement_amount, order_date}]. All queries resolved at build time; no runtime API calls.
parsed.security_and_compliance: HSTS preloading: CloudFront response headers policy with Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; must be approved by agency CIO and submitted to HSTS preload list. CSP: gatsby-plugin-csp generates per-build nonce via build-time env variable injection; nonce injected into Content-Security-Policy header; Dynatrace RUM script tag includes nonce attribute matching the header. SRI: SHA-256 hashes on all third-party script tags; automated rotation on script version updates. X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking. CloudFront WAF: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet blocks SQL injection, XSS, and common web exploits; rate limiting 5,000 requests per 5 minutes per IP. Section 508: axe-core CI scan on 30 sampled pages; zero critical violations required before production deploy. Plain Writing Act: Flesch-Kincaid grade level check on summary_plainlanguage, violation_description, and abstract fields; grade 12 or higher fails build. FedRAMP: AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1; Dynatrace GovCloud instance; no third-party SaaS with data-processing terms outside FedRAMP boundary.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: Gatsby incremental build (target under 10 minutes), axe-core scan (zero critical violations required), Flesch-Kincaid readability check on all content fields, S3 sync to GovCloud bucket, CloudFront invalidation for changed paths. Environments: Production (CloudFront + S3 us-gov-west-1, agency domain), Staging (S3 website endpoint, PR branch previews). Production deploy: push to main triggers build and deploy; PR triggers staging preview. Comment closure workflow: cron schedule '0 0 23 * *' triggers nightly build for comment deadlines within 24 hours. Rollback: S3 object versioning; previous version restored via S3 sync from previous GitHub Actions run.
parsed.observability: Dynatrace RUM: real user performance monitoring with page load times, JavaScript error rates, and visitor behavior. Dynatrace synthetic monitor: pings /proposed-rules every 5 minutes; alert if response time exceeds 3 seconds. CloudFront access logs to S3 GovCloud: 90-day retention for traffic analysis. GitHub Actions audit log: all content deployments recorded with commit SHA and timestamp. Alerting: Dynatrace alert if RUM error rate exceeds 1% over 5 minutes; CloudFront 5xx rate exceeds 0.5% over 5 minutes; axe scan fails with critical violations.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $180/month: CloudFront data transfer ($0.008/GB) for moderate regulatory traffic; S3 storage for PDFs and static output ($0.023/GB); CloudFront WAF ($5/month); Dynatrace RUM GovCloud ($150/month for moderate traffic). Scales to high traffic volumes via CloudFront edge caching; no scaling configuration required for static content.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, and comment docket migration from legacy CMS; automated Section 508 axe-core CI check; per-build CSP nonce for Dynatrace RUM; Flesch-Kincaid readability check on all content fields; CloudFront + S3 GovCloud hosting; HSTS preloading submission. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Agency guidance documents, press releases, RSS feeds, Spanish-language rule summaries (Year 2 digital strategy). Phase 3 (4 weeks): Full-text search via Pagefind, Federal Register cross-reference linking, RSS subscription widgets.
parsed.development_guidelines: Gatsby TypeScript strict mode. USWDS 3.x components only for regulatory content pages; no custom React components without USWDS equivalent. All regulatory content stored as Markdown YAML frontmatter; no hardcoded content in JavaScript files. Flesch-Kincaid readability check must pass on all summary_plainlanguage, violation_description, and abstract fields before production deploy. Comment_deadline conditional in ProposedRuleDetail template must be tested with both open and closed deadline dates in staging. GitHub Actions scheduled workflow must be tested with a mock comment closure scenario before enabling cron trigger.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HSTS preloading submission is irreversible; agency CIO approval required before submission. Mitigation: test on staging for minimum 6 months before production submission. Risk: Regulations.gov API changes break comment link rendering. Mitigation: Regulations.gov is a federal government service; API changes are rare; fallback to static comment period notice if API is unavailable. Tradeoff: Gatsby incremental builds over full rebuilds reduces build time but requires careful content node management during large regulatory corpus migrations. Tradeoff: CloudFront 10-minute TTL for active docket pages means a single S3 sync invalidation does not immediately update docket status pages; acceptable given docket status is updated by content editors, not by automated systems.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed per-build CSP nonce via gatsby-plugin-csp over static nonce for Dynatrace RUM compatibility. Confirmed CloudFront WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet over custom rules for FedRAMP audit simplicity. Confirmed S3 GovCloud public bucket for PDFs over private bucket with pre-signed URLs to avoid FedRAMP authorization complexity. Confirmed nightly GitHub Actions scheduled workflow over a Lambda-triggered build for comment closure events to avoid Lambda cold start latency.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 of the federal regulatory agency static website. ISS-001 (static CSP nonce) and ISS-002 (incomplete Plain Writing Act check) were resolved in rounds 1 and 2 respectively. This round addresses ISS-003: the Regulations.gov comment submission link remaining visible on proposed rule pages after the comment_deadline passes. The fix requires two changes: a template conditional in ProposedRuleDetail and a GitHub Actions scheduled nightly build to update pages on comment-period-closing days without waiting for a content commit.

fix_report.action_taken: Added comment_deadline conditional in the ProposedRuleDetail Gatsby template: if today's date > frontmatter.comment_deadline, render a 'Comment period closed on {date}' notice with a link to the comment summary document instead of the Regulations.gov submission button. Added a GitHub Actions workflow scheduled at 6am ET daily using a cron trigger that runs gatsby build --incremental only on days when any proposed_rule has a comment_deadline matching today's date, ensuring closed-period pages are updated before the business day. Flesch-Kincaid check extended to also scan the comment_summary_description field added to proposed_rules frontmatter
fix_report.expected_outcome: Proposed rule pages accurately reflect comment period status within hours of deadline passing; public users see 'Comment period closed' rather than a link that Regulations.gov will reject; APA public participation accuracy requirement satisfied

reference.title: Federal Regulatory Agency Public Website - Round 3
reference.executive_summary: A compliance-hardened static website for a federal regulatory agency on AWS GovCloud CloudFront plus S3, publishing proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, and public comment dockets under strict Section 508 WCAG 2.1 AA, Plain Writing Act, and Administrative Procedure Act publication requirements. Built with Gatsby 5 and USWDS 3.x components, the site enforces accessibility and readability in CI and uses a nightly scheduled build to keep comment period status accurate for proposed rule pages. This revision resolves the final open issue: the Regulations.gov comment submission link incorrectly shown after comment deadlines pass.
reference.architecture_overview: Fully static delivery architecture using Gatsby 5 generating HTML at build time for proposed rules, final rules, enforcement actions, docket listings, guidance documents, and press releases. Source content lives as structured Markdown with YAML frontmatter and JSON files in a Git monorepo. Gatsby's GraphQL data layer ingests all content nodes at build time and applies ProposedRuleDetail, FinalRuleDetail, EnforcementActionDetail, and DocketListing templates with USWDS 3.x components. The ProposedRuleDetail template conditionally renders a Regulations.gov comment submission link or a 'Comment period closed' notice based on the comment_deadline frontmatter field evaluated against build time. Gatsby static output is deployed to S3 GovCloud and distributed via CloudFront with path-based TTL policies. A GitHub Actions nightly scheduled workflow runs an incremental Gatsby build on days when comment deadlines are reached, updating closure notices without requiring a content commit. No server-side runtime exists — all content is statically rendered.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Gatsby 5 with TypeScript, USWDS 3.x component library, gatsby-plugin-csp for per-build CSP nonce injection for Dynatrace RUM. Analytics: Dynatrace Real User Monitoring (GovCloud instance) with IP anonymization, session recording disabled. Infrastructure: AWS GovCloud CloudFront with WAF (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet), S3 us-gov-west-1 with versioning and OAC, Route 53, ACM GovCloud for TLS. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with axe-core scan, Flesch-Kincaid readability scorer, S3 sync via aws s3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter and JSON in Git monorepo.
reference.functional_feature_map: Proposed Rules: detail pages with comment_deadline countdown while open; 'Comment period closed on {date}' notice with comment summary link when deadline passed. Final Rules: archive with CFR citation, Federal Register link, PDF download, and related proposed rule cross-reference. Enforcement Actions: case listings with respondent name, violation type, case_status badge (open/settled/dismissed), and settlement amount. Public Dockets: per-docket page with comment count and open/closed status. Agency Guidance: document library with guidance_type filter and PDF download. Press Releases: chronological archive with date and headline. All pages: USWDS breadcrumb navigation, skip-to-content link, Section 508 compliant table structures for regulatory listings.
reference.system_components: CloudFront distribution (WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, rate limiting 5,000 req/5min per IP, OAC for S3 origin), S3 GovCloud public-read-via-OAC bucket with versioning, GitHub Actions CI runner (Gatsby build, axe-core sampler, Flesch-Kincaid CI script, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation), GitHub Actions scheduled nightly workflow (comment deadline checker — scans proposed_rules frontmatter for comment_deadline == today, triggers incremental Gatsby build if any match), gatsby-plugin-csp (per-build nonce injection for Dynatrace RUM), Dynatrace RUM tag loaded via USWDS-compatible nonce-tagged script injection, ProposedRuleDetail template (comment_deadline conditional: submission link vs. closure notice).
reference.workflows: Proposed Rule Comment Period Close: GitHub Actions cron runs at 6am ET daily. Script scans all proposed_rules Markdown frontmatter for comment_deadline matching today's date (America/New_York). If any match, triggers gatsby build --incremental. ProposedRuleDetail template evaluates comment_deadline at build time: if build_date > comment_deadline, renders 'Comment period closed on {formatted_date}. View comment summary.' with link to comment_summary_document_url instead of the Regulations.gov submission button and external link disclaimer. Incremental build completes in under 3 minutes for a single changed content node. S3 sync and CloudFront invalidation scoped to affected proposed-rule paths complete within 5 minutes. Public users see accurate closure status before 7am ET on the day comment periods close. New Enforcement Action Published: agency staff creates enforcement_actions/2026-ea-042.md in the Git repository with frontmatter fields. PR reviewed by communications officer confirms Flesch-Kincaid grade level of violation_description is under 12. PR merged to main triggers GitHub Actions production build. axe-core scans the rendered enforcement action page — passes required before S3 sync. Updated enforcement actions listing and detail page live within 8 minutes of merge.
reference.data_model: proposed_rules frontmatter (slug, docket_id, title, summary_plainlanguage, comment_deadline DATE, comment_summary_document_url: str | null, status ENUM open/closed/withdrawn, regulations_gov_url, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, agency_contact, published_at). final_rules frontmatter (slug, docket_id, title, effective_date, cfr_citation, federal_register_citation, federal_register_url, pdf_s3_key, related_proposed_rule_slug: str | null, published_at). enforcement_actions frontmatter (slug, case_number, respondent_name, violation_type, violation_description, case_status ENUM open/settled/dismissed, settlement_amount: number | null, order_date, published_at). public_dockets frontmatter (docket_id, title, comment_count: number, comment_period_open BOOLEAN, last_updated). agency_guidance frontmatter (slug, title, guidance_type, abstract, issued_date, pdf_s3_key). press_releases frontmatter (slug, headline, summary, published_at, pdf_s3_key). All content stored as Markdown + JSON in Git; PDFs in S3 GovCloud with versioning.
reference.api_design: No server-side API endpoints. Static data access patterns: Gatsby GraphQL queries content nodes at build time from gatsby-source-filesystem; no runtime API calls from server. Client-side: Dynatrace RUM script loaded via nonce-tagged script tag. Regulations.gov integration: external link to https://www.regulations.gov/docket/{docket_id}/comment only rendered when comment_deadline >= build_date in ProposedRuleDetail template. Federal Register cross-references: static links to federalregister.gov using federal_register_citation frontmatter field. No fetch calls in browser JavaScript except Dynatrace RUM telemetry to Dynatrace GovCloud endpoint.
reference.security_and_compliance: HTTPS-only enforced via CloudFront with HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload) — preload list submission pending agency CIO sign-off after 6-month staging validation. CSP per-build: gatsby-plugin-csp injects a unique nonce per build into both the Dynatrace RUM script tag and the Content-Security-Policy header; nonce value sourced from BUILD_NONCE environment variable set by GitHub Actions. SRI applied to all third-party script and stylesheet tags. X-Frame-Options: DENY. CloudFront WAF AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet blocks known exploit patterns; rate limiting at 5,000 req/5min per IP. S3 OAC ensures direct S3 URL access is blocked — all traffic must traverse CloudFront. Dynatrace RUM configured with IP anonymization enabled and session recording disabled per agency privacy impact assessment. No cookies set by the site itself; Dynatrace may set a first-party analytics cookie with 13-month retention.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions production workflow on push to main: (1) gatsby build with BUILD_NONCE env variable set to uuid, GATSBY_BUILDING_FOR_PROD=true; (2) axe-core scan on 30 sampled pages — fail CI on any critical or serious violation; (3) Flesch-Kincaid script scoring proposed_rules.summary_plainlanguage, final_rules frontmatter summary, enforcement_actions.violation_description, agency_guidance.abstract — fail CI if any field scores above grade 12; (4) aws s3 sync --delete to GovCloud bucket; (5) aws cloudfront create-invalidation scoped to changed paths. PR workflow deploys to staging S3 prefix for reviewer preview. Nightly scheduled workflow at 6am ET: Python script enumerates proposed_rules frontmatter for comment_deadline == today (America/New_York); if any match, runs gatsby build --incremental followed by targeted S3 sync and invalidation. Full deploy time: under 10 minutes. Nightly incremental: under 4 minutes. Secrets: BUILD_NONCE injected per workflow run; no persistent secrets stored in GitHub Actions beyond AWS OIDC role ARN.
reference.observability: Dynatrace RUM (GovCloud instance) tracks page load performance (Largest Contentful Paint, First Input Delay, Cumulative Layout Shift) per page type — proposed rule pages, enforcement action listings, and docket detail pages segmented separately. Dynatrace synthetic monitor pings /proposed-rules and /enforcement-actions every 5 minutes from US-East and US-West synthetic nodes; alert on HTTP non-200 or LCP > 3 seconds triggers email to agency digital team. CloudFront access logs to S3 GovCloud bucket with 90-day retention; AWS Athena queries analyze traffic by content type and identify high-demand regulatory documents. GitHub Actions deployment log provides audit trail for each content change including commit author, changed content files, and build status.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $600/month at 500,000 monthly page views: S3 GovCloud storage $30/month for 20GB regulatory content and PDFs, CloudFront transfer $400/month at $0.085/GB GovCloud pricing, CloudFront requests $80/month, Route 53 $10/month, ACM $0, GitHub Actions under free tier for public repo. Dynatrace GovCloud RUM licensing separate from infrastructure budget. CloudFront auto-scales to handle traffic surges around major regulatory announcements (final rule publications, enforcement actions against prominent firms) without pre-provisioning.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Proposed rules, final rules, and enforcement actions migrated from legacy CMS; automated Section 508 axe-core CI check; Flesch-Kincaid check on proposed rule summaries; Dynatrace RUM with IP anonymization; CloudFront + S3 GovCloud deployment with HSTS and CSP. Phase 2 Content Parity (8 weeks): Public dockets, agency guidance, press release archive migrated; Flesch-Kincaid check extended to enforcement action and guidance summaries (ISS-002 resolved in round 2); comment period closure handling (ISS-003 resolved in round 3); legacy CMS decommission. Phase 3 Enhancements (future): Pagefind full-text search index, RSS feeds per regulatory category, Federal Register citation auto-linking, Spanish-language rule summaries.
reference.development_guidelines: All Markdown content frontmatter must include a last_reviewed_date field set at publication. Proposed rule pages must include comment_summary_document_url when the rule has a closed comment period — CI warns if comment_deadline is in the past and comment_summary_document_url is null. USWDS components must not be modified inline — override via CSS custom properties only. PDF links must have an HTML summary page for Section 508 compliance — CI checks for the presence of a matching HTML summary slug for each pdf_s3_key. PR descriptions must confirm Plain Writing Act Flesch-Kincaid score of any new summary text is under grade 12.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Nightly comment deadline build does not trigger if GitHub Actions is down at 6am ET; a proposed rule page could show an open comment button for up to 24 hours after deadline on a GitHub outage day. Mitigation: Dynatrace synthetic monitor checks /proposed-rules homepage for stale open-comment notices as a secondary detection signal; documented manual deploy runbook for GitHub outages. Risk: HSTS preloading is irreversible for the agency domain — if a future migration requires temporary HTTP access it will be impossible. Mitigation: 6-month staging validation period and agency CIO sign-off required before preload list submission. Tradeoff: Gatsby incremental builds over full builds for nightly deadline updates — incremental reduces comment-close latency from 10 minutes to 3 minutes but requires careful gatsby-cache management to prevent stale node artifacts.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Dynatrace GovCloud instance (FedRAMP authorized) over Google Analytics for APA privacy compliance. Selected gatsby-plugin-csp over manual CSP injection for per-build nonce reliability (resolving ISS-001). Confirmed nightly scheduled GitHub Actions build over Regulations.gov webhook integration for comment period close detection — webhook integration would require server-side infrastructure absent from the static website contract. Flesch-Kincaid grade threshold of 12 confirmed with agency Plain Language coordinator as consistent with Plain Writing Act implementation guidance.

raw:
{
