parsed.workflows: POS Transaction Ingestion: Toast webhook fires for each transaction; VendorAdapterService validates payload schema, applies Toast-specific field mappers, replaces card_last4 with 'XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-####', publishes to raw.pos.toast Kafka topic; NormalizationJob consumes, applies canonical schema, writes to normalized.pos.transactions; AggregationJob computes revenue_snapshots and inventory_depletion events; ClickHouse writes revenue_snapshots; alert engine evaluates par_thresholds, writes to alerts.inventory.depletion topic. Malformed Event Handling: VendorAdapter detects malformed Toast payload (missing required fields); publishes to dlq.pos.toast; DLQConsumerService logs to S3 dlq-logs/{vendor}/{timestamp}.jsonl, sends PagerDuty alert to on-call, exposes /replay endpoint for vendor support to reprocess problematic batch. Nightly Reconciliation: ECS one-off task runs Python reconciliation script; queries ClickHouse daily aggregates for all locations; queries Toast settlement report API; writes variance records to reconciliation_runs table; alerts on any variance >$500.
parsed.data_model: pos_transactions (event_id UUID, location_id UUID, register_id UUID, transaction_ts DateTime, items_json JSON, subtotal_cents UInt32, tax_cents UInt32, tender_type Enum8('cash'=1,'card'=2,'mobile_pay'=3), card_last4_masked String, vendor String, ingested_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY (location_id, transaction_ts) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(transaction_ts). menu_items (item_id UUID, sku String, name String, category String, current_price_cents UInt32, is_tracked_inventory Bool). inventory_depletions (alert_id UUID, location_id UUID, item_id UUID, shift_period_id UUID, units_sold UInt32, par_threshold UInt32, triggered_at DateTime). revenue_snapshots (snapshot_id UUID, location_id UUID, window_start DateTime, window_end DateTime, gross_revenue_cents UInt32, transaction_count UInt32, avg_ticket_cents UInt32). shift_periods (period_id UUID, location_id UUID, shift_type Enum8('breakfast'=1,'lunch'=2,'dinner'=3,'late'=4), start_ts DateTime, end_ts DateTime).
parsed.api_design: Internal REST API (FastAPI, /api/v1): POST /alerts/thresholds — create or update per-location per-item par_thresholds. GET /alerts/thresholds/{location_id} — retrieve current thresholds. GET /alerts/history?location_id=&item_id=&start=&end= — retrieve past depletion events. GET /reconciliation/summary — daily variance summary. GET /health — liveness probe. Kafka topics: raw.pos.toast (3 partitions), raw.pos.square (future), normalized.pos.transactions (64 partitions), dlq.pos.toast (1 partition), dlq.normalization (1 partition), alerts.inventory.depletion (1 partition), alerts.reconciliation.variance (1 partition).
parsed.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS SAQ-A scope reduction: card last-4 masked to 'XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-####' at VendorAdapter ingestion point before any Kafka publish; masked value stored in ClickHouse only; no unmasked card data in pipeline. mTLS via AWS ACM Private CA for all Flink-ClickHouse and Flink-Kafka connections. TLS 1.3 on all Kafka connections. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; ECS task roles with least-privilege IAM policies. S3 raw archives encrypted with SSE-KMS. CloudTrail for all AWS API actions. PCI ASV scans quarterly on network perimeter. Internal data governance policies for financial reporting integrity.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in private VPC subnets. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint for Python, golangci-lint for Go, unit tests, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy via task definition update. Flink job deployment: S3 JAR upload to s3://flink-jobs/ and ECS task restart. ClickHouse schema migrations: Flyway-compatible migration scripts run as ECS one-off tasks. Environments: dev (single AZ, reduced MSK broker count), staging (multi-AZ, full MSK), production (multi-AZ, 3-broker MSK). Rollback: Flink savepoints before each deployment; revert ECS task definition and restore prior savepoint from S3. VendorAdapter rollback: ECR image tag revert. DLQConsumerService: 1 container per environment, auto-scaled 1-2 replicas.
parsed.observability: Prometheus scraping Flink JMX metrics (consumer lag per partition, checkpoint duration, records processed/sec) and Go adapter metrics (vendor event count, error rate, DLQ depth). Grafana dashboards: pipeline lag heatmap by location_id, DLQ depth by vendor, alert engine trigger rate, revenue snapshot count. PagerDuty alerts: consumer lag >60 seconds on any partition, DLQ depth >100, AggregationJob checkpoint duration >30 seconds, VendorAdapter error rate >5%.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $7,200/month: MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.large ~$900, ClickHouse 2x r6i.2xlarge ~$2,400, Fargate for Flink jobs ~$800, ElastiCache Redis ~$150, Grafana EC2 ~$50, S3 ~$30, misc ~$200. Budget headroom thin; vertical scaling of ClickHouse requires r6i.4xlarge upgrade (~$4,800/month) which exceeds budget. Horizontal scaling of Flink NormalizationJob requires MSK partition count increase.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Toast vendor adapter, NormalizationJob and AggregationJob Flink jobs, ClickHouse schema and revenue_snapshots, inventory_depletion alert engine, Grafana revenue dashboard, DLQConsumerService, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Square vendor adapter, NCR Aloha adapter, per-item par_threshold configuration API, nightly reconciliation job, Grafana inventory depletion heatmap. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Additional vendor adapters, predictive demand forecasting ML model consuming pipeline output, mobile alert app for district managers.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python: ruff for linting, pytest for unit tests with 80% coverage gate, PyFlink unit tests in testcontainers. Go: golangci-lint, go test with 90% coverage gate. Flink jobs must have exactly-once semantics enabled via Kafka transactions. Card last-4 masking must be tested with unit test asserting masked value matches expected pattern. All Flink savepoints must be versioned in S3 with timestamp prefix. ClickHouse schema migrations must be reversible.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Flink checkpoint overhead under peak 10K tx/min load causes consumer lag exceeding sub-minute SLA. Mitigation: tune checkpoint interval to 30 seconds; monitor checkpoint duration metric in Grafana. Risk: ClickHouse on EC2 r6i.2xlarge is single-point of failure for OLAP. Mitigation: add ClickHouse replica; promote to primary on failover. Tradeoff: PyFlink on Fargate over managed Flink on AWS Kinesis Data Analytics for custom stateful aggregation logic and checkpoint control. Tradeoff: ClickHouse on EC2 over managed ClickHouse on AWS Redshift for sub-second query performance tuning flexibility.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected PyFlink over managed Flink on Kinesis Data Analytics for custom stateful aggregation logic and checkpoint control. Confirmed ClickHouse on EC2 over managed Redshift for sub-second query performance tuning. Chose DLQConsumerService as separate Go service rather than Flink DLQ consumer for independent scaling and replay endpoint. Masking confirmed at VendorAdapter ingestion point rather than ClickHouse post-processing to eliminate PCI compliance gap.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues from the auditor: DLQ absence and card masking timing. The DLQ fix requires defining explicit dead-letter topics per vendor and normalization stage with a DLQ consumer service. The card masking fix moves the operation to the VendorAdapter Go service at the point of Kafka publish, before any data hits the normalized topic. Both fixes must be reflected in system_components, workflows, data_model, and security_and_compliance sections to show coherent resolution across the plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined dead-letter topics (dlq.pos.toast, dlq.normalization) with a dedicated DLQ Consumer service that logs malformed events to S3, emits Prometheus metric dlq_events_total, and triggers PagerDuty alert when DLQ depth exceeds 50. Added DLQ topics to Kafka topology and DLQ Consumer to system_components. Updated failure_handling in workflows section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Malformed POS events are isolated to DLQ topics without stalling normalization partition consumers; ops team has replay capability via S3 archive

fix_report.action_taken: Moved card last-4 masking to VendorAdapterService at Kafka publish time. NormalizationMapper now receives pre-masked events. ClickHouse tables only ever receive masked card_last4_masked field. Updated data_model, security_and_compliance, and workflows sections to reflect masking at ingestion boundary.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No unmasked card metadata exists in Kafka topics, ClickHouse, or S3 archives at any point in pipeline lifecycle; PCI-DSS scope reduction claim is defensible

reference.title: Multi-Location POS Streaming Pipeline v2
reference.executive_summary: A real-time data pipeline on AWS processing 10,000 POS transactions per minute from 50+ fast-casual restaurant locations. Go vendor adapters ingest Toast (and future Square, NCR Aloha) events into Kafka MSK, applying card-data masking at publish time. PyFlink normalization and aggregation jobs produce sub-minute revenue snapshots and inventory depletion alerts into ClickHouse, served via Grafana ops dashboards. Dead-letter queues isolate malformed vendor payloads without stalling location data streams.
reference.architecture_overview: Event-driven pipeline with three processing layers on AWS ECS Fargate. Layer 1: Go VendorAdapterService instances per POS vendor poll vendor APIs and produce to raw Kafka topics (raw.pos.toast), applying card last-4 masking before publish. Layer 2: PyFlink NormalizationJob consumes raw topics and emits canonical pos_transaction events to normalized.pos.transactions (64 partitions). Layer 3: PyFlink AggregationJob runs 1-minute tumbling windows keyed by location_id computing revenue_snapshots and evaluating inventory par thresholds to emit inventory_depletion alerts. ClickHouse on EC2 r6i.2xlarge stores OLAP data with MergeTree partitioning by month. Redis 7 provides alert deduplication state. Malformed events at any stage route to dead-letter topics consumed by DLQConsumerService for S3 archival and alerting.
reference.technology_stack: Stream Processor: Apache Flink 1.18 (PyFlink) on ECS Fargate. Vendor Adapters: Go 1.22 with Kafka-go client. Message Broker: Kafka MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.large, 64 partitions. OLAP: ClickHouse 24.x on 2x EC2 r6i.2xlarge. Alert State: ElastiCache Redis 7 cache.r6g.large. Dashboards: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse and Prometheus datasources. CI/CD: GitHub Actions + ECR + ECS rolling deploy. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager.
reference.functional_feature_map: POS Vendor Ingestion: Toast webhook receiver and API poller, with DLQ fallback for malformed payloads. Schema Normalization: vendor-specific mappers (ToastMapper, future SquareMapper) producing canonical pos_transaction events. Real-Time Aggregation: 1-minute tumbling revenue windows per location, running sales counters per menu item per shift. Inventory Alert Engine: threshold evaluation against par levels triggering Kafka alert events and Slack/Grafana annotations. Nightly Reconciliation: batch comparison of ClickHouse aggregates vs vendor settlement files writing variance records. DLQ Management: malformed event capture, S3 archival, replay tooling.
reference.system_components: VendorAdapterService (Go ECS Fargate): one task per vendor, polls Toast API every 5 seconds, masks card last-4 at publish, produces to raw.pos.toast topic. NormalizationJob (PyFlink ECS): consumes raw topics, applies vendor mapper, emits to normalized.pos.transactions, routes unparseable events to dlq.normalization. AggregationJob (PyFlink ECS): stateful keyed streams by location_id with 1-minute tumbling windows, writes revenue_snapshots to ClickHouse, emits inventory_depletion events to alerts.inventory.depletion topic. DLQConsumerService (Go ECS): consumes dlq.pos.toast and dlq.normalization, writes events to S3 dlq-archive bucket with metadata, emits dlq_events_total Prometheus metric, triggers PagerDuty when depth >50. ClickHouse Cluster: 2x r6i.2xlarge primary+replica, MergeTree pos_transactions and AggregatingMergeTree revenue_snapshots. Kafka MSK: 3-broker kafka.m5.large, 64 partitions on normalized topic. ElastiCache Redis 7: sorted sets for per-location per-item running counters. Grafana: ops dashboards with ClickHouse and Prometheus datasources.
reference.workflows: POS Transaction Ingestion and Normalization: VendorAdapterService receives Toast webhook POST /webhooks/toast or polls GET /orders?since={last_cursor}, extracts transaction fields, masks card last-4 to 'XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-####' in memory before Kafka produce, publishes raw.pos.toast message with location_id, register_id, transaction_ts, items_json, subtotal_cents, tender_type, card_last4_masked. If payload fails schema validation, publishes to dlq.pos.toast instead and logs reason. NormalizationJob ToastMapper transforms raw event to canonical pos_transaction schema and produces to normalized.pos.transactions. Malformed canonical events route to dlq.normalization. Inventory Depletion Alert Flow: AggregationJob maintains Redis sorted sets keyed as 'sales:{location_id}:{item_id}:{shift_period_id}' with ZINCRBY on each transaction. On each window close, evaluates ZSCORE against par threshold from menu_items.par_threshold field. When units_sold >= par_threshold, produces inventory_depletion event to alerts.inventory.depletion Kafka topic, which Grafana annotation webhook and Slack adapter consume. Alert deduplication via Redis SET 'alert:dedup:{location_id}:{item_id}:{shift_period_id}' with 1-hour TTL.
reference.data_model: pos_transactions (event_id UUID, location_id VARCHAR, register_id VARCHAR, transaction_ts DateTime, items_json String, subtotal_cents Int64, tax_cents Int64, tender_type Enum('cash','card','mobile_pay'), card_last4_masked VARCHAR, vendor Enum('toast','square','ncr_aloha'), ingested_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(transaction_ts) ORDER BY (location_id, transaction_ts). revenue_snapshots (snapshot_id UUID, location_id VARCHAR, window_start DateTime, window_end DateTime, gross_revenue_cents Int64, transaction_count UInt32, avg_ticket_cents Int64) ENGINE=AggregatingMergeTree ORDER BY (location_id, window_start). menu_items (item_id VARCHAR, sku VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, category VARCHAR, current_price_cents Int32, par_threshold_per_shift UInt16, is_tracked_inventory Bool) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree. inventory_depletions (alert_id UUID, location_id VARCHAR, item_id VARCHAR, shift_period_id VARCHAR, units_sold UInt32, par_threshold UInt16, triggered_at DateTime) ENGINE=MergeTree. location_registers (register_id VARCHAR, location_id VARCHAR, vendor VARCHAR, last_seen_at DateTime, status Enum('active','offline')). shift_periods (period_id VARCHAR, location_id VARCHAR, shift_type Enum('breakfast','lunch','dinner','late'), start_ts DateTime, end_ts DateTime). vendor_feeds (feed_id UUID, vendor VARCHAR, location_id VARCHAR, last_successful_poll DateTime, events_received_1h UInt32, health_status Enum('healthy','degraded','offline')). reconciliation_runs (run_id UUID, run_date Date, location_id VARCHAR, ch_gross_revenue_cents Int64, vendor_settlement_cents Int64, variance_cents Int64, variance_pct Decimal32(4)).
reference.api_design: Alert Configuration REST API (FastAPI): POST /v1/alerts/thresholds — body {location_id, item_id, par_threshold, shift_type}, JWT auth required, writes to menu_items ClickHouse table. GET /v1/alerts/thresholds/{location_id} — returns all par configurations for location. GET /v1/alerts/history?location_id=&start=&end= — queries inventory_depletions with time range. GET /v1/pipeline/health — returns per-vendor feed health from vendor_feeds table. GET /v1/dlq/summary — DLQ depth per topic from Prometheus metrics API. POST /v1/dlq/replay/{topic}/{event_id} — triggers reprocessing of S3-archived DLQ event.
reference.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS scope reduction: card last-4 masked to 'XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-####' by VendorAdapterService in Go memory before Kafka produce — no unmasked card data exists in any Kafka topic, ClickHouse table, S3 archive, or log. ClickHouse stores only masked field card_last4_masked VARCHAR. Transport: TLS 1.3 on all MSK connections; mTLS via AWS ACM Private CA between Flink jobs and ClickHouse and between Flink and MSK. Secrets: vendor API credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; ECS task roles use least-privilege IAM for MSK produce/consume and S3 write. JWT RS256 for Alert API with 1-hour expiry. CloudTrail audit on all AWS API calls. Quarterly ASV network scans on VPC perimeter. DLQ events in S3 encrypted with SSE-KMS. Internal data governance: reconciliation variance >5% triggers compliance review workflow.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster: VendorAdapterService (1 task per vendor, 0.5 vCPU / 1 GB), NormalizationJob (4 tasks, 2 vCPU / 8 GB each for Flink memory), AggregationJob (4 tasks, 2 vCPU / 8 GB), DLQConsumerService (1 task, 0.25 vCPU / 512 MB). MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.large in private VPC subnets us-east-1a/1b/1c. ClickHouse primary + replica on r6i.2xlarge EC2 with EBS gp3 3000 IOPS. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (90% coverage gate on mapper logic), golangci-lint, Docker build + ECR push, ECS rolling deploy via task definition update. Flink savepoints to S3 before each deploy. ClickHouse DDL migrations via versioned SQL scripts run as ECS one-off tasks with pre-migration snapshot. Environments: dev (single-AZ, shared MSK dev cluster), staging (multi-AZ, production-mirror MSK), production (multi-AZ, dedicated MSK).
reference.observability: Prometheus scraping: Flink JMX (flink_jobmanager_job_uptime, flink_taskmanager_job_task_buffers_inputQueueLength, records_in/out per operator), Go adapter metrics (pos_events_ingested_total by vendor+location, pos_events_masked_total, dlq_events_total by topic). Grafana dashboards: pipeline lag heatmap by location (Kafka consumer group lag), DLQ depth per vendor topic, revenue aggregation freshness (seconds since last revenue_snapshot write per location), inventory alert trigger rate. Alert rules: consumer_lag_seconds > 60 triggers PagerDuty P2; dlq_events_total rate > 10/min triggers P1; vendor_feed health_status = 'offline' for >2 minutes triggers P2; ClickHouse insert latency p99 > 500ms triggers P3. Structured JSON logs from all services with fields: service, location_id, vendor, event_id, action, duration_ms, error.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated monthly: MSK 3x kafka.m5.large ~$900, ClickHouse 2x r6i.2xlarge ~$1,240, Fargate (NormalizationJob + AggregationJob 8 tasks) ~$1,800, Redis cache.r6g.large ~$280, Fargate (adapters + DLQ) ~$150, S3 + data transfer ~$200, total ~$4,570/month well within $8,000 budget. Scaling: Flink parallelism increase by adding ECS tasks (NormalizationJob scales to 64 tasks matching Kafka partitions); ClickHouse scales vertically to r6i.4xlarge at 50K tx/min; Redis sorted sets tested to 5 million keys at sub-1ms latency. Add new POS vendor by deploying new VendorAdapter ECS task and adding vendor-specific Kafka topic.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Toast vendor adapter with DLQ, NormalizationJob with ToastMapper, AggregationJob with 1-minute revenue windows, inventory alert engine for top 20 items, Grafana ops dashboard, Alert Config API, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, PCI masking at ingestion. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Square vendor integration, DLQ replay tooling, reconciliation batch job, historical backfill from S3, alert threshold UI via existing dashboard. Phase 3 (8 weeks): NCR Aloha adapter, predictive par threshold suggestions from 30-day sales patterns, district manager mobile push alerts via SNS, vendor feed health SLA reporting.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: ruff formatting + isort, type hints required on all Flink operator functions, pytest with 90% coverage on mapper and aggregation logic. Go: golangci-lint, table-driven tests for card masking and DLQ routing logic. Kafka consumer groups named by service and environment (e.g., 'normalization-job-prod'). ClickHouse schema changes require backward-compatible ADD COLUMN only in production; breaking changes via new table + migration job. Feature flags (LaunchDarkly) for new vendor rollout enabling per-location activation. ADR docs for mapper schema decisions.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PyFlink checkpoint overhead under 10K tx/min peak causing latency spikes exceeding sub-minute SLA. Mitigation: tune checkpoint interval to 30 seconds with incremental checkpointing; pre-validate under load in staging with realistic Toast payload volumes. Risk: ClickHouse single-shard write bottleneck at multi-vendor scale (50K tx/min). Mitigation: introduce sharding by location_id hash when transaction volume exceeds r6i.4xlarge capacity. Tradeoff: chosen ECS Fargate over EMR for Flink to reduce managed service cost, accepting manual Flink savepoint management. Tradeoff: single ClickHouse cluster over distributed ClickHouse cluster for cost; acceptable at current scale with replica for read HA. Risk: POS vendor API instability causing adapter polling gaps. Mitigation: dead-letter topic capture and S3 archival allow gap-fill replay without data loss.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected PyFlink over Kafka Streams for richer stateful windowing. Confirmed card masking at VendorAdapter ingestion point resolving PCI scope concern. DLQ topics defined per vendor and per normalization stage with S3-backed replay. ClickHouse MergeTree partitioning by month chosen for query performance on 3-year retention window. Reconciliation batch job scheduled nightly at 02:00 local time per location.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the two critical security gaps from round 2: mTLS enforcement on the service-to-broker transport path and rotation notification via SNS so bound services can flush stale cached values. The INSERT-only audit log enforcement is also addressed at the DB role grant level. The plan quality is weak per manifest because the manifest specifies a weak quality target and the round 3 fixes are targeted at the two remaining blocking issues from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Added mTLS client certificate requirement to internal ALB listener configuration and Go TLS config. Service identities must present a valid certificate issued by the internal CA; JWT alone is insufficient. Added mTLS enforcement middleware to the service router handler chain.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Service-to-broker transport is now fully mTLS-protected; no plaintext connections possible

fix_report.action_taken: Added SNS rotation notification topic with per-namespace subscription. On each rotation completion, the broker publishes a RotationEvent to the topic. Bound services subscribed to their namespace topic receive the event and invalidate their in-process value cache, forcing a fresh SM read on next access.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bound services receive rotation notification and flush stale values within 30 seconds of rotation completion

parsed.title: Secrets Broker API v3
parsed.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Chi router service on AWS ECS Fargate that centralizes secret distribution for internal microservices, replacing environment variable patterns with namespace-scoped REST access backed by AWS Secrets Manager. Service identity JWTs with mTLS client certificates authenticate microservices; operators use OIDC tokens. All secret values are stored in AWS SM with immutable audit log records in PostgreSQL 16. This revision adds mTLS enforcement on the service-to-broker transport path and rotation notification via SNS to close the stale-value gap.
parsed.architecture_overview: Two Chi router instances in a single Go binary: the service router on port 8443 (mTLS + JWT) and the operator router on port 9443 (OIDC). The service router enforces mTLS client certificate validation via the internal ALB listener and Go tls.Config with client certificate verification enabled. The operator router enforces OIDC token validation via the AWS OIDC endpoint. Both routers share a common handler chain for secrets, rotation, and audit modules. AWS Secrets Manager holds encrypted secret values; PostgreSQL 16 stores all metadata, policies, rotation schedules, and audit log records. S3 stores migration import job archives. SNS rotation topic notifies bound services on rotation completion.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Router: Chi v5. Auth: go-jwt for JWT validation, AWS OIDC endpoint for operator tokens. TLS: Go tls.Config with client certificate verification enabled. Database: PostgreSQL 16 via lib/pq with monthly partitioned audit log. Backing store: AWS Secrets Manager with KMS CMK. Notification: AWS SNS for rotation events. Container: Docker multi-stage build. CI: GitHub Actions with gosec, gitleaks, race detector, Trivy. Monitoring: Datadog APM.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Secret CRUD: read current version value with atomic audit log write, create initial version, create new immutable version, soft-delete with SM value purge queued. Namespace RBAC: service identities scoped to declared namespaces, operators scoped to team namespaces. Rotation Scheduling: configure cron expressions, execute rotation jobs with SM value rotation, publish SNS event to bound services. Audit Log Query: paginated search by namespace, actor, action, time range. Migration Import: CLI tool for batch importing secrets from env var files, validating checksums, and creating versions in SM. Vault Adapter: interface for future HashiCorp Vault backend.
parsed.system_components: ECS Fargate service (2 tasks, private subnets, internal ALB). Internal ALB (port 8443 mTLS listener, port 9443 standard TLS listener). Service router (Chi, mTLS enforcement middleware, JWT validation middleware). Operator router (Chi, OIDC token validation middleware). Secrets module (AWS SM client, 30-second in-process value cache per namespace). Rotation module (cron scheduler, SM value rotation, SNS publisher). Audit module (INSERT-only Postgres writer, monthly partition trigger). Migration CLI (Go binary, env var import, SM version creation). SNS rotation topic (per-namespace subscriptions). PostgreSQL 16 RDS (multi-AZ, monthly partitioned audit log). AWS Secrets Manager (encrypted values, KMS CMK). S3 (migration import archives).
parsed.workflows: Service Secret Read: Microservice sends GET /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name with mTLS client certificate and JWT. Service JWT validated by middleware, mTLS certificate verified by Go tls.Config. Secrets module fetches current SM version ARN from secret_versions table, calls SM GetSecretValue, stores result in 30-second in-process cache keyed by namespace+name. Audit record inserted atomically before response. Response includes X-Next-Value-Version header for cache invalidation. Rotation Notification: Rotation job completes in SM, writes success status to rotation_schedules. SNS RotationEvent published to topic with namespace and secret name. Bound service subscribed to topic receives event, invalidates in-process cache, forces next SM read on next access. Migration Import: Platform engineer runs broker migrate import --namespace my-namespace --env-file secrets.env. CLI reads env var lines, validates checksums against SM values, creates new versions in SM, inserts secret_records and secret_versions rows, logs success count.
parsed.data_model: secret_records (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, current_version_id UUID FK). secret_versions (id UUID PK, secret_id UUID FK, version_number INT, sm_arn VARCHAR, checksum SHA256, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, no updates permitted). access_policies (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR, grantee_identity VARCHAR, permission_level ENUM read/write/admin, granted_by VARCHAR, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, secret_namespace VARCHAR, secret_name VARCHAR, version_accessed UUID, actor_identity VARCHAR, actor_type ENUM service/operator, source_ip INET, ecs_task_id VARCHAR, action ENUM read/write/delete/rotate, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (occurred_at). rotation_schedules (id UUID PK, secret_id UUID FK, cron_expression VARCHAR, last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, next_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rotation_handler_type VARCHAR, last_status ENUM success/failed/pending, notification_status BOOLEAN).
parsed.api_design: GET /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name - read current version value, returns 200 with X-Next-Value-Version header. POST /v1/secrets/:namespace - create initial version, returns 201 with version_id. PUT /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name/versions - create new immutable version, returns 201. DELETE /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name - soft-delete, returns 204. GET /v1/audit?namespace=&actor=&action=&from=&to= - paginated audit log query. POST /v1/rotation-schedules - create rotation schedule. GET /v1/rotation-schedules/:id - status. POST /v1/rotation-schedules/:id/trigger - manual trigger. GET /v1/migration/import-status - batch import status. All service routes require mTLS client certificate and JWT. All operator routes require OIDC token.
parsed.security_and_compliance: mTLS: internal ALB listener on port 8443 requires client certificate; Go tls.Config client certificate verification enabled; service identities must present valid certificate issued by internal CA. JWT: go-jwt validates service identity tokens on every request; no caching of authorization decisions. Namespace RBAC: access_policies table enforces permission checks before any secret access; cross-namespace grants require explicit policy entry with audit record. Audit log: audit_log_entries INSERT-only enforced via Postgres trigger on audit_log_entries table; application DB role has INSERT privilege only. Secret values: never written to logs, error responses, or audit records; zerolog configured to redact 'value' and'secret' fields. SOC 2 CC6: all secret access events logged with actor identity, source IP, and ECS task ID. GDPR Article 32: secrets referencing PII-adjacent endpoints require explicit policy review and documented justification.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service in private subnets with no public route table association. Internal ALB with mTLS listener on port 8443 and standard TLS listener on port 9443. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: go vet, staticcheck, golangci-lint, gosec SAST, gitleaks secrets scan, race-detector test run, integration tests against dockerized Postgres 16 and LocalStack for SM, Trivy container scan, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check gate. Environments: dev (single task, shared RDS dev instance), staging (multi-AZ, shared RDS staging), production (multi-AZ, RDS Multi-AZ, SNS rotation topic). Postgres migrations are additive-only; rollback does not require schema changes. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager holds DB credentials and SM ARN; never stored in environment variables.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM on each ECS task. Custom metrics: secret_reads_total by namespace (counter), rotation_job_duration_seconds histogram, audit_write_latency_ms histogram, policy_eval_duration_ms. Alerts: rotation failure count > 0 in 5-min window (P1), audit write p99 > 100ms (P2), secret read error rate > 0.1% (P1), mTLS certificate verification failure (P1 immediate alert). SNS rotation topic subscription confirmation required before namespace activation.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2 tasks at 0.5 vCPU / 1 GB ~$80/month. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.t3.medium ~$180/month. SNS topic ~$5/month. S3 migration archives ~$2/month. Total ~$270/month. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% target; RDS read replica for audit log queries if query volume exceeds 5000/minute.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (8 weeks): AWS SM backend, service identity JWT auth, namespace RBAC, secret CRUD, immutable audit log, migration import CLI for 10 pilot services, mTLS enforcement on service router, SNS rotation notification. Phase 2 Full Org (6 months): HashiCorp Vault adapter, dynamic credential generation, Kubernetes external-secrets operator integration, GDPR policy review workflow, SOC 2 evidence collection. Phase 3 Hardening (ongoing): rotation schedule automation, audit log query analytics, migration import tooling for legacy env var files.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with go vet and staticcheck enforced in CI. golangci-lint with errcheck and gosec enabled. Integration tests must cover: JWT validation with expired/invalid tokens, mTLS certificate verification failure, cross-namespace secret read, INSERT-only audit log enforcement, rotation SNS publication. No secret values in test fixtures; use SHA256 checksums only. Trivy must pass with no HIGH CVEs. ADR documents required for vault adapter interface design and rotation notification protocol.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AWS SM throttling at high read concurrency during service startup. Mitigation: 30-second in-process value cache per namespace with SNS invalidation on rotation. Risk: HashiCorp Vault adapter adds complexity before it is needed. Mitigation: vault adapter interface defined from day one; HashiCorp Vault support deferred to Phase 2. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL monthly partitioned audit log over TimescaleDB for simpler migration path. Tradeoff: SNS rotation notification over direct service-side polling for simpler deployment.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: mTLS enforced on service-to-broker transport via internal ALB client certificate requirement and Go tls.Config client cert verification. Rotation notification via SNS with per-namespace subscriptions chosen over direct service polling for simpler deployment. INSERT-only audit log enforcement at Postgres trigger level confirmed. Vault adapter interface defined from day one with AWS SM as Phase 1 backend.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close the critical mTLS gap and the rotation notification gap that blocked passing in round 2. Adding mTLS to the internal ALB listener and Go TLS config for the service-facing router. Rotation notification uses SNS per namespace so bound services can flush in-process caches. The audit INSERT-only DB role grant is now explicitly documented and enforced via migration script. All three focus issues resolved.

fix_report.action_taken: Added mTLS configuration to the internal ALB listener requiring client certificates signed by the internal CA, and to the Go TLS server config with ClientAuth=RequireAndVerifyClientCert. Service identity JWT validation now layers on top of mutual TLS for defense in depth.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every service-to-broker connection authenticated at both transport and application layers, eliminating risk of rogue VPC process reaching the broker without a valid client certificate.

fix_report.action_taken: Added rotation notification subsystem: on successful rotation the broker publishes a rotation_completed event to an SNS topic scoped to the secret namespace. Each bound service subscribes via SQS and flushes its in-process cache on receipt. rotation_schedules table now tracks notification_status.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bound services refresh to new secret version within seconds of rotation, eliminating the stale-value window that previously lasted until service restart.

fix_report.action_taken: Explicitly documented DB role permission model: application role has INSERT-only on audit_log_entries with no UPDATE or DELETE grants. A Postgres trigger raises an exception on any UPDATE or DELETE attempt as a secondary guard. Enforced in migration scripts and validated in integration tests.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Audit log entries are tamper-proof at the database layer, satisfying SOC 2 CC7 immutability requirement.

reference.title: Secrets Broker API - Centralized Vault Abstraction Layer
reference.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 secrets management API on AWS ECS Fargate that abstracts AWS Secrets Manager behind a unified REST interface, enabling internal microservices to migrate from environment variable secret distribution to namespace-scoped mTLS-authenticated access with rotation scheduling, SNS-driven refresh notifications, and an immutable audit trail enforced at the DB role level. Two listeners serve separate concerns: a service-facing mTLS endpoint on port 8443 and an OIDC-authenticated operator management endpoint on port 9443.
reference.architecture_overview: Two-listener architecture on ECS Fargate private subnet: service-facing listener on port 8443 with mTLS (client certs signed by internal CA) layered under service identity JWT validation, and operator management listener on port 9443 gated by OIDC tokens from the corporate IdP. Chi router with middleware stack handles routing, auth, and synchronous audit logging. AWS Secrets Manager stores encrypted secret values referenced by ARN from PostgreSQL 16 metadata records. Rotation scheduler runs as a separate ECS scheduled task and publishes rotation_completed events to per-namespace SNS topics on success. Bound services consume events from SQS subscriptions and flush in-process caches. All audit writes are synchronous and block the originating request to ensure log integrity.
reference.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Chi router, aws-sdk-go-v2 for Secrets Manager and SNS, golang-jwt/jwt for token validation, crypto/tls for mTLS config, pgx/v5 for Postgres. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with monthly partitioning on audit_log_entries. Messaging: AWS SNS for rotation notifications, SQS for per-service subscription queues. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate private subnets, internal ALB with mTLS listener, AWS KMS CMK for SM encryption, ACM Private CA for service certificates. Observability: Datadog APM sidecar. CI: GitHub Actions with gosec, staticcheck, race detector, gitleaks, Trivy.
reference.functional_feature_map: Secret Lifecycle: create with initial version, read current version value plus metadata, create new immutable version, soft-delete with immediate SM purge queue. Rotation Management: configure cron-based schedules, execute rotation with new SM version creation, publish SNS completion event, track notification delivery status per rotation. Access Policies: grant and revoke namespace-scoped RBAC, enumerate active policies, cross-namespace grant with mandatory audit record. Audit Query: paginated log with filters by namespace, actor identity, action type, and time range. Migration Import: batch import from env var manifest YAML, parallel secret creation with progress tracking, onboarding status report by service name.
reference.system_components: Internal ALB with mTLS listener (client cert CA pinned to internal PKI CA). ECS Fargate service: single Go binary exposing two Chi router instances on separate ports. Rotation ECS scheduled task publishing to per-namespace SNS topics. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ in private subnet with audit_log_entries partitioned by month. AWS Secrets Manager as value store with KMS CMK. SNS topics per secret namespace for rotation events. SQS queues (consumer-managed) for rotation subscriptions. S3 bucket for migration import job archives with 90-day lifecycle. Datadog agent sidecar on each Fargate task.
reference.workflows: Secret Read by Microservice: service presents client certificate and service identity JWT on connection to port 8443; Chi mTLS middleware validates cert against internal CA cert pool; auth middleware validates JWT and extracts namespace claims; RBAC middleware checks access_policies for the requested namespace; handler fetches sm_arn from Postgres, calls SM GetSecretValue, writes audit_log_entry INSERT atomically, returns value in response. Target p99 under 5ms with in-process cache. Rotation Execution: ECS scheduled task reads due rotation_schedules records; calls rotation handler to generate new value in SM; writes new secret_version record with new SM ARN; updates rotation_schedule last_run_at, next_run_at, and last_status; publishes rotation_completed SNS message with namespace and secret name; updates notification_status on rotation record. Bound service SQS consumer flushes in-process cache entry on receipt. Migration Import: operator uploads env var manifest YAML to S3 import bucket; migration ECS task reads manifest, calls POST /v1/secrets in batches of 20 with retry, records onboarding status per service, generates migration report JSON with created/failed/skipped counts and posts to operator API.
reference.data_model: secret_records (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR NOT NULL, name VARCHAR NOT NULL, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, current_version_id UUID FK, UNIQUE(namespace, name)). secret_versions (id UUID PK, secret_id UUID FK NOT NULL, version_number INT NOT NULL, sm_arn VARCHAR NOT NULL, checksum VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL; no updates permitted by app role). access_policies (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR NOT NULL, grantee_identity VARCHAR NOT NULL, permission_level permission_level_enum NOT NULL, granted_by VARCHAR, granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, secret_namespace VARCHAR, secret_name VARCHAR, version_accessed UUID, actor_identity VARCHAR, actor_type actor_type_enum, source_ip INET, ecs_task_id VARCHAR, action action_enum, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (occurred_at); app role has INSERT-only grant; trigger raises exception on UPDATE or DELETE. rotation_schedules (id UUID PK, secret_id UUID FK, cron_expression VARCHAR, last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, next_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rotation_handler_type VARCHAR, last_status rotation_status_enum, notification_status VARCHAR). service_identities (id UUID PK, service_name VARCHAR, task_role_arn VARCHAR, allowed_namespaces TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: Service API port 8443 (mTLS + JWT): GET /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name returns current version value and metadata, audit logged. POST /v1/secrets/:namespace creates secret with initial version, returns secret_id and version_id. PUT /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name/versions creates new immutable version. DELETE /v1/secrets/:namespace/:name soft-deletes, queues SM value purge. GET /v1/secrets/:namespace lists secret names without values. Operator API port 9443 (OIDC): POST /v1/policies grants namespace access. DELETE /v1/policies/:id revokes. GET /v1/audit?namespace=&from=&to=&actor=&action=&page= returns paginated audit entries. POST /v1/rotation-schedules/:namespace/:name configures cron. POST /v1/migration/import submits import job from S3 key. GET /v1/migration/status/:job_id returns progress and onboarding report.
reference.security_and_compliance: mTLS: internal ALB listener requires client certificates from internal PKI CA; Go TLS config sets ClientAuth=RequireAndVerifyClientCert with internal CA cert pool loaded at startup. Service identity JWT: HS256 tokens with sub=service_name and namespaces claims, 15-minute expiry, validated on every request with no caching. RBAC: Chi middleware evaluates access_policies before handler; 403 with no detail on policy miss to avoid namespace enumeration. Audit immutability: application DB role has INSERT-only grant on audit_log_entries; Postgres trigger raises exception on UPDATE or DELETE; enforced in migration scripts and validated in integration tests. Secret values never in logs: zerolog configured to redact fields named value and secret; error responses contain only error codes. SOC 2 CC6: all access logged, least-privilege service identities, policy grants audited. SOC 2 CC7: immutable audit trail, rotation events logged with notification status. GDPR Art.32: KMS CMK encryption for values at rest, TLS 1.3 minimum in transit.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: go vet, staticcheck, golangci-lint, gosec SAST, gitleaks secrets scan, race-detector test run, integration tests against dockerized Postgres 16 and LocalStack for SM simulation, Trivy container scan, ECR push tagged with git SHA, ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy. Environments: dev (single AZ Fargate 1 task, shared RDS dev), staging (multi-AZ Fargate 2 tasks, dedicated RDS), production (multi-AZ Fargate 3 tasks min / 6 max auto-scaling, RDS Multi-AZ with automated daily backups). Internal ALB only with no public route table entries for broker subnets. mTLS listener cert rotation: 90-day CA-signed cert with 30-day pre-expiry alert and automated ACM Private CA renewal. DB migrations via golang-migrate applied in a pre-deploy ECS one-off task. Broker's own DB password stored in Secrets Manager with 30-day automated rotation.
reference.observability: Datadog APM traces every secret read and rotation execution with namespace and actor_type tags. Custom metrics: secret_reads_total counter by namespace, rotation_job_duration_seconds histogram, rotation_notification_delivery_latency_ms histogram, audit_write_latency_ms histogram, policy_eval_duration_ms histogram. Alerts: rotation failure count > 0 in any 5-minute window triggers PagerDuty P1; audit write p99 > 100ms triggers P2; secret read error rate > 0.1% triggers P1; SNS delivery failure rate > 1% triggers P2. CloudTrail captures all AWS SM GetSecretValue calls as a secondary audit layer independent of application logs.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 3 tasks at 0.5 vCPU / 1GB each approximately $120/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium approximately $180/month. AWS Secrets Manager at 400 secrets with 10K reads/day approximately $40/month. SNS and SQS rotation notifications approximately $5/month. Total under $400/month at pilot scale. Scale path: Fargate task count auto-scales on CPU target 50%. SM read throttling at high concurrency handled by 30-second in-process value cache per namespace reducing SM API calls by approximately 80%, with SNS-triggered cache invalidation on rotation events.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP Pilot (8 weeks): service identity JWT auth with mTLS, namespace RBAC, secret CRUD with AWS SM backing, immutable audit log with INSERT-only DB role enforcement, migration import CLI, onboard 10 pilot services with migration report. Phase 2 Rotation and Notifications (4 weeks): rotation schedule engine with cron evaluation, SNS notification per namespace on rotation completion, SQS subscription helper library for consuming services, rotation history on operator API. Phase 3 Full Migration (8 weeks): all internal services onboarded, migration completion reports by team, decommission env var injection in service manifests, HashiCorp Vault backend adapter. Phase 4 Dynamic Credentials (roadmap): database credential generation via Vault dynamic secrets, Kubernetes external-secrets operator integration.
reference.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 strict golangci-lint config including errcheck, govet, staticcheck, gosec. Race detector enabled in all CI test runs. Secret values zeroed from memory after use with explicit byte slice zeroing. No secret values in struct fields implementing fmt.Stringer. Integration tests use dockertest for Postgres 16 and LocalStack for SM. PR requires two approvals for any change touching auth middleware, audit log writes, or policy evaluation logic. ADR document required for every decision affecting secret value handling paths.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AWS SM API throttling at high read concurrency from hundreds of services simultaneously starting up. Mitigation: 30-second in-process TTL cache per namespace with SNS-triggered invalidation on rotation events reducing SM calls by ~80%. Risk: Internal CA certificate expiry causing mTLS failures across all services. Mitigation: 90-day cert rotation with 30-day pre-expiry alert and automated ACM Private CA renewal. Tradeoff: synchronous audit write blocking secret reads provides stronger integrity than async at cost of p99 latency; acceptable given SOC 2 CC7 requirement. Tradeoff: INSERT-only audit log prevents correction of erroneous entries; compensated by correction_notes addendum pattern for post-hoc documentation.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed AWS Secrets Manager over HashiCorp Vault for MVP given existing AWS footprint; Vault adapter added post-pilot. Confirmed SNS plus SQS over polling for rotation notifications to eliminate stale-value windows. Confirmed synchronous audit write over async to satisfy SOC 2 CC7 immutability. mTLS client cert CA pinned to internal PKI rather than public CA to avoid external dependency.

raw:

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a static legislative transparency site with no backend. The key architectural decisions are: Astro 4 for zero-JS-by-default static shells with targeted island hydration for search and filter components; client-side fetch to the legislature's public open data API with sessionStorage caching to avoid rate limits; CloudFront + S3 for global edge delivery at sub-50ms latency; CSP headers restricting fetch directives to the legislature API domain. No persistence layer, no auth, no cookies.
parsed.title: State Legislature Transparency Website
parsed.executive_summary: A public-facing static website built with Astro 4 that embeds live legislative data from the state legislature's open data API. Bill search, status tracking, committee schedules, and legislator directories are served from CloudFront-backed S3 with sub-50ms global edge delivery. Client-side JavaScript islands hydrate only the bill search component and vote breakdown charts, keeping page loads under 2 seconds on 4G. No backend, no database, no cookies.
parsed.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture: Astro 4 generates HTML for all pages at build time; no server-side rendering. Client-side JavaScript islands hydrate only the bill search form, committee calendar, and vote breakdown charts. All dynamic legislative data is fetched directly from the legislature's open data REST API at api.legislature.state.gov using vanilla JS fetch. sessionStorage caches API responses for the browser session to reduce redundant calls during navigation. The Astro build output is deployed to a private S3 bucket in AWS Oregon, served exclusively through a CloudFront distribution with origin access control. Route53 aliases the custom domain to CloudFront. GA4 with IP anonymization tracks bill page engagement and search query terms. No user data is stored anywhere.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, JavaScript ES2022, Tailwind CSS custom properties, DOMPurify for API description sanitization. API: legislature's open data REST API at api.legislature.state.gov (public read-only). Analytics: GA4 with anonymize_ip: true. Hosting: AWS CloudFront (global edge), S3 (private bucket), Route53, ACM. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: CloudWatch for S3 and CloudFront metrics.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Bill Search: keyword and sponsor filters, paginated results from API, link to bill detail page. Bill Detail: status timeline (introduced, committee, floor, passed, failed, signed), sponsor list, committee assignments, roll call vote links. Committee Schedule: upcoming hearings with bill links, sorted by date. Legislator Directory: chamber filter (House/Senate), district search, voting history chart (yea/nay/absent breakdown). Vote Record: yea/nay/absent breakdown by party, legislator photo and district.
parsed.system_components: GitHub Actions runner (build and deploy trigger), Astro 4 build process (static HTML generation), S3 bucket (private, Oregon region, origin for CloudFront), CloudFront distribution (global edge, origin access control, response headers policy with CSP), Route53 (A record alias to CloudFront), ACM (custom domain certificate), legislature's open data API (external, public read-only), GA4 (external analytics tag).
parsed.workflows: Bill Search: Citizen visits /bills page, types keyword 'climate change', clicks search button. Astro island component calls GET /api/v1/bills?search=climate+change on the legislature API. Response paginated list of matching bills returned in JSON. sessionStorage caches the response under key 'bills:search:climate+change'. BillSearchIsland renders a list of bill titles with status and sponsor names. On click of a bill, the island calls GET /api/v1/bills/{id} and renders the bill detail page. Committee Schedule: On the /committees page, the CommitteeCalendar island calls GET /api/v1/committee-schedules?date_gte=2026-03-01&date_lte=2026-03-31. Response list of upcoming hearings with bill_id and bill_title. Island renders a calendar grid with bill links. Vote Breakdown: On a /votes/{bill_id} page, the VoteBreakdownChart island calls GET /api/v1/roll-call-votes?bill_id={id}. Response list of vote records. Island renders a bar chart with yea/nay/absent counts by legislator party using Chart.js client:visible directive.
parsed.data_model: No local data model. All data sourced from the legislature's open data API. Key API resources: bills (id, number, session_year, title, status ENUM introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed, sponsors[], committees[]), legislators (id, name, chamber ENUM house/senate, district, party ENUM republican/democrat/independent, photo_url), committee_schedules (id, committee_name, date, time, hearing_items[{bill_id, bill_title}]), roll_call_votes (id, bill_id, vote_date, yea_count, nay_count, absent_count, legislator_votes[{legislator_id, vote ENUM yea/nay/absent/excused}]).
parsed.api_design: No custom API. Client-side calls to legislature's open data REST API: GET /api/v1/bills?search=&sponsor=&page= (paginated), GET /api/v1/bills/{id}, GET /api/v1/legislators?chamber=&district=, GET /api/v1/committee-schedules?date_gte=&date_lte=, GET /api/v1/roll-call-votes?bill_id=, GET /api/v1/legislators/{id}/voting-history. All responses cached in sessionStorage under key 'api:{endpoint}:{query_params}' with 1-hour TTL. No authentication headers required; API is public read-only.
parsed.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at CloudFront. CSP response headers policy on CloudFront: default-src'self'; script-src'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src'self' data: https://api.legislature.state.gov; connect-src'self' https://api.legislature.state.gov https://www.google-analytics.com. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. No cookies set by the site. GA4 with anonymize_ip: true. No user accounts, no form submissions, no personal data stored. Open Government Data Act compliance: all legislative data is sourced from the official API; no local copies stored.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, setup-node, npm ci, astro build (outputs to dist/), aws s3 sync dist/ s3://legislature-site-prod/ --delete, aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*'. Deploy on merge to main. Staging deployment: CloudFront distribution from staging branch branch, separate S3 bucket legislature-site-staging. Rollback: revert GitHub commit, re-run GitHub Actions workflow. CloudFront invalidation triggered on every deploy to flush browser caches. S3 bucket policy restricts access to CloudFront OAC only.
parsed.observability: GA4 custom events: bill_searched (with search_term), bill_detail_viewed (bill_id), committee_schedule_viewed, legislator_directory_viewed. GA4 conversion event: bill_status_changed (when citizen clicks on status timeline). CloudWatch metrics: S3 GetObject requests, CloudFront 4xx and 5xx errors, CloudFront data transfer. Alert: CloudFront 5xx rate > 1% over 5 minutes triggers Slack notification to legislative communications team.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3-5/month: S3 storage for static assets ~$1, CloudFront data transfer ~$2, CloudFront requests ~$0.50 at 5M/month estimated legislative session traffic. Scales automatically with legislative session traffic spikes; CloudFront handles 10Gbps at peak without configuration changes.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Astro project setup, bill search and status pages, committee schedule list, legislator directory, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, CloudFront deployment, CSP headers, GA4 integration. Phase 2 (weeks 5-6): Roll call vote detail pages with yea/nay/absent breakdown, voting history charts, session status banner. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): Downloadable CSV exports of vote records, email alert integration via third-party notification service, embeddable bill status widgets for news sites.
parsed.development_guidelines: Astro components for all static pages; no client-side JavaScript outside of src/components/ island directories. API calls must use fetch with the legislature API base URL from astro.config.js env var; no dynamic URL construction. sessionStorage keys follow pattern 'api:{endpoint}:{query_params}' for cache invalidation. DOMPurify.sanitize() on all API-returned HTML description fields before insertion. Chart.js charts must use client:visible directive; never hydrate on initial render. PR requires one approval from legislative communications team member.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legislature API rate limiting during high-traffic legislative sessions. Mitigation: sessionStorage caching reduces repeat calls; API team has rate limit headers that can be surfaced to the site. Risk: API schema changes breaking bill detail pages. Mitigation: API version pinned in Astro config; alert on 4xx responses with error handling. Tradeoff: Astro over Next.js for zero-JS-by-default simplicity at the cost of less server-side rendering flexibility. Tradeoff: client-side Chart.js for vote breakdown charts over server-side rendering for better mobile performance.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Astro over Next.js for zero-JS-by-default static shells. Confirmed client-side Chart.js for vote breakdown charts over server-side rendering for mobile performance. Confirmed sessionStorage over localForage for API caching to avoid adding a third-party dependency. Confirmed GA4 over Plausible for richer engagement analytics.

reference.thinking_summary: Static Astro site consuming the legislature's existing open data API client-side. The key architectural constraint is no data proxying or storage, which means client-side fetch with sessionStorage caching is the only caching layer available. Astro islands pattern minimizes JavaScript payload while enabling interactive bill search and vote visualization components. CloudFront + S3 delivers static assets globally with minimal cost.
reference.title: State Legislature Transparency Site - Astro Static Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A public Astro-generated static website delivering live legislative data -- bill status, roll call votes, committee schedules, and legislator directories -- by fetching client-side from the state legislature's existing open data API. Deployed to AWS CloudFront + S3 with no backend, no database, and no user data collection. Interactive search and filter components use Astro's island hydration pattern to minimize JavaScript delivery.
reference.architecture_overview: Fully static deployment with client-side data integration. Astro builds static HTML shells for all page routes and bundles only the JavaScript needed for interactive islands: bill search, committee calendar, and vote breakdown chart. All legislative data is fetched on page load or user interaction directly from api.legislature.state.gov using the browser's Fetch API. Results are cached in sessionStorage keyed by API request URL for the duration of the browser session. The built site is synced to a private S3 bucket and served globally through CloudFront with security headers enforced at the distribution level. No server-side processes, no database connections, and no user data ever touches the hosting infrastructure.
reference.technology_stack: Static Generator: Astro 4 with JavaScript (no TypeScript requirement per engineering decision). API Integration: vanilla Fetch API with sessionStorage caching layer. Styling: CSS custom properties with legislature brand color palette; no CSS framework to minimize dependency surface. Interactive Islands: bill search with live API typeahead (client:load), committee calendar with date navigation (client:visible), roll call vote breakdown bar chart using lightweight Chart.js island (client:visible). Hosting: AWS CloudFront + S3 (private bucket with origin access control). CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Analytics: GA4 with IP anonymization. DNS/TLS: Route53 + ACM.
reference.functional_feature_map: Bill Search Module: keyword and sponsor-name search querying /v1/bills endpoint with pagination, status filter (introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed), and session year selector. Bill Detail Pages: status timeline showing each stage transition date, sponsor list linking to legislator profiles, committee assignment history, and link to roll call vote record. Legislator Directory: filter by chamber (House/Senate), party, and district; each profile shows photo, contact info, and current session voting record summary. Committee Schedule Module: weekly calendar view of upcoming hearings with linked bill agenda items per hearing slot. Vote Record Pages: yea/nay/absent/excused counts with party breakdown bar chart and individual legislator vote list sortable by name or party.
reference.system_components: GitHub Repository (Astro source, page templates, island components), GitHub Actions Runner (build and deploy pipeline), AWS S3 Bucket (private static asset storage, Oregon region), CloudFront Distribution (edge delivery, TLS termination, security response headers, WAF rate limiting), ACM Certificate (custom domain TLS), Route53 (DNS), Legislature Open Data API (external authoritative data source at api.legislature.state.gov), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized engagement tracking). No databases or application servers operated by this project.
reference.workflows: Bill Lookup Workflow: Citizen navigates to /bills, types a sponsor name into the BillSearchIsland (client:load hydrated), component calls GET api.legislature.state.gov/v1/bills?sponsor=Smith&session=2026, checks sessionStorage for cached response keyed by full URL, on miss fetches and caches result for session duration, renders paginated bill cards; citizen clicks bill title and navigates to /bills/SB-1042 static shell which on mount fetches bill detail and committee history from API and renders status timeline. Roll Call Vote Review Workflow: Citizen on /bills/SB-1042 clicks 'View Final Vote', navigates to /votes/SB-1042-final, page fetches roll_call_votes endpoint, VoteBreakdownChart island hydrates on viewport entry, renders party-segmented bar chart with yea 32/nay 18/absent 5 breakdown, individual legislator vote list sortable client-side without additional API calls.
reference.data_model: No data stored on site infrastructure. API-sourced data shapes consumed client-side: bills {id, number, session_year YYYY, title, status ENUM introduced/committee/floor/passed/failed/signed, primary_sponsor_id, co_sponsor_ids[], committee_ids[], last_action_date, full_text_url}. legislators {id, name_full, chamber ENUM house/senate, district_number, party ENUM dem/rep/ind, photo_url, email, capitol_phone}. committee_schedules {id, committee_id, committee_name, hearing_date, hearing_time, room, hearing_items [{bill_id, bill_number, bill_title, hearing_purpose}]}. roll_call_votes {id, bill_id, vote_date, vote_type ENUM passage/amendment/procedural, yea_count, nay_count, absent_count, excused_count, legislator_votes [{legislator_id, vote ENUM yea/nay/absent/excused}]}.
reference.api_design: Consumes legislature open data API (no custom backend). Key endpoints consumed: GET /v1/bills?keyword=&sponsor=&session=&status=&page= (bill search with filter params). GET /v1/bills/{bill_number} (bill detail with status history array). GET /v1/legislators?chamber=&party=&district= (legislator directory). GET /v1/legislators/{id}/votes?session= (legislator voting history). GET /v1/committees/{id}/schedule?from_date=&to_date= (committee hearings). GET /v1/votes/{vote_id} (roll call vote with legislator breakdown). All requests include Accept: application/json header; API returns paginated JSON with next_page_url cursor. No authentication required for public read-only API.
reference.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at CloudFront; S3 bucket blocks all public access, served only through CloudFront origin access control. CloudFront response headers policy: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.legislature.state.gov; img-src 'self' data: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). DOMPurify sanitizes any HTML content returned by API description fields. No cookies set by the site beyond GA4 measurement. WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility validated via axe-core in CI.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI/CD: (1) actions/checkout, (2) actions/setup-node with Node 22, (3) npm ci, (4) astro build outputs to dist/, (5) aws s3 sync dist/ s3://legislature-site-prod/ --delete --cache-control 'max-age=86400', (6) aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DIST_ID --paths '/*', (7) notify Slack on success/failure. Environments: production deploys from main; staging CloudFront distribution deploys from staging branch for comms office previews. Secrets: AWS credentials stored in GitHub Actions OIDC (no long-lived keys). Rollback: prior S3 state is restorable via git revert + pipeline re-run in under 5 minutes.
reference.observability: GA4 custom events: bill_search (search_term, result_count), bill_detail_view (bill_number, status), vote_record_view (bill_number, vote_type), legislator_profile_view (legislator_id, chamber). CloudWatch dashboard for CloudFront: request count by page, 4xx/5xx error rates, cache hit ratio. UptimeRobot monitors homepage and one bill detail page every 5 minutes with email alert on downtime. Legislature API availability not monitored by this project; graceful error states display 'Legislative data temporarily unavailable' when API returns non-200.
reference.cost_and_scaling: S3 storage: ~$0.50/month for ~20 GB static assets. CloudFront: ~$3-8/month depending on session traffic (spikes during floor votes and budget debates). Route53: $0.50/month per hosted zone. Total: approximately $5-10/month. CloudFront auto-scales at the edge; no capacity planning required. During high-traffic legislative events, CloudFront serves cached static shells immediately; API call volume scales with concurrent users but does not impact hosting cost.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Astro project scaffolding, CloudFront + S3 pipeline, bill search and bill detail pages, legislator directory, GA4 integration. Deploy to staging for communications office review. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Committee schedule calendar island, roll call vote detail pages with Chart.js breakdown chart, sessionStorage caching layer for API responses, WCAG 2.1 AA audit and remediation, production launch. Phase 3 (post-launch): Bill status email alert integration via third-party notification widget, CSV export of vote records generated client-side, embedding widget package for news site integration.
reference.development_guidelines: All Astro component files use .astro extension with frontmatter for build-time data and named exports for island components. No inline event handlers; all event listeners attached in client-side island scripts. API fetch calls wrapped in a shared fetchWithCache(url) utility that checks sessionStorage before fetching. DOMPurify must be imported and used for any API-returned HTML string rendered via innerHTML. CloudFront distribution ID and S3 bucket name stored only in GitHub Actions secrets, never in source code. WCAG 2.1 AA automated check runs in CI via axe-core; PR blocked on accessibility violations.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legislature's open data API rate limits triggered during high-traffic legislative sessions with many concurrent visitors. Mitigation: sessionStorage caching reduces repeat calls; if rate limiting becomes a problem, a lightweight CloudFront Functions cache layer can be added without architectural changes. Risk: Legislature API schema changes breaking client-side rendering. Mitigation: API response validation with zod shapes at the component boundary; error boundaries display graceful fallback UI. Tradeoff: No server-side rendering means bill detail pages are blank shells until client-side fetch completes, trading initial content completeness for zero server infrastructure. Tradeoff: No data proxy means cannot rate-limit or pre-aggregate API responses, but satisfies the no-data-storage constraint.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Astro over Next.js static export given Astro's superior zero-JS-default behavior for a content-heavy site. Confirmed client-side direct API fetch over CloudFront Functions proxy to satisfy the no-data-storage constraint imposed by Legislative IT. Confirmed sessionStorage over localStorage for API response caching to ensure fresh data on new browser sessions.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 4 revision addressing two high-severity issues: model artifact S3 path must use content-addressable versioning (s3://models/{model_type}/{sha256_hash}.onnx) to prevent silent overwrites during concurrent retraining runs, and the velocity counter logic must be replaced with atomic Redis INCR + EXPIRE commands to eliminate the race condition. The chargeback-to-authorization join performance issue (ISS-003) is addressed by pre-aggregating the join result in a ClickHouse materialized view updated incrementally on chargeback insert.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed S3 model artifact path scheme to s3://models/{model_type}/{sha256_hash}.onnx (e.g., s3://models/xgboost/4a2d3f6a.onnx). ModelVersions table now stores sha256_hash as a required column. InferenceService loads model by path hash, not filename, preventing silent overwrites. Rollback procedure: POST /internal/model/reload accepts S3 path with hash, loads model by hash, not filename.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model artifact versioning is content-addressable; silent overwrites eliminated; rollback safe by hash lookup; audit trail of all model versions with hash in model_versions table.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all velocity counter logic with atomic Redis INCR + EXPIRE commands. Increment operation now: INCR card_hash:velocity:{window} EXPIRE {window_ttl}. Velocity lookup: GET card_hash:velocity:{window} + EXPIRE check. If EXPIRE < 0, Redis returns -1 (expired) and InferenceService falls back to stale counter value with explicit stale_flag=true in response. RetrainingService uses INCR for all counter updates.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Velocity counter increments are atomic; no race window during burst load; stale counter fallback with explicit flag prevents silent undercounting.

parsed.title: Transaction Anomaly Detection Platform — Round 4 Plan
parsed.executive_summary: A high-risk ai_system for real-time card-not-present fraud scoring, serving XGBoost and transformer-based models on ECS Fargate with sub-50ms p99 latency. Round 4 resolves two high-severity issues: model artifact S3 paths now use content-addressable SHA-256 hashes (s3://models/{model_type}/{sha256_hash}.onnx) eliminating silent overwrites, and velocity counters use atomic Redis INCR + EXPIRE commands eliminating the race condition. A ClickHouse materialized view pre-aggregates the chargeback-to-authorization join, reducing nightly retraining job runtime from 40+ minutes to under 10 minutes.
parsed.architecture_overview: Two ECS Fargate services: InferenceService (FastAPI + ONNX Runtime sidecar, 4 tasks min, auto-scales to 20 on queue depth) and RetrainingService (scheduled ECS task triggered by EventBridge nightly at 01:00 UTC). InferenceService receives authorization requests via ALB with mTLS termination, extracts features from ClickHouse via async query, loads the active ONNX model from S3 by SHA-256 hash, runs XGBoost scoring layer, and returns fraud_score and reason_codes within 50ms p99. Velocity counters use atomic Redis INCR + EXPIRE commands. RetrainingService joins pre-aggregated chargeback data from a ClickHouse materialized view to authorization_requests, trains XGBoost, evaluates F1, and promotes the challenger if improvement exceeds 0.005. Model artifacts stored in S3 with content-addressable paths (s3://models/{model_type}/{sha256_hash}.onnx).
parsed.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy for ClickHouse, Pydantic v2 for request/response validation. Inference: ONNX Runtime 1.17 with GPU acceleration on Fargate GPU-optimized tasks. Feature engineering: custom Python library with ClickHouse async client. Model storage: S3 with versioning and SSE-KMS. Feature store: ClickHouse MergeTree on EC2 r6i.2xlarge (3-node cluster). Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache (single primary for atomic operations). Auth: Cognito JWT for internal API, mTLS via ACM for processor endpoints. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with model evaluation gates. Monitoring: Datadog APM with custom model drift metrics.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Real-Time Scoring API: POST /v1/score accepts authorization_request JSON, returns fraud_score (0.0-1.0), decision (approve/review/decline), reason_codes[], model_version_id, latency_ms, and stale_flag. Velocity-Based Features: Redis INCR + EXPIRE counters for 15-min, 1-hour, and 24-hour card_hash velocity windows. Model Promotion: POST /v1/models/promote triggers champion evaluation and promotion if F1 improvement exceeds 0.005. Chargeback Ingestion: nightly RetrainingService pulls chargeback files from S3 drop zone, joins pre-aggregated materialized view, computes updated fraud labels. Merchant Risk Profiling: GET /v1/merchants/{id}/risk returns 30-day chargeback rate and risk_tier. Explainability: GET /v1/explainations/{request_id} returns SHAP feature contributions.
parsed.system_components: ALB with mTLS termination (processor-facing /v1/score endpoints only). InferenceService (ECS Fargate, 4 tasks min, auto-scale 20 max). ONNX Runtime sidecar (loads model from S3 by SHA-256 hash on startup). RetrainingService (ECS scheduled task, nightly at 01:00 UTC). ClickHouse cluster (3-node EC2 r6i.2xlarge, MergeTree tables for authorization_requests and fraud_scores, materialized view labeled_authorization_features). Redis 7 ElastiCache (single primary for atomic velocity counter operations). S3 model artifact bucket (versioned, SSE-KMS, content-addressable paths). S3 chargeback drop zone. EventBridge Scheduler (triggers RetrainingService). AWS Secrets Manager (model S3 paths, DB credentials, Redis auth token). Cognito user pool (internal API). ACM (mTLS certificates for processor endpoints).
parsed.workflows: Real-Time Scoring: Processor sends POST /v1/score with mTLS certificate and X-Merchant-API-Key header. InferenceService validates mTLS, extracts card_hash from request, queries Redis for velocity counters using INCR + EXPIRE pattern, queries ClickHouse for feature vector, loads active ONNX model from S3 by SHA-256 hash, runs XGBoost scoring layer, returns fraud_score, decision, reason_codes, model_version_id, latency_ms, stale_flag. Velocity Counter Update: InferenceService calls INCR card_hash:velocity:15min EXPIRE 900 (15-min window, 15-min TTL). If Redis returns -1 (EXPIRE < 0), InferenceService returns stale_flag=true and stale_score with confidence=low. Nightly Retraining: EventBridge triggers RetrainingService at 01:00 UTC. Service queries ClickHouse materialized view labeled_authorization_features (pre-aggregated chargeback labels), trains XGBoost on stratified split, evaluates F1 against holdout set, computes SHAP explainability, promotes challenger if F1 improvement > 0.005, updates model_versions table with sha256_hash, reloads active model on InferenceService.
parsed.data_model: authorization_requests (id UUID, merchant_id UUID, card_bin VARCHAR(6), card_last4 VARCHAR(4), card_hash VARCHAR(64), amount_cents INT64, currency CHAR(3), device_fingerprint VARCHAR(128), ip_hash VARCHAR(64), mcc CHAR(4), timestamp DateTime64(3)). fraud_scores (id UUID, request_id UUID, model_version_id UUID, score FLOAT32, decision ENUM('approve','review','decline'), reason_codes Array(String), latency_ms INT, stale_flag BOOL, scored_at DateTime64(3)). chargebacks (id UUID, request_id UUID, reason_code VARCHAR(50), dispute_amount_cents INT64, received_at DateTime64(3), label BOOL). merchant_profiles (id UUID, merchant_id UUID UNIQUE, risk_tier ENUM('low','medium','high'), monthly_volume_cents INT64, chargeback_rate_30d FLOAT32, updated_at DateTime64(3)). model_versions (id UUID, model_type ENUM('xgboost','transformer'), s3_path VARCHAR(256), sha256_hash VARCHAR(64), training_run_id VARCHAR(64), precision_score FLOAT32, recall_score FLOAT32, f1_score FLOAT32, promoted_at DateTime64(3), status ENUM('challenger','champion','retired')). labeled_authorization_features (materialized view, pre-aggregated: request_id, label, merchant_id, timestamp) — updated incrementally on chargeback insert.
parsed.api_design: POST /v1/score — body: {merchant_id, card_bin, card_last4, amount_cents, currency, device_fingerprint, ip_hash, mcc}; returns {fraud_score, decision, reason_codes[], model_version_id, latency_ms, stale_flag, stale_score}. POST /v1/models/promote — body: {model_version_id}; returns {status, evaluation_result}. GET /v1/models/active — returns current champion metadata. POST /internal/model/reload — body: {s3_path}; internal only, Cognito JWT required. GET /v1/merchants/{id}/risk — returns 30-day chargeback rate and risk_tier. GET /v1/explainations/{request_id} — returns SHAP feature contributions. All processor endpoints require mTLS; internal endpoints require Cognito JWT.
parsed.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS Level 1: card data masked to BIN + last4 + SHA-256 card_hash before ClickHouse persistence; full PAN never stored. mTLS via ACM for all processor-facing /v1/score endpoints; certificate rotation every 90 days. Cognito MFA enforced for all internal API users. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. S3 model artifacts encrypted with SSE-KMS. CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. Audit log in ClickHouse: every /v1/score call recorded with request_id, merchant_id, card_hash, model_version_id, score, decision, latency_ms, timestamp. Logs retained 7 years in S3 Glacier after 90 days in ClickHouse.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a and 1c. InferenceService: 4 tasks min, auto-scale to 20 max on ALB queue depth > 10. RetrainingService: single-AZ scheduled task, EventBridge trigger at 01:00 UTC. GitHub Actions CI: lint, unit tests (pytest), integration tests against staging ClickHouse replica, model evaluation gate (challenger F1 > champion F1 + 0.005), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Model promotion requires manual GitHub Environments approval. Environments: dev (single-AZ, shared ClickHouse), staging (multi-AZ, dedicated ClickHouse replica), production (multi-AZ, 3-node ClickHouse cluster). Rollback: ECS task definition revision rollback; POST /internal/model/reload for model rollback by hash.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM distributed traces across InferenceService and Redis. Custom Datadog metrics: fraud_score_distribution (histogram by score bucket), model_drift_kl_divergence (gauge, computed from score distribution histogram vs baseline), velocity_cache_hit_rate (gauge), retraining_job_duration_minutes (gauge).
