EU GMP Annex 13: audit_trail records every IMP lot status transition. FDA 21 CFR Part 312: IMP chain-of-custody JSONB in lot record. ICH Q10: IMP lot records retained 15 years.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: Maven build, Spring Boot test (unit + integration with Testcontainers Aurora/Redis), React build + Vitest, Docker push to ECR, Helm chart deploy to EKS via ArgoCD. Blue/green deployment via ArgoCD Rollouts for zero-downtime releases. Database migrations via Flyway applied as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook. EKS HPA: api-service scales 3-8 pods on CPU 60% target. Aurora Global Database: us-east-1 primary with automated failover, eu-west-1 replica with write forwarding. Redis: ElastiCache Multi-AZ cluster mode. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. ArgoCD Rollouts: canary with 10% traffic split for 5 minutes before full promotion.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM on Spring Boot (dd-java-agent). Custom metrics: resupply_orders_generated_total (counter), expiry_alerts_active_count (gauge, labels: depot_region), stock_below_threshold_count (gauge, labels: product_code), shipment_transit_time_p99 (timer). PagerDuty: stock_below_threshold > 0 for critical product_codes -> P1, expiry_alert unacknowledged >48h -> P2, ResupplyEvaluationJob failure -> P2. Structured JSON logs with org_id and user_id for multi-tenant correlation.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$3,200/month for production multi-region deployment at Phase 1 scale (50 depots, 5,000 lots). EKS us-east-1: ~$800/month. EKS eu-west-1: ~$800/month. Aurora Global Database: ~$1,200/month (us-east-1 primary + eu-west-1 replica). ElastiCache Redis: ~$200/month. CloudFront: ~$100/month. S3: ~$50/month. Datadog: ~$800/month. Total: ~$3,200/month. Scaling: EKS HPA scales api-service pods on CPU 60% target; Aurora read replica scales automatically with write load.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): IMP lot registration, depot stock level tracking, automated resupply order generation with nightly Spring Batch job, multi-tier approval workflow with Spring StateMachine, expiry alert engine, Okta OIDC auth with MFA, PostgreSQL RLS tenancy, GitLab CI with ArgoCD, Datadog supply chain metrics. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Shipment tracking API integration with DHL/FedEx, patient-level IMP dispensation tracking integration with EDC systems, Recharts analytics dashboard for stock turns and expiry risk, admin panel for depot configuration and threshold management. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Predictive resupply using trial enrollment velocity, real-time stock level push notifications via AWS SNS, SOC 2 Type II evidence package generation.
parsed.development_guidelines: Java 21 with strict null safety via @NonNull annotations. Spring Boot test coverage: 80% for domain packages; integration tests with Testcontainers for Aurora and Redis. React TypeScript strict mode. Vitest for React component tests. Flyway migration scripts reviewed by DBA before merge. Spring Batch job unit tests with embedded H2. All IMP lot state transitions validated against EU GMP Annex 13 state machine rules in unit tests. GDPR erasure endpoint tested with anonymized user_id replacement.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Aurora Global Database write forwarding adds ~100ms latency for EU depot writes routed through us-east-1 primary. Mitigation: evaluate eu-west-1 Aurora cluster with async replication for high-frequency depot scan operations. Risk: Spring Batch CronJob failure leaves resupply orders in draft state. Mitigation: SNS alert on ResupplyEvaluationJob failure; manual emergency resupply endpoint for critical situations. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer filtering for tenant isolation — RLS is more reliable but requires careful migration planning. Tradeoff: Spring Batch over Quartz Scheduler for resupply evaluation — Spring Batch provides partitioned step execution suitable for 50+ depot parallel evaluation.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Aurora Global Database with write forwarding over eu-west-1 Aurora cluster for EU data residency. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer tenant filtering for regulated data. Chose Spring Batch over Quartz Scheduler for resupply evaluation due to partitioned step support. Decided Recharts over D3 for analytics charts for faster React team onboarding. DHL/FedEx shipment tracking API integration deferred to Phase 2.

reference.thinking_summary: High-scale clinical trial IMP supply chain application with complex multi-organization, multi-region requirements. Key architectural decisions: Aurora Global Database with write forwarding satisfies EU data residency without dual-write complexity; PostgreSQL RLS at the DB layer is more robust than application filtering for regulated multi-tenant data; Spring Batch for resupply evaluation provides partitioned parallel depot assessment and supports on-demand trigger; Spring StateMachine for IMP lot transitions ensures all regulatory state changes are auditable. SOC 2 Type II controls (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, access reviews) integrated from the start.
reference.title: Clinical Trial IMP Supply Chain Management Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A high-scale fullstack application managing investigational medicinal product inventory across 50+ global depot sites for concurrent Phase 3 clinical trials. React 18 dashboards give logistics coordinators real-time stock visibility; Spring Boot 3 and Spring Batch automate resupply order generation with multi-tier approval workflows and expiry alert management. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database (us-east-1 primary, eu-west-1 with write forwarding) enforces EU data residency while row-level security provides organization-scoped multi-tenant isolation. IMP lot state transitions from manufacture through patient dispensation are tracked by a Spring StateMachine with full audit trail for EU GMP Annex 13 and FDA 21 CFR Part 312 compliance. Randomization list data is field-encrypted via AWS KMS. Deployed on AWS EKS multi-region via ArgoCD with GitLab CI.
reference.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over TLS 1.3 with a Spring Boot 3 REST API on AWS EKS. Okta OIDC MFA gates all access; the JWT org_id claim is injected into each PostgreSQL connection as SET LOCAL app.current_org_id to activate row-level security policies enforcing organization tenancy. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database maintains the primary writer in us-east-1 with a write-forwarding replica in eu-west-1 to satisfy EU trial data residency. ElastiCache Redis 7 caches depot stock levels (TTL 60s, invalidated on inventory events). Spring Batch CronJobs run nightly ResupplyEvaluationJob (partitioned by depot, generates draft resupply orders for threshold breaches) and ExpiryAlertJob (scans IMP lots expiring within alert_lead_days). A weekly ReconciliationJob produces GMP Annex 13 reconciliation reports stored immutably in S3. IMP lot status transitions are managed by Spring StateMachine with optimistic locking and full audit_trail writes on every transition. Datadog APM monitors all services with supply chain-specific metrics.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v6, React Query, Zustand, Recharts, Okta React SDK. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Data JPA, Spring Batch, Spring StateMachine, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, Flyway, Testcontainers for integration tests. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database with RLS, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3 for reconciliation reports and audit exports. Infrastructure: AWS EKS (us-east-1 + eu-west-1), CloudFront, ALB with WAF (OWASP CRS), AWS KMS, CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, ECR, GitLab CI, ArgoCD with Rollouts. Observability: Datadog APM (dd-java-agent), PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: IMP Lot Registry: register lot from manufacture with product_code, quantity, expiry_date; track status through manufactured/in_transit/at_depot/dispensed/destroyed via Spring StateMachine. Depot Stock Dashboard: real-time stock levels per depot with threshold indicators; stock level updated on lot arrival scan and dispensation record. Automated Resupply: nightly Spring Batch job evaluates stock vs minimum_threshold per depot; generates draft ResupplyOrder; routes through L1 (LogisticsCoordinator) and L2 (ClinicalOpsManager) approval; dispatches to shipment on final approval. Expiry Alert Engine: nightly scan of imp_lots expiring within configurable lead days; creates expiry_records, pushes SNS notification to depot contacts; escalates unacknowledged alerts after 48h. Chain-of-Custody Audit: every IMP lot state transition appended to audit_trail with actor identity and timestamp. Analytics: stock turns by depot (Recharts), expiry risk heat map, resupply lead time trends. Admin Panel: depot configuration, threshold management, trial-depot association, user-org assignment.
reference.system_components: CloudFront (React SPA CDN + multi-origin failover). ALB with WAF OWASP CRS (per EKS region). EKS api-service Deployment (Spring Boot 3, 3-8 pods HPA). EKS frontend-service Deployment (Nginx, 2-4 pods). Spring Batch CronJobs: ResupplyEvaluationJob (nightly 02:00 UTC), ExpiryAlertJob (03:00 UTC), ReconciliationJob (weekly). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database (primary us-east-1, eu-west-1 write-forwarding replica). ElastiCache Redis 7 (per region). AWS KMS (randomization list field encryption). SNS (expiry and escalation notifications). S3 (reconciliation report PDFs). CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs (SOC 2). Datadog Agent DaemonSet. ArgoCD (GitOps deployment).
reference.workflows: Resupply Order Workflow: Nightly at 02:00 UTC, ResupplyEvaluationJob Spring Batch job starts. Partitioner creates one JobStep per active depot. Each step queries stock_levels WHERE current_quantity < minimum_threshold for that depot. For each breach, creates ResupplyOrder entity in status=draft with computed quantity_requested = (target_level - current_quantity). Job completes; total draft orders logged to Datadog metric resupply_orders_generated_total. L1 approver (LogisticsCoordinator) receives email notification; logs into dashboard, reviews draft orders, clicks Approve to transition status=draft -> pending_l2. L2 approver (ClinicalOpsManager) approves pending_l2 order; status transitions to approved; shipment record created with depot routing and carrier assignment. IMP Lot State Transition: DepotStaff scans lot barcode on arrival; PATCH /api/v1/lots/{id}/transition body {event: ARRIVE, depot_id}. Spring StateMachine validates in_transit -> at_depot transition. Optimistic lock version check prevents concurrent scan conflicts. audit_trail record appended: lot_id, in_transit, at_depot, actor_user_id, timestamp. Stock level for product_code at depot_id incremented by lot quantity; Redis cache invalidated for depot stock key.
reference.data_model: organizations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, type ENUM('sponsor','cro','depot'), region ENUM('us','eu')). trials (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, protocol_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, phase SMALLINT, status ENUM('active','completed','closed')). depot_sites (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, country CHAR(2), region ENUM('us','eu'), contact_email VARCHAR). imp_lots (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, lot_number VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL, product_code VARCHAR, quantity_units INTEGER, manufacture_date DATE, expiry_date DATE NOT NULL, status ENUM('manufactured','in_transit','at_depot','dispensed','quarantined','destroyed'), current_depot_id UUID FK->depot_sites, chain_of_custody JSONB, version INTEGER DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). stock_levels (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, depot_id UUID FK, product_code VARCHAR, trial_id UUID FK, current_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, minimum_threshold INTEGER NOT NULL, last_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). resupply_orders (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, depot_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, product_code VARCHAR, quantity_requested INTEGER, status ENUM('draft','pending_l1','pending_l2','approved','dispatched','cancelled'), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). shipments (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, from_depot_id UUID FK, to_depot_id UUID FK, resupply_order_id UUID FK, carrier VARCHAR, tracking_number VARCHAR, status ENUM('preparing','in_transit','delivered'), dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail (id BIGSERIAL PK, org_id UUID, entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID, from_state VARCHAR, to_state VARCHAR, actor_user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, event_type VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). expiry_records (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, alert_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, acknowledged_by VARCHAR, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). randomization_lists (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, list_data_encrypted TEXT, kms_key_id VARCHAR). PostgreSQL RLS policy on all org_id tables: USING (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid). Clinical trial entities: trial_patients (id UUID PK, site_id FK, subject_id_encrypted VARCHAR UNIQUE, enrollment_date DATE, treatment_arm VARCHAR, diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, current_visit_number INT, withdrawal_date DATE), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, visit_number INT, encounter_date DATE, investigator_id UUID, vitals_json JSONB, adverse_event_flags JSONB), investigational_prescriptions (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, encounter_id FK, kit_id FK, dosage_mg NUMERIC, administration_route VARCHAR, prescribed_by_physician_id UUID, dispensed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: RESTful API /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0. Auth: Okta Bearer JWT on all endpoints; org_id claim sets RLS context. GET /depots: list depot sites for caller org. GET /depots/:id/stock: current stock levels per product for depot, backed by Redis cache. GET /lots: paginated IMP lot registry with filters status, trial_id, expiry_before. PATCH /lots/:id/transition: state transition event (ARRIVE/DISPENSE/QUARANTINE/DESTROY), body {event, depot_id?}. GET /resupply-orders: list orders with status filter. PATCH /resupply-orders/:id/approve: L1/L2 approval action based on caller role. POST /resupply-orders/evaluate: on-demand trigger for SystemAdmin to run resupply evaluation outside nightly schedule. GET /expiry-records: active expiry alerts with depot and lot detail. PATCH /expiry-records/:id/acknowledge: acknowledge expiry alert. GET /analytics/stock-turns?depot_id=&from=&to=: stock turn analytics aggregation. GET /audit-trail?entity_type=imp_lot&entity_id=: audit trail for entity.
reference.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC with enforced MFA; Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server validates JWT on every request; org_id claim injected as SET LOCAL app.current_org_id for PostgreSQL RLS enforcement before each transaction. PostgreSQL RLS USING policies on all 10 clinical tables preventing cross-organization data access at database layer. AWS WAF on ALB with OWASP Core Rule Set 3.3 blocking SQLi and XSS. Randomization list list_data_encrypted via AWS KMS GenerateDataKey with per-trial data key; decrypted in-memory only for ClinicalOpsManager and SystemAdmin. audit_trail: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE FROM api_role; append-only. IMP lot state transitions guarded by Spring StateMachine ensuring only valid regulatory transitions are executed. CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs enabled for SOC 2 Type II evidence collection. Access review automation via Okta Lifecycle Management for quarterly user certification. EU GMP Annex 13 satisfied by complete lot-level audit trail and weekly ReconciliationJob reports. FDA 21 CFR Part 312 satisfied by chain-of-custody JSONB on imp_lots and audit_trail.
reference.deployment_and_operations: AWS EKS clusters in us-east-1 (primary) and eu-west-1 (EU depot data residency). api-service Deployment: minReplicas 3, maxReplicas 8, HPA on CPU 60%. Spring Batch CronJobs: ResupplyEvaluationJob (02:00 UTC), ExpiryAlertJob (03:00 UTC), ReconciliationJob (Sundays 04:00 UTC). ArgoCD manages GitOps sync from GitLab CI artifact registry. ArgoCD Rollouts: Blue/Green for api-service with analysis run (error rate <1%, p99 <500ms) before traffic shift. Flyway migrations applied as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook; down migrations tested in CI. GitLab CI pipeline: Maven build, Spring Boot integration tests (Testcontainers Aurora + Redis), React build, Vitest, Docker push ECR, Helm chart update triggers ArgoCD sync. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared Aurora), staging (multi-AZ, Aurora Global disabled), production (multi-region, Aurora Global, ElastiCache per region). Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with EKS IRSA. Aurora Global Database: us-east-1 primary writer, eu-west-1 reader with write forwarding enabled.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: dd-java-agent on api-service pods, traces correlated with Spring Batch job spans. Structured JSON logs with trace_id, org_id, user_id, lot_id context fields. Custom metrics: resupply_orders_generated_total (counter, labels: trial_id, depot_region), expiry_alerts_active_count (gauge, labels: depot_region, days_to_expiry_bucket), stock_below_threshold_count (gauge, labels: product_code, depot_region), shipment_transit_time_p99 (timer, labels: carrier). PagerDuty alerts: stock_below_threshold >0 for product_codes flagged critical -> P1, expiry_alert unacknowledged >48h -> P2, Spring Batch job failure -> P2, Aurora Global replication lag >30s -> P1.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,200/month for multi-region production at Phase 1 scale (50 depots, 5 concurrent trials). EKS EC2 worker nodes: ~$1,200/month (m5.xlarge x4 per region). Aurora Global Database (r6g.large primary + eu replica): ~$800/month. ElastiCache Redis (cache.m6g.large per region): ~$400/month. CloudFront + ALB + WAF: ~$200/month. Datadog: ~$400/month for APM + log management. S3 + KMS + CloudTrail: ~$200/month. HPA scales api-service to 8 pods for peak resupply approval windows. Spring Batch partitioned resupply job evaluates 50 depots in parallel under 2 minutes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-6): Core infrastructure — EKS clusters us-east-1 and eu-west-1, Aurora Global Database with RLS policies and Flyway schema, ElastiCache Redis, Okta OIDC integration with org_id claim mapping, ArgoCD setup. IMP lot registration and Spring StateMachine state transitions. PostgreSQL RLS validation with multi-org test fixtures. Phase 2 (weeks 7-10): Depot stock level tracking with Redis cache, React 18 stock dashboard with 60s polling, minimum threshold configuration. Spring Batch ResupplyEvaluationJob with partitioned depot steps; draft order generation. Phase 3 (weeks 11-14): Multi-tier approval workflow, Resupply order UI for L1/L2 approvers, shipment record creation on order approval. ExpiryAlertJob with SNS notifications and 48h escalation logic. Phase 4 (weeks 15-16): Analytics dashboards (stock turns, expiry risk), audit trail viewer, ReconciliationJob for GMP Annex 13 reports, Admin panel for depot and threshold configuration. SOC 2 evidence collection infrastructure (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, access review automation) activated from week 1.
reference.development_guidelines: Java: JUnit 5 + Mockito for unit tests; Testcontainers for Spring Boot integration tests with Aurora-compatible PostgreSQL and Redis. Spring StateMachine transitions must have unit tests covering all valid and invalid event scenarios. Spring Batch job steps tested with in-memory JobRepository. 85% line coverage gate. React: Vitest for component tests; React Testing Library. ESLint + Prettier in GitLab CI. OpenAPI 3.0 spec auto-generated from Spring controllers via springdoc-openapi; spec committed and validated in CI. Flyway migration naming: V{version}__{description}.sql; down migrations required for all DDL changes. ADR documents required for: RLS policy changes, new IMP lot state transitions, Spring Batch job additions. No direct Aurora access from developer laptops; all DB access via EKS Jobs with temporary IAM credentials.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Aurora Global Database write forwarding adds ~100ms latency for EU depot writes routing through us-east-1; mitigation is evaluate eu-west-1 independent Aurora cluster if depot scan throughput requires sub-50ms writes. Risk: Spring Batch ResupplyEvaluationJob runtime grows linearly with active depot count; mitigation is partition granularity tuning and timeout monitoring per partition step. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy misconfiguration could expose cross-org data; mitigation is mandatory multi-org data isolation test suite run on every schema migration. Tradeoff: Spring StateMachine adds framework complexity vs custom state validation; accepted because regulated IMP lot transitions require enforceable state machine with clear audit semantics. Risk: SOC 2 Type II timeline requires 6-month observation period starting at go-live; CloudTrail and access review automation must be operational from day one.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Aurora Global Database write forwarding selected over dual Aurora clusters for EU data residency — simpler operational model with acceptable latency. Spring StateMachine selected for IMP lot transitions over custom state validation — provides auditable transition history and enforced valid-transition rules. DHL/FedEx shipment tracking API integration deferred to Phase 2 — manual tracking_number entry sufficient for Phase 1. On-demand resupply evaluation via POST /resupply-orders/evaluate admin endpoint added to support emergency restocking outside nightly batch schedule.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues: SQLCipher key derivation (ISS-001) and audit trail PKCS#7 signature scheme (ISS-002). The key derivation should use PBKDF2 with 310,000 iterations on the user's AD DN and password hash, stored in the encrypted safeStorage token. The audit trail signature uses PKCS#7 with the scientist's Windows Certificate Store certificate, stored in the audit_trail_entries record. VB6 parity tests should be documented in the phased_implementation section using a golden dataset approach with OQ tests run against the dedicated validation workstation.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified PBKDF2 key derivation from user AD DN and password hash with 310,000 iterations. Key stored in Electron safeStorage encrypted token. SQLCipher key file generated at first launch and stored in user's AppData directory.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SQLCipher key cannot be hardcoded; key derivation is deterministic from AD credentials; password change invalidates local DB access until re-keying prompt

fix_report.action_taken: Defined PKCS#7 digital signature scheme for audit_trail_entries using Windows Certificate Store certificates. Scientist selects certificate from Windows Store on first use; certificate thumbprint stored in scientist profile. Each audit entry signed with SHA-256 digest of calculation inputs + result hashes + timestamp, stored as PKCS#7 detached signature in audit_trail_entries record.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Each audit entry is electronically signed per 21 CFR Part 11; signature cannot be forged without scientist's private key

parsed.title: Dose Calculation Desktop Tool Architecture v2
parsed.executive_summary: A cross-platform Electron 30 desktop application replacing the aging VB6 dose-calculation tool for formulation scientists. A Python FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18080 handles the calculation engine, excipient limit checks, and audit trail writing. Local dose records are stored in SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256 encryption, derived from the user's Active Directory credentials via PBKDF2. Audit trail entries are electronically signed with the scientist's Windows Certificate Store PKCS#7 certificate. Nightly sync pushes records to a corporate SQL Server 2019 database. The application is deployed via SCCM to on-premise workstations and requires formal IQ/OQ/PQ validation before production use.
parsed.architecture_overview: Electron 30 main process manages the application lifecycle: spawns a Python FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18080 at startup, kills it on quit, and handles AD LDAP authentication via ldap3. The React 18 renderer communicates with the main process via contextBridge for auth state and with the FastAPI sidecar via localhost HTTP for all calculation and data operations. Local dose records are stored in SQLite 3.45 encrypted via SQLCipher AES-256. The SQLCipher key is derived from the user's AD DN and password hash via PBKDF2 with 310,000 iterations, stored in Electron's safeStorage encrypted token. Audit trail entries are written by the FastAPI sidecar with PKCS#7 digital signatures using the scientist's Windows Certificate Store certificate. Nightly MaterialsDBSync job pushes local materials records to SQL Server 2019 over the corporate LAN. The application is deployed as an MSI (Windows) and PKG (macOS) via SCCM, with update checks on startup.
parsed.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 30, React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query, contextBridge. Sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg, pyodbc for SQL Server sync, ldap3 for AD auth, cryptography for PKCS#7 signing, SQLCipher via pysqlcipher3. Database: SQLite 3.45 with SQLCipher AES-256, SQL Server 2019 for corporate sync. Auth: Active Directory LDAP via ldap3. Certificates: Windows Certificate Store for PKCS#7 signing; certifi for certificate loading. Build: electron-builder for MSI/PKG, PyInstaller for frozen sidecar. Crash reporting: on-premise Sentry instance. CI: GitHub Actions. Validation: CSV with IQ/OQ/PQ phases.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Dose Calculation: scientist selects active ingredient from materials database, enters concentration and excipient ratios, FastAPI sidecar runs calculation engine, displays dose_per_unit and overage_percent with pass/fail indicator. Excipient Limit Check: ExcipientLimitChecker queries excipient_limits table against regulatory_limit_mg; violations flagged in calculation result. Audit Trail: scientist clicks Sign to write audit_trail_entries record with PKCS#7 signature; read-only paginated view of all signed entries. Materials Lookup: search corporate materials database via SQL Server sync; display CAS number, supplier, and regulatory limit. Admin: manage excipient limit tables, trigger manual sync, view sync history. Supervisor Review: view cross-site audit trail with signature verification indicator.
parsed.system_components: Electron Main Process: AD LDAP auth, SQLCipher key derivation (PBKDF2 on user DN + password hash), sidecar lifecycle management, safeStorage token management. React Renderer: Calculation screen, Materials Lookup screen, Audit Trail screen, Admin screen. Python FastAPI Sidecar: DoseCalculationEngine, ExcipientLimitChecker, AuditTrailWriter, MaterialsDBSync. SQLite (SQLCipher): local encrypted dose records. SQL Server 2019: corporate central sync target. Windows Certificate Store: scientist PKCS#7 certificates. On-premise Sentry: crash reporting. GitHub Actions: CI and installer build. SCCM: workstation deployment.
parsed.workflows: Dose Calculation and Audit: Scientist opens Calculation screen, selects active ingredient from materials list, enters concentration and excipient ratios. React calls POST /api/calculate on FastAPI sidecar with JSON body. FastAPI runs DoseCalculationEngine, queries excipient_limits table, writes dose_calculations record to SQLite. FastAPI calls AuditTrailWriter.write_audit_entry with calculation inputs, result hashes, and scientist AD DN. AuditTrailWriter loads scientist's Windows Certificate Store certificate via certifi, computes SHA-256 digest of input+result+timestamp, signs with private key, stores PKCS#7 signature in audit_trail_entries record. Scientist clicks Sign; FastAPI writes audit entry to SQLite. Audit entry verified by supervisor review screen which calls FastAPI GET /api/audit/verify/{id} with scientist's certificate thumbprint to verify PKCS#7 signature. SQL Server Sync: Nightly MaterialsDBSync job runs at 02:00 via Windows Task Scheduler. Job queries local materials table, inserts or updates materials records in SQL Server via pyodbc. Sync status written to sync_history table. VB6 Parity OQ Test: Validation OQ test runs calculation engine against golden VB6 dataset (pre-exported CSV from VB6 tool) and asserts numerical output equivalence within 1e-6 tolerance. Test runs in dedicated validation environment with isolated SQLite DB.
parsed.data_model: formulations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, active_ingredient VARCHAR, target_dose_mg NUMERIC, batch_size NUMERIC, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). excipients (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, cas_number VARCHAR, regulatory_limit_mg NUMERIC, limit_source VARCHAR). dose_calculations (id UUID PK, formulation_id UUID FK, excipient_ratios JSONB, calculated_dose_per_unit NUMERIC, overage_percent NUMERIC, limit_violations JSONB, scientist_ad_dn VARCHAR, calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail_entries (id UUID PK, calculation_id UUID FK, scientist_ad_dn VARCHAR, inputs_hash CHAR(64), result_hash CHAR(64), pkcs7_signature TEXT, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). materials (id UUID PK, material_code VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, cas_number VARCHAR, supplier VARCHAR, regulatory_limit_mg NUMERIC, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scientist_profiles (id UUID PK, ad_dn VARCHAR UNIQUE, certificate_thumbprint VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, role ENUM scientist/supervisor/admin).
parsed.api_design: FastAPI sidecar REST API on localhost:18080. POST /api/calculate {formulation_id, excipient_ratios, concentration_mg_per_ml} -> {dose_per_unit, overage_percent, limit_violations, calculation_id}. POST /api/audit/write {calculation_id, scientist_ad_dn, inputs_hash, result_hash} -> {audit_entry_id, pkcs7_signature}. GET /api/audit/verify/{id} {certificate_thumbprint} -> {verified: bool, signature_valid: bool}. GET /api/materials?query= -> [{material_code, description, cas_number, regulatory_limit_mg}]. GET /api/sync/history -> [{sync_id, status, started_at, completed_at}]. POST /api/admin/sync/trigger -> {sync_id}.
parsed.security_and_compliance: 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 controls: AD LDAP authentication for all user sessions; SQLCipher AES-256 encryption for local SQLite with key derived from PBKDF2 on user AD DN and password hash (310,000 iterations). Each audit_trail_entries record signed with PKCS#7 digital signature using scientist's Windows Certificate Store certificate; signature covers calculation inputs hash, result hash, and timestamp. Audit entries immutable: REVOKE UPDATE DELETE on audit_trail_entries from app user role. 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements met. EU Annex 11 access controls: role-based auth for scientist, supervisor, admin; supervisor review screen verifies PKCS#7 signature before displaying. Computer system validation: CSV package required; IQ/OQ/PQ phases mandatory. VB6 numerical parity: OQ tests run against golden dataset in validation environment.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Python pytest (calculation engine parity tests against VB6 golden dataset, unit tests), TypeScript ESLint + Vitest for React components, electron-builder for MSI/PKG artifacts, PyInstaller for frozen sidecar, upload to internal release server. Installer hash published to internal checksum registry for IT security validation. SCCM pushes MSI to workstation fleet on version update. Update check on startup: app calls internal update server to check version; if new version available, downloads MSI from internal file server. Validation environment: dedicated OQ/PQ workstation with golden VB6 dataset loaded; runs OQ parity tests on every build. Production: scientist workstations via SCCM deployment. Rollback: SCCM rollback procedure; previous MSI retained on internal file server.
parsed.observability: Sentry on-premise instance captures Electron renderer crashes with no PII in payloads. Structured log file on workstation (daily rotation) with fields: timestamp, level, component, message. Log levels: DEBUG (calculation inputs/outputs), INFO (calculation result, audit entry written), WARNING (sync failure, SQLite write failure), ERROR (sidecar startup failure, crash). Log file shipped to IT on request. No external telemetry; no network calls during calculation. Sync status visible in Admin screen: last sync timestamp, sync history, error count.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost; on-premise deployment. SCCM licensing cost is existing IT infrastructure. On-premise Sentry instance is a one-time hardware cost. PyInstaller-frozen sidecar increases installer size to approximately 150MB; IT security pre-approval required before production deployment. SQLite scales to thousands of dose records per scientist without performance concerns; SQL Server sync is nightly batch.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (12 weeks): AD LDAP auth, dose calculation engine matching VB6 algorithm, excipient limit check, audit trail with PKCS#7 signature, SQLite SQLCipher local storage with PBKDF2 key derivation, Electron MSI/PKG installer, SCCM deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 - Validation (4 weeks): VB6 golden dataset import, OQ numerical parity tests on validation workstation, PQ user acceptance testing, CSV package preparation for regulatory submission. Phase 3 - Production (4 weeks): Production SCCM deployment, IT security AV whitelist submission, on-premise Sentry deployment, supervisor review portal, cross-site audit trail visibility.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python: black and flake8 enforced in CI; pytest for unit tests; 85% coverage gate for calculation engine and audit writer modules. TypeScript: ESLint + Vitest; 90% coverage gate for React components. Electron: contextBridge API surface must be typed; no untyped IPC calls. SQLCipher key must never be logged or printed in debug output; use key file path only. PKCS#7 certificate thumbprint stored in scientist profile, not in audit record; certificate loaded from Windows Store at runtime. VB6 golden dataset stored in version control as CSV; OQ parity tests run against this golden set in CI.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SQLCipher key derivation from AD credentials means password change invalidates local DB access until re-keying prompt; mitigation is to cache re-keying prompt on next login after AD password change detection. Risk: PyInstaller-frozen sidecar increases installer size and may trigger AV false positives; mitigation is pre-submission to IT security for AV whitelist. Risk: PKCS#7 certificate management (certificate selection on first use, rotation) adds user friction; mitigation is guided wizard on first use. Tradeoff: localhost HTTP bridge between Electron and FastAPI over Electron IPC for easier sidecar testability in CI, but adds complexity of managing a second process. Tradeoff: nightly SQL Server sync over real-time sync to avoid network dependency during calculations.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: SQLCipher key derived from PBKDF2 on user AD DN and password hash with 310,000 iterations; key stored in safeStorage token. ISS-002 resolved: audit trail entries signed with PKCS#7 using scientist's Windows Certificate Store certificate; signature covers inputs+result+timestamp. ISS-003 resolved: VB6 golden dataset approach documented; OQ parity tests run against dedicated validation workstation in CI.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 must address ISS-001 (SQLCipher key derivation) and ISS-002 (audit trail electronic signature). Both are 21 CFR Part 11 blockers. The plan intentionally introduces a flaw per manifest quality=flawed: the deployment section omits the IQ/OQ/PQ validation lifecycle details that were explicitly required by the compliance_context contract, creating a contract violation. All other sections are addressed with reasonable detail for a regulated desktop application.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified PBKDF2-SHA256 key derivation for SQLCipher using user AD DN + AD password hash as input with 310,000 iterations and per-user 32-byte salt stored in Windows DPAPI-protected registry key; key never stored in plaintext
fix_report.expected_outcome: SQLCipher key is user-specific and not extractable without AD credentials, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 access control requirements

fix_report.action_taken: Defined PKCS#7 detached digital signature for each audit_trail_entry using scientist certificate loaded from Windows Certificate Store; signature covers calculation_id, scientist_ad_dn, inputs_hash, result_hash, and timestamp; pkcs7_signature column added to audit_trail_entries table
fix_report.expected_outcome: Each dose calculation audit entry is cryptographically signed by the responsible scientist, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements

reference.title: Dose Calculation Desktop Tool Architecture v2
reference.executive_summary: A cross-platform Electron 30 and React 18 desktop application replacing the VB6 dose-calculation tool for formulation scientists. A Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18080 handles dose calculation, excipient limit checking, and audit trail writes. SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher using PBKDF2-derived user-specific keys stores calculations and audit entries locally. Each dose calculation audit entry is signed with the scientist's PKCS#7 certificate from the Windows Certificate Store to satisfy 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements. Active Directory LDAP provides authentication with role-based access for Scientist, Supervisor, and Admin roles. Nightly batch sync pushes confirmed calculation records to corporate SQL Server. Deployed via MSI/PKG installer distributed through SCCM.
reference.architecture_overview: Electron main process manages the Python FastAPI sidecar lifecycle (spawned on startup, killed on quit) and holds the AD LDAP session and SQLCipher database connection. React renderer communicates with main process via contextBridge IPC for auth state and with the FastAPI sidecar via localhost HTTP for calculation and data operations. FastAPI modules: DoseCalculationEngine matches VB6 algorithm for numerical parity, ExcipientLimitChecker queries local SQLite excipient_limits, AuditTrailWriter signs and appends audit entries, MaterialsDBSync runs nightly SQL Server sync. SQLite DB encrypted via SQLCipher with PBKDF2-derived key (AD credentials, 310,000 iterations, Windows DPAPI-protected salt). PKCS#7 detached signatures on audit entries loaded from Windows Certificate Store.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 30, React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query, React Router v6, electron-builder for MSI/PKG. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, ldap3, pyodbc, pywin32 (Certificate Store access), cryptography library for PKCS#7, PyInstaller for frozen binary. Data: SQLite 3.45 + SQLCipher, SQL Server 2019. Testing: pytest with VB6 golden dataset parity fixtures, Vitest for React components. CI: GitHub Actions.
reference.functional_feature_map: Dose Calculation: scientist selects formulation, enters active ingredient concentration and excipient ratios, sidecar computes dose_per_unit and overage_percent, flags excipient limit violations. Electronic Sign: scientist clicks Sign, sidecar generates PKCS#7 signature over calculation record, appends to audit_trail_entries. Materials Lookup: search materials table synced from SQL Server. Audit Trail Viewer: read-only paginated list of audit entries with signature verification status. Admin Panel: excipient limit management, manual SQL Server sync trigger. VB6 Migration: one-time CSV import of VB6 export during IQ phase.
reference.system_components: Electron main process (AD LDAP auth, SQLCipher DB connection, sidecar lifecycle manager, contextBridge IPC). React renderer (calculation UI, materials lookup, audit trail view). Python FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18080 (DoseCalculationEngine, ExcipientLimitChecker, AuditTrailWriter, MaterialsDBSync). SQLite with SQLCipher (local encrypted storage). Windows Certificate Store (PKCS#7 scientist certificates). SQL Server 2019 (corporate central sync target). On-premise Sentry (crash reporting). SCCM (installer distribution).
reference.workflows: Dose Calculation and Sign Workflow: Scientist logs in via AD LDAP; Electron main process binds to AD, derives SQLCipher key via PBKDF2, opens encrypted SQLite DB. Scientist opens Calculation screen, selects formulation, enters concentration and excipient_ratios JSON. POST /calculate sent to FastAPI sidecar; DoseCalculationEngine computes dose_per_unit, overage_percent; ExcipientLimitChecker queries excipient_limits and returns violations list. Result displayed. Scientist reviews and clicks Sign; sidecar loads scientist PKCS#7 cert from Windows Certificate Store, signs {calculation_id, scientist_ad_dn, SHA256(inputs), SHA256(result), timestamp}, appends audit_trail_entry with pkcs7_signature. Nightly Sync: MaterialsDBSync connects to SQL Server via pyodbc, pulls materials and excipient_limits delta, updates local SQLite. Pushes unsynchronized dose_calculations records to SQL Server sync table; marks local records sync_status='synced'.
reference.data_model: formulations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, active_ingredient VARCHAR, target_dose_mg NUMERIC, batch_size INTEGER, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). excipients (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, cas_number VARCHAR, regulatory_limit_mg NUMERIC, limit_source VARCHAR, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). dose_calculations (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK->formulations, excipient_ratios JSONB, calculated_dose_per_unit NUMERIC(10,4), overage_percent NUMERIC(5,2), limit_violations JSONB, scientist_id VARCHAR, calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sync_status ENUM('pending','synced')). audit_trail_entries (id UUID PK, calculation_id UUID FK->dose_calculations, scientist_ad_dn VARCHAR NOT NULL, inputs_hash CHAR(64) NOT NULL, result_hash CHAR(64) NOT NULL, pkcs7_signature TEXT NOT NULL, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). materials (id UUID PK, material_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, description VARCHAR, supplier VARCHAR, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). SQLCipher key: PBKDF2-SHA256(AD_dn + AD_password_hash, salt=DPAPI_protected_registry_value, iterations=310000, dklen=32). Clinical use entities: patient_weight_profiles (id UUID PK, patient_id_hash VARCHAR, weight_kg NUMERIC, bsa_m2 NUMERIC, creatinine_clearance_ml_min NUMERIC, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, recorded_by_pharmacist_id UUID), dose_calculations (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK, patient_weight_profile_id FK, calculated_dose_mg NUMERIC, adjusted_dose_mg NUMERIC, adjustment_reason TEXT, clinical_notes TEXT, approved_by_physician_id UUID, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ), contraindication_alerts (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK, condition_icd10 VARCHAR, severity ENUM contraindicated/caution, alert_text TEXT).
reference.api_design: FastAPI sidecar endpoints on localhost:18080 (not externally accessible). POST /calculate: body {formulation_id, excipient_ratios JSONB, scientist_id}; returns {dose_per_unit, overage_percent, limit_violations}. POST /sign-calculation: body {calculation_id, scientist_ad_dn}; loads cert from Windows Certificate Store, signs, writes audit_trail_entry; returns {audit_entry_id, signed_at}. GET /materials?q=search_term: returns paginated materials list from local SQLite. GET /audit-trail?scientist_id=&from=&to=: returns paginated audit entries with signature_valid flag. POST /sync/trigger: manually triggers SQL Server sync; returns {synced_count, errors}.
reference.security_and_compliance: AD LDAP authentication via ldap3; Electron safeStorage for session token. SQLCipher AES-256 with PBKDF2-SHA256 derived key (310,000 iterations, per-user DPAPI-protected salt in Windows registry); key never stored in plaintext or exposed to renderer. PKCS#7 detached digital signature on each audit_trail_entry; scientist certificate loaded from Windows Certificate Store; signature verified on read and verification status surfaced in Audit Trail UI. REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE on audit_trail_entries from application DB user. 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements met by PKCS#7 scheme. EU Annex 11 access controls met by RBAC (Scientist/Supervisor/Admin). Sentry crash reports contain no dose calculation data.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Electron app packaged via electron-builder as MSI (Windows) and PKG (macOS). Python sidecar frozen via PyInstaller and bundled in installer. SCCM pushes installer to workstation fleet. Update check on startup against internal release server; new installer version triggers download and silent install prompt. GitHub Actions CI builds MSI/PKG artifacts and runs pytest parity tests. NOTE: This plan does not detail the IQ/OQ/PQ validation phases or provide a validation test protocol structure, which is an omission given the compliance_context contract requirement for computer system validation documentation.
reference.observability: On-premise Sentry for crash reporting; Sentry SDK in Electron main process and Python sidecar. Structured JSON log file on workstation at %APPDATA%/DoseCalc/logs/ rotated daily, retained 90 days. Log entries include: auth events, calculation IDs, sync status, signature verification results. No external telemetry.
reference.cost_and_scaling: No cloud hosting costs. On-premise Sentry instance is shared infrastructure. Installer distribution via SCCM at no additional cost. SQLite local storage ~50MB per active year per scientist. SQL Server central DB estimated 10GB after 5 years across 50 scientists.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-6): Electron shell with AD LDAP auth, FastAPI sidecar with DoseCalculationEngine (numerical parity verified against VB6 golden dataset of 200 reference calculations), SQLite schema via Alembic, SQLCipher with PBKDF2 key derivation. Phase 2 (weeks 7-10): Audit trail with PKCS#7 signatures, excipient limit checker, materials lookup. Phase 3 (weeks 11-14): SQL Server nightly sync, Admin panel, Supervisor review flow. Phase 4: IQ/OQ/PQ validation execution.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: pytest with 90% coverage gate on DoseCalculationEngine and AuditTrailWriter. Numerical parity tests load VB6 golden dataset fixtures and assert output within 0.001% tolerance. TypeScript: Vitest for React components, ESLint + Prettier. contextBridge API surface reviewed in security PR checklist. electron-builder config pinned to specific Electron and Node versions for reproducible installers. Alembic migrations required for all schema changes with down migrations.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PBKDF2 key derivation means AD password change invalidates local SQLite access; mitigated by re-keying workflow triggered on next login after AD password change detection. Risk: PyInstaller-frozen sidecar may trigger AV false positives on workstations; mitigated by pre-submission to IT security team for AV whitelist before SCCM deployment. Tradeoff: PKCS#7 using Windows Certificate Store limits macOS compatibility to macOS Keychain integration requiring separate implementation path.
reference.open_questions_resolved: SQLCipher key derivation: PBKDF2-SHA256 from AD credentials with DPAPI-protected salt resolves ISS-001. Audit trail electronic signature: PKCS#7 from Windows Certificate Store resolves ISS-002. VB6 parity: golden dataset of 200 reference calculations used for OQ numerical parity tests.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 must fix ISS-001 (TF-IDF clustering without prefix anchoring) and ISS-002 (missing NetworkPolicy in Helm skeleton). The prefix-anchoring fix requires documenting the longest-non-overlapping-prefix extraction algorithm with a concrete billing/billing-admin example. The NetworkPolicy fix requires adding a mandatory Jinja2 template component to every generated Helm chart. Both fixes are local to the HelmGenerator and AclParser modules and do not require architectural changes to the overall CLI or PostgreSQL state model.

fix_report.action_taken: Added PrefixAnchoredClusterer module that extracts the longest non-overlapping prefix from each rule path before TF-IDF vectorisation. The algorithm iterates rules in descending path length order, assigning each rule to the first cluster where its prefix does not overlap with any existing cluster centroid. The billing/billing-admin example: /api/v1/billing and /api/v1/billing-admin both share /api/v1/billing as a prefix, but the algorithm assigns them to separate clusters because /api/v1/billing-admin has a longer unique prefix. Documented in architecture_overview and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved; billing and billing-admin domains no longer merged in clustering output

fix_report.action_taken: Added NetworkPolicy Jinja2 template component to HelmGenerator. Every generated Helm chart now includes a NetworkPolicy resource restricting egress from the OPA sidecar to the OPA bundle registry namespace and the Cloud SQL proxy service account only. Documented in architecture_overview and api_design sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved; OPA sidecars restricted to bundle registry egress only; lateral movement risk eliminated

parsed.title: ACL Decomposition Tool — Round 2 Plan
parsed.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Typer CLI that ingests a monolithic ACL file from GitLab, clusters rules using prefix-anchored TF-IDF (longest non-overlapping resource prefix extraction before vectorisation), generates per-domain OPA Rego bundles and Helm charts with NetworkPolicy egress restrictions, and orchestrates deployment to GKE Autopilot with a 7-day shadow evaluation period before cutover. Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (prefix anchoring) and ISS-002 (NetworkPolicy).
parsed.architecture_overview: Five-subcommand Typer CLI running in GitLab CI. AclParser ingests ACL file from GitLab file API, decrypts the encrypted blob using pgcrypto, and parses YAML/JSON rules into a list of (path, action, subject) tuples. PrefixAnchoredClusterer extracts the longest non-overlapping prefix from each rule path before passing to TfidfVectorizer; rules with overlapping prefixes are assigned to separate clusters. DomainPlanBuilder maps clusters to decomposition_plans rows with domain_owner, resource_prefix, and rule_count. HelmGenerator renders a Jinja2 chart skeleton including a NetworkPolicy resource restricting egress to the OPA bundle registry namespace and the Cloud SQL proxy service account only. PolicyTestRunner runs OPA test runner against the Rego bundle and captures pass/fail per rule fixture. All state persisted to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via Workload Identity Federation. Generated Helm charts and Rego bundles stored in GCS with plan_id prefix. GitLab MR created per domain with generated files attached. Cutover gate: 7-day shadow divergence_count = 0 and domain-owner approval.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI: Typer 0.12, Click 8.1. Clustering: scikit-learn 1.4 TfidfVectorizer, scipy.spatial.distance.cosine for similarity calculation. Templating: Jinja2 3.1 for Helm chart rendering. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto for encrypted ACL blobs. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager via google-cloud-secret-manager. Auth: Workload Identity Federation OIDC for GCP and GitLab. Logging: Cloud Logging with structured JSON. Monitoring: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics. Hosting: GKE Autopilot. CI: GitLab CI with Workload Identity Federation OIDC.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Analyse: ingests ACL file from GitLab, decrypts encrypted blob, parses rules, runs PrefixAnchoredClusterer, writes cluster assignments to PostgreSQL. Plan: reads clusters, creates decomposition_plans records with domain ownership metadata, stores in PostgreSQL. Generate: renders Helm chart and Rego bundle per domain using Jinja2 templates, stores in GCS, writes generated_artifacts record to PostgreSQL. Deploy: creates GitLab MR per domain with generated files, sets cutover gate. Validate: runs OPA test runner against Rego bundle, captures pass/fail per rule fixture, writes test_coverage_report. Shadow: monitors divergence_count over 7-day period, alerts on divergence.
parsed.system_components: GitLab CI runner (runs Typer CLI subcommands as GitLab job steps). AclParser (GitLab file API client, pgcrypto decryption, YAML/JSON rule parsing). PrefixAnchoredClusterer (longest non-overlapping prefix extraction, TfidfVectorizer with cosine similarity threshold 0.65). DomainPlanBuilder (maps clusters to decomposition_plans rows). HelmGenerator (Jinja2 chart skeleton with NetworkPolicy template). PolicyTestRunner (subprocess OPA test runner). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (decomposition_plans, acl_rule_sets, migration_batches, test_coverage_reports). GCS bucket (generated Helm chart archives and Rego bundle tarballs, versioned by plan_id). GCP Secret Manager (PostgreSQL DSN, GitLab personal access token). GKE Autopilot (OPA sidecar DaemonSet per domain namespace). Cloud Logging (structured JSON logs).
parsed.workflows: ACL Decomposition and Helm Generation: GitLab CI triggers acl-decomp analyse subcommand on MR. AclParser fetches ACL file from GitLab file API, decrypts pgcrypto-encrypted blob, parses YAML/JSON rules into (path, action, subject) tuples. PrefixAnchoredClusterer iterates rules in descending path length order, assigns each rule to the first cluster where its prefix does not overlap with any existing cluster centroid. TfidfVectorizer vectorises cluster centroids, cosine similarity threshold 0.65. DomainPlanBuilder maps clusters to decomposition_plans rows with domain_owner, resource_prefix, rule_count. HelmGenerator renders Jinja2 chart skeleton including NetworkPolicy restricting egress to OPA bundle registry namespace and Cloud SQL proxy service account only. Generated Helm chart and Rego bundle stored in GCS with plan_id prefix. GitLab MR created with generated files attached. Cutover Orchestration: GitLab CI job blocked until MR approved by domain-owner. After approval, deploy subcommand creates migration_batches record with shadow_start. OPA sidecar DaemonSet deployed to domain namespace. 7-day shadow period: PolicyTestRunner runs OPA test runner against Rego bundle against monolith ACL baseline fixtures. divergence_count incremented per failed rule. If divergence_count = 0 at shadow_end, cutover_at set and monolith ACL service disabled via DNS switch.
parsed.data_model: decomposition_plans (id UUID PK, acl_source_ref TEXT, domain TEXT, resource_prefix TEXT, rule_count INT, similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.65, status ENUM planning/generating/deployed/cutover, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). acl_rule_sets (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, encrypted_blob BYTEA using pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt, rule_count INT). migration_batches (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, gitlab_mr_iid INT, shadow_start TIMESTAMPTZ, shadow_end TIMESTAMPTZ, divergence_count INT DEFAULT 0, cutover_approved_by TEXT, cutover_at TIMESTAMPTZ). test_coverage_reports (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, total_fixtures INT, passed INT, failed INT, opa_version TEXT, report_jsonb JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: CLI subcommands only — no HTTP API. Subcommands: acl-decomp analyse --acl-ref <gitlab-file-ref> --domain-owner <gitlab-user> --output-dir <local-dir>. acl-decomp plan --plan-id <uuid> --output-dir <local-dir>. acl-decomp generate --plan-id <uuid> --output-dir <local-dir>. acl-decomp deploy --plan-id <uuid> --gitlab-token <token> --domain-owner <gitlab-user>. acl-decomp validate --plan-id <uuid> --opa-version <version>. acl-decomp shadow-status --plan-id <uuid>. acl-decomp cutover --plan-id <uuid> --approval-token <token>. All subcommands accept --debug flag for verbose logging. Generated Helm chart files: values.yaml, deployment.yaml, networkpolicy.yaml, configmap.yaml, rego-bundle.tar.gz. Stored in GCS under gs://acl-decomp-artifacts/{plan_id}/.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Workload Identity Federation: GKE service account bound to GCP service account; GitLab CI job uses OIDC token exchanged for GCP access token; no static service account key files. pgcrypto encryption: ACL blobs encrypted at rest with AES-256 using pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt; decryption key in GCP Secret Manager. NetworkPolicy: every generated Helm chart includes a NetworkPolicy resource restricting egress from OPA sidecar to the OPA bundle registry namespace and the Cloud SQL proxy service account only. SOC 2 CC6.1: ACL data encrypted at rest. SOC 2 CC6.3: domain-owner named reviewer required for every GitLab MR. SOC 2 CC7.1: every Helm MR links to decomposition_plans record and clustering output artifact; 7-day shadow period documented in change management runbook. GCP Secret Manager: PostgreSQL DSN and GitLab personal access token rotated quarterly.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: lint (ruff + mypy --strict), unit-test (pytest with 80% coverage gate on clustering and Helm generation modules), integration-test (spins Cloud SQL proxy, runs end-to-end analyse/plan/generate with 500-rule fixture), deploy-dry-run (runs generate against staging ACL snapshot, verifies Helm templating, does not push MR). Environments: dev (minikube with local PostgreSQL), staging (GKE staging namespace with Cloud SQL staging instance), production (GKE prod namespace with Cloud SQL production instance in eu-west1). GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency mitigated by pre-warming OPA sidecar DaemonSet 24 hours before cutover window. Rollback: OPA sidecar rollback by reverting DaemonSet image tag via kubectl rollout undo; monolith ACL service remains active during shadow period.
parsed.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: acl_decomposition_duration_seconds (histogram per subcommand), helm_charts_generated_per_domain (counter), shadow_divergence_rate (gauge per plan_id), opa_evaluation_latency_ms (histogram per domain). Cloud Logging structured JSON: plan_id, subcommand, actor (GitLab user), outcome, duration_ms, error_details. Alert: shadow_divergence_rate > 0 triggers PagerDuty P2 for any plan_id. Alert: acl_decomposition_duration_seconds > 600 triggers PagerDuty P2 for any subcommand.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Cloud SQL db-g1-small: ~$50/month. GCS storage for generated Helm charts and Rego bundles: ~$5/month. GCP Secret Manager: ~$5/month. GKE Autopilot node provisioning: ~$30/month for 20 sidecar pods. Total: ~$90/month. Scaling: GKE Autopilot scales OPA sidecar pods automatically; 20 domains at 50k rules each completes in under 10 minutes with parallelised Helm generation.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Pilot (6 weeks): acl-decomp analyse with PrefixAnchoredClusterer, acl-decomp plan, acl-decomp generate with NetworkPolicy template, acl-decomp deploy with GitLab MR automation, acl-decomp validate with OPA test runner, 7-day shadow mode, 5-domain pilot. Phase 2 — Full Rollout (6 weeks): Full 20-domain decomposition, cutover orchestration with DNS switch, SOC 2 CC7.1 evidence collection, GKE Autopilot node pre-warming for cutover window, automated drift detection comparing live OPA decisions to monolith baseline.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy --strict. ruff for linting. 80% coverage gate on clustering and Helm generation modules enforced in GitLab CI. Integration tests use Cloud SQL proxy spun up in CI. PrefixAnchoredClusterer must be unit-tested with the billing/billing-admin example. HelmGenerator must be tested with a NetworkPolicy template that fails to render without the mandatory egress restrictions. All generated Helm chart files must be reviewed by a platform engineer before any OPA sidecar deployment.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PrefixAnchoredClusterer misclassifies rules with identical longest non-overlapping prefixes across multiple domains. Mitigation: cosine similarity threshold 0.65 defaults to 0.65; domain engineers can override in plan config. Risk: OPA sidecar DaemonSet pod startup delay during cutover window. Mitigation: pre-warm pods 24 hours before cutover window. Tradeoff: 7-day shadow period over automated cutover reduces risk of silent access regressions but increases time-to-cutover from 2 weeks to 3 weeks.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Prefix anchoring confirmed as longest non-overlapping prefix extraction before TF-IDF. NetworkPolicy confirmed as mandatory component of every generated Helm chart. 7-day shadow period confirmed as minimum for SOC 2 CC7.1 change management evidence. GKE Autopilot confirmed over standard GKE for node management simplicity.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 must fix two high-severity blocking issues before this plan can advance. ISS-001 requires replacing flat TF-IDF tokenisation with a longest-non-overlapping-prefix extraction step before vectorisation, so that /api/v1/billing and /api/v1/billing-admin are split at the point of divergence rather than sharing a /api/v1/billing token. ISS-002 requires adding a NetworkPolicy resource to the Jinja2 Helm chart template that restricts OPA sidecar ingress to the owning namespace and egress to the bundle registry URL and Cloud SQL proxy port only. The rest of the round-1 architecture is sound: Workload Identity Federation, pgcrypto encryption, and the five-subcommand CLI structure all pass security review. The plan quality target is moderate — data_model and cost sections provide adequate but not exhaustive detail.

fix_report.action_taken: Added longest-non-overlapping-prefix extraction step to PrefixAnchoredClusterer before TF-IDF vectorisation. The extractor splits each rule's resource path at the deepest prefix that does not overlap with any other domain's prefix set, using a trie over all rule paths. /api/v1/billing-admin rules now anchor to /api/v1/billing-admin rather than sharing the /api/v1/billing token.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Integration test with billing/billing-admin fixture produces two distinct clusters with zero cross-domain rule contamination in 5 of 5 runs.

fix_report.action_taken: Added NetworkPolicy Jinja2 template block to Helm chart skeleton. Every generated chart now includes a NetworkPolicy that allows ingress from the owning namespace only and egress to the OPA bundle registry (port 443) and Cloud SQL proxy (port 5432). All other egress denied by default.
fix_report.expected_outcome: kubectl apply of generated Helm chart in staging shows OPA sidecar pod cannot reach pods outside its namespace or any non-whitelisted external endpoint.

reference.title: ACL Decomposition Tool — Round 2 Plan
reference.executive_summary: This revision fixes the two blocking round-1 issues: TF-IDF clustering now anchors to longest non-overlapping path prefixes via a trie-based extractor, eliminating cross-domain contamination; and every generated Helm chart now includes a NetworkPolicy restricting OPA sidecar traffic to the owning namespace and whitelisted egress only. The five-subcommand Python/Typer CLI architecture, Workload Identity Federation, pgcrypto encryption of ACL blobs, and 7-day shadow evaluation period are confirmed. Plan quality is moderate — adequate for the 3-person platform team scope.
reference.architecture_overview: The ACL decomposition pipeline runs as a Python 3.12/Typer CLI with five subcommands. acl-decomp analyse reads the monolithic ACL YAML from GitLab file API, decrypts the ACL blob, and runs the PrefixAnchoredClusterer: a trie is built over all resource paths, then each rule is anchored to its longest non-overlapping prefix before TF-IDF vectorisation. This fix (ISS-001) ensures /api/v1/billing and /api/v1/billing-admin are split correctly. acl-decomp plan writes per-domain decomposition_plans rows. acl-decomp generate renders Helm charts and Rego bundles per domain via Jinja2; the chart skeleton now includes a NetworkPolicy (ISS-002 fix) restricting ingress to the owning namespace and egress to bundle registry port 443 and Cloud SQL proxy port 5432. acl-decomp deploy creates a GitLab MR per domain with the generated artifacts, assigning the domain owner as required reviewer. acl-decomp validate runs OPA test suite against Rego bundle and compares shadow-mode decisions against the monolith baseline for 7 days before cutover is permitted.
reference.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12 with Typer 0.12 and Click 8.1 for CLI scaffolding; scikit-learn 1.4 TfidfVectorizer with custom tokeniser for prefix-anchored clustering; Jinja2 3.1 for Helm and Rego template rendering; psycopg3 for PostgreSQL; google-cloud-secret-manager for credential retrieval. Infrastructure: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (eu-west1, db-g1-small) with pgcrypto extension; GCS bucket for Helm chart archives and Rego bundle tarballs; GKE Autopilot cluster for OPA sidecar deployment; GitLab CI with Workload Identity Federation OIDC. Tooling: ruff and mypy --strict in CI; pytest with 80% coverage gate; OPA test runner for Rego policy regression tests.
reference.functional_feature_map: F1 — ACL ingestion: read monolithic ACL file from GitLab repository via REST API; decrypt and parse YAML/JSON into rule objects; store encrypted snapshot in acl_rule_sets. F2 — Prefix-anchored clustering (ISS-001 fix): build trie over all resource paths; assign each rule to longest non-overlapping prefix; run TF-IDF on prefix-anchored token sets with configurable threshold (default 0.65); write cluster assignments to decomposition_plans. F3 — Helm and Rego generation: render chart per domain from Jinja2 templates including NetworkPolicy (ISS-002 fix), OPA DaemonSet, ConfigMap referencing versioned bundle URL; generate Rego bundle from cluster rule set. F4 — GitLab MR automation: create MR per domain with generated files; assign domain owner as required reviewer; link MR to decomposition_plans record for SOC 2 traceability. F5 — Shadow validation: run acl-decomp validate in 24-hour cron job for 7 days; compare OPA decisions to monolith baseline; block cutover if divergence_count > 0.
reference.system_components: acl-decomp CLI (Python/Typer): five subcommands, each stateless except for PostgreSQL state reads/writes. PrefixAnchoredClusterer: trie-based prefix extraction followed by TF-IDF vectorisation with configurable similarity threshold; produces domain-to-rules mapping. HelmGenerator: Jinja2 rendering of chart skeleton including NetworkPolicy, OPA DaemonSet, and ConfigMap; writes chart archive to GCS. PolicyTestRunner: subprocess wrapper for OPA test runner; captures per-rule pass/fail; writes report to test_coverage_reports. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: stores decomposition_plans, acl_rule_sets (encrypted), migration_batches, test_coverage_reports. GCS bucket (eu-west1): versioned Helm chart archives and Rego bundle tarballs keyed by plan_id/domain. GitLab CI: MR-per-domain pipeline stages gated on platform engineer approval.
reference.workflows: ACL Decomposition Workflow: platform engineer runs acl-decomp analyse --input refs/heads/main:access/acl.yaml --gitlab-project 42; CLI fetches file via GitLab API with short-lived OIDC token, decrypts blob, builds trie over 50k resource paths, anchors each rule to longest non-overlapping prefix, runs TF-IDF clustering, writes 20 decomposition_plans rows with domain/prefix/rule_count; engineer reviews cluster assignment report before proceeding. Helm Generation and MR Workflow: acl-decomp generate --plan-id <uuid> renders Helm chart and Rego bundle for each domain; chart includes NetworkPolicy restricting OPA sidecar to owning namespace; artifacts uploaded to GCS; acl-decomp deploy creates GitLab MR per domain with domain owner as required reviewer and decomposition_plans link in MR description for SOC 2 CC7.1 evidence. Shadow Validation Workflow: after MR merge and OPA sidecar deployment, acl-decomp validate runs daily for 7 days; each run sends test requests to both monolith ACL and OPA sidecar endpoints and records divergence in migration_batches.divergence_count; cutover is permitted only when divergence_count = 0 for 3 consecutive days.
reference.data_model: decomposition_plans (id UUID PK, acl_source_ref TEXT, domain TEXT, resource_prefix TEXT, rule_count INT, similarity_threshold FLOAT, status ENUM planning/generating/deployed/cutover/failed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). acl_rule_sets (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK REFERENCES decomposition_plans, encrypted_blob BYTEA encrypted via pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt with GCP KMS-derived key, rule_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). migration_batches (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, gitlab_mr_iid INT, shadow_start TIMESTAMPTZ, shadow_end TIMESTAMPTZ, divergence_count INT DEFAULT 0, cutover_approved_by TEXT, cutover_at TIMESTAMPTZ). test_coverage_reports (id UUID PK, plan_id UUID FK, total_fixtures INT, passed INT, failed INT, opa_version TEXT, report_jsonb JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: Internal CLI API (Typer subcommands): acl-decomp analyse --input <git-ref:path> --gitlab-project <id> --threshold <float=0.65>. acl-decomp plan --analyse-id <uuid>. acl-decomp generate --plan-id <uuid> --domain <name> (or --all for all domains). acl-decomp deploy --plan-id <uuid> --domain <name>. acl-decomp validate --batch-id <uuid> --compare-endpoint <monolith-url>. All subcommands accept --dry-run flag for no-write preview. GitLab API used internally: GET /api/v4/projects/:id/repository/files/:path, POST /api/v4/projects/:id/merge_requests. No public-facing HTTP API — tool is operator-only.
reference.security_and_compliance: Workload Identity Federation: GKE service account annotated with iam.gke.io/gcp-service-account; CI job presents OIDC token exchanged for short-lived GCP access token; no static SA key files. pgcrypto: acl_rule_sets.encrypted_blob stored with pgp_sym_encrypt using AES-256 key derived from Cloud KMS. NetworkPolicy (ISS-002 fix): every generated Helm chart includes a NetworkPolicy allowing ingress from owning namespace only and egress to bundle registry FQDN port 443 and Cloud SQL proxy port 5432; all other traffic denied. SOC 2 compliance: CC6.1 — Workload Identity enforces least-privilege; CC6.3 — domain owner MR approval before deployment; CC7.1 — decomposition_plans and GitLab MR IID linked in migration_batches for change evidence. Secret rotation: GitLab token and PostgreSQL DSN rotated quarterly via GCP Secret Manager versions.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline (5 stages): lint (ruff check + mypy --strict, blocks on any type error), unit-test (pytest -x --cov=acl_decomp --cov-fail-under=80, covers clustering and Helm generation modules), integration-test (Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar in CI runner, end-to-end analyse/plan/generate with 500-rule fixture), helm-validate (helm template | kubectl apply --dry-run=client against staging kubeconfig), deploy-dry-run (acl-decomp generate against staging ACL snapshot, verifies Jinja2 rendering and NetworkPolicy presence, does not push MR). Production deployment: acl-decomp deploy creates GitLab MR; after domain owner approval and 7-day shadow period with zero divergence, platform engineer runs acl-decomp cutover --batch-id <uuid> which updates domain router to OPA sidecar endpoint. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo deployment/opa-sidecar-<domain> restores previous image; monolith ACL service remains active as fallback during shadow period. Environments: dev (minikube, local PostgreSQL), staging (GKE staging namespace, Cloud SQL staging eu-west1), production (GKE prod namespace, Cloud SQL prod eu-west1 with automated daily backups to GCS).
reference.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring: structured JSON logs from every Typer subcommand (fields: plan_id, domain, subcommand, actor, duration_ms, outcome). Custom metrics: clustering_duration_seconds (histogram per domain), helm_charts_generated_total (counter), shadow_divergence_count (gauge per batch), opa_policy_eval_latency_ms (histogram from OPA sidecar prometheus endpoint). Alerting: Cloud Monitoring alert if shadow_divergence_count increases after previously reaching zero (potential OPA bundle corruption). GitLab CI pipeline duration tracked; alert if analyse stage exceeds 10 minutes for 50k-rule input.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Cloud SQL db-g1-small (PostgreSQL 16, eu-west1): approximately $30/month during active decomposition cycles; instance paused when no pipeline is running to reduce cost. GCS bucket storage: Helm chart archives and Rego bundle tarballs for 20 domains estimated under 500 MB — negligible cost. GKE Autopilot OPA sidecars: 20 DaemonSets at 0.1 CPU / 128 MB each — approximately $40/month per cluster. Total tool infrastructure cost under $80/month. Scaling: TF-IDF clustering is the compute-intensive step; parallelising analyse across domain subsets via concurrent GitLab CI jobs reduces pipeline duration linearly with job count.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — MVP (Weeks 1-3): CLI scaffold with Typer, PostgreSQL schema and Alembic migrations, ACL file ingestion from GitLab API, PrefixAnchoredClusterer with trie-based prefix extraction (ISS-001 fix), basic Helm skeleton with NetworkPolicy (ISS-002 fix), unit tests for clustering and Helm generation at 80% coverage. Phase 2 — Pilot (Weeks 4-6): full Jinja2 Rego bundle generation, OPA test runner integration, GitLab MR automation for 5 pilot domains, shadow validation for pilot domains, CI pipeline with all 5 stages. Phase 3 — Full Rollout (Weeks 7-12): generate and deploy remaining 15 domains, cutover orchestration for approved domains, SOC 2 audit trail report generation, documentation and runbook for platform team handoff.
reference.development_guidelines: Typer subcommands must be idempotent: re-running generate for the same plan_id overwrites previous artifacts without duplicating PostgreSQL records. PrefixAnchoredClusterer threshold is configurable at runtime; document the algorithm with a billing/billing-admin worked example in the module docstring. All Jinja2 templates must include a NetworkPolicy block — CI helm-validate stage enforces this via grep check on rendered output. mypy --strict enforced in CI; no Any types in clustering or Helm generation modules. pytest fixtures use real PostgreSQL via testcontainers to avoid mock drift.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Cross-domain rules (ACL rules spanning multiple resource prefixes) cannot be automatically assigned by the trie extractor and require manual domain owner classification — mitigated by acl-decomp analyse flagging ambiguous rules and blocking plan phase until resolved. OPA sidecar cold start latency on GKE Autopilot node provisioning may cause brief policy evaluation gaps during initial deployment — mitigated by pre-warming sidecar pods 24 hours before cutover window. Tradeoff: trie-based prefix anchoring adds 15-20 seconds to the analyse phase for 50k rules versus raw TF-IDF, but eliminates the domain contamination risk that makes raw TF-IDF unsafe for this use case.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Q1 (OPA sidecar egress scope): NetworkPolicy template allows egress to OPA bundle registry FQDN and Cloud SQL proxy port 5432 only; all other egress denied. Q2 (similarity threshold): default 0.65 chosen based on integration tests with billing/billing-admin fixture; configurable via --threshold flag to accommodate domain-specific ACL structures. Q3 (cutover approval authority): cutover approved by domain owner MR approval plus platform engineering lead sign-off in migration_batches.cutover_approved_by field.

raw:
{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a simple Go CLI tool for warehouse supervisors. Key architectural decisions: Bubbletea Elm-architecture with a goroutine-based SSE listener feeding a channel into the Bubbletea Update loop, so the reconnect logic is independent of the TUI render loop and does not freeze the terminal. The SSE listener owns reconnect logic with exponential backoff capped at 30 seconds. Webhook dispatch is a separate goroutine to avoid blocking the SSE consumer. API key stored in config file created with 0600 permissions on first run.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "Warehouse Floor Monitoring CLI Tool",
 "executive_summary": "A Go 1.22 terminal TUI application for warehouse supervisors to monitor live pick queue depths, worker productivity rates, and conveyor throughput metrics

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a simple Go CLI tool for warehouse supervisors. Key architectural decisions: Bubbletea Elm-architecture with a goroutine-based SSE listener feeding a channel into the Bubbletea Update loop, so the reconnect logic is independent of the TUI render loop and does not freeze the terminal. The SSE listener owns reconnect logic with exponential backoff capped at 30 seconds. Webhook dispatch is a separate goroutine to avoid blocking the SSE consumer. API key stored in config file created with 0600 permissions on first run.
parsed.title: Warehouse Floor Monitoring CLI Tool
parsed.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 terminal TUI application for warehouse supervisors to monitor live pick queue depths, worker productivity rates, and conveyor throughput metrics from a WMS via Server-Sent Events. The tool runs as a single compiled binary on Linux amd64 shift-office workstations, consuming the WMS SSE stream and rendering a color-coded dashboard with configurable alert thresholds and webhook escalation on breach. No database, no persistent state, no web server.
parsed.architecture_overview: Single Go binary with two concurrent goroutines: the Bubbletea TUI program running on the main goroutine, and an SSE listener goroutine that subscribes to the WMS /api/v1/metrics/stream endpoint, parses JSON metric events, and sends MetricUpdateMsg values through a buffered channel to the Bubbletea Update function. The Bubbletea Model holds all in-memory metric state (pick_queues, pack_stations, conveyor_segments, alert_state) and drives the terminal render loop. A separate webhook goroutine dispatches POST requests to the configured alert endpoint on threshold breach. Cobra CLI parses command flags (--config, --zone, --threshold, --webhook-url, --refresh, --debug) and initializes the Bubbletea App with the parsed config. The binary has zero shared library dependencies and runs on Linux amd64 bare-metal workstations.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. TUI: Bubbletea 0.23 with Lip Gloss 0.3 for color-coded terminal styling. CLI flags: Cobra 1.8. HTTP/SSE: net/http with built-in SSE client. Webhook: net/http for POST dispatch. Config: encoding/yaml for config file parsing. Build: goreleaser for Linux amd64 binary distribution. CI: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint and goreleaser. Distribution: Ansible playbook for workstation deployment.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Live Dashboard: renders current pick queue depth per zone, pack station throughput per station, conveyor throughput per segment, all color-coded by threshold status. Alert Thresholds: configurable per metric via --threshold flag (e.g., pick_queue_depth:zone1:10, pack_station_rate:station2:80); when metric exceeds threshold, dashboard row turns red and webhook is dispatched. Webhook Escalation: POSTs to configured URL with JSON payload {zone, metric, value, threshold, timestamp} on threshold breach. Reconnect Indicator: flashing 'WMS DISCONNECTED - reconnecting...' status row during SSE reconnect period. Config File: stores WMS API key, zone, threshold rules, webhook URL, refresh interval, and debug flag in /etc/warehouse-monitor/config.yaml with 0600 permissions. Debug Logging: --debug flag enables structured JSON log lines to stderr for SSE reconnect and webhook dispatch debugging.
parsed.system_components: warehouse-monitor binary (single Go binary, Linux amd64): Cobra CLI flag parser, Bubbletea App with Model and Update function, SSE listener goroutine with reconnect logic, webhook dispatch goroutine, debug logging. /etc/warehouse-monitor/config.yaml: config file created by tool on first run with 0600 permissions.
