parsed.executive_summary: A 12-week research prototype decomposing a monolithic construction project management platform into three bounded-context services (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, cost-mgmt-svc) communicating over Kafka 3.6 on GKE Autopilot. This version specifies orchestrator-based saga with a dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc and defines Kafka topic names, partition counts, and Avro schema format. The prototype measures inter-service event latency, saga transaction duration, and eventual-consistency resolution lag under synthetic load, producing a publishable architecture decision record for the enterprise architecture committee.
parsed.architecture_overview: Orchestrator-based saga pattern: a dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc receives publish_document commands, emits document_revised events to Kafka document_revised topic, and awaits ack events from both scheduling-svc and cost-mgmt-svc before marking the saga_transaction as completed. On downstream failure or 30-second timeout, the orchestrator emits compensating revert_document_revision events to all consumers and marks the saga_transaction as rolled_back. Three services share a single PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL instance with four schemas (document_schema, scheduling_schema, cost_schema, saga_schema) isolated by PostgreSQL roles. Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud manages topics: document_revised (3 partitions, key=doc_id, Avro schema via Schema Registry), scheduling_ack, cost_ack, document_revised_dlq. Kubernetes network policies enforce service boundaries: document-control-svc can only write to document_schema; scheduling-svc can only write to scheduling_schema.
parsed.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, saga-orchestrator-svc), Python 3.12 (cost-mgmt-svc). Event bus: Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud (basic tier). Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (single instance, four schemas). Schema registry: Confluent Schema Registry. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana on GKE. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Load testing: k6. Hosting: GKE Autopilot. Storage: GCS for benchmark results.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Document Control Service: manages document_revisions with content_hash tracking, emits document_revised events on revision publish. Scheduling Service: subscribes to document_revised, updates task_dependencies referencing affected documents, emits scheduling_ack. Cost Management Service: subscribes to document_revised, updates cost_code_mappings referencing affected documents, emits cost_ack. Saga Orchestrator: receives publish_document commands, coordinates downstream services, tracks saga_transactions, emits compensating events on failure. Consistency Audit: Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboard showing resolution_lag_ms distribution per service. Load Testing: k6 script simulates synthetic document_revised events at configurable rate, measures p99 latency and dlq depth.
parsed.system_components: GKE Autopilot: four Deployments (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, cost-mgmt-svc, saga-orchestrator-svc) in research namespace. Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud: document_revised topic (3 partitions, key=doc_id, Avro schema via Schema Registry), scheduling_ack topic, cost_ack topic, document_revised_dlq topic. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL: four schemas (document_schema, scheduling_schema, cost_schema, saga_schema) isolated by PostgreSQL roles. Prometheus + Grafana: ServiceMonitors scrape /metrics from each service. GitLab CI: benchmark pipeline runs k6 load test weekly. GCS: benchmark result storage.
parsed.workflows: Document Revision Saga: Engineer calls POST /saga/orchestrator/publish_document with project_id and doc_id. Saga orchestrator inserts saga_transaction row with status=pending, emits document_revised event to Kafka document_revised topic. document-control-svc consumes event, inserts document_revisions record, emits document_revised_ack. saga-orchestrator-svc awaits scheduling_ack and cost_ack within 30 seconds; on receipt, marks saga_transaction status=completed. On timeout or nack, emits revert_document_revision events to all consumers, marks saga_transaction status=rolled_back, inserts compensating document_revisions record with revision_number decremented. Consistency Audit: Grafana dashboard shows p99 resolution_lag_ms per service; alert fires if p99 exceeds 2 seconds. Load Test: k6 script injects synthetic document_revised events at configurable rate; Prometheus captures document_event_publish_rate, saga_transaction_duration_seconds, consistency_resolution_lag_seconds, dlq_depth; results stored in GCS.
parsed.data_model: document_schema.document_revisions (id UUID PK, doc_id UUID, revision_number INT, content_hash TEXT, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, event_id UUID). scheduling_schema.schedule_baselines (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, task_dependencies JSONB, last_sync_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cost_schema.cost_commitments (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, cost_code TEXT, amount NUMERIC, linked_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). saga_schema.saga_transactions (id UUID PK, command_type TEXT, status ENUM pending/completed/rolled_back, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, participant_acks JSONB). consistency_audit_logs (id UUID PK, saga_id UUID FK, service_name TEXT, event_type TEXT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_lag_ms INT). Kafka topics: document_revised (key=doc_id, Avro schema via Confluent Schema Registry), scheduling_ack, cost_ack, document_revised_dlq.
parsed.api_design: Each service exposes a minimal REST API for research tooling: GET /health (liveness probe), GET /metrics (Prometheus scrape endpoint), POST /simulate (body: {event_type, project_id, doc_id, payload}, injects synthetic Kafka event for load testing). Saga orchestrator: POST /saga/orchestrator/publish_document (body: {project_id, doc_id, revision_number, content_hash}, returns {saga_id, status}). GET /saga/orchestrator/saga/{id} (returns saga_transaction status). GET /saga/orchestrator/health.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Service-to-service JWT via shared signing key (acceptable for research prototype; production would use mTLS or service mesh). Kubernetes network policies: document-control-svc can only write to document_schema PostgreSQL role; scheduling-svc can only write to scheduling_schema role; cost-mgmt-svc can only write to cost_schema role. No external network exposure; all services are internal GKE cluster only. GKE Autopilot node security: no public IP; all ingress via internal load balancer.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot: four Deployments in research namespace. GitLab CI pipeline: go test, pytest, Docker build, push to GCR, kubectl apply. Pre-warm job: kubectl scale deployment document-control-svc --replicas=1 before each benchmark run to prevent node provisioning latency. Benchmark pipeline: GitLab scheduled job runs k6 load test script weekly against both microservices cluster and monolith baseline, stores results in GCS, generates Markdown comparison report. Environments: research only; no production promotion path.
parsed.observability: Prometheus ServiceMonitors scrape /metrics from each service. Custom metrics: document_event_publish_rate (events/second), saga_transaction_duration_seconds (histogram), consistency_resolution_lag_seconds (histogram per service), dlq_depth (document_revised_dlq topic). Grafana dashboards: Saga Transaction Timeline (saga_id vs status over time), Consistency Resolution Latency Distribution (p50/p99 per service), Service-to-Service Event Flow (event rate per topic). Alert: p99 consistency_resolution_lag_seconds exceeds 2 seconds for any service.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated USD 380/month: Confluent Cloud Kafka basic tier USD 60/month, GKE Autopilot for 4 services approximately USD 120/month, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL single instance USD 100/month, GCS USD 10/month, GitLab CI USD 5/month. Within USD 500/month budget with buffer for load testing burst costs.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-4): Kafka setup on Confluent Cloud, PostgreSQL schema isolation, document-control-svc and scheduling-svc Go services, saga-orchestrator-svc with orchestrator-based saga, basic document_revised event flow. Phase 2 (Weeks 5-8): cost-mgmt-svc Python service, Avro schema via Schema Registry, compensating event flows for all failure cases, consistency_audit_logs Prometheus metrics. Phase 3 (Weeks 9-12): k6 load testing script, benchmark comparison against monolith baseline, Grafana dashboards, research findings report for architecture committee.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go services: go test 80% coverage, table-driven tests for saga compensation event flows. Python services: pytest 80% coverage, unit tests for cost_code_mapping update logic. Kafka consumers: idempotency keys (doc_id) on all consumer records. Consistency audit metrics must include resolution_lag_ms measured as downstream ack_at minus event publish_at. All benchmark results stored in GCS with timestamp and service configuration for reproducibility.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: orchestrator-based saga introduces a single point of failure; mitigated by monitoring saga_transaction status and alerting on pending transactions. Risk: Avro schema registry adds operational overhead; mitigated by using JSON with shared struct validation for prototype simplicity. Tradeoff: single PostgreSQL instance shared across services simplifies prototype but is not production-ready; findings must note that separate databases are required for production. Tradeoff: orchestrator-based saga over choreography reduces operational complexity but requires a dedicated service; acceptable for research prototype.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Chose orchestrator-based saga over choreography for simpler compensation event management and better auditability. Chose Avro with Confluent Schema Registry over JSON for future schema evolution support. Confirmed single PostgreSQL instance shared across services for prototype simplicity. Selected k6 for load testing over Locust for better concurrency control at low cost.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a weak revision round for a microservices research prototype. ISS-001 is addressed: orchestrator-based saga with saga-orchestrator-svc is chosen, with compensating revert_document_revision events on downstream failure. ISS-002 is addressed: Kafka topics specified with partition counts and Avro schema format. However, plan quality is intentionally weak: the analytics_and_benchmarking section is thin (does not detail the k6 load test scenarios or the specific monolith baseline comparison methodology), and the phased_implementation section is at minimum word count.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified orchestrator-based saga pattern with dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc tracking saga_transactions; defined compensation event revert_document_revision emitted on downstream service timeout or nack.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-service transaction pattern is now implementable; saga orchestrator tracks state in saga_transactions table with rollback path defined

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Kafka topics: document_revised (3 partitions, key=doc_id), scheduling_ack (1 partition), cost_ack (1 partition), document_revised_dlq (1 partition); Avro schema with Schema Registry chosen; consistency_audit_logs.resolution_lag_ms defined as downstream ack timestamp minus event publish timestamp.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Kafka topic design now implementable; resolution lag measurement methodology defined for research outputs

reference.title: Construction Platform Microservices Decomposition Research Architecture v2
reference.executive_summary: A research prototype on GKE Autopilot decomposing a monolithic construction project management platform into three services (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, cost-mgmt-svc) communicating over a Kafka 3.6 event bus. An orchestrator-based saga pattern manages cross-domain transactions with compensating events on failure. Per-service PostgreSQL schemas enforce data isolation boundaries. Prometheus metrics capture inter-service event latency, saga transaction duration, and eventual-consistency resolution lag for comparison against a monolith baseline under equivalent synthetic k6 load, producing an architecture decision record for the enterprise committee.
reference.architecture_overview: Three bounded-context services deployed on GKE Autopilot in a research namespace: document-control-svc (Go 1.22) owns document_revisions in document_schema; scheduling-svc (Go 1.22) owns schedule_baselines in scheduling_schema; cost-mgmt-svc (Python 3.12) owns cost_commitments in cost_schema. A dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc (Go 1.22) receives publish_document commands, coordinates the cross-service transaction by emitting document_revised to Kafka (key=doc_id, 3 partitions), awaiting scheduling_ack and cost_ack events within a 30-second window, and marking the saga complete or emitting revert_document_revision on timeout or nack. Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud with Avro schemas managed in Schema Registry. consistency_audit_logs in a shared analytics schema records the resolution_lag_ms from event publish to downstream ack for each service, providing the primary research metric. Prometheus scrapes custom metrics from each service; Grafana renders saga timeline and consistency latency distribution dashboards. A Python 3.12 analytics script aggregates audit logs into weekly benchmark comparison reports against the monolith baseline.
reference.technology_stack: Services: Go 1.22 (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, saga-orchestrator-svc), Python 3.12 (cost-mgmt-svc, analytics scripts). Event bus: Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud with Avro schema serialisation and Schema Registry. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (four isolated schemas via separate PostgreSQL roles). Orchestration: GKE Autopilot with GitLab CI. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana on GKE, custom metrics per service. Load testing: k6 with synthetic construction project event scripts.
reference.functional_feature_map: Document Revision Publication: saga-orchestrator-svc receives publish_document command, document-control-svc writes document_revision and emits document_revised Avro event. Downstream Consistency Updates: scheduling-svc and cost-mgmt-svc consume document_revised and update task_dependencies and cost_code_mappings respectively, emitting ack events. Saga Rollback: orchestrator emits revert_document_revision if any downstream service times out or returns a nack event; services revert their local changes. Consistency Measurement: consistency_audit_logs records event publish_at and downstream service ack_at timestamps; resolution_lag_ms computed per saga step. Research Benchmarks: k6 load test runs against microservices cluster and monolith baseline.
reference.system_components: document-control-svc (Go, Kafka producer, document_schema owner), scheduling-svc (Go, Kafka consumer for document_revised, scheduling_schema owner), cost-mgmt-svc (Python, Kafka consumer for document_revised, cost_schema owner), saga-orchestrator-svc (Go, Kafka producer of document_revised and revert_document_revision, Kafka consumer of scheduling_ack and cost_ack, saga_schema owner), Kafka 3.6 Confluent Cloud (document_revised 3-partition topic, scheduling_ack, cost_ack, document_revised_dlq), Schema Registry (Avro schemas), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL (four schemas), Prometheus + Grafana, k6 load tester.
reference.workflows: Cross-Service Document Publish: Research engineer calls POST /simulate on saga-orchestrator-svc with a publish_document payload; orchestrator writes saga_transactions record with status pending, calls document-control-svc to create a document_revision, then produces an Avro document_revised event to Kafka with doc_id as partition key. scheduling-svc consumer group processes the event, updates schedule_baselines.task_dependencies to reference the new revision, writes a consistency_audit_log entry (service=scheduling, resolution_lag_ms=event.ack_at - event.publish_at), and emits scheduling_ack to Kafka. cost-mgmt-svc Python consumer similarly updates cost_commitments and emits cost_ack. Orchestrator receives both acks, marks saga_transaction completed. Saga Rollback: if cost-mgmt-svc consumer raises an exception (simulated via a chaos flag in /simulate payload), orchestrator receives no cost_ack within 30 seconds; it emits revert_document_revision event; document-control-svc and scheduling-svc consumers handle the revert by deleting the provisional changes; orchestrator marks saga_transaction rolled_back.
reference.data_model: document_schema.document_revisions (id UUID PK, doc_id UUID, revision_number INT, content_hash TEXT, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, saga_id UUID). scheduling_schema.schedule_baselines (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, task_dependencies JSONB, last_sync_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cost_schema.cost_commitments (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, cost_code TEXT, amount NUMERIC, linked_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). saga_schema.saga_transactions (id UUID PK, command_type TEXT, status ENUM pending/completed/rolled_back, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, participant_acks JSONB). analytics_schema.consistency_audit_logs (id UUID PK, saga_id UUID FK, service_name TEXT, event_type TEXT, event_publish_at TIMESTAMPTZ, service_ack_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_lag_ms INT). Kafka topics: document_revised (Avro: {doc_id: string, revision_id: string, content_hash: string, publish_at: long}), scheduling_ack and cost_ack (Avro: {saga_id: string, service: string, status: enum, ack_at: long}), document_revised_dlq.
reference.api_design: Minimal REST on each service for research tooling: GET /health (liveness check); GET /metrics (Prometheus exposition format); POST /simulate on saga-orchestrator-svc accepts {doc_id: string, inject_failure: bool} to trigger a publish_document saga with optional downstream failure injection for rollback testing. Python analytics script exposes no HTTP API; it queries consistency_audit_logs directly via SQL and outputs Markdown benchmark tables.
reference.security_and_compliance: Service-to-service JWT using a shared HS256 signing key distributed via Kubernetes Secret; each service validates the JWT on incoming REST calls. Kafka consumers use Confluent Cloud API keys stored in Kubernetes Secrets. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricts: document-control-svc can reach PostgreSQL document_schema port only; scheduling-svc to scheduling_schema; cost-mgmt-svc to cost_schema; saga-orchestrator-svc to saga_schema. No external ingress; research engineers access services via kubectl port-forward. Shared PostgreSQL cluster is a prototype simplification; finding will note that production requires separate databases per service to achieve true data isolation.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot with four Deployments and one ConfigMap per service. GitLab CI: go test, golangci-lint, pytest, Docker build, push to GCR, kubectl apply --dry-run in staging then apply in research namespace. Benchmark pipeline: GitLab scheduled job runs k6 against microservices and monolith endpoints weekly, stores JSON results in GCS, triggers Python analytics script to produce Markdown comparison report committed to the research repo.
reference.observability: Prometheus ServiceMonitors scrape /metrics from each service. Custom Prometheus histograms: document_event_publish_latency_seconds, saga_transaction_duration_seconds, consistency_resolution_lag_seconds labelled by service_name. Grafana dashboard panels: Saga Transaction Timeline (Gantt-style per saga step), Consistency Resolution Latency Distribution (histogram by service), DLQ Depth over time. Research output: weekly k6 benchmark comparison showing microservices p50/p99 latency vs monolith baseline under 100 concurrent synthetic document publish events.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated USD 310/month: Confluent Cloud Kafka basic USD 60/month, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db.f1-micro USD 80/month, GKE Autopilot 4 lightweight services USD 120/month, GCS for benchmark results USD 5/month, Grafana Cloud free tier. Under USD 500/month budget. Load test burst costs covered by budget buffer. Prototype is not designed for scale; GKE Autopilot handles the 4 services without manual node management.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): Scaffold three services, PostgreSQL schema setup, Kafka topics and Avro schemas, document_revised event flow end-to-end, consistency_audit_logs recording. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Saga orchestrator with rollback path, chaos failure injection via /simulate, k6 load test scripts and monolith baseline comparison runs, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Analytics script producing comparative benchmark report, architecture decision record draft, operational complexity findings documented.
reference.development_guidelines: Go services use golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck. Python cost-mgmt-svc uses ruff and mypy. All Kafka consumer groups use explicit consumer group IDs to prevent partition assignment conflicts. Each service owns its schema migration files; no cross-schema migrations allowed. Research findings documented in /docs/findings at end of each sprint. Avro schema changes versioned in Schema Registry with backward-compatibility enforcement.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Confluent Cloud Kafka on basic tier has limited partition throughput; insufficient for production-scale document events but adequate for synthetic research load of 100 concurrent events. Risk: shared PostgreSQL cluster undermines true data isolation; findings must note this limitation explicitly. Tradeoff: orchestrator-based saga over choreography chosen because orchestrator state in saga_transactions table makes rollback paths observable and testable, which is more valuable for research insights than choreography's lower coupling. Risk: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency inflates p99 latency measurements during cold-start periods; pre-warm jobs added to benchmark pipeline.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Orchestrator-based saga chosen over choreography for research observability: saga_transactions table makes every transaction step queryable. Avro with Schema Registry chosen over JSON for schema evolution research as an additional data point. Synthetic data seeded from anonymised production project snapshots; real project data not used. PostgreSQL shared cluster confirmed acceptable for prototype; production recommendation will specify separate databases per service.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve the emergency access timer persistence gap. The fix is to store each emergency_access_request as a row in audit.db with requested_at timestamp, then on app start query all pending requests and enforce the approval_delay_hours window by comparing (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours. This is a timestamp-based persistence pattern that survives app restarts without a dedicated timer process. ECDH for team vault key sharing is also a straightforward fix: each member generates a public/private key pair, the vault owner encrypts the team vault key with each member's public key using X25519, and the encrypted key is stored in access_grants. The screen capture protection flag is a Tauri window flag set on sensitive entry views.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced in-memory emergency access timer with timestamp-based persistence in audit.db. Each emergency_access_request row stores requested_at DATETIME. On app start, vault_service queries all pending requests WHERE approved_at IS NULL AND requested_at < NOW() - INTERVAL approval_delay_hours AND approved_at IS NULL. If any pending request exceeds the window, the app displays a warning to the vault owner and logs a security event to audit.db. The approval window is enforced without a dedicated timer process.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Emergency access approval window is enforced across app restarts using audit.db timestamp persistence; no dedicated timer process required.

parsed.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager - Round 3
parsed.executive_summary: A Tauri+Svelte desktop application with a Rust Axum IPC backend that stores enterprise credentials in a SQLCipher vault protected by a TPM/Secure Enclave-derived master key with argon2id PIN fallback. Round 3 resolves the emergency access timer persistence gap by storing requests in audit.db with timestamp-based enforcement. Team vault sharing uses ECDH key agreement to encrypt the shared vault key per member. All audit log entries are in a separate SQLCipher database with a hash chain to prevent tampering.
parsed.architecture_overview: Tauri Svelte frontend communicates with a Rust Axum IPC server over a local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows). The IPC server owns a SQLCipher vault.db connection and an in-memory DecryptedVault state. On unlock, the Rust vault_service derives the master key from the TPM/Secure Enclave (or argon2id PIN fallback) and unwraps the vault key. All vault operations (create_entry, read_entry, delete_entry) verify the vault MAC before any SQLCipher write. The audit_log_entries table in a separate audit.db stores every vault operation with a cryptographic hash chain. Emergency access requests are persisted as rows in audit.db with requested_at timestamps; on app start, pending requests are evaluated against the approval_delay_hours window. Team vault sharing uses ECDH (X25519) to encrypt the shared vault key per member; the encrypted key is stored in access_grants. The app is packaged as a signed installer for macOS (Apple notarised), Windows (Authenticode), and Linux (GPG-signed).
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.x, Svelte 5, TypeScript, Svelte stores, WASM TOTP implementation. Backend: Rust 1.78, Axum 0.7, ring crate (AES-256-GCM, SHA-256), argon2 crate, sqlx with SQLCipher extension, serde_json for IPC serialisation. Database: SQLite with SQLCipher (AES-256-CBC page encryption). Audit: separate SQLite with SQLCipher and hash chain. Signing: Apple notarisation (macOS), Windows Authenticode (Azure Code Signing), Linux GPG. Build: GitHub Actions matrix with cross-platform targets. Crash reporting: Sentry with data scrubbing.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Vault Unlock: biometric prompt via Tauri plugin (macOS Secure Enclave, Windows TPM 2.0, Linux TSS2), PIN fallback with argon2id stretching, master key derivation. Personal Vault: password entries (title, username, URL, notes), OTP seed entries (issuer, digits, period), SSH key entries (public key fingerprint, key type). Team Vault: share with team members, assign owner/editor/viewer roles, revoke access. Emergency Access: request access for a time window (24h default), approve/deny via separate app instance. Audit Log: view all vault operations with timestamp and actor identity. Settings: vault file export (encrypted portable file), backup frequency, approval delay hours.
parsed.system_components: Tauri Svelte frontend (VaultUnlock, VaultEntryList, EntryDetail, TeamVaultPanel, EmergencyAccessPanel, AuditLogViewer, Settings). Rust Axum IPC server (VaultCommand, TeamVaultCommand, EmergencyAccessCommand, AuditCommand). vault_service module: SQLCipher vault.db connection, DecryptedVault in-memory state, MAC verification on every open. audit_service module: SQLCipher audit.db connection, hash chain verification on open. ECDH key agreement module: X25519 public/private key pair per team member, encrypts shared vault key. Sentry crash reporter (data-scrubbed). GitHub Actions build matrix: macOS universal binary, Windows x86_64, Linux x86_64+arm64.
parsed.workflows: Vault Unlock: Tauri biometric dialog opens via Tauri plugin; on success, Rust vault_service calls TPM Seal/Unseal (macOS Secure Enclave via CryptoKit, Windows TPM 2.0 via NCrypt) to derive master key; unwraps vault key from SQLCipher header; verifies vault MAC from vault_metadata; opens SQLCipher vault.db; populates DecryptedVault in-memory state; emits vault:unlocked event to Tauri. Entry CRUD: Tauri invoke(vault:read_entry, id) calls Rust VaultCommand; vault_service reads ciphertext from vault.db; decrypts with AES-256-GCM using entry nonce; ZeroizingVec zeroes plaintext after use; returns DecryptedEntry; Tauri stores only metadata in Svelte state. Emergency Access: vault owner calls EmergencyAccessCommand:request_access with grantee_identity and approval_delay_hours; Rust inserts emergency_access_request row into audit.db with requested_at; on app start, vault_service queries all pending requests WHERE approved_at IS NULL AND requested_at < NOW() - INTERVAL approval_delay_hours AND approved_at IS NULL; if any pending request exceeds window, app displays warning to vault owner and logs security event to audit.db; grantee receives notification via Tauri push notification. Team Vault Sync: vault owner calls TeamVaultCommand:share; Rust generates ECDH public/private key pair; encrypts shared vault key with each member's X25519 public key; stores encrypted key in access_grants; member receives encrypted key via P2P encrypted blob exchange; member decrypts with their private key to obtain shared vault key.
parsed.data_model: vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, notes_encrypted BLOB, ciphertext BLOB, nonce BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint VARCHAR). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_id VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, encrypted_vault_key BLOB, encrypted_key_nonce BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). emergency_access_requests (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, requested_at DATETIME, approved_at DATETIME, approved_by VARCHAR). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM unlock/lock/read_entry/write_entry/delete_entry/share_vault/emergency_access_request/emergency_access_approved/emergency_access_denied, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash VARCHAR, row_hash VARCHAR). audit.db: same schema as vault.db with independent SQLCipher key derived from master_key XOR audit_key_salt.
parsed.api_design: Tauri IPC commands: vault:unlock (returns bool success, bool biometric_used). vault:lock (returns void). vault:create_entry (entry_type, title, username, url, notes) -> EntryId. vault:read_entry (id) -> DecryptedEntry. vault:delete_entry (id) -> void. vault:share_vault (name, members) -> TeamVaultId. vault:revoke_access (vault_id, grantee) -> void. emergency_access:request_access (grantee_identity, approval_delay_hours) -> RequestId. emergency_access:approve_access (request_id) -> void. emergency_access:deny_access (request_id) -> void. audit:query_log (from, to) -> Vec<AuditLogEntry>. audit:verify_chain (returns bool). All commands return serde_json serialised Rust structs.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Master key: TPM Seal/Unseal on macOS (Secure Enclave via CryptoKit) and Windows (TPM 2.0 via NCrypt); fallback: argon2id(PIN, salt=device_id_hash, m=65536, t=3, p=4) producing 32-byte key. Vault key: AES-256-GCM wrapped with master key; wrapped key stored in vault_metadata header. Vault MAC: HMAC-SHA-256 over all entry_ids and their updated_at timestamps; stored in vault_metadata; verified on every vault open. Audit log: separate audit.db with independent SQLCipher key derived from master_key XOR audit_key_salt; each audit_log_entries row includes prev_hash and row_hash; chain verified on audit DB open. Emergency access: emergency_access_requests stored in audit.db with requested_at; on app start, pending requests evaluated against approval_delay_hours window; grantee notified via Tauri push notification; vault owner approves/denies via separate app instance. Team vault: ECDH X25519 public/private key pair per member; shared vault key encrypted with each member's public key; never stored in plaintext on sync server. NIST SP 800-132: PBKDF2 fallback uses argon2id with m=65536, t=3, p=4; vault MAC uses HMAC-SHA-256; vault file is portable and USB-transportable.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: macOS universal binary (lipo x86_64+aarch64) builds on macOS runner, Windows x86_64 MSVC builds on Windows runner, Linux x86_64+arm64 AppImage and.deb builds on Linux runner. Code-signing: Apple notarisation (macOS only on main branch tag) via notarytool, Windows Authenticode via Azure Code Signing, Linux GPG via GitHub Actions GPG action. Release artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update: Tauri updater checks GitHub Releases on app launch; downloads signed installer; verifies signature before install. Vault file export: Tauri command exports vault.db as encrypted portable file (AES-256-GCM with master key wrapped in hardware); export file includes metadata header with wrapped key and nonce.
parsed.observability: Local structured audit log in audit.db: every vault operation with actor identity, timestamp, and hash chain. Sentry crash reports: Tauri error events with data scrubbed (no vault entry IDs, no plaintext fields). Sentry custom events: vault_unlock_failed (reason), emergency_access_window_violation (grantee, window_exceeded), vault_mac_mismatch. Tauri window event listeners: vault:unlocked (emitted to Sentry), vault:locked (emitted to Sentry), vault:mac_mismatch (emitted to Sentry). Audit log query: Tauri command audit:query_log returns paginated audit_log_entries for IT security review.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost for local vault operation. GitHub Actions free tier covers cross-platform matrix builds. Apple notarisation adds ~10min to macOS release pipeline. Sentry free tier covers crash reporting for internal team. At scale (enterprise-wide deployment), consider Sentry paid plan for custom event filtering and alerting. Vault file size: ~50MB for 10,000 entries at ~5KB per entry (SQLCipher overhead). Team vault sync: P2P encrypted blob exchange; no central server bottleneck.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 months): Personal vault with TPM unlock, password and OTP seed storage, SQLCipher AES-256-CBC encryption, local audit log with hash chain, auto-lock on idle, biometric and PIN fallback, GitHub Actions cross-platform build and signing. Phase 2 (3 months): Team vault sharing with ECDH key agreement, emergency access with timestamp-based persistence in audit.db, SSH key storage, browser extension autofill (Tauri browser extension), Tauri screen capture protection on sensitive views. Phase 3 (3 months): FIDO2 hardware key as additional unlock factor, SOC 2 audit evidence package, Linux AppImage and.deb distribution, Windows auto-update via Microsoft Store.
parsed.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy with deny(warnings) in CI. All SQLCipher queries via sqlx with parameterised queries; no string interpolation in queries. Vault MAC verification must be in a separate module (vault_service::verify_mac) with unit tests. ECDH key pair generation must use secure random via ring::rand::SystemRandom. Svelte: no vault plaintext in Svelte state; all plaintext fetched via IPC and zeroed. Sentry events must not include vault entry IDs or plaintext fields. Vault file export command must verify MAC before returning file path. Each new feature requires IT security team review before merge.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TPM/Secure Enclave unavailable on non-TPM-capable devices (e.g. virtual machines, some Linux distributions). Mitigation: argon2id PIN fallback with enforced complexity; IT security team must evaluate device TPM capability before deployment. Risk: ECDH key agreement requires each team member to have a public key stored in the vault; if a member loses their key, they cannot access the shared vault. Mitigation: each member's public key is stored in access_grants; if a member loses their key, the owner can regenerate the public key pair and re-share the vault. Tradeoff: timestamp-based emergency access persistence in audit.db over a dedicated timer process reduces complexity but requires audit.db to be available on app start; if audit.db is corrupted, the timer is lost.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Emergency access timer persistence resolved: timestamp-based in audit.db with requested_at. ECDH key agreement confirmed for team vault key sharing. Screen capture protection confirmed via Tauri window flag on sensitive entry views. Browser extension autofill deferred to phase 2. FIDO2 hardware key deferred to phase 3.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by replacing the in-memory emergency access timer with a persistent timestamp-based approach: emergency_access_requests stored as rows in audit.db with requested_at and approval_delay_hours. On every app start, the vault_service queries pending emergency_access_requests and evaluates (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours; if elapsed, the access is auto-approved or expired depending on owner response. This design is restart-safe because the state lives in audit.db, not in memory. Also specifying X25519 ECDH for team vault key handoff to close the key sharing gap from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced in-memory emergency access timer with persistent emergency_access_requests table in audit.db storing requested_at DATETIME and approval_delay_hours INT. On app start, vault_service queries SELECT * FROM emergency_access_requests WHERE status = 'pending' and evaluates (julianday('now') - julianday(requested_at)) * 24 >= approval_delay_hours. Expired pending requests auto-resolve. Notifications sent to vault owner via OS notification on app start if action required.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Emergency access approval window is enforced correctly even after multiple app restarts between request submission and expiry, preserving the security guarantee of the time-delay mechanism.

reference.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager
reference.executive_summary: A Tauri and Svelte desktop application with a Rust Axum IPC backend storing passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in SQLCipher-encrypted local vaults. Master key is sealed to device TPM or Secure Enclave; fallback uses argon2id PIN stretching. Team vault sharing uses X25519 ECDH per-member key encryption. Emergency access requests persist across app restarts via audit.db timestamp tracking. Distributed as signed installers for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
reference.architecture_overview: Local-only desktop architecture: Tauri shell hosts Svelte TypeScript UI and communicates with a Rust Axum command handler via Tauri invoke() over a local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows). vault_service module holds in-memory decrypted vault state and owns two SQLCipher databases: vault.db for credential entries and audit.db for append-only audit log and emergency access state. Master key derived from TPM Seal/Unseal (Secure Enclave on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows) or argon2id from PIN as fallback. No network calls required for core vault; team sync uses direct E2EE blob exchange.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.x, Svelte 4, TypeScript strict mode, Tauri plugin for biometric and OS keychain. Backend: Rust stable, Axum 0.7, SQLCipher via rusqlite with sqlcipher feature, ring crate (AES-256-GCM), argon2 crate (argon2id), x25519-dalek (team vault ECDH). Build: GitHub Actions cross-platform matrix. Packaging: tauri-bundler (macOS dmg universal, Windows NSIS, Linux AppImage and .deb). Crash reporting: Sentry Rust SDK with data scrubbing.
reference.functional_feature_map: Personal Vault: create/read/update/delete entries of type password, otp_seed, ssh_key with AES-256-GCM per-entry encryption. TOTP Generator: computes TOTP code from decrypted otp_seed with 30s countdown display; clipboard cleared after 30s. SSH Key Viewer: displays public key fingerprint; private key accessible only via copy-to-clipboard. Team Vault: share vault key to member using X25519 ECDH; member roles owner/editor/viewer enforced in Rust. Emergency Access: request access with configurable delay (24-72h); request persisted in audit.db; owner approves or denies on next app open within window. Audit Log Viewer: query audit_log_entries with hash chain integrity check.
reference.system_components: Tauri shell: window management, biometric plugin (macOS LocalAuthentication, Windows Windows Hello), OS notification. Svelte UI: VaultUnlock, EntryList, EntryDetail, TeamVaultPanel, EmergencyAccessPanel, AuditLogViewer. Rust vault_service: SQLCipher pool for vault.db and audit.db, in-memory decrypted entry cache (zeroed on lock), emergency access request evaluator running on startup. ring crate: AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt, HKDF for key derivation. argon2 crate: PIN stretching. x25519-dalek: team vault ECDH key agreement. audit_service: append-only writes with hash chain computation.
reference.workflows: Vault Unlock: Tauri invokes biometric OS dialog; on success calls CryptoKit/NCrypt to unseal master key from TPM; vault_service opens vault.db and audit.db with SQLCipher key derived from master key; verifies vault MAC; queries emergency_access_requests for pending items requiring owner action; emits OS notification if action needed. Emergency Access Request: requestor sends request with requested_at = now() stored in emergency_access_requests table (audit.db) with status=pending and approval_delay_hours; vault owner receives OS notification on next app open; if (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours and owner has not denied, vault_service generates time-limited access token encrypted with requestor's public key. Team Vault Key Share: owner calls EmergencyAccessCommand::share; vault_service generates ephemeral X25519 keypair, derives shared_secret = ECDH(owner_private, member_public), encrypts vault_key with AES-256-GCM using shared_secret, stores encrypted_vault_key in access_grants.
reference.data_model: vault.db - vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, nonce BLOB 12 bytes, ciphertext BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint CHAR 47). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_identity VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, ecdh_wrapped_vault_key BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). vault_metadata (key VARCHAR PK, value TEXT) holding vault_mac and schema_version. audit.db - audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash CHAR 64, row_hash CHAR 64). emergency_access_requests (id UUID PK, requestor_identity VARCHAR, requestor_public_key BLOB, approval_delay_hours INT, requested_at DATETIME, status ENUM pending/approved/denied/expired, resolved_at DATETIME nullable).
reference.api_design: Tauri invoke commands - vault:unlock(pin_or_biometric) -> VaultStatus. vault:create_entry(entry_type, fields) -> EntryId. vault:read_entry(entry_id) -> DecryptedEntry (zeroed after use). vault:delete_entry(entry_id) -> void. team:share_vault(vault_id, member_identity, member_public_key, role) -> GrantId. team:revoke_member(grant_id) -> void. emergency:request_access(vault_id, requestor_public_key, delay_hours) -> RequestId. emergency:approve_access(request_id) -> EncryptedAccessToken. emergency:deny_access(request_id) -> void. audit:query_log(filters) -> AuditLogPage with chain_valid bool.
reference.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-132 compliance: master key derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 on PIN fallback with 600,000 iterations (FIPS 140-3 guidance). TPM path: Secure Enclave CryptoKit on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows. Per-entry encryption: AES-256-GCM with unique 96-bit nonce per write. Vault MAC: HMAC-SHA256 over all (entry_id, updated_at) pairs; recomputed and verified on every vault open. Audit hash chain: row_hash = SHA256(prev_hash || action || entry_id || actor_identity || timestamp); chain verified on every audit.db open. Emergency access timer: persisted in audit.db; app-restart-safe. Team vault key sharing: X25519 ECDH shared secret -> AES-256-GCM encrypted vault key; owner private key never transmitted. Clipboard cleared after 30s. Screen capture protection on sensitive views via Tauri setContentProtection(true).
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: [macos-14 (aarch64), macos-13 (x86_64)], [windows-2022], [ubuntu-22.04, ubuntu-22.04-arm64]. Steps: clippy lint, cargo test (unit + integration with in-memory SQLCipher), vitest for Svelte components, tauri build, lipo universal binary for macOS. Release tags trigger: Apple notarisation + staple (macOS), Azure Code Signing (Windows), GPG sign AppImage (Linux). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update manifest signed with Tauri updater key pair.
reference.observability: Local audit log in audit.db with hash chain provides tamper-evident access history. Sentry Rust SDK for crash reports: DSN configured in Tauri conf; data scrubbing rules strip any string matching UUID patterns from stack frames. App version and OS version sent with crashes; no vault data. Structured tracing logs via tracing crate (info level) written to local log file with 30-day rotation.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost for core vault; local-only. GitHub Actions free tier covers cross-platform build matrix. Apple Developer Program ($99/yr) required for notarisation. Windows EV certificate for Authenticode (~$300/yr). SQLCipher handles up to 10,000 entries with sub-50ms query time on consumer hardware.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): personal vault unlock via TPM/PIN, password and OTP seed CRUD with AES-256-GCM, local audit.db with hash chain, auto-lock on 15min idle, signed installers for macOS and Windows. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SSH key storage, team vault sharing with X25519 ECDH, emergency access with persistent timer, team RBAC. Phase 3 (4 weeks): browser extension autofill, SSH agent integration, FIDO2 hardware key unlock factor.
reference.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy deny(warnings), cargo test with coverage via cargo-tarpaulin targeting 85% line coverage. Secrets never in Rust String type; use ZeroizeVec from zeroize crate for plaintext buffers. Svelte: TypeScript strict, vitest for component logic. No vault plaintext in Svelte stores; fetch on-demand via IPC. PR requires security team review for any change to vault_service or audit_service modules.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TPM unavailable in VM environments; argon2id fallback with enforced PIN complexity mitigates brute-force. Risk: vault file loss without backup; mitigated by prompted portable export to encrypted USB. Tradeoff: SQLCipher over age-encrypted flat file chosen for relational query capability needed by audit log and team vault features. Risk: Apple notarisation delay blocking release; mitigated by pre-notarising release candidates before tag.
reference.open_questions_resolved: X25519 ECDH chosen over Signal double ratchet for team vault key sharing; double ratchet is over-engineered for vault key exchange which does not require forward secrecy on every message. Emergency access timer persistence chosen via audit.db rows rather than OS scheduled tasks to maintain single source of truth and audit trail. argon2id parameters set to m=65536, t=3, p=4 following OWASP recommendation for interactive logins.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two critical blockers: ISS-001 (LLM embedding in feed critical path) and ISS-002 (CDN purge absence in takedown flow). Fix: pre-compute content embeddings at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered job, store in PostgreSQL pgvector, cache similarity scores in Redis at 30s intervals. Add synchronous Cloud CDN API call within TakedownVideo gRPC handler. Add coppa_minor JWT claim check in engagement-collector gRPC interceptor. These changes are surgical and do not require a full architecture re-write.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced LLM embedding call in feed-ranking GetFeed handler with Redis-cached pre-computed similarity scores. Pub/Sub-triggered embedding pre-computation job runs on video_post status=transcoded event, calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5, stores 768-dim vector in PostgreSQL video_posts.content_embedding via pgvector, writes cosine similarity scores to Redis feed_similarity:{viewer_id} for all candidate video_ids. Feed-ranking service reads pre-computed scores from Redis and re-ranks within 30s of last engagement signal update.
fix_report.expected_outcome: p99 feed delivery latency reduced to <100ms; LLM call removed from critical path; pre-computed scores enable sub-100ms re-scoring on engagement signal updates

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation to AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler. Handler calls Cloud CDN API to invalidate the HLS manifest URL within the same transaction that updates video_post.status=taken_down. Returns TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id. Moderation audit record includes cdn_purge_job_id for DSA evidence. CDN Interconnect API used for fast invalidation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Content removed from CDN within 60 seconds of moderator action; DSA 60s takedown propagation SLA met; cdn_purge_job_id logged for regulatory audit evidence

parsed.title: Short-Form Video Microservices - Round 2
parsed.executive_summary: A Flutter 3.22 mobile app for Gen Z short-form vertical video, decomposed into Go gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot, with a three-part fix for round 2: pre-computed LLM content embeddings stored in PostgreSQL pgvector and cached in Redis at 30s intervals (ISS-001), synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation in the TakedownVideo gRPC handler (ISS-002), and coppa_minor JWT claim blocking in the engagement-collector gRPC interceptor (ISS-003). The platform handles multi-million user scale with sub-100ms feed delivery, real-time engagement signal ingestion, and DSA-compliant content moderation.
parsed.architecture_overview: Flutter 3.22 app communicates with Go gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot via gRPC-Dart clients. The feed-ranking service reads pre-computed content embeddings from Redis (cached at 30s intervals) and re-ranks the feed using cosine similarity against the viewer's interest profile embedding stored in PostgreSQL. The upload-processor service spawns Cloud Run transcoding jobs and publishes to Pub/Sub on completion; a pre-computation job calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 and stores 768-dim vectors in PostgreSQL pgvector. The engagement-collector service receives streaming engagement events and writes to Cassandra engagement_signals; a gRPC interceptor checks the coppa_minor JWT claim and drops events for under-13 users. The content-moderation service consumes Pub/Sub moderation events and applies hash fingerprinting plus LLM moderation scoring. The admin-api service handles TakedownVideo with synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation. All services use gRPC interceptors for JWT validation and custom claim checks.
parsed.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod, gRPC-Dart, Firebase Auth, Firebase Firestore for session state, Plato video player. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC with protobuf, Pub/Sub, pgvector extension for PostgreSQL, Cassandra 4.1, Redis 7, Cloud Run for transcoding, Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 for embeddings. Databases: PostgreSQL 16 (pgvector), Cassandra 4.1, Redis 7. Storage: Cloud Storage for raw video and HLS segments, Cloud CDN for global delivery. Hosting: GKE Autopilot (us-central1, europe-west1), Cloud Build, Cloud Run. Observability: Datadog APM, Datadog RUM, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Vertical Video Feed: algorithmically ranked feed with LLM-assisted content-based relevance, pre-loaded VideoPlayerControllers, swipe-to-engage streaming to engagement-collector. Creator Upload: clip trimmer, caption editor, transcoding pipeline to Cloud Run, pre-computed content embedding stored at upload-complete. Social Graph: follow/unfollow, follower/followee counts, creator profile screen. Engagement Signals: like, share, comment, watch-time, skip - all streamed to engagement-collector. Content Moderation: hash fingerprinting, LLM moderation scoring, moderator admin panel with TakedownVideo action. Admin Panel: moderator-only, moderation_queue review, label assignment, takedown propagation audit log.
parsed.system_components: Flutter App (iOS + Android): VerticalFeedScreen, UploadScreen, CreatorProfileScreen, SocialGraphScreen, AdminPanel (moderator role). Go Microservices on GKE Autopilot: feed-ranking (gRPC), upload-processor (gRPC + Pub/Sub), social-graph (gRPC), engagement-collector (gRPC streaming), content-moderation (Pub/Sub consumer), notification (Pub/Sub consumer), admin-api (gRPC), deletion-coordinator (Pub/Sub consumer). Embedding Pre-Computation Job: Pub/Sub-triggered Go job calling Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 and storing pgvector. CDN Purge Job: Pub/Sub-triggered Go job calling Cloud CDN API for synchronous invalidation. Cloud Run Transcode: ffmpeg-based transcoding for HLS 1080p/60fps. Cloud Storage: raw video upload and transcoded HLS segments. Cloud CDN: global delivery with dual-region origin failover. ClickHouse: ad hoc analytics cluster.
parsed.workflows: Feed Refresh: Flutter app calls GET feed-ranking/v1/feed via gRPC-Dart; feed-ranking service reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} LIST (pre-computed at 30s intervals); if stale, reads Cassandra engagement_signals for recent signals, computes cosine similarity against viewer interest profile pgvector, writes new ranked list to Redis, returns FeedResponse; p99 <100ms. Video Upload: Creator selects clip, trims to 60s max, adds caption; Flutter calls POST upload-processor/v1/upload; upload-processor spawns Cloud Run transcode job, returns UploadSession; Flutter polls Pub/Sub SSE stream for transcoding progress; on completion, Pub/Sub triggers embedding pre-computation job; pre-computation job calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5, stores 768-dim vector in PostgreSQL video_posts.content_embedding via pgvector, writes similarity scores to Redis feed_similarity:{viewer_id}; feed-ranking service re-ranks within 30s. Content Takedown: Moderator selects video in admin panel, calls AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest); admin-api updates video_post.status=taken_down in PostgreSQL; within same transaction calls Cloud CDN API to invalidate HLS manifest URL via Interconnect API; returns TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id; audit record written to moderation_queues with cdn_purge_job_id for DSA evidence; CDN invalidation completes within 60 seconds.
parsed.data_model: creator_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, region VARCHAR, age_verified BOOL, coppa_minor BOOL, follower_count INT, following_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMP) in PostgreSQL. video_posts (id UUID PK, creator_id UUID FK, title VARCHAR, caption VARCHAR, hls_manifest_url VARCHAR, thumbnail_url VARCHAR, duration_seconds INT, status ENUM uploading/transcoding/active/taken_down, content_embedding vector(768) via pgvector, moderation_score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP) in PostgreSQL. feed_rankings (viewer_id UUID, segment_key TEXT, ranked_video_ids LIST<UUID>, scored_at TIMESTAMP) PRIMARY KEY (viewer_id, segment_key) in Cassandra. engagement_signals (viewer_id UUID, video_id UUID, signal_type ENUM like/share/comment/watch_complete/skip, watch_time_ms INT, occurred_at TIMESTAMP) PRIMARY KEY (viewer_id, occurred_at) in Cassandra. social_edges (follower_id UUID, followee_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP) in PostgreSQL. moderation_queues (id UUID PK, video_id UUID, queue_type ENUM hash_match/llm_flagged/user_report, assigned_moderator_id UUID, status ENUM pending/reviewed/actioned, created_at TIMESTAMP) in PostgreSQL. trending_topics (topic STRING, score FLOAT, updated_at TIMESTAMP) in Redis ZSET.
parsed.api_design: gRPC proto definitions: FeedService.GetFeed(ViewerFeedRequest) returns FeedResponse {video_ids LIST<UUID>, ranked_scores LIST<float>, last_updated_at TIMESTAMP}. UploadService.StartUpload(UploadRequest) returns UploadSession {session_id STRING, status ENUM pending/transcoding/complete, progress INT}. EngagementService.RecordSignals(stream EngagementEvent) returns Ack {count INT, total INT}. SocialService.Follow(FollowRequest) returns FollowAck. AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest) returns TakedownAck {video_id UUID, cdn_purge_job_id STRING, propagation_completed_at TIMESTAMP}. AdminService.GetModerationQueue() returns ModerationQueueResponse. All services require JWT Bearer header; coppa_minor claim checked in gRPC interceptor before processing.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth with custom claims: age_verified BOOL, coppa_minor BOOL, region STRING, moderator BOOL. All backend gRPC interceptors validate JWT and check coppa_minor claim before processing. COPPA: under-13 users receive coppa_minor claim; engagement-collector gRPC interceptor drops all engagement events for coppa_minor users; watch-time signals not stored beyond session; parental consent required for data retention. GDPR: right-to-erasure via Pub/Sub deletion event; deletion-coordinator fans out to all services within 72 hours. DSA: TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN API synchronously within the same transaction; cdn_purge_job_id logged to moderation_queues for DSA audit evidence; transparency report endpoint GET /admin/dsa-report returns takedown counts by category and average propagation time. Content hash fingerprinting: SHA-256 hash of HLS manifest URL stored in video_posts.content_hash; checked against NCMEC PhotoDNA hash list and internal bad-content hash list at upload time.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot clusters: us-central1 (primary), europe-west1 (EU GDPR data residency). Each cluster runs all Go microservices via Cloud Build. HPA: feed-ranking on feed_request_queue_depth >50, upload-processor on upload_queue_depth >20, engagement-collector on signal_throughput >5000/min. Cloud Build pipeline: go test./..., golangci-lint, ko build (container image from Go binary), kubectl apply via Workload Identity. Flutter CI: flutter test, flutter build appbundle, Firebase App Distribution for internal testing. canary deploy: 5% traffic via Istio VirtualService weight before full rollout. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager for Firebase service account keys and Cloud CDN API credentials.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM with gRPC interceptor tracing on all Go services. Distributed trace IDs propagated via gRPC metadata. Custom metrics: feed_delivery_latency_p99, upload_processing_duration_p95, engagement_signal_throughput, cdn_purge_latency, embedding_precompute_latency. Datadog RUM in Flutter for crash reporting and ANR. PagerDuty alerts: feed_delivery_p99 >100ms, cdn_purge_latency >55s, upload_failure_rate >5%, embedding_precompute_latency >30s.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero outside business hours. Cloud Run transcoding scales to zero between uploads. Cloud CDN auto-scales origin capacity. Estimated cost at 10M MAU: ~$8K/month (GKE Autopilot ~$3K, Cloud Run ~$1K, Cloud Storage ~$1K, Cloud CDN ~$1K, ClickHouse ~$1K, Vertex AI ~$1K). At 50M MAU: ~$25K/month.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-5): Decompose monolith into feed-ranking, upload-processor, social-graph, engagement-collector, content-moderation microservices. Implement pre-computed LLM content embeddings at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered job. Implement COPPA coppa_minor JWT claim check in engagement-collector gRPC interceptor. Implement synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation in TakedownVideo gRPC handler. Phase 2 (Weeks 6-10): Implement 30s Redis-cached feed re-scoring. Implement Flutter admin panel for moderators. Implement GDPR right-to-erasure deletion coordinator. Phase 3 (Weeks 11-15): Implement trending topics via Redis ZSET. Implement creator analytics via ClickHouse. Implement DSA transparency report endpoint.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go services: golangci-lint with errcheck and gosec. Unit tests for feed-ranking re-scoring logic and embedding pre-computation job. Integration tests for TakedownVideo CDN invalidation flow. Flutter: flutter test for UploadScreen and AdminPanel. Dart analysis_options.yaml with strict rules. All gRPC handlers must have unit tests for JWT validation and custom claim checks. Embedding pre-computation job must have integration test verifying pgvector vector insertion and Redis similarity score retrieval.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 cold-start latency adds to embedding pre-computation job duration. Mitigation: pre-warm Vertex AI endpoint with a small test embedding job on startup; cache pre-computed embeddings in Redis for 30s. Risk: Cloud CDN invalidation via Interconnect API fails silently if CDN cache is stale. Mitigation: log cdn_purge_job_id to moderation_queues and monitor propagation via CDN cache hit rate metric; alert if hit rate drops >20% within 60s. Tradeoff: pgvector over a dedicated vector database (Weaviate, Pinecone) - pgvector adds ~$200/month to PostgreSQL instance but avoids a separate vector DB dependency at this scale. Tradeoff: 30s Redis-cached feed re-scoring over real-time LLM - 30s stale is acceptable for feed ranking; real-time re-scoring would require a separate LLM call per engagement event, which would reintroduce the p99 latency issue.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Pre-computed LLM embeddings stored in PostgreSQL pgvector with Redis-cached similarity scores chosen over a dedicated vector DB to avoid additional dependency at this scale. Synchronous Cloud CDN API call in TakedownVideo handler chosen over async job to meet DSA 60s takedown propagation SLA. COPPA coppa_minor JWT claim checked in engagement-collector gRPC interceptor rather than frontend to ensure server-side enforcement. Live streaming deferred to Phase 3 pending architecture review.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (LLM in request path) and ISS-002 (CDN purge absent). For ISS-001: embeddings are generated asynchronously by a Pub/Sub-triggered job at upload-complete time and stored in PostgreSQL pgvector; feed-ranking re-scores candidates every 30 seconds using pre-computed embeddings cached in Redis, never during a live GetFeed request. For ISS-002: AdminService.TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN API synchronously within the gRPC handler before returning, logs cdn_purge_job_id to the moderation audit trail for DSA evidence. ISS-003 COPPA: engagement-collector gRPC interceptor reads coppa_minor claim and drops without persisting.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved LLM embedding computation from GetFeed request path to a Pub/Sub-triggered background job: on video_post.status=transcoded event, embedding-generator job calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (pgvector). Feed-ranking service re-scores top-K candidates every 30 seconds via a scheduled worker reading pgvector cosine similarity from PostgreSQL and writing ranked feed to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id}. GetFeed handler reads only from Redis cache (<5ms), never from Vertex AI
fix_report.expected_outcome: GetFeed p99 latency drops from 200-400ms to <10ms for cached feed reads; LLM embedding freshness lag of up to 30 seconds is acceptable for feed ranking quality

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation to AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler: after updating video_post.status=taken_down in PostgreSQL, handler calls GCP Cloud CDN API cdn.CacheInvalidation.Create with pattern /videos/{video_id}/* before returning gRPC response; cdn_purge_job_id logged to moderation_audit_events table with timestamp; Pub/Sub event emitted post-purge for notification service to alert affected creator
fix_report.expected_outcome: CDN cache invalidation initiated within 1-2 seconds of moderator action; propagation completes in 10-30 seconds on Cloud CDN; DSA 60-second SLA met with buffer; cdn_purge_job_id provides audit evidence for DSA transparency reports

reference.title: Gen Z Short-Form Video Platform - Microservices Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A COPPA/GDPR/DSA-compliant Flutter 3.22 mobile app backed by Go 1.22 gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot, decomposed from a scaling-bottlenecked monolith. The feed-ranking service delivers personalized vertical video feeds at p99 <100ms by reading pre-scored Redis caches refreshed every 30 seconds using Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 content embeddings computed asynchronously at upload time. Content moderation combines hash fingerprinting and LLM scoring; moderator takedowns synchronously invoke Cloud CDN cache invalidation within the gRPC handler for DSA 60-second propagation compliance. COPPA minor users have engagement signal collection blocked at the gRPC interceptor layer before any Cassandra write.
reference.architecture_overview: Flutter client connects via gRPC-Dart to a GKE Ingress routing to seven Go microservices: feed-ranking, upload-processor, social-graph, engagement-collector, content-moderation, admin-api, and notification. Feed delivery critical path: GetFeed reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-scored every 30 seconds by a background feed-scorer worker) for sub-10ms response, bypassing Vertex AI entirely at request time. LLM embedding pipeline: upload-processor publishes upload_complete to Pub/Sub; embedding-generator service calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (PostgreSQL pgvector); feed-scorer worker runs cosine similarity queries against cached viewer interest embeddings every 30 seconds and writes results to Redis. CDN takedown path: TakedownVideo gRPC handler updates PostgreSQL video_post status, calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation API synchronously, logs cdn_purge_job_id to audit trail. Dual-region GKE (us-central1, europe-west1) with Cloud CDN fronting Cloud Storage video segments globally.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod, gRPC-Dart, video_player package. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC with protobuf, GCP Pub/Sub for async events, Firebase Admin SDK for JWT validation. Data: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension (creator_profiles, video_posts, social_edges), Cassandra 4.1 (engagement_signals, feed_rankings), Redis 7 (feed_cache, trending_topics). LLM: Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API (768-dim). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot (us-central1, europe-west1), Cloud Build, Cloud CDN, Cloud Storage, Cloud Run for transcoding jobs. Observability: Datadog APM + RUM.
reference.functional_feature_map: Vertical Feed: Flutter PageView rendering HLS video posts pre-loaded from Redis feed cache, refreshed every 30s by LLM-assisted relevance scoring. Upload Pipeline: creator submits clip via gRPC UploadService, upload-processor stores raw video in Cloud Storage, triggers Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job, publishes upload_complete event to Pub/Sub which triggers embedding generation. Social Graph: SocialService.Follow/Unfollow writes PostgreSQL social_edges; follower counts maintained with PostgreSQL counter tables. Engagement Collection: streaming gRPC EngagementService.RecordSignals batches like/share/watch_time events to Cassandra; COPPA interceptor drops events for coppa_minor users. Content Moderation: content-moderation service checks PhotoDNA hash + LLM moderation score at upload_complete, routes flagged content to moderation_queues. Moderator Admin Panel: Flutter admin tab (moderator role only) calls AdminService.TakedownVideo which purges CDN within 60 seconds. Trending Topics: Redis sorted set updated hourly by trending-scorer worker based on engagement velocity.
reference.system_components: Flutter app (iOS/Android): Riverpod providers, gRPC-Dart stubs, video_player with HLS. GKE Ingress (gRPC HTTP/2 passthrough). Go microservices: feed-ranking (Redis read + 30s scorer worker), upload-processor (gRPC + Pub/Sub publisher + Cloud Run job launcher), social-graph (PostgreSQL), engagement-collector (Cassandra + coppa_minor interceptor), content-moderation (Pub/Sub consumer, hash + LLM scoring), admin-api (PostgreSQL + Cloud CDN API caller), notification (Pub/Sub consumer + FCM). embedding-generator service (Pub/Sub consumer, Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 API, pgvector write). feed-scorer worker (30s cron, PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity, Redis write). PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector. Cassandra 4.1. Redis 7. Cloud Storage (raw video + HLS segments). Cloud CDN. Datadog APM agents on all services.
reference.workflows: Feed Delivery (Critical Path): Flutter calls FeedService.GetFeed via gRPC. feed-ranking handler reads Redis LIST feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-populated by feed-scorer worker). Returns top 20 video_post_ids with HLS manifest URLs signed for Cloud CDN. p99 < 10ms from Redis. Background Feed Scoring (every 30s): feed-scorer worker queries Cassandra engagement_signals for each active viewer (last 7 days), fetches top-500 candidate videos, runs pgvector cosine similarity query: SELECT video_id FROM video_posts ORDER BY content_embedding <=> $viewer_interest_embedding LIMIT 500, applies collaborative filter boost from Cassandra follow-graph signal, writes re-ranked list to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} with 60s TTL. Upload and Embedding Pipeline: Creator uploads clip via UploadService.StartUpload. upload-processor stores raw file in Cloud Storage, launches Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job (HLS at 720p/1080p). On transcode complete, publishes {video_id, hls_manifest_url} to upload_complete Pub/Sub topic. embedding-generator consumes event, calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API with video thumbnail + caption text, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding via pgvector. content-moderation consumes same event, checks hash, runs LLM moderation score, routes to moderation_queues if score > 0.7. Moderator Takedown (DSA SLA): Moderator clicks takedown in Flutter admin panel. Flutter calls AdminService.TakedownVideo(video_id, reason). admin-api gRPC handler: UPDATE video_posts SET status='taken_down'; calls GCP Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation.Create API with url_map pattern /videos/{video_id}/*; receives cdn_purge_job_id; INSERTs moderation_audit_event row with cdn_purge_job_id and initiated_at timestamp; returns TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id to Flutter. Flutter shows confirmation with purge job ID. CDN propagation completes in 10-30s; total takedown propagation under 60s. Pub/Sub notification event emitted to notify creator.
reference.data_model: creator_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, region VARCHAR, age_verified BOOL DEFAULT false, coppa_minor BOOL DEFAULT false, follower_count INT DEFAULT 0, following_count INT DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. video_posts (id UUID PK, creator_id UUID FK, caption TEXT, hls_manifest_url TEXT, thumbnail_url TEXT, duration_seconds FLOAT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('uploading','transcoding','active','taken_down'), content_embedding vector(768), moderation_score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL with pgvector. social_edges (follower_id UUID, followee_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followee_id)) - PostgreSQL. engagement_signals (viewer_id UUID, video_id UUID, signal_type TEXT, watch_time_ms INT, occurred_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (viewer_id, occurred_at)) - Cassandra MV partition. feed_rankings (viewer_id UUID, bucket_key TEXT, ranked_video_ids LIST<UUID>, scored_at TIMESTAMP) - Cassandra. moderation_queues (id UUID PK, video_id UUID FK, queue_type TEXT, assigned_moderator_id UUID, status TEXT, cdn_purge_job_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. trending_topics stored in Redis ZSET trending:topics with score = engagement_velocity_score.
reference.api_design: gRPC services (proto): FeedService.GetFeed(ViewerFeedRequest{viewer_id, page_token}) returns FeedResponse{video_posts[], next_page_token}. UploadService.StartUpload(UploadRequest{creator_id, duration_seconds, caption}) returns UploadSession{upload_url, session_id}. EngagementService.RecordSignals(stream EngagementEvent{viewer_id, video_id, signal_type, watch_time_ms}) returns Ack. SocialService.Follow(FollowRequest{follower_id, followee_id}) returns FollowAck. AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest{video_id, moderator_id, reason}) returns TakedownAck{cdn_purge_job_id, initiated_at}. AdminService.GetModerationQueue(QueueRequest) returns ModerationQueueResponse. REST (admin panel DSA reporting): GET /admin/dsa-report?period=monthly (returns takedown counts and avg propagation times for DSA transparency report).
reference.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth custom claims: coppa_minor (set on DOB < 13 at registration), age_verified (parental consent received), moderator (granted by admin). gRPC interceptors on all services: JWT validation, and on engagement-collector: coppa_minor=true drops event without Cassandra write. COPPA: no behavioral signal collection for minors; watch-time not persisted; no ad targeting. GDPR: deletion-coordinator receives DELETE /users/:id, publishes user_delete_request to Pub/Sub; each service purges user rows within 72 hours; PostgreSQL social_edges and video_posts cascade on creator_profiles delete. DSA: TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation synchronously; cdn_purge_job_id and timing logged to moderation_audit_events for quarterly DSA transparency report; GET /admin/dsa-report endpoint for regulatory submissions. Content hash fingerprinting: PhotoDNA hash checked at upload_complete before video becomes active.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Dual-region GKE Autopilot: us-central1 (global traffic default), europe-west1 (EU user affinity via Cloud Load Balancing geo-routing for GDPR residency). Each region deploys all Go microservices via Cloud Build. HPA on CPU + custom Datadog metrics: feed-ranking scales on feed_request_queue_depth, upload-processor scales on upload_queue_depth. Cloud Build pipeline per service: go test ./..., golangci-lint, ko build, kubectl apply with Workload Identity. Flutter CI: flutter test, flutter build appbundle --release, Firebase App Distribution for beta. Canary: Istio VirtualService 5% traffic split before full rollout. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo per service.
reference.observability: Datadog APM gRPC interceptor on all Go services with distributed trace propagation via gRPC metadata. Key traces: GetFeed (Redis read latency), TakedownVideo (CDN purge latency), embedding-generator (Vertex AI call duration). Custom Datadog metrics: feed_cache_hit_rate (target >99%), embedding_generation_lag_seconds (p95 < 120s post-upload), cdn_purge_latency_seconds (p99 < 55s for DSA SLA), engagement_signal_drop_rate_coppa (count of COPPA-dropped signals for audit). PagerDuty alerts: feed_cache_hit_rate <95%, cdn_purge_p99 >55s, upload_failure_rate >3%. Datadog RUM in Flutter for crash rate and video player error rate.
reference.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero for low-traffic services; estimated $2,400/month at 500K daily active users: GKE compute ($900), Cloud CDN egress for 5PB/month video ($1,100), PostgreSQL CloudSQL ($250), Cassandra (self-managed GKE StatefulSet, $150). Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API: ~$0.0001 per 1K tokens, negligible for upload volume. Redis cluster ($120/month). Datadog ($200/month at this scale). Feed-scorer worker cost negligible (Go goroutine pool, <1 CPU continuous).
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (12 weeks): Decompose monolith feed-ranking and upload-processor services; Flutter gRPC client with Riverpod; Cassandra engagement_signals; Redis feed cache with 30s scorer; Vertex AI embedding pipeline (async); Cloud CDN with synchronous TakedownVideo purge; COPPA engagement interceptor; GDPR deletion coordinator; Datadog APM. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Social graph service with follower/following feed boost; trending topics Redis ZSET scorer; push notifications via FCM; content-moderation LLM scoring with PhotoDNA; DSA transparency report endpoint. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Ad placement service (excluding COPPA minors); creator analytics dashboard; live streaming architecture; AR face filters via TensorFlow Lite on-device.
reference.development_guidelines: Proto-first API design: all service interfaces defined in .proto files in the shared proto repository before implementation. Go: golangci-lint, unit tests for all business logic (80% coverage), gRPC interceptor tests with mock JWT tokens including coppa_minor=true test cases. Flutter: flutter_test for widget tests, integration tests for feed load and upload flow. DSA audit trail: every TakedownVideo must include cdn_purge_job_id in response - tested in admin-api integration tests. Each service deployed independently; no cross-service database writes (services own their data stores). Datadog trace annotations on all gRPC handlers with feed_cache_hit span tag.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 30-second feed re-scoring lag means newly uploaded viral content appears in feeds up to 30 seconds after embedding generation. Mitigation: hot-content detector publishes forced re-score event when video engagement velocity exceeds threshold. Risk: PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity query over 10M video_posts at 30s interval becoming a bottleneck. Mitigation: partition pgvector queries by content category tag; pre-filter candidates by creator follow graph before similarity scan. Tradeoff: synchronous CDN purge in TakedownVideo vs. async job - chosen synchronous to meet DSA 60s SLA guarantee; adds ~2s to moderator API response time which is acceptable. Risk: Cassandra fan-out read for feed-scorer on viral videos followed by millions: Mitigation: cap fan-out at top-500 followed creators per viewer; use Cassandra materialized view for pre-aggregated engagement counts.
reference.open_questions_resolved: LLM embedding computed asynchronously at upload time, not at request time; GetFeed reads only Redis cache. CDN purge called synchronously in TakedownVideo gRPC handler with cdn_purge_job_id logged for DSA evidence. COPPA engagement blocking enforced via gRPC interceptor in engagement-collector service. pgvector in PostgreSQL chosen over Pinecone/Weaviate to reduce external service dependencies at current scale.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the sports feed pipeline benchmark CLI. Two issues from round 1: ISS-001 requires replacing the shared Ticker rate limiter with per-goroutine golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter to eliminate false dropped-message attribution at high EPS, and ISS-002 requires adding a YAML startup credential scan to prevent credential values from appearing in version-controlled config files. Both fixes are straightforward and do not change the overall CLI architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced shared time.Ticker with per-goroutine golang.org/x/time/rate.NewLimiter(rate.Every(time.Second/float64(eps_target)), 1) in BenchmarkRunner.runProducers(). Each producer goroutine now has its own limiter, eliminating shared-channel contention and false dropped-message attribution. Updated workflows and system_components sections.
