Mitigation: versioned sync protocol with backward-compatible idempotency key format; migration test in staging before production release. Risk: Cognito user pool outage prevents staff login during a shift. Mitigation: pre-login cached JWT in Expo SecureStore with 1-hour TTL; staff can complete checklists offline and sync on reconnection. Tradeoff: last-write-wins conflict resolution over merge-based resolution — simpler to implement but may lose partial submissions in rare concurrent multi-staff scenarios; acceptable for FSMA audit trail integrity given the uniqueness constraint. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over a custom sync library — adds dependency complexity but provides reactive queries and built-in conflict resolution primitives.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed WatermelonDB over expo-sqlite directly for sync primitives. Confirmed last-write-wins on (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) uniqueness. Confirmed X-Idempotency-Key header for server-side deduplication. Confirmed S3 for compliance report PDFs over in-process generation. Confirmed Cognito custom auth challenge for staff PIN flow.

reference.thinking_summary: The key gap in rounds 1-2 was underspecified sync semantics. ISS-001 needs a concrete unique key on checklist_completion records and explicit server adjudication rules for duplicate client UUIDs. ISS-002 needs a WatermelonDB-level retry queue with sync_status field and exponential backoff. The health authority export template versioning (ISS-003) was resolved in round 2 and just needs to be carried forward correctly. HACCP compliance demands that these sync details are precise — ambiguity in conflict resolution could produce duplicate or missing regulatory records.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified that checklist_completion records are unique on composite key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date); server treats client-generated UUID as idempotency key — duplicate UUID returns 200 with existing record; composite key collision returns 409 with existing record payload so client can reconcile; documented in api_design and data_model sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent submissions from multiple staff on the same checklist type and shift date are deterministically handled without duplicate compliance records

fix_report.action_taken: Defined WatermelonDB sync_status field on local checklist_completion model (values: pending, synced, failed, retrying); POST /sync/push returns per-record status array; mobile client iterates response and marks failed records as sync_status=failed; a Zustand-managed retry scheduler re-queues failed records on next connectivity event with exponential backoff capped at 4 retries before escalating to supervisor alert; described in workflows and deployment sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial sync failures are tracked at the record level in WatermelonDB and retried automatically without data loss or silent drops

reference.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile Platform — Round 3 Architecture
reference.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile application enabling kitchen staff to complete HACCP-required food safety checklists — including temperature logs, allergen handling confirmations, and equipment sanitization records — with up to 8-hour offline capability via WatermelonDB SQLite storage and deterministic conflict-resolved sync to a FastAPI backend on AWS ECS Fargate. Compliance managers access a cross-location audit dashboard and can export tamper-evident audit trails in health authority-accepted formats.
reference.architecture_overview: React Native Expo SDK 51 app with WatermelonDB as the offline-first reactive data layer, syncing to a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate via two purpose-built sync endpoints. Checklist completions carry client-generated UUIDs as idempotency keys; the server enforces a composite unique constraint on (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) to prevent duplicate regulatory records. Authentication flows through Cognito with MFA mandatory for supervisors and managers. All completed records are hashed with SHA-256 and chained into a per-location append-only audit_trail_events sequence, making retroactive tampering detectable. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all server-side compliance data; a Redis ElastiCache instance handles sync session state and rate limiting.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native, Expo SDK 51, Expo Router, WatermelonDB 0.27, Zustand, Expo NetInfo, Expo TaskManager, Expo SecureStore. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, SQLite (WatermelonDB on device), Redis 7 ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, RDS Multi-AZ us-east-1, S3 for compliance PDF exports. CI/CD: Buildkite with iOS and Android lanes, TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Sentry for mobile crash reporting, CloudWatch for sync metrics.
reference.functional_feature_map: Offline Checklist Completion: staff select shift type and work through CCP rows, entering temperature probe readings, allergen confirmation checkboxes, and sanitization log entries entirely offline with no network dependency. Sync Engine: WatermelonDB push/pull sync to FastAPI with idempotency, composite key conflict detection, and per-record retry queue with exponential backoff. Compliance Audit Trail: server-side SHA-256 hash chaining of all submitted records, detectable chain breaks trigger manager alerts. Health Authority Export: server-side export template registry supporting jurisdiction-specific PDF and CSV formats keyed by format_version param, no app release required for new format additions. Admin Compliance Dashboard: multi-location managers view overdue checklist alerts, temperature exceedance events, completion rate heatmaps, and initiate audit exports.
reference.system_components: React Native Expo app (WatermelonDB local SQLite, Zustand UI state, Expo NetInfo connectivity detection, Expo TaskManager background sync), FastAPI sync service (POST /sync/push with per-record idempotency, GET /sync/pull delta by last_sync_at), FastAPI compliance service (audit trail query, export template rendering), Cognito user pool (MFA enforced for supervisor/manager roles), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (checklist_completions, temperature_records, audit_trail_events), Redis ElastiCache (sync session locks, rate limit counters), S3 (exported compliance PDFs with server-side encryption), ALB with WAF (rate limiting, OWASP rule set).
reference.workflows: Offline Checklist Completion and Sync: Staff log in (Cognito, cached JWT in Expo SecureStore), app pre-fetches active checklist templates and location config into WatermelonDB on login. During shift, staff complete opening temperature checks, allergen station confirmations, and sanitization logs — all persisted locally with sync_status=pending. When network restored, Expo TaskManager triggers POST /sync/push with all pending records as a batch; server processes each record, enforces (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) unique constraint, returns per-record status array. Mobile client iterates response: synced records marked sync_status=synced, failed records marked sync_status=failed and scheduled for retry with exponential backoff (30s, 2m, 8m, 30m); after 4 failures supervisor alert is surfaced. Compliance Audit Trail Generation: After sync, server writes each accepted completion to audit_trail_events with SHA-256 hash of the completion payload chained to the previous event hash for the location. Compliance managers request an export via GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y, server renders the records using the registered jurisdiction template and returns a signed S3 PDF URL valid for 1 hour.
reference.data_model: safety_checklists (id UUID PK, location_id FK, checklist_type ENUM opening/closing/hourly/custom, template_version INT, active BOOL, created_at). checklist_completions (id UUID PK client-generated idempotency key, checklist_id FK, staff_id FK, location_id FK, shift_date DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completion_hash VARCHAR, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed/retrying, UNIQUE(checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date)). temperature_records (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, probe_point VARCHAR, celsius_value DECIMAL, within_range BOOL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, allergen_type VARCHAR, confirmed_by_staff_id FK, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). equipment_sanitization_logs (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, equipment_id FK, sanitizer_type VARCHAR, contact_time_seconds INT, pass BOOL). audit_trail_events (id UUID PK, location_id FK, sequence_num BIGINT, action VARCHAR, actor_id FK, record_id UUID, content_hash VARCHAR, chained_hash VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(location_id, sequence_num)). location_profiles (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, jurisdiction_code VARCHAR, export_format_version VARCHAR, timezone VARCHAR).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /sync/push body: {records: [{id, checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, items: [...], submitted_at}]} returns {results: [{id, status: synced|conflict|error, existing_record_id?}]}. GET /sync/pull?location_id=X&since=ISO8601 returns delta of records modified since timestamp. GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y&to_date=Z returns {download_url, expires_at}. GET /checklists?location_id=X&active=true returns checklist templates for pre-fetch. PATCH /checklists/{id}/completions/{cid}/approve for supervisor sign-off. Auth: Cognito JWT bearer on all endpoints; location_id scope enforced server-side.
reference.security_and_compliance: FSMA compliance: all checklist_completion and temperature_record data retained 3 years with automated RDS snapshot lifecycle policy. HACCP audit integrity: SHA-256 content hash per completion record, chained to per-location sequence in audit_trail_events; hash chain verification endpoint available for health inspector review. Cognito MFA mandatory for supervisor and manager roles; staff use PIN-based custom auth challenge. Expo SecureStore for JWT token storage on device (hardware-backed keystore on Android). TLS 1.3 on all API traffic; WAF OWASP rule set on ALB. RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency injection: staff scope to assigned location_id, managers have read-all. Allergen declaration records are immutable post-submission — PATCH forbidden on allergen_declarations.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1: API service (min 2 tasks, max 6, CPU 60% scale-out), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary in us-east-1a, standby in us-east-1b, automated failover <60s. Redis ElastiCache single-node for sync session state (non-critical). ALB with WAF in front of ECS tasks. Buildkite CI pipeline: Python unit tests (pytest, 85% coverage gate), FastAPI integration tests against Dockerized PostgreSQL, Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight distribution) and Android (Play Store internal track), Lighthouse-equivalent API smoke tests against staging. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task before new API deployment. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. OTA updates for non-native JS changes via Expo EAS Update, bypassing app store review cycle.
reference.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI service: trace sync push/pull endpoints, alert on p99 latency >1.5s or error rate >2%. CloudWatch custom metrics: sync_queue_depth per location, conflict_rate (409 responses / total sync requests), retry_escalation_count (records reaching 4th retry). Sentry for React Native: crash reports tagged by device OS version and app version. Grafana dashboard (Datadog source): sync health per location, overdue checklist count by location, temperature exceedance rate by facility. PagerDuty alert on: API error rate >2% for 5 minutes, hash chain integrity check failure for any location, sync queue depth >500 records for >30 minutes.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month at 50 locations with 200 daily checklist completions. ECS Fargate auto-scales from 2 to 6 tasks on CPU threshold; peak sync load at shift-change handled by Redis-based sync session concurrency control. RDS Multi-AZ ~$350/month; Fargate tasks ~$250/month at baseline. S3 export storage minimal (<5GB/year). EAS Build credits managed under Expo plan. Scaling to 500 locations requires read replica for compliance dashboard queries and potential move to Aurora Serverless for cost efficiency at variable load.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Cognito auth with MFA, opening and closing checklist templates for 10-location pilot, offline WatermelonDB storage, POST /sync/push and GET /sync/pull with idempotency and conflict resolution, basic admin dashboard showing completion rates, Buildkite CI with TestFlight distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Temperature probe integration (manual entry and future Bluetooth), allergen declaration workflow, equipment sanitization logs, health authority export templates for 3 target jurisdictions, audit trail hash chain verification UI. Phase 3 (8 weeks): IoT Bluetooth temperature probe auto-capture via Expo BLE, automated health department submission API integration, predictive non-conformance alerts based on historical temperature trend analysis, 500-location scalability hardening with Aurora Serverless migration.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: black + isort + mypy strict in CI. FastAPI endpoints typed with Pydantic v2 models; no raw dict responses. 85% pytest coverage required for sync and compliance endpoints. React Native: ESLint + Prettier, TypeScript strict mode. WatermelonDB model changes require migration files in the models/migrations directory — no schema changes without a migration. All sync endpoint changes require integration test coverage against a Dockerized PostgreSQL instance in CI. FSMA retention policies encoded as Alembic data migrations, not application-layer logic, to ensure they survive code refactors. ADR documents required for any change to the conflict resolution or audit chain strategy.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync protocol complexity — custom push/pull adds implementation burden vs. using a managed sync service. Mitigation: strict integration test suite for sync semantics; conflict resolution encapsulated in a single SyncService class. Risk: Expo OTA update delivery fails on devices with poor connectivity — compliance checklist templates could be stale. Mitigation: version-check at launch; if template_version mismatch exceeds 1 minor version, force full sync before allowing checklist access. Tradeoff: SHA-256 hash chaining adds per-record compute on server but is required for tamper-evident audit trail; acceptable overhead at expected record volume. Risk: iOS background sync limitations — Expo TaskManager background fetch is throttled by iOS. Mitigation: primary sync triggered on foreground resume; background fetch as secondary mechanism only. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for relational integrity of checklist-completion-temperature record hierarchy; cost higher but correctness critical for compliance data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Composite unique key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) confirmed as the conflict detection boundary — a completion is per-staff per shift, not shared. Export template versioning uses server-side registry keyed by jurisdiction_code avoiding app store release dependency. WatermelonDB chosen over direct expo-sqlite for its built-in sync primitives and reactive query subscriptions. EAS Update (OTA) confirmed for JS-layer changes; native module changes still require app store submission. Buildkite selected over GitHub Actions for superior iOS simulator support and parallelism.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing three contract violations: Next.js 15 App Router vs Next.js 14 Pages Router, custom JWT vs Cognito, and PCI-DSS scope for Stripe payments. The plan is intentionally flawed: ISS-001 and ISS-002 are resolved by replacing Next.js 14 with Next.js 15 and removing the custom JWT service, but ISS-003 remains unresolved because the PCI-DSS scope section still describes sending card details to Stripe from the backend, which would require SAQ D not SAQ A. This creates a security compliance gap that the auditor should flag.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all Next.js 14 Pages Router references with Next.js 15 App Router patterns throughout the plan. Updated technology_stack, frontend section, and api_design to reflect App Router routing and edge functions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan now correctly references Next.js 15 App Router matching the contract frontend_stack field.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed custom JWT service entirely; replaced with AWS Cognito user pools with patient and physician app clients. Updated security_and_compliance, auth_design, and data_model to reflect Cognito JWT verification in Express middleware.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan now correctly uses Cognito user pools for authentication matching the contract access_model field.

parsed.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Platform - Round 2 Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A full-stack telemedicine platform for urgent care virtual visits built with Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel and Node.js Express on ECS Fargate. Patients enter the virtual queue with a chief complaint, receive real-time wait time estimates via SSE, and launch WebRTC video sessions with on-call physicians via Twilio Video. Encounter notes are completed on the physician dashboard and payment copays collected via Stripe Payment Element. Cognito user pools handle patient and physician authentication with MFA enforced for physicians. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 stores PHI encounter data with KMS encryption.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Next.js 15 App Router frontend on Vercel communicates over HTTPS to an Express backend on ECS Fargate. The backend manages the virtual queue via Redis sorted sets, creates Twilio Video rooms server-side, and persists encounter notes and payment transactions to Aurora PostgreSQL 16. Cognito user pools issue JWTs for both patients and physicians; physician JWTs require MFA TOTP. Stripe.js Payment Element handles copay collection on the patient queue entry form; the backend receives only the PaymentIntent ID, never the card number, keeping PCI scope to Stripe's servers. WebSocket via Socket.io pushes real-time queue position updates to physician dashboard clients. Datadog APM sidecar on ECS Fargate provides distributed tracing and custom metrics for queue depth and video session success rate.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, App Router, Vercel Pro for edge functions, Twilio Video React SDK, Zustand for queue state, React Query for server state. Backend: Node.js 22 with TypeScript, Express, Socket.io for WebSocket, Stripe Node SDK for PaymentIntent management. Auth: AWS Cognito user pools with patient and physician app clients, @aws-amplify/auth for client-side JWT management. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK, Redis 7 on ElastiCache r6g.large for queue state. Video: Twilio Video SDK with TURN fallback via Twilio Network Traversal Service. Payments: Stripe.js Payment Element for copay collection, Stripe Node SDK for PaymentIntent management. Observability: Datadog APM sidecar, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: chief complaint form, insurance and payment info, Stripe Payment Element copay collection, real-time wait time estimate via SSE, confirmation with queue position. WebRTC Video Session: Twilio Video room auto-launch on physician availability, video stream with audio, screen sharing, chat bubbles for clinical notes. Encounter Note Completion: structured assessment, diagnosis ICD10 codes, plan, and prescription routing. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active encounter cards with video join, queue depth indicator, encounter note completion form, payment status. After-Visit Summary: automated email with encounter summary and prescription list. Payment Collection: Stripe Payment Element copay, Stripe webhook for payment success/failed.
parsed.system_components: Vercel (Next.js 15 App Router frontend, edge functions, HIPAA BAA). ECS Fargate (Express backend, 3-10 tasks autoscaling, 3 min task duration). ElastiCache Redis r6g.large (virtual_queue sorted set, queue position scores). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ (patients, virtual_queue_entries, video_sessions, encounter_notes, payment_transactions, encounter_access_log). Cognito user pools (patient and physician app clients, MFA TOTP enforced for physicians). Twilio Video (WebRTC room creation, access token generation, TURN fallback). Stripe (PaymentIntent creation, hosted card fields, webhook events). Datadog APM sidecar (ECS task metadata). CloudTrail (AWS API audit logging).
parsed.workflows: Patient Queue Entry: Patient submits chief complaint and payment copay via Stripe Payment Element on queue entry form. Backend creates Stripe PaymentIntent server-side, returns client_secret to PaymentElement. Patient enters copay amount, PaymentElement submits card details to Stripe.js, receives PaymentIntent confirmation. Backend verifies PaymentIntent status, creates virtual_queue_entries record with status=waiting, pushes patient position to Redis sorted set. SSE stream pushes wait_time_minutes to patient browser. On physician availability, backend creates Twilio Video room, generates access token, pushes video_room_sid to Redis for patient. Patient browser joins room via Twilio Video SDK. Encounter Note Completion: Physician joins video room, completes structured encounter note on dashboard form. On finalize, backend persists encounter_notes record to Aurora, updates video_sessions.status=in_session. After encounter ends, physician submits encounter as finalized. Backend generates after-visit summary email via SES, persists encounter_access_log record with physician_id, patient_id, encounter_id, and timestamp.
parsed.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted, insurance_member_id_encrypted, created_at). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint TEXT, estimated_wait_minutes INT, status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, physician_id FK, twilio_room_sid, started_at, ended_at, duration_seconds INT). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id FK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint_text, assessment, plan, diagnosis_codes ICD10[], status ENUM draft/finalized). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, amount_cents INT, currency CHAR(3), status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, created_at). encounter_access_log (id UUID PK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, encounter_id FK, accessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /auth/token (Cognito user pool token endpoint, returns JWT). GET /queue/positions (returns patient queue sorted by wait_minutes, physician role required). POST /queue/entries (create queue entry, patient role required). GET /queue/entries/:id (queue entry detail, patient role required). POST /video/rooms (create Twilio Video room, server-side, physician role required). GET /video/rooms/:id (room status, physician role required). POST /encounters (create encounter note, physician role required). PATCH /encounters/:id (finalize encounter, physician role required). GET /encounters/:id (encounter detail, patient or physician role required). POST /payments/intents (create Stripe PaymentIntent, server-side, patient role required). GET /payments/intents/:id (PaymentIntent status, patient role required). WebSocket: GET /ws/queue (Socket.io, physician role required, pushes queue depth and room join events).
parsed.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with patient and physician app clients; physician client enforces MFA TOTP via @aws-amplify/auth. Cognito JWT verified in Express middleware on all routes via jsonwebtoken. Role claim from Cognito JWT: patient routes filter to authenticated patient's own encounters; physician routes require physician role claim. TLS 1.3 enforced on all ECS Fargate task network interfaces. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 encrypted at rest via KMS CMK. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora, ECS, and Cognito. PCI-DSS SAQ A: Stripe.js PaymentElement handles card fields entirely client-side; backend receives only PaymentIntent ID, never card number. Encounter note content must not be logged in Express request logging middleware; use log scrubbing for /encounters routes. CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. encounter_access_log records all PHI read events for audit.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: eslint + tsc, jest unit tests, docker build and push to ECR, ECS deploy with rolling update (minimum 50% healthy before traffic shift). Vercel deploy triggered by Vercel GitHub integration. Environments: dev (ECS single task, Aurora single AZ), staging (ECS 2 tasks, Aurora multi-AZ), production (ECS 3-10 tasks autoscale on CPU 60%, Aurora multi-AZ with read replica). ECS Fargate task definition: 0.5 vCPU, 1 GB memory, 3 min task duration. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Cognito app client secrets, Stripe secret key, Twilio account SID and auth token. Database migrations via db-migrate run as ECS one-off task before deploy.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on ECS Fargate task metadata. Custom Datadog metrics: queue_depth (Redis sorted set size), video_session_success_rate (Twilio room join success rate), payment_processing_latency_p99 (time from PaymentIntent creation to confirmed), encounter_note_completion_time (time from video start to finalized). PagerDuty alerts: queue_depth >100 for 5 minutes, video_success_rate <95% for 10 minutes, payment_latency_p99 >30 seconds.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2800/month at 200 concurrent video sessions: Vercel Pro $300, ECS Fargate 3 tasks $180, Aurora multi-AZ db.r6g.large $600, ElastiCache r6g.large $180, Twilio Video $800, Stripe $200, Datadog $400, CloudTrail $50. Scaling: ECS autoscaling adds tasks on CPU 60% target; Twilio Video scales to 1000 concurrent rooms on Twilio Video plan.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito user pool setup with patient and physician clients, queue entry with chief complaint and copay via Stripe PaymentElement, Twilio Video room creation and access token generation, basic encounter note completion, Stripe payment webhook for copay confirmation, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, Datadog APM setup. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Physician encounter dashboard with active encounter cards and queue depth indicator, structured encounter note completion with ICD10 diagnosis codes, after-visit summary email via SES, encounter_access_log audit table. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Prescription routing to patient portal, payer claim submission API, scheduled telehealth appointment booking.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both Next.js and Express. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/recommended and custom rules for PHI field masking in log statements. Jest unit tests for queue position calculation, Twilio Video room creation, and Stripe PaymentIntent state machine. Integration tests via Playwright for patient queue entry flow and physician encounter dashboard. Feature flags via Vercel environment variables for new encounter note fields. ADR documents for Twilio Video vs GCP Live Stream and Stripe.js vs Stripe Node SDK.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Twilio Video room creation latency causes patient to wait longer than estimated. Mitigation: Twilio Video room creation is asynchronous; backend returns room SID immediately and patient joins via Twilio Video SDK; estimated wait time is from queue entry to room SID generation, not room join. Risk: Stripe PaymentIntent creation failure causes patient to lose queue position. Mitigation: Stripe PaymentIntent creation is idempotent; patient can re-enter copay amount and backend will create new PaymentIntent with same amount. Tradeoff: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ over RDS Comprehend for PHI processing; Comprehend adds latency and complexity for PHI queries. Tradeoff: Socket.io WebSocket over WebSockets for queue position updates; Socket.io provides server-side room management and broadcast to all physician dashboard clients.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Next.js 15 App Router over Next.js 14 Pages Router. Confirmed Cognito user pools over custom JWT service. Confirmed Stripe.js PaymentElement for copay collection to achieve PCI SAQ A. Confirmed Twilio Video over GCP Live Stream for TURN fallback and corporate network compatibility. Confirmed Aurora PostgreSQL 16 over RDS Comprehend for PHI processing.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision with three contract violations to fix: ISS-001 (Next.js 14 vs 15), ISS-002 (custom JWT vs Cognito), and ISS-003 (PCI scope). The plan quality is intentionally flawed - while I am addressing the focus issues in the fix_report, the revised plan will still contain a subtle internal inconsistency: the deployment_and_operations section will reference the Vercel frontend as requiring no HIPAA BAA while the contract security_baseline explicitly requires a Vercel Pro HIPAA BAA, creating a compliance gap that an auditor should flag.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all Next.js 14 Pages Router references with Next.js 15 App Router patterns throughout technology_stack, functional_feature_map, and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan technology stack matches contract frontend_stack specification enabling correct implementation.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed custom JWT issuance service; replaced with AWS Cognito user pools using patient and physician app clients with Cognito JWT verification in Express middleware via aws-jwt-verify library.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auth implementation matches contract access_model Cognito specification and eliminates custom key management overhead.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields so raw card numbers never reach the backend; backend only creates PaymentIntent and receives PaymentIntent ID from frontend, achieving PCI SAQ A scope.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PCI-DSS scope reduced to SAQ A; no card data handled in backend code or stored in Aurora.

reference.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Virtual Visit Platform Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Next.js 15 App Router and Node.js Express full-stack telemedicine platform on AWS ECS Fargate enabling patients to join on-demand urgent care video queues, pay copays via Stripe, and complete WebRTC video encounters with on-call physicians. Cognito user pools authenticate both patient and physician roles. Aurora PostgreSQL stores encounter notes and PHI with KMS encryption. Redis sorted sets manage the real-time virtual queue. Twilio Video handles WebRTC room creation and TURN server fallback.
reference.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 App Router deployed on Vercel communicates over HTTPS to an Express API on ECS Fargate. Patients enter the virtual queue via the Next.js patient flow; the backend maintains queue position in a Redis sorted set and pushes wait time updates via SSE. When a physician accepts the next patient, the backend creates a Twilio Video room and returns access tokens to both participants. Cognito user pools issue JWTs verified by aws-jwt-verify in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element collects copay card data client-side; the backend creates a PaymentIntent and receives only the ID. Aurora PostgreSQL with KMS CMK stores encounter notes, diagnoses, and patient PHI. Note: Vercel deployment does not have a HIPAA BAA configured, which creates a compliance gap for any PHI in Next.js server actions or edge functions.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS, Zustand for queue state, React Query, @twilio/video React hooks, Stripe.js Payment Element. Backend: Node.js 22, Express with TypeScript, aws-jwt-verify for Cognito JWT validation, Twilio Video Node SDK, Stripe Node SDK. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK, ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, Cognito user pools, CloudWatch Logs, Datadog APM sidecar.
reference.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: Next.js 15 App Router page with chief complaint multi-select, insurance lookup, and Stripe Payment Element for copay pre-authorization. Virtual Waiting Room: SSE-driven real-time wait time counter with queue position. WebRTC Video Session: Twilio Video room with camera/mic controls, participant tiles, and in-session chat for non-PHI messages. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active encounter cards showing patient chief complaint, queue depth badge, one-click video join. Encounter Note Completion: structured SOAP note form with ICD-10 autocomplete, plan text, and prescription routing dropdown. After-Visit Summary: automated generation on note finalization with patient email delivery.
reference.system_components: Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel Pro (HIPAA BAA status unclear in this revision). Express API on ECS Fargate (3 min, 10 max instances) behind ALB with WAF. Cognito user pools (patient and physician app clients, physician MFA TOTP enforced). ElastiCache Redis r6g.large for virtual queue sorted set and session tokens. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with KMS CMK. Twilio Video rooms for WebRTC. Stripe for copay payment processing with hosted card fields. Datadog APM sidecars on ECS tasks.
reference.workflows: Patient Queue Workflow: Patient completes chief complaint form, Stripe Payment Element pre-authorizes copay, POST /queue-entries creates queue entry in Redis sorted set with score=joined_at_unix; SSE stream /queue/wait-time pushes updated estimates every 30 seconds; physician accepts next patient triggering Twilio Video room creation via POST /video-sessions; Cognito access tokens exchanged for Twilio Video access tokens; video session auto-launches in browser. Encounter Completion Workflow: Physician completes SOAP note with ICD-10 codes and plan, POST /encounter-notes with finalized=true triggers after-visit summary generation, Stripe captures pre-authorized payment, note status updated to finalized.
reference.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted BYTEA, insurance_member_id_encrypted BYTEA, created_at). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint TEXT[], status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, payment_intent_id TEXT, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, physician_id FK, twilio_room_sid TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id FK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, subjective TEXT, objective TEXT, assessment TEXT, plan TEXT, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], status ENUM draft/finalized, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, amount_cents INT, status ENUM pending/captured/failed).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /queue-entries (patient joins queue; body: chief_complaint[], payment_intent_id). GET /queue/wait-time (SSE stream; physician_count, estimated_wait_minutes). POST /video-sessions (backend creates Twilio room; returns twilio_access_token for patient and physician). POST /encounter-notes (physician creates note). PATCH /encounter-notes/{id}/finalize (triggers after-visit summary and Stripe capture). GET /physician/queue (active queue for physician dashboard). All routes require Cognito JWT in Authorization header.
reference.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with physician app client enforcing MFA TOTP; patient app client with optional MFA. aws-jwt-verify library validates Cognito JWTs in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields: backend creates PaymentIntent via Stripe API, returns client_secret to frontend, Stripe.js confirms payment client-side - raw card numbers never reach Express API, achieving PCI SAQ A. Aurora KMS CMK encrypts PHI at rest. TLS 1.3 on ALB. WAF rules blocking SQLi and XSS on ALB. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora and ECS. Note: Vercel HIPAA BAA not confirmed for this deployment - Next.js server actions or middleware touching PHI would fall outside HIPAA BAA coverage.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc + jest, docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy to staging, integration smoke tests, manual gate, ECS rolling deploy to production. Vercel deploys triggered by GitHub push via Vercel GitHub integration with no HIPAA configuration applied. ECS Fargate autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget >500 per task. Aurora multi-AZ with automated backups. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single node r6g.large) for simplicity. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day Stripe webhook secret rotation.
reference.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task with distributed traces. Custom metrics: virtual_queue_depth (alert >100), video_session_success_rate (alert <95%), payment_capture_success_rate (alert <99%), encounter_note_completion_time_p90. CloudWatch Logs for ECS stdout. PagerDuty escalation for all production alerts.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 3 min tasks at $0.04048/vCPU/hr: ~$200/month baseline. Aurora db.r6g.large multi-AZ: ~$400/month. ElastiCache r6g.large: ~$150/month. Twilio Video: ~$400/month at 500 daily sessions (0.004/min * 10 min avg * 500). Stripe fees pass-through to encounter billing. Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. Total ~$1300/month well under $3000 budget.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Patient queue entry with chief complaint and Stripe copay pre-auth, physician dashboard with queue view, Twilio Video session launch, basic SOAP note, Cognito auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): ICD-10 autocomplete, after-visit summary email, Stripe capture on note finalization, encounter history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Prescription routing integration, payer claim submission, physician performance analytics dashboard.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on frontend and backend. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any enforced in CI. Encounter note content must not appear in Express request logs; use log scrubbing middleware on /encounter-notes routes. Stripe webhook signature verification required in payment handler. Cognito JWT claims must be the sole source of role authorization; no role stored in DB. PHI fields in Aurora must use encrypted column types.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vercel deployment without HIPAA BAA exposes PHI if Next.js server actions or edge middleware process encounter data. Mitigation incomplete in this revision - PHI access in Next.js server components must be audited. Risk: Twilio Video TURN server dependency for patients on restrictive networks; mitigated by Twilio Network Traversal Service automatic fallback. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for WebSocket connection persistence; Lambda cold starts incompatible with sub-second queue update requirements.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Twilio Video over Amazon Chime per existing Twilio vendor relationship. Confirmed Stripe SAQ A eligibility via Stripe.js hosted fields - backend never handles raw card data. Auth provider corrected to Cognito per contract. Next.js version corrected to 15 App Router per contract.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision of the distribution grid switching order management platform. Two remaining medium-severity issues from round 2: step lock timeout specification and CIM import idempotency. Adding explicit 10-minute Redis TTL with PagerDuty alert on auto-release for step locks, and ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE with import_batch_id rollback for CIM topology_nodes. The plan is strong overall with good topology_rule_violations materialized view and Istio mTLS from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit 10-minute Redis TTL for step locks via SET NX EX 600; StepLockManager auto-releases lock and sends PagerDuty P1 alert to on-call dispatcher via PagerDuty Events API when lock expires; alert includes order_id, step_id, and crew_id for rapid resolution
fix_report.expected_outcome: Field crew step execution locks automatically release after 10 minutes with PagerDuty alert; dispatcher can manually release via API if crew is delayed

fix_report.action_taken: Added ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE to CIM topology_nodes insert; import_batch_id assigned to each import transaction; DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id reverts bad import; validation_report captures element IDs causing parse errors
fix_report.expected_outcome: Re-import of corrected CIM XML safely upserts without duplicate topology_node insertions; bad imports are fully reversible

parsed.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform
parsed.executive_summary: A Vue 3 + FastAPI web application for medium-voltage distribution grid operators, deployed on GKE Autopilot with Istio mTLS, Keycloak OIDC, and real-time SVG single-line topology visualization. Dispatchers create and approve switching orders with pre-validation against topology_rule_violations; field crews execute step-by-step isolation sequences with 10-minute auto-releasing locks; CIM IEC 61968/61970 network models are imported with idempotent ON CONFLICT upsert. All topology state changes broadcast via Redis pub/sub to connected control room display clients.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three FastAPI services on GKE Autopilot with Istio service mesh in mTLS STRICT mode: topology-api handles CIM import, topology state, and WebSocket broadcast; switching-order-api manages switching order state machine, step_lock acquisition, and topology_rule validation; integration-api receives GIS/SCADA data. Vue 3 SPA communicates over HTTPS to Cloud Load Balancing with IAP enforcing VPN-only access. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA stores switching orders, topology nodes, and audit logs. Redis 7 Memorystore handles topology pub/sub and step_lock atomic lock acquisition. Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation provides 15-minute JWTs. All three services use Istio sidecar injection with mTLS for encrypted internal service-to-service traffic.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, keycloak-js, D3.js for SVG single-line diagram, Vitest for component tests. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, WebSocket via asyncio, pandapower for CIM XML parsing, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg, Redis 7 via aioredis. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA. Cache: Redis 7 Memorystore. Auth: Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation. Service mesh: Istio with mTLS STRICT. CI/CD: GitLab CI, Helm, ArgoCD. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, Google Cloud Logging, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Switching Order Creation: dispatcher creates order with title, region, outage_ticket_id, and step list; Topology Rule Engine validates sequence against network_segments connectivity graph before approval. Step Execution: field crew member acquires 10-minute lock on isolation_point, confirms action (open/close/tag/remove_tag), uploads photo evidence, marks step completed; StepLockManager auto-releases after 10 minutes with PagerDuty alert. Real-Time Topology: SVG single-line diagram updates via WebSocket broadcast on device_state_changed events from SCADA integration. CIM Import: dispatcher uploads IEC 61968/61970 CIM XML; ON CONFLICT upserts topology_nodes; validation_report captures parse errors. Audit Log Viewer: all topology-modifying actions and switching order events captured in CIP-002-compliant audit_logs table.
parsed.system_components: Cloud Load Balancing with IAP (VPN-only enforcement), GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1), Istio service mesh (mTLS STRICT), topology-api (2-6 pods, HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api (2-4 pods), integration-api (1-2 pods), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (3-node), Redis 7 Memorystore 6 GB, Keycloak OIDC (GKE deployment), Google Cloud Logging, Grafana + Prometheus, PagerDuty Events API.
parsed.workflows: Switching Order Execution: Dispatcher creates order with step list; TopologyRuleEngine validates sequence against network_segments connectivity graph; if violations detected, order status set to pending_approval with violation list; dispatcher reviews and approves; field crew member opens step execution form, acquires step_lock via Redis SET NX EX 600 (10-minute TTL), confirms action and uploads photo evidence, marks step completed; StepLockManager auto-releases lock after 10 minutes with PagerDuty P1 alert to on-call dispatcher. CIM Import: Dispatcher uploads IEC 61968/61970 CIM XML file; POST /topology/cim-import creates import_batch_id transaction; pandapower parses CIM network model; ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE upserts topology_nodes and network_segments; validation_report captures any parse errors; if errors detected, import_batch_id assigned to failed rows; dispatcher reviews validation_report before accepting import.
parsed.data_model: switching_orders (id UUID PK, title, region_code, outage_ticket_id UUID FK, approved_by keycloak_sub, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled, planned_start, planned_end, created_at). switching_steps (id UUID PK, order_id UUID FK, step_number INT, isolation_point_id UUID FK, action ENUM open/close/tag/remove_tag, assigned_crew_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending/locked/completed/failed, completed_by, completed_at, photo_evidence_url). isolation_points (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, network_segment_id UUID FK, device_type ENUM breaker/recloser/switch/fuse, current_state ENUM open/closed/unknown, last_state_changed_at). network_segments (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, voltage_kv DECIMAL, upstream_node_id UUID FK, downstream_nodes JSONB, segment_type ENUM feeder/lateral/tie). topology_nodes (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, substation_id, bus_voltage_kv, connected_segments JSONB, import_batch_id UUID). outage_tickets (id UUID PK, order_id UUID FK, affected_customer_count INT, cause, status ENUM open/in_progress/restored/closed, opened_at, closed_at). crew_assignments (id UUID PK, switching_order_id UUID FK, crew_leader_id keycloak_sub, crew_members JSONB, assigned_steps JSONB). audit_logs (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub, display_name, action, resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp). topology_rule_violations (id UUID PK, order_id UUID FK, violation_type, violating_segments JSONB, resolved_at) - materialized view refreshed after each CIM import.
parsed.api_design: REST: POST /switching-orders (create order), GET /switching-orders/:id (detail), PATCH /switching-orders/:id/approve (dispatcher approval), POST /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute (field crew step execution), GET /topology/network-model (CIM XML export), POST /topology/cim-import (multipart CIM XML, idempotent on cim_mrid), GET /audit-logs?order_id= (CIP-002 audit trail). WebSocket: ws://topology-api/ws/topology-updates (server-sent device_state_changed events). Auth: Keycloak OIDC JWT validated on all endpoints; field_crew role required for step execution; dispatcher role for approval and order creation.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation; 15-minute JWT with silent refresh via hidden iframe. Istio mTLS STRICT on all three services. VPN-only enforcement via Cloud Load Balancing IAP. Row-level security in PostgreSQL: field_crew can only execute steps assigned to their crew_assignment_id. Audit log captures keycloak_sub, display_name, action, resource_type, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, and timestamp; shipped to Google Cloud Storage immutable bucket for 10-year NERC CIP retention. NERC CIP-002 BES Cyber System identification: all switching orders and topology state changes logged with operator identity. IEC 61968/61970 CIM import validated against cim_mrid uniqueness before write.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1) with Istio sidecar injection. Three deployments: topology-api (2-6 pods, HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api (2-4 pods), integration-api (1-2 pods). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA with automatic failover. Memorystore Redis 6 GB. Cloud Load Balancing with IAP. GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest (90% coverage gate), Vitest (85% gate), docker buildx, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart update in GitOps repo, ArgoCD sync. Staging auto-deploy on merge to main; production requires control room supervisor approval in GitLab. ArgoCD automated rollback on failed readiness probe. PostgreSQL PITR to 1-minute granularity. CIM import rollback: DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id.
parsed.observability: Prometheus: switching_orders_created_total, step_locks_acquired_total, step_lock_timeout_total (PagerDuty P1 alert when >0), step_locks_released_total, topology_rule_violations_blocked_total, websocket_connections_active, cim_import_errors_total. Grafana dashboard: switching order pipeline state (draft/approved/in_execution/completed), active step locks by crew, topology change rate per minute. Google Cloud Logging: structured JSON audit trail with keycloak_sub and display_name. PagerDuty P1 alerts: step lock timeout, CIM import error rate >5%, Cloud SQL connection pool >80%.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales three services on connection count and pod CPU. Cloud SQL HA (3-node) ~$400/month. Memorystore Redis 6 GB ~$150/month. Cloud Load Balancing ~$50/month. Estimated total ~$700/month for 10 dispatcher workstations. Scaling: HPA scales topology-api pods on WebSocket connection count; switching-order-api scales on order creation rate.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Switching order creation with step authoring, dispatcher approval workflow, field crew step execution with 10-minute auto-releasing locks and PagerDuty alert, single-line SVG topology diagram with device state color coding, Keycloak OIDC with VPN enforcement, CIM import with ON CONFLICT upsert and validation_report, CIP-002 audit log. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Outage ticket management, crew assignment and step assignment, topology_rule_violations materialized view, mobile-friendly step execution form, audit log viewer. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Mobile app for field crew step execution, predictive outage scheduling via ML, SCADA direct device control integration.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest with 90% coverage gate, Vitest for WebSocket composable unit tests. Vue: ESLint + tsc, Vitest for Pinia store and WebSocket composable, Playwright E2E for step execution and CIM import flows. All FastAPI endpoints require unit tests with mocked Redis and PostgreSQL. StepLockManager unit tests must cover: lock acquisition, auto-release on timeout, manual release via API, concurrent lock attempt. CIM import pipeline must have unit tests for ON CONFLICT upsert and import_batch_id rollback. ADR required for any change to switching order state machine or topology_rule_violations logic.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Field crew step execution lock timeout causes auto-release without crew completion - dispatcher must manually re-lock. Mitigation: StepLockManager sends PagerDuty P1 alert to on-call dispatcher; dispatcher can manually re-lock via API. Risk: CIM import ON CONFLICT upsert silently fails on duplicate cim_mrid if GIS export contains duplicate device IDs. Mitigation: validation_report captures parse errors; dispatcher reviews before accepting import. Tradeoff: 10-minute step lock TTL is conservative for field crew operations but prevents indefinite lock holding; acceptable given typical 30-minute switching time. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL materialized view for topology_rule_violations refreshed after each CIM import adds latency but is acceptable for control room use case where topology is stable between imports.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected D3.js force layout for SVG single-line diagram over custom canvas rendering for maintainability. Confirmed 10-minute step lock TTL based on field crew switching time estimates. Chose ON CONFLICT upsert over application-level idempotency for CIM import to avoid duplicate topology_node insertions on re-import. Confirmed Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation over internal DB for user management to leverage existing utility LDAP infrastructure.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002 (step lock timeout) and ISS-003 (CIM import idempotency). Adding explicit 10-minute Redis TTL with SET NX EX 600 for step_lock atomic acquisition and PagerDuty alert on auto-release. Adding ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE to CIM import pipeline with import_batch_id for rollback. ISS-001 (Istio mTLS) was resolved in round 2 and carried forward. All three issues now resolved - plan should converge in this round.

fix_report.action_taken: StepLockManager now uses Redis SET NX EX 600 (10-minute TTL) for atomic lock acquisition. Added lock_timeout Prometheus counter. PagerDuty alert fires when step_lock_timeout_total increments, notifying dispatcher that a field crew step lock expired and the step is unlocked. switching_steps.status transitions back to pending on auto-release.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Step locks automatically release after 10 minutes with dispatcher notification, preventing permanently locked isolation points from blocking switching order completion

fix_report.action_taken: CIM XML import pipeline now uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE SET ... for topology_nodes and isolation_points, making re-imports safe and idempotent. Each import assigned a UUID import_batch_id. Added DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id rollback endpoint that removes all topology_nodes and isolation_points with matching import_batch_id using a database transaction. Added import idempotency_key validation to reject duplicate concurrent imports.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Corrected CIM XML files can be safely re-imported without duplicating topology nodes; bad imports can be atomically rolled back by batch_id

reference.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform v3
reference.executive_summary: Vue 3 + FastAPI web application for control room dispatchers and field crews to manage switching orders on medium-voltage distribution networks. Deployed on GKE Autopilot with Istio mTLS service mesh and Keycloak OIDC authentication. Features real-time SVG single-line diagram with WebSocket topology updates, switching order approval workflow with step-level field crew execution tracking, and TopologyRuleEngine pre-validation. Round 3 resolves all three tracked issues: Istio mTLS (ISS-001 round 2), step lock 10-minute timeout (ISS-002), and CIM import idempotency (ISS-003).
reference.architecture_overview: Three FastAPI services on GKE Autopilot with Istio sidecar mTLS STRICT: topology-api (CIM import, device state, WebSocket broadcast to Vue clients via Redis pub/sub), switching-order-api (order CRUD, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with 10-minute Redis TTL, TopologyRuleEngine), and integration-api (GIS/SCADA ingest). Vue 3 SPA connects to topology-api WebSocket for live single-line diagram updates, patching Pinia topology store on device_state_changed events. Keycloak OIDC with 15-minute JWT and silent refresh governs all route access. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA persists switching orders, topology, and audit trail. Redis Memorystore backs step locks (SET NX EX 600) and topology pub/sub channel.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, Vue Query, keycloak-js, D3.js v7 (SVG single-line diagram rendering), Vitest. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, alembic, pandapower (CIM XML parsing), redis-py, websockets. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Istio 1.21 (mTLS STRICT), Keycloak 24, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, Memorystore Redis 6 GB, Cloud Load Balancing with IAP. CI/CD: GitLab CI, ArgoCD, Helm, Artifact Registry. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Google Cloud Logging, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Single-Line Diagram Viewer: SVG topology rendering with isolation_points color-coded by device state (closed/open/unknown) and network_segments highlighted during active switching orders. Switching Order Lifecycle: dispatcher creates order with step sequence, TopologyRuleEngine validates sequence against connectivity graph and returns topology_rule_violations list, dispatcher approves, field crews execute step-by-step with StepLockManager preventing concurrent operations on the same isolation_point. Step Execution: field crew acquires lock via PATCH /steps/:id/execute, completes action, uploads optional photo evidence, marks complete. CIM Network Model Import: POST multipart CIM XML triggers pandapower parse, idempotent upsert on cim_mrid, import_batch_id assigned for rollback capability. Audit Trail: all topology-modifying actions written to audit_logs with keycloak_sub, display_name, changed_fields JSONB.
reference.system_components: topology-api pod (FastAPI, pandapower CIM parser, WebSocket manager broadcasting to Redis topology_updates channel, 2-6 pods HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api pod (FastAPI, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with Redis SET NX EX 600, TopologyRuleEngine with materialized view query, 2-4 pods), integration-api pod (FastAPI, SCADA/GIS ingest handlers, 1-2 pods), Istio sidecar proxies (mTLS STRICT between all pods), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL HA (switching_orders, switching_steps, isolation_points, network_segments, topology_nodes, outage_tickets, crew_assignments, audit_logs), Redis Memorystore 6 GB (step_lock entries with 600s TTL, topology_updates pub/sub), Keycloak 24 (LDAP-federated, GKE Deployment), Cloud Load Balancing with IAP (VPN enforcement).
reference.workflows: Switching Order Creation and Approval: dispatcher creates switching order with title, region_code, planned window, and ordered list of steps (each specifying isolation_point_id and action open/close/tag). switching-order-api calls TopologyRuleEngine which queries topology_rule_violations materialized view to verify the step sequence does not create an unintended island or violate N-1 connectivity. If violations exist, order returns to draft with violation list. Dispatcher resolves and resubmits. On clean validation, order status transitions to pending_approval; second dispatcher approves (PATCH .../approve), status transitions to approved. Step Execution with Lock Management: field crew opens Step Execution Form for their assigned step. Frontend POSTs PATCH /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute. StepLockManager calls Redis SET NX EX 600 keyed by isolation_point_id. If lock acquired, step.status transitions to locked; crew performs physical switching action, confirms in UI, optionally uploads photo. PATCH .../complete transitions step.status to completed, Redis lock deleted. If crew does not complete within 10 minutes, Redis TTL expires, step.status reverts to pending, Prometheus step_lock_timeout_total increments, PagerDuty alert fires to dispatcher. CIM XML Import: operator uploads CIM XML via POST /topology/cim-import. pandapower parses CIM elements. PostgreSQL transaction executes INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE for topology_nodes and isolation_points. import_batch_id UUID assigned to all inserted/updated records. On parse failure, transaction rolls back atomically. DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id removes all records with matching import_batch_id.
reference.data_model: switching_orders (id UUID PK, title, region_code, outage_ticket_id FK, created_by keycloak_sub, approved_by keycloak_sub, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled, planned_start TIMESTAMPTZ, planned_end TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at). switching_steps (id UUID PK, order_id FK, step_number INT, isolation_point_id FK, action ENUM open/close/tag/remove_tag, assigned_crew_id FK, status ENUM pending/locked/completed/failed, completed_by keycloak_sub, completed_at, photo_evidence_url). isolation_points (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, network_segment_id FK, device_type ENUM breaker/recloser/switch/fuse, current_state ENUM open/closed/unknown, last_state_changed_at, import_batch_id UUID). network_segments (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, name, voltage_kv DECIMAL, upstream_node_id FK, downstream_nodes JSONB, segment_type ENUM feeder/lateral/tie, import_batch_id UUID). topology_nodes (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, substation_id, bus_voltage_kv DECIMAL, connected_segments JSONB, import_batch_id UUID). outage_tickets (id UUID PK, order_id FK, affected_customer_count INT, cause, status ENUM open/in_progress/restored/closed, opened_at, closed_at). crew_assignments (id UUID PK, switching_order_id FK, crew_leader keycloak_sub, crew_members JSONB, assigned_step_ids JSONB). audit_logs (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, action ENUM, resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: POST /api/v1/switching-orders - create with steps array, triggers TopologyRuleEngine validation. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/approve - dispatcher role required, transitions to approved. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute - field-crew role, acquires Redis step lock. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/complete - releases lock, sets completed_by. GET /api/v1/topology/network-model - returns current topology graph. POST /api/v1/topology/cim-import - multipart CIM XML, returns {import_batch_id, import_summary: {nodes_upserted, isolation_points_upserted, violations}}. DELETE /api/v1/topology/cim-import/:batch_id - rollback import batch. WebSocket ws:/topology-api/ws/topology-updates - broadcasts {event: device_state_changed, isolation_point_id, new_state, changed_at}. All routes require Keycloak JWT; role-specific routes enforce Keycloak realm role claims via FastAPI dependency.
reference.security_and_compliance: NERC CIP-002 BES asset identification: all switching order actions and topology modifications recorded in audit_logs with keycloak_sub, display_name, and changed_fields JSONB. Shipped to Google Cloud Storage immutable bucket for 10-year retention. Istio mTLS STRICT on all three service pods prevents lateral movement within GKE cluster. Keycloak OIDC 15-minute JWT with silent refresh; httpOnly cookie refresh token with Secure and SameSite=Strict. GKE Network Policy denies ingress from CIDR ranges outside utility VPN allocation. IAP enforces authentication before Cloud Load Balancer allows connection. PostgreSQL row-level security: field_crew role can only read/write switching_steps assigned to their crew_assignment_id. IEC 61968/61970 CIM interoperability satisfied by pandapower CIM XML parser consuming standard CIM profiles.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1 with Istio 1.21 installed via GKE managed Istio add-on. PeerAuthentication resource enforces mTLS STRICT across the grid-ops namespace. Three Deployments with HPA: topology-api (2-6 pods on WebSocket connection count > 100 per pod), switching-order-api (2-4 pods on CPU 70%), integration-api (1-2 pods). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (2 vCPUs, 8 GB) with PITR 1-minute granularity. Memorystore Redis 6 GB. GitLab CI pipeline: ruff, mypy strict, pytest 90% gate, Vue ESLint + tsc, Vitest 85% gate, docker buildx multi-arch, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart version bump, ArgoCD auto-sync to staging. Production deployment requires control room supervisor GitLab approval and scheduled maintenance window. ArgoCD automated rollback on failed readiness probe.
reference.observability: Prometheus metrics: switching_orders_created_total, switching_orders_completed_total, step_locks_acquired_total, step_lock_timeout_total (PagerDuty alert on increment), topology_rule_violations_blocked_total, cim_imports_total (labeled by success/failure), websocket_connections_active (gauge). Grafana dashboards: Switching Order Pipeline (orders by status, avg time-to-complete per phase), Step Lock Health (active locks, timeout rate), Topology Change Rate. Google Cloud Logging: structured JSON with switching_order_id and step_id correlation fields. PagerDuty P2 on step_lock_timeout (crew unresponsive), P1 on switching-order-api pod crash during in_execution order.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ,800/month for control room pilot (10 dispatchers, 50 field crew): GKE Autopilot ~, Cloud SQL HA ~, Memorystore Redis ~, Cloud Load Balancing ~, Istio/Keycloak overhead ~, Artifact Registry/GitLab ~, Google Cloud Logging ~, PagerDuty ~. Scale to 200 concurrent users: GKE autoscaling handles without configuration change; Cloud SQL scale to 4 vCPUs at ~,200.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (8 weeks): Keycloak OIDC + LDAP federation, switching order CRUD with TopologyRuleEngine validation, step execution with StepLockManager 10-minute TTL, basic SVG single-line diagram, CIM XML import with idempotency, audit log, GKE deployment with Istio mTLS, GitLab CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): real-time WebSocket topology updates via Redis pub/sub, photo evidence upload to Cloud Storage, outage ticket management, crew assignment module, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 (4 weeks): CIM import batch rollback endpoint, SCADA device state integration via integration-api, advanced topology rule validation (N-1 analysis), Prometheus SLO alerting, NERC CIP-002 audit report export.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff and mypy strict enforced in CI. SQLAlchemy 2.0 async throughout; no raw SQL except topology_rule_violations materialized view definition. All switching step state transitions go through SwitchingOrderStateMachine - no direct status field updates in route handlers. Vue 3 Composition API with explicit TypeScript types for all Pinia store state. Step lock logic has 100% pytest coverage with mocked Redis including TTL expiry simulation. CIM parser tests use real IEC 61968 CIM XML sample fixtures, not synthetic test data.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GKE Autopilot pod eviction during active switching order with locked steps. Mitigation: switching-order-api PodDisruptionBudget ensures minimum 1 pod available during cluster maintenance; Redis TTL auto-release handles lock cleanup on pod restart. Risk: TopologyRuleEngine materialized view refresh blocks CIM import transaction during high-frequency imports. Mitigation: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY used to avoid table lock. Risk: WebSocket connection loss drops real-time topology updates without client notification. Mitigation: client-side reconnection with exponential backoff, server sends full topology snapshot on reconnect. Tradeoff: pandapower for CIM parsing adds 80 MB to container image - accepted for rich CIM profile support over writing custom parser. Risk: Cloud SQL PITR restore to 1-minute granularity could lose up to 60 seconds of switching order state. Mitigation: acceptable for pilot; production version should add synchronous replication with 0-RPO for safety-critical switching data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Chose D3.js SVG rendering over Leaflet/GeoJSON for single-line diagram - SVG schematic is the correct representation for distribution network topology, not geographic map. Confirmed pandapower for CIM XML parsing over custom lxml-based parser - pandapower handles complex CIM profile variations in utility GIS exports. Memorystore Redis chosen over Pub/Sub for topology broadcast due to sub-millisecond pub/sub latency requirement for real-time diagram updates. step_lock_timeout set to 10 minutes based on field crew task analysis showing 95th-percentile manual switching time is 7 minutes.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues: warehouse capacity validation must be server-side before any Pub/Sub publish, and the promotion approval endpoint must be restricted to category_manager role only. The SSE reconnect replay fix (ISS-003) is also addressed by adding a numeric event_id field to all SSE events and storing the last N events per supplier in Redis. The plan quality is weak — several sections are thin and the cost section is absent.

fix_report.action_taken: Added WarehouseCapacityService integration to InventoryService.submitReplenishment(): service calls internal WarehouseCapacityService.checkCapacity(productId, quantity) synchronously before writing to inventory_replenishments and publishing to Pub/Sub. If capacity check fails, returns HTTP 422 with available_capacity field. Updated API doc to reflect synchronous capacity validation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Over-submissions prevented at API layer; downstream warehouse system rejections reduced to zero; supplier receives clear capacity error response

fix_report.action_taken: Updated PromotionApprovalService @PreAuthorize annotation to hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') only — removed OR condition on PLATFORM_ADMIN. Added unit test asserting that supplier_admin and platform_admin roles cannot approve promotions. Updated API doc to reflect single-role requirement.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Promotion self-approval via dual-role bypass eliminated; marketplace pricing governance policy satisfied

parsed.title: Grocery Delivery Vendor Portal — Round 2 Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A modern Next.js 15 + Spring Boot 3 web application on GKE Autopilot replacing a legacy JSP/Struts vendor portal for food suppliers. This revision adds server-side warehouse capacity validation in the inventory replenishment API, removes the promotion approval self-approval risk by restricting approval to category_manager role only, and adds numeric event_id fields to SSE alerts for Last-Event-ID replay on reconnect.
parsed.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster in europe-west1 with three Deployments: portal-api (Spring Boot 3, 2-10 pods HPA on CPU 70%), portal-frontend (Next.js 15, 2-6 pods), pubsub-consumer (fulfillment event consumer, 1-3 pods). GCP Load Balancer + Cloud Armor WAF in front of portal-api. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with read replica for analytics. Memorystore Redis for sessions and SSE event buffer. Keycloak OIDC realm per environment. Pub/Sub for internal marketplace event bus. WarehouseCapacityService is a separate GCP Cloud Run service called synchronously from InventoryService before any Pub/Sub publish.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, EventSource for SSE. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Data JPA, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, Spring Cloud GCP Pub/Sub, Spring @Aspect for audit logging. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with read replica. Cache: Memorystore Redis 7. Auth: Keycloak OIDC. CI/CD: GitLab CI with LaunchDarkly feature flags. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, GCP Cloud Logging.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Inventory Management: suppliers submit replenishment quantities per SKU; backend validates against WarehouseCapacityService, writes to inventory_replenishments, publishes to Pub/Sub. Promotion Scheduler: multi-step wizard for product selection, discount type, effective dates, supporting materials upload; category_manager approves or rejects with comment; audit trail recorded. Fulfillment Feed: real-time SSE stream of order event updates (shipped, delayed, partial_delivered, delivered). Food Safety Compliance: upload and track HACCP, BRC, or SQF certifications with expiry tracking. Vendor Performance Scorecard: fill rate, on-time delivery, return rate, promotion compliance rate computed monthly.
parsed.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster (portal-api, portal-frontend, pubsub-consumer pods). GCP Load Balancer + Cloud Armor WAF. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (primary + read replica). Memorystore Redis 7. WarehouseCapacityService (Cloud Run, synchronous capacity check). Pub/Sub (inventory-updated and order-events topics). Keycloak OIDC (per-environment realms). LaunchDarkly (feature flag service). GCP Cloud Logging. Grafana + Prometheus.
parsed.workflows: Inventory Replenishment: Supplier submits POST /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/replenish with product_id and quantity. Spring Security extracts supplier_account_id from JWT. InventoryService calls WarehouseCapacityService.checkCapacity(productId, quantity) synchronously — if capacity check fails, returns HTTP 422 with available_capacity field. On success, writes inventory_replenishments record and publishes to Pub/Sub inventory-updated topic with message_id. Returns 200 with confirmation. Promotion Approval: Supplier submits POST /api/v1/promotions with product selection and discount details. PromotionApprovalService transitions state from DRAFT to PENDING_APPROVAL. Category manager views pending queue, selects promotion, submits POST /api/v1/promotions/{id}/approve with required comment. @PreAuthorize enforces hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') — supplier_admin and platform_admin roles cannot approve. AuditAspect records state transition in audit_events. On approval, transitions to APPROVED and activates promotion for effective_start date.
parsed.data_model: supplier_accounts (id UUID PK, company_name, keycloak_group_id, tier ENUM bronze/silver/gold, created_at, is_active BOOL). product_catalogue (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, sku VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, category VARCHAR, barcode VARCHAR, current_price DECIMAL(10,2), is_active BOOL). inventory_levels (product_id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, quantity_on_hand INT, quantity_incoming INT, reorder_threshold INT, last_updated TIMESTAMP). inventory_replenishments (id UUID PK, product_id FK, supplier_account_id FK, quantity_submitted INT, warehouse_capacity_at_submission INT, status ENUM submitted/capacity_rejected/confirmed, pub_sub_message_id VARCHAR, submitted_at). promotion_schedules (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, product_ids UUID[], discount_type ENUM percentage/fixed_price/bogo, discount_value DECIMAL, effective_start DATE, effective_end DATE, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/rejected/active/cancelled, submitted_at, reviewed_by FK NULLABLE, review_comment TEXT). purchase_orders (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, marketplace_order_id VARCHAR, total_units INT, status ENUM created/confirmed/shipped/delivered/cancelled, created_at). fulfillment_events (id UUID PK, purchase_order_id FK, supplier_account_id FK, event_type ENUM shipped/delayed/partial_delivered/delivered, pub_sub_message_id, event_at TIMESTAMP). compliance_certifications (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, cert_type VARCHAR, issuing_body VARCHAR, cert_number VARCHAR, issued_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, document_s3_key VARCHAR, status ENUM active/expiring_soon/expired). vendor_performance_scores (supplier_account_id FK, period_month DATE, fill_rate FLOAT, on_time_delivery_rate FLOAT, return_rate FLOAT, promotion_compliance_rate FLOAT, composite_score FLOAT, computed_at — PRIMARY KEY supplier_account_id, period_month).
parsed.api_design: POST /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/replenish — body: {product_id, quantity}; returns 200 with {id, quantity_submitted, warehouse_capacity_at_submission, status: confirmed} or 422 with {error: 'capacity_exceeded', available_capacity}. GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/replenishments?status=submitted — paginated replenishment history. POST /api/v1/promotions — body: {product_ids, discount_type, discount_value, effective_start, effective_end, supporting_materials[]}; returns 201 with {id, status: pending_approval}. GET /api/v1/promotions?status=pending_approval — category_manager queue. POST /api/v1/promotions/{id}/approve — body: {review_comment}; @PreAuthorize: hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER'); returns 200 with {id, status: approved}. GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/alerts — SSE stream of low-stock and capacity breach events; client sends Last-Event-ID in EventSource headers. GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/fulfillment-events — paginated order event history. GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/performance?period_month=2026-03-01 — vendor scorecard.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC JWT validated by Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server; supplier_account_id extracted from custom claim. All service layer queries include WHERE supplier_account_id = :supplierFromJwt — never accept supplier_account_id from request body. PromotionApprovalService @PreAuthorize: hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') — supplier_admin and platform_admin roles cannot approve promotions. AuditAspect: @Aspect intercepts all service write methods; records actor_id, supplier_account_id, action, before_state JSONB, after_state JSONB, created_at in append-only audit_events table. PostgreSQL trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events. Cloud Armor WAF blocks OWASP top 10 patterns. TLS 1.3 enforced by GCP Load Balancer. Food safety certification documents stored in GCP Cloud Storage with private access.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: spotbugs + checkstyle for Java, ESLint for Next.js, JUnit 5 tests (85% coverage gate), Docker build + Artifact Registry push, GKE rolling deploy via kubectl. Feature flag service (LaunchDarkly) for per-supplier migration toggle during 6-month parallel run. GKE Autopilot handles pod scaling; HPA on portal-api targets CPU 70% with min 2 max 10 pods. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL automated backups with 7-day retention. Cloud Run for WarehouseCapacityService with 1 min timeout. Smoke test suite run against staging after each deploy.
parsed.observability: Prometheus metrics exposed on /actuator/prometheus by Spring Boot. Grafana dashboards: inventory_submission_latency_p95, pub_sub_publish_success_rate, promotion_approval_cycle_time_avg, sse_reconnect_rate. GCP Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs with supplier_account_id as indexed field. Alerting: pub_sub_publish_latency > 400ms (warn), > 500ms (critical PagerDuty), inventory_submission_capacity_rejection_rate > 5% (warn), promotion_approval_cycle_time > 24h (warn).
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero outside business hours. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL read replica for analytics queries reduces compute cost. Memorystore Redis auto-scales to 0.5 GB. WarehouseCapacityService on Cloud Run scales to 0 outside replenishment windows. Estimated cost: ~$800/month for 50 suppliers at moderate replenishment frequency.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Portal (6 weeks): Inventory replenishment with server-side capacity validation, promotion scheduling, category manager approval workflow, SSE alert feed, Keycloak OIDC, GitLab CI. Phase 2 — Migration and Analytics (4 weeks): LaunchDarkly feature flag migration toggle, parallel run with legacy panel, vendor performance scorecard, food safety certification upload. Phase 3 — Decommission (2 weeks): Legacy JSP/Struts system decommissioned after 6-month parallel run, full supplier migration, promotion audit trail analytics.
parsed.development_guidelines: Java: spotbugs + checkstyle enforced in CI. 85% JUnit 5 test coverage gate. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations must be reviewed by security lead before merge. All SSE event handlers must include numeric event_id field. WarehouseCapacityService must be mocked in unit tests — never call external service in test. Next.js: ESLint + TypeScript strict mode. React Query cache invalidation must be tested with SSE reconnect replay. Feature flag changes require LaunchDarkly API key in GitLab CI secrets.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WarehouseCapacityService Cloud Run latency spikes during end-of-day replenishment batches.
