parsed.thinking_summary: This is a multi-campus faculty governance platform with four microservices on AKS. Key decisions: Spring Boot microservices with separate PostgreSQL schemas per service to isolate contract, scheduling, HR, and reporting data; Azure AD SSO with RBAC enforced at both API gateway and service layer; daily batch SIS ingestion for contract generation to avoid event-driven SIS API latency; Workday sync with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue for failed records. The plan quality is weak — several sections are thin and the data model is not fully specified.
parsed.title: State University Faculty Governance Platform Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A fullstack platform for a large state university system built on four Spring Boot microservices on AKS, managing faculty workload contracts, course assignments, tenure milestones, and HR sync across multiple campuses. Vue 3 frontend with Pinia state management provides role-scoped views for faculty, department chairs, deans, provosts, and HR staff. Azure AD SSO with MFA enforces RBAC at both the API gateway and service layer. Daily batch jobs ingest SIS enrollment projections to generate draft workload contracts and reconcile Workday faculty records.
parsed.architecture_overview: Four Spring Boot microservices deployed on AKS: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, and ReportingService, each with its own PostgreSQL schema. Vue 3 SPA communicates with the ContractsService API gateway via Azure API Management, which enforces Azure AD JWT validation and RBAC. Each microservice writes to its own append-only audit_log table. Daily batch jobs run on SchedulingService and HRSyncService using a custom scheduler. Workday HR integration uses the Workday REST API with exponential backoff and a dead-letter queue for failed sync records.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Query, PrimeVue, Vite. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security OIDC, Spring Data JPA, Flyway. Database: PostgreSQL 16 per microservice schema. Cache: Redis 7 for distributed locking. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. API Gateway: Azure API Management. Scheduler: custom Java Quartz scheduler. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Grafana, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Contract Authoring: faculty members create workload contracts specifying course sections, research hours, and service commitments; department chairs approve and return with comments. Course Assignment: department chairs assign course sections to faculty members from the contract pool. Tenure Milestone Tracking: provost office tracks publication, grant, and promotion milestones against faculty contracts. HR Sync: nightly batch reconciles Workday faculty records with contract assignments. Department Allocation Dashboard: department chairs view contract distribution across their department. Batch Report Generation: provost office generates PDF workload reports for accreditation review.
parsed.system_components: Vue 3 SPA (Azure Static Web Apps), Azure API Management (API gateway with JWT validation and RBAC), ContractsService (workload contract state machine, PostgreSQL schema), SchedulingService (daily batch SIS ingestion, contract generation), HRSyncService (Workday REST API integration, dead-letter queue), ReportingService (PDF report generation), PostgreSQL 16 per microservice, Redis 7 (distributed locking), Azure Key Vault (secrets), Azure Blob Storage (archival), Azure DevOps Pipelines (CI/CD), Azure Monitor (metrics), Grafana (dashboards), PagerDuty (alerts).
parsed.workflows: Contract Authoring: Faculty member creates a draft workload contract specifying course sections and research hours; department chair receives approval notification; chair reviews and approves via the Provost Approval Queue; contract state transitions to dean_approved; dean approves; contract state transitions to executed; contract record stored in ContractsService PostgreSQL schema. Daily Batch Contract Generation: SchedulingService Quartz scheduler triggers at 2 AM; ingests SIS enrollment projections for the next academic year; generates draft workload contracts for each department; writes contracts to ContractsService PostgreSQL schema; sends department chair notification emails via SendGrid. Workday HR Sync: HRSyncService Quartz scheduler triggers at 3 AM; queries Workday faculty records; matches records to faculty_members in ContractsService; updates contract assignments; writes hr_sync_records; failed records written to dead-letter queue for manual resolution.
parsed.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, first_name, last_name, email, role ENUM faculty/department_chair/dean/provost/hr_staff, campus_id FK). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, contract_year, contract_type ENUM tenure/adjunct/visiting, course_sections JSONB, research_hours INT, service_commitments JSONB, state ENUM draft/submitted/department_approved/dean_approved/provost_approved/executed, created_by, created_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, course_code, course_name, section_number, enrollment_capacity, assigned_faculty_id FK). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, contract_id FK, milestone_type ENUM publication/grant/promotion, target_date, achieved BOOLEAN, achieved_at). department_allocations (id UUID PK, department_id, contract_type, total_fte, allocated_fte, year). campus_calendars (id UUID PK, campus_id, academic_year, start_date, end_date). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, workday_record_id, faculty_id FK, contract_id FK, sync_status ENUM matched/pending/failed, error_code, created_at). audit_logs (id UUID PK, microservice, operation, resource_type, resource_id, user_id, correlation_id, created_at).
parsed.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. GET /contracts?faculty_id&state=department_approved (department chair queue). POST /contracts (create draft). PATCH /contracts/{id}/sections (assign course sections). POST /contracts/{id}/approve (department chair approval). GET /tenure-milestones?faculty_id (provost milestone view). GET /hr-sync/status (HR sync dashboard). GET /reports/{year}/workload (provost PDF report). All endpoints require Azure AD JWT; RBAC enforced at API gateway and service layer.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC SSO with MFA enforced at Azure API Management. Spring Security OIDC JWT validation at each microservice. RBAC enforced at API gateway and service layer via Spring Security annotations. Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — no secrets in pod environment variables. Each microservice writes to its own append-only audit_log table on every state-changing operation. Cross-service audit correlation via correlation_id HTTP header. TLS 1.3 in transit. AES-256 at rest via Azure Blob Storage encryption. SOC 2 Type II: audit trail, access controls, change management, and data classification. FERPA: no student enrollment data in faculty workload records.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster with 3-node general-purpose node pool and 1-node high-memory node pool for ReportingService batch jobs. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit tests, SonarQube quality gate, Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS. Helm rollback on failed health checks. Flyway migrations versioned per microservice. Environments: dev (single node pool), staging (multi-node), production (multi-node with 3-node pool for ReportingService). Database migrations run as Flyway scripts in Helm init container before pod startup.
parsed.observability: Azure Monitor for AKS cluster metrics: pod CPU/memory, node CPU/memory, disk usage. Grafana dashboards: contract approval throughput, Workday sync lag (records pending vs matched), batch job duration. PagerDuty alerts: SchedulingService Quartz job failure, HRSyncService dead-letter queue depth > 50, contract approval queue depth > 20, Azure API Management error rate > 5%.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6000/month for 4 microservices on AKS general-purpose node pool. ReportingService high-memory node pool adds ~$800/month. Azure Container Registry ~$50/month. Azure Blob Storage archival ~$200/month. Total within $8000/month budget. Scaling: AKS auto-scales node pool on CPU 70% target; ReportingService scales on batch job queue depth.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (12 months): ContractsService and SchedulingService for one pilot campus; Azure AD SSO; basic contract authoring and approval; daily batch contract generation; Workday HR sync; Azure DevOps CI/CD. Phase 2 Multi-Campus (18 months): All four microservices; course section assignment; tenure milestone tracking; department allocation dashboard; Provost Approval Queue; PDF report generation; multi-campus analytics. Phase 3 Mobile and Analytics (24 months): Faculty self-service mobile app; cross-campus workload comparison analytics; faculty performance review integration.
parsed.development_guidelines: Java 21 with strict null safety via @NonNull annotations. Spring Boot 3 with OpenAPI 3.0 generated from JPA entities. JUnit 5 with 80% coverage gate. SonarQube with security and complexity rules. Flyway migrations versioned and tested in CI. Vue 3 TypeScript strict mode. Pinia stores typed with TypeScript interfaces. Vue Query for all server state. No credentials in pod environment variables.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SIS enrollment projections delayed, causing contract generation to miss deadline. Mitigation: SchedulingService has a 2-hour grace period before contract deadline; department chairs can manually create contracts if projections are late. Risk: Workday REST API rate limits causing sync failures. Mitigation: exponential backoff with dead-letter queue; manual resolution by HR staff. Tradeoff: daily batch SIS ingestion over event-driven — safer for university system where SIS data is batch-updated overnight, but may introduce 24-hour lag in contract generation.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected daily batch SIS ingestion over event-driven to avoid SIS API latency and simplify Quartz scheduler management. Confirmed Workday REST API rate limits handled with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue. Chose Flyway over Liquibase for database migrations due to university central IT team familiarity with Flyway.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a flawed first-pass row — the plan intentionally contains a contract violation: the backend_stack field in the frozen_requirement_contract correctly specifies Java 21 with Spring Boot, but the technology_stack section of the plan mentions Node.js as the backend runtime, creating an internal inconsistency. Additionally, the compliance_context field is present but the security_and_compliance section omits SOC 2 audit controls, which are required given the confidential data sensitivity and high risk level.
reference.title: Faculty Workload and Tenure Governance Platform — Initial Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A fullstack academic governance platform for a multi-campus state university system managing faculty workload contracts, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking. Built with Vue 3 on a microservices backend using Java 21 Spring Boot services on Azure Kubernetes Service, with PostgreSQL 16 per-service schema isolation, Azure AD SSO, and full audit logging. Integrates with the university Workday HR system via nightly batch sync. NOTE: this plan contains intentional flaws for training purposes — technology_stack section incorrectly references Node.js backend.
reference.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA authenticates via Azure AD Entra ID OIDC and calls four Spring Boot microservices through Azure API Management: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, and ReportingService. Each microservice owns its PostgreSQL 16 schema. Redis 7 provides distributed locking for concurrent contract edits. Daily batch jobs in SchedulingService ingest SIS enrollment projections to generate draft workload contracts. Tenure milestone tracking and multi-level approval workflows are orchestrated by ContractsService with role-gated endpoints for department_chair, college_dean, and provost reviewers.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, PrimeVue, Vue Router. Backend: Node.js 20 with Express (intentional contract violation — contract specifies Java 21 Spring Boot). Database: PostgreSQL 16 per-microservice schema. Cache: Redis 7. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure API Management. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Grafana.
reference.functional_feature_map: Contract Authoring Wizard: faculty or department chair drafts workload contract with course assignment grid and credit hour calculation. Multi-Level Approval Workflow: state machine routing contracts through department_chair > college_dean > provost reviewers with email notifications at each stage. Tenure Milestone Timeline: faculty view of tenure review schedule with document upload for dossier submission. Department Allocation Dashboard: chair view of total workload hours across all faculty in department. Batch Contract Generation: SchedulingService automatically generates draft contracts from SIS enrollment projections each semester. HR Sync Status: admin panel showing Workday sync last run, records updated, and error counts.
reference.system_components: Azure API Management (JWT validation, RBAC routing), Vue 3 SPA (Azure Blob Static Sites), ContractsService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL contracts schema), SchedulingService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL scheduling schema, batch job), HRSyncService (Spring Boot, Workday REST API integration), ReportingService (Spring Boot, ClickHouse read-only reporting replica), Redis 7 (session cache, distributed locking), Azure Key Vault (secrets management), Azure Monitor (cluster and application metrics).
reference.workflows: Semester Contract Generation: SchedulingService batch job runs at 02:00 on the Monday before the semester opens; fetches course_sections with enrollment projections from SIS via REST; generates draft workload_contracts for each faculty_member with assigned sections; notifies department chairs via email. Tenure Review Initiation: the milestones service detects faculty members within 60 days of their tenure_review_date; generates a review record; routes dossier submission task to faculty; upon submission, routes approval workflow to department, college, and provost levels sequentially with configurable deadline escalation.
reference.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, azure_oid, employee_id, name, rank ENUM assistant/associate/full/adjunct, department_id FK, tenure_status, hire_date, campus_id FK). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, academic_year, semester, status ENUM draft/submitted/dept_approved/dean_approved/provost_approved/executed, total_credit_hours, created_at, approved_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, course_code, section_number, credit_hours, enrollment_cap, sis_section_id). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, milestone_type ENUM annual_review/midpoint/tenure_vote/promotion, due_date, status, dossier_s3_key). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, employee_id, workday_worker_id, sync_status, last_synced_at, error_detail). audit_logs (id UUID PK, service_name, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, correlation_id, timestamp).
reference.api_design: ContractsService: GET /contracts?faculty_id= (role scoped), POST /contracts (draft creation), PATCH /contracts/:id/submit, PATCH /contracts/:id/approve (role: chair/dean/provost), GET /contracts/:id/audit-log. SchedulingService: GET /course-sections?semester=, POST /batch/generate-contracts (admin trigger). HRSyncService: GET /sync/status, POST /sync/trigger (admin). TenureService: GET /milestones/:faculty_id, POST /milestones/:id/submit-dossier. All services: GET /health, X-Correlation-ID header required.
reference.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with MFA enforced. JWT validation at Azure API Management layer with RBAC claim checking. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations at method level for defense in depth. TLS 1.3 on all ingress. Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — pod identity bound to service account for secret access. NOTE: SOC 2 Type II audit controls are intentionally underspecified here — audit log retention policy and access review procedures missing despite compliance_context requiring them.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit unit and integration tests, SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage), Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS namespace. Environments: dev (shared 2-node cluster), staging (production mirror, single campus dataset), production (3-node general pool + 1-node high-memory for reporting). Flyway database migrations applied pre-deploy via Kubernetes Job. Helm rollback on failed liveness probes. Azure Blob lifecycle policy archives records exceeding retention tiers.
reference.observability: Azure Monitor collects AKS node and pod metrics. Grafana dashboards: contract approval throughput by campus, Workday sync lag per faculty cohort, tenure milestone overdue count. Application logs structured JSON with correlation_id. PagerDuty alerts: batch job failure (SchedulingService), Workday sync failure for >100 records, API error rate >2%, AKS node NotReady.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6500/month on AKS for 3 campuses at 800 faculty. AKS general-purpose nodes 3x D4s_v3 ~$700/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2x ~$400/month; Azure Cache for Redis ~$150/month; API Management Developer tier ~$50/month. Reserved instance discounts reduce compute ~30%. Scale path: horizontal pod autoscaling on CPU 70% for ContractsService during semester start spikes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Pilot Campus (12 weeks): ContractsService with approval workflow, Vue 3 frontend for contract authoring, Azure AD SSO, PostgreSQL schema setup, CI/CD pipeline, Workday HR sync (nightly batch). Phase 2 — Scheduling Automation (8 weeks): SchedulingService batch contract generation from SIS, course section assignment grid, campus calendar integration. Phase 3 — Tenure Tracking (8 weeks): tenure milestone timeline, dossier upload, provost approval workflow, department allocation dashboard. Phase 4 — Multi-Campus Rollout (6 weeks): multi-campus namespace isolation, cross-campus reporting in ReportingService, analytics dashboards.
reference.development_guidelines: Java 21 with virtual threads for blocking I/O in sync tasks. Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security for auth. Flyway for database migrations — no manual schema changes. OpenAPI 3.0 specs code-generated from annotations. SonarQube quality gate in CI. RBAC annotations required on all controller methods — no unannotated endpoints. Correlation ID injected into all outbound Feign client calls via RequestInterceptor.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Workday REST API rate limiting during large HR sync batches — mitigation: chunked batches of 100 records with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue for failed records. Risk: multi-campus PostgreSQL schema drift if migrations run out of order — mitigation: Flyway with ordered versioned migrations and pre-deploy validation. Tradeoff: per-microservice PostgreSQL schema over a single shared database for service isolation at the cost of cross-service reporting complexity, addressed by a read-only ClickHouse replica fed by logical replication.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected per-service PostgreSQL schema over separate database instances for cost efficiency while maintaining logical isolation. Chose daily batch Workday sync over event-driven for compatibility with Workday's batch-update cadence. Confirmed AKS Helm deployments over Azure App Service for microservice orchestration flexibility.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the pumped hydro investment landing page. ISS-002 requires server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in the Next.js API route before Salesforce forward. ISS-001 requires Salesforce Lead field mapping specification. Both are straightforward fixes to the static Next.js landing page architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Added server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry using Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API. reCAPTCHA token is validated before any Salesforce API call. reCAPTCHA v3 score below 0.5 logged silently to Vercel log drain without bot feedback.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bot form submissions cannot bypass spam protection; legitimate investor inquiries proceed to Salesforce CRM

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Salesforce Lead field mapping: FirstName = name, LastName = empty (not required by Salesforce), Email = email, Company = organization, Description = investment_interest_text, LeadSource = 'Investor Inquiry - Pumped Hydro', Campaign_Tag__c = 'Series A Fundraising'. CRM team can configure lead routing and campaign tracking before campaign launch.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CRM team can configure lead routing and campaign tracking before campaign launch; no field mapping surprises during Series A fundraising

parsed.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page deployed to Vercel Edge for a 800 MW grid-scale pumped hydro storage project, targeting institutional investors and project finance advisors. The page presents project capacity specifications, construction timeline milestones, and team profiles. An investor inquiry form with server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification and Salesforce Lead API integration captures lead conversions. Plausible Analytics tracks investor engagement without cookies. This revision adds server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification and Salesforce Lead field mapping.
parsed.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export with TypeScript generates a fully pre-rendered HTML page at build time, served from Vercel Edge Network for global investor audience. Project specs and milestones are loaded from YAML config files (milestones.yaml, project_specs.yaml) validated by Zod schemas at build time. The investor inquiry form submits to a Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry, which validates the reCAPTCHA v3 token server-side via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API, validates the form fields with Zod, and forwards the lead to Salesforce Lead API with field mapping. Rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket prevents spam. Plausible Analytics tracks page views and form conversions without cookies. No database, no server-side application state.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vercel Edge. Form validation: Zod schemas for milestone and project spec YAML, Zod for form field validation in API route. reCAPTCHA: Google reCAPTCHA v3 client-side widget, server-side token verification via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API. CRM: Salesforce Lead API v51. Analytics: Plausible Analytics. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network. CI: GitHub Actions. Rate limiting: Vercel Edge middleware token bucket.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Project Overview: 800 MW capacity, 4-hour storage duration, 2.5 billion MWh reservoir capacity, 800 MW turbine units, expected annual generation 6.4 GWh. Construction Timeline: interactive SVG timeline showing permitting, civil works, turbine installation, commissioning milestones. Financial Highlights: expected IRR, payback period, debt service coverage ratio, project cost breakdown. Investor Contact Form: name, email, organization, investment interest text, reCAPTCHA v3 challenge; server-side validation and Salesforce Lead API forward. Downloadable Project Summary PDF: generated from project_specs.yaml at build time and served as static asset.
parsed.system_components: Vercel Edge Network: serves pre-rendered HTML, caches static assets globally. Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry: validates reCAPTCHA v3 token via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API, validates form fields with Zod, forwards to Salesforce Lead API, rate-limits via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket. Vercel Edge middleware: token bucket rate limiting for POST /api/inquiry. Vercel KV: stores rate limit token bucket state. Plausible Analytics: cookieless page view and form conversion tracking. Salesforce Lead API: receives lead records with field mapping. GitHub Actions: Lighthouse CI and WCAG 2.1 AA gate on every push.
parsed.workflows: Investor Inquiry Submission: Investor fills out contact form on page; reCAPTCHA v3 widget renders client-side; on submit, browser POSTs to POST /api/inquiry with {name, email, organization, investment_interest_text, recaptcha_token, hp_field}; Next.js API route calls Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API with recaptcha_token to verify score >= 0.5; if score below 0.5, logs to Vercel log drain silently and returns 200; if score >= 0.5, validates form fields with Zod InquirySchema; if valid, calls Salesforce Lead API v51 POST /services/data/v51/sobjects/Lead with field mapping: {FirstName: name, Email: email, Company: organization, Description: investment_interest_text, LeadSource: 'Investor Inquiry - Pumped Hydro', Campaign_Tag__c: 'Series A Fundraising'}; on Salesforce success, returns 200; on Salesforce failure, retries once with 1-second delay and returns 200 to user, logging full payload to Vercel log drain for manual CRM entry. Project Spec PDF Generation: At build time, next export generates project_specs.yaml-driven PDF via pdf-lib library, embedded as static asset /project-summary.pdf.
parsed.data_model: milestones.yaml: [{id: string, label: string, date: string ISO8601, status: 'completed' | 'in_progress' | 'upcoming', description: string}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw: number, storage_hours: number, reservoir_capacity_mwh: number, upper_reservoir_volume_af: number, head_elevation_ft: number, turbine_units: number, expected_annual_generation_gwh: number, permitting_status: 'in_progress' | 'completed', target_cod: number, project_name: string, location: string} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name: string, title: string, bio: string, photo_filename: string}] validated by TeamProfileSchema Zod. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c. No app-side database; all data in YAML config files.
parsed.api_design: POST /api/inquiry: body {name: string, email: string, organization: string, investment_interest_text: string, recaptcha_token: string, hp_field: string}. Server-side validation: reCAPTCHA v3 token verified via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API (score >= 0.5 required), form fields validated with Zod InquirySchema, honeypot field must be empty. Salesforce forward: POST /services/data/v51/sobjects/Lead with field mapping. Rate limiting: 5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket. Response: 200 for all outcomes (including bot feedback prevention). Error handling: Salesforce failure retries once with 1-second delay; on second failure returns 200 to user and logs full payload to Vercel log drain.
parsed.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel; HSTS header set via Vercel headers config. CSP header: default-src'self'; script-src'self''sha256-{reCAPTCHA script hash}'; connect-src'self' https://www.google.com https://www.googleapis.com https://api.salesforce.com. reCAPTCHA v3 server-side token verification: POST /recaptcha/api/siteverify with secret key, validates score >= 0.5 before Salesforce forward. Honeypot field: hidden input hp_field must be empty; form submissions with non-empty hp_field silently logged and discarded. No PII stored in app; Salesforce is the sole data store for inquiry leads. Plausible Analytics is cookieless; no consent banner required. Robots noindex meta tag on page until public launch verified via Vercel headers.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub repository. GitHub Actions CI: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI gate >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check on built pages. Vercel deployment triggered automatically on GitHub push via Vercel Git integration. Environments: Vercel preview deployments on PR branches; production deployment on main branch push. Rollback: Vercel instant rollback to any previous deployment via Vercel dashboard; deployment history retained 30 days. Vercel KV used for Edge middleware token bucket state; no database migration required.
parsed.observability: Plausible Analytics: page views, form conversion rate (POST /api/inquiry success rate), PDF download events (project-summary.pdf). Vercel deployment logs: build duration, API route error count, Salesforce API failure count. Vercel log drain: Salesforce API failure payloads for manual CRM entry. Alert: Vercel deployment failure triggers Slack notification to engineering team.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby or Pro plan: $0-20/month for static page traffic. Vercel Edge: global CDN with no per-request cost. Salesforce Lead API: included in Salesforce Developer Edition or higher. Plausible Analytics: $9/month. Total estimated $9-29/month. Scales to any global investor traffic without infrastructure changes.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Launch (6 weeks): Project specs and timeline YAML config files, Next.js static export, Vercel deployment, reCAPTCHA v3 server-side validation, Salesforce Lead API integration with field mapping, Plausible Analytics, Lighthouse 95+ gate, WCAG 2.1 AA gate. Phase 2 - Post-Launch (4 weeks): Downloadable project summary PDF, investor NDA request form, construction progress photo gallery, multilingual support for Japanese and Korean sections.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. Zod schemas for all YAML config files in src/schemas/; no raw YAML parsing in components. reCAPTCHA v3 token must be verified server-side in API route before any Salesforce call. Salesforce field mapping must be documented in README.md before any production deployment. Lighthouse CI gate >= 95 enforced in GitHub Actions. WCAG 2.1 AA enforced via axe-core in GitHub Actions. No sensitive financial projections rendered client-side without authentication gate.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Salesforce Lead API rate limits during Series A fundraising campaign surge. Mitigation: Salesforce rate limit of 200 requests per 24 hours per user; campaign team notified if approaching limit. Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 score below 0.5 on legitimate investor submissions. Mitigation: log silently and return 200; CRM team reviews log drain for manual entry. Tradeoff: server-side reCAPTCHA v3 adds latency to form submission; acceptable for investor inquiry form where speed is not critical. Tradeoff: Vercel Edge over custom CDN for simplicity at current traffic volumes.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed server-side reCAPTCHA v3 over client-side only for bot protection. Confirmed Salesforce Lead API over custom CRM webhook for standard field mapping and lead routing. Confirmed Plausible Analytics over Google Analytics for cookieless GDPR compliance. Confirmed Vercel Edge over Netlify for native Next.js API route support.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-002 by adding server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in the POST /api/inquiry route via Google siteverify API before any Salesforce forwarding. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with IntersectionObserver only when inquiry form enters viewport to protect Lighthouse score. Also specifying Salesforce Lead field mapping to unblock the CRM team's configuration. The landing_page class means no persistent backend; the API route is a thin serverless function.

fix_report.action_taken: POST /api/inquiry now calls https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api/siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY Vercel environment variable and submitted token; requests with score below 0.5 silently return 200 without Salesforce forward (bot feedback prevention); RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY stored in Vercel encrypted environment variables never exposed to client. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with async defer via IntersectionObserver triggered when inquiry section enters viewport, protecting Lighthouse performance score.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bot form submissions blocked server-side; Lighthouse score maintained >= 95 with deferred reCAPTCHA load; no exploitable bot feedback channel via error responses

reference.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page - v2 Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page presenting the 800 MW Mountain Creek Pumped Hydro Storage project to institutional energy infrastructure investors. Features a construction timeline visualization driven by YAML milestone config, technical specifications, team profiles, and an investor inquiry form with server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification and Salesforce Lead API integration. Deployed to Vercel Edge; Plausible cookieless analytics tracks form conversion and PDF download events.
reference.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 statically generated site deployed to Vercel Edge. All page content generated at build time from Zod-validated YAML config files (milestones.yaml, project_specs.yaml) and static assets. Single serverless API route POST /api/inquiry handles form submissions: validates reCAPTCHA token server-side via Google siteverify API, applies IP-based rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket backed by Vercel KV, validates form body with Zod InquirySchema, then maps and forwards validated lead data to Salesforce Lead API. No persistent backend service; no database; no server-side sessions. Vercel handles HTTPS, CDN distribution, and automatic preview deployments on PR branches.
reference.technology_stack: Next.js 14 with TypeScript (static generation via getStaticProps). Tailwind CSS for responsive styling. Framer Motion for timeline animation. Plausible Analytics self-proxied via Next.js rewrites. reCAPTCHA v3 with async defer and IntersectionObserver load. Zod for form and YAML schema validation. js-yaml for YAML parsing at build time. Vercel Edge runtime for API route and middleware. Vercel KV for rate limit token bucket state. GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI and axe-core for CI.
reference.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: project name, 800 MW and 8-hour storage highlights, primary CTA scrolling to inquiry form. Project Specifications: capacity_mw, storage_hours, annual_generation_gwh, reservoir details from project_specs.yaml. Construction Timeline: animated horizontal timeline from milestones.yaml with status indicators and date labels. Team Profiles: static cards from team_profiles.yaml. Investment Thesis: narrative section with downloadable project summary PDF tracked as Plausible goal event PDF_Download. Investor Inquiry Form: name, email, organization, interest description, honeypot field, server-side reCAPTCHA v3, Salesforce Lead API.
reference.system_components: Next.js static pages: index page with all sections as scroll-linked components. Serverless API route pages/api/inquiry.ts: reCAPTCHA siteverify, Zod InquirySchema validation, honeypot check, Salesforce Lead API POST with field mapping. Vercel Edge middleware middleware.ts: rate limit check using Vercel KV token bucket (5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes). YAML config loader lib/config.ts: reads and validates milestones.yaml and project_specs.yaml against Zod schemas in getStaticProps. Plausible Analytics: self-proxied via Next.js rewrites to /p/js/script.js to prevent ad-blocker interference. reCAPTCHA v3: IntersectionObserver loads script only when inquiry section enters viewport.
reference.workflows: Investor Inquiry Submission: investor fills form and submits; Next.js client validates required fields; reCAPTCHA v3 executes and appends token to POST body; POST /api/inquiry sent; Vercel Edge middleware checks rate limit token bucket in Vercel KV; API route calls Google siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY; if score >= 0.5, validates body with Zod InquirySchema, maps to Salesforce Lead fields (FirstName+LastName from name, Email, Company from organization, Description from investment_interest_text, LeadSource='Mountain_Creek_Landing_2026', Campaign_Tag__c='series_a_2026'), POSTs to Salesforce Lead API with Bearer token; returns {success: true} regardless of Salesforce outcome to prevent information leakage. Milestone Update: project team updates milestones.yaml via GitHub PR; Vercel creates preview deployment with updated timeline; PM reviews at preview URL; PR merged to main; production deployment live within 2 minutes.
reference.data_model: milestones.yaml: [{id: str, label: str, date: str ISO8601, status: 'completed'|'in_progress'|'upcoming', description: str}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw: number, storage_hours: number, reservoir_capacity_mwh: number, upper_reservoir_volume_af: number, head_elevation_ft: number, turbine_units: number, expected_annual_generation_gwh: number, permitting_status: str, target_cod: str, project_name: str, location: str} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name, title, bio, photo_filename}]. No database. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c.
reference.api_design: POST /api/inquiry request body: {name: string, email: string, organization: string, investment_interest_text: string, recaptcha_token: string, hp_field: string (honeypot, must be empty)}. Responses: 200 {success: true} always returned regardless of outcome; 429 returned by Vercel Edge middleware for rate limit exceeded before reaching route. Internal logic: honeypot non-empty returns 200 without forward; reCAPTCHA score < 0.5 logs score and returns 200; Salesforce API error logs payload to Vercel log drain and returns 200. Plausible goal events: PDF_Download and Inquiry_Submitted tracked via plausible custom events.
reference.security_and_compliance: Server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification via Google siteverify API; RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY in Vercel encrypted environment variables. Honeypot field hp_field validated server-side; non-empty returns 200 silently. Rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket in Vercel KV: 5 POST /api/inquiry per IP per 10 minutes. Salesforce access token stored in SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN Vercel environment variable; never exposed to client bundle. HTTPS enforced by Vercel; HSTS and CSP headers in next.config.js headers config: script-src 'self' www.google.com/recaptcha plausible.io 'nonce-{build_nonce}', img-src 'self' data:, frame-src www.google.com. No cookies; Plausible is cookieless.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub; automatic preview on PR push; production deploy on main push. GitHub Actions: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI performance >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check. Custom domain with Vercel-managed SSL. Vercel KV on Hobby tier sufficient for landing page rate limit traffic. Rollback: Vercel dashboard instant rollback to any previous deployment retained 30 days.
reference.observability: Plausible Analytics goals: Inquiry_Submitted (POST /api/inquiry success callback), PDF_Download (onclick on project summary link), CTA_Click (hero button). Plausible dashboard: page views, inquiry conversion rate, referrer sources (LinkedIn, direct, investor newsletters). Vercel deployment logs: API route outcomes logged via console.error with structured JSON including Salesforce API response code. No APM needed; Vercel Edge function invocation metrics available in Vercel dashboard.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $20/month: Vercel Pro $20 for custom domain, team members, Vercel KV for rate limiting. Plausible Analytics $9/month up to 10K pageviews. Salesforce Lead API within existing CRM subscription. GitHub Actions free tier for CI. Scales automatically via Vercel Edge CDN during investor campaign traffic spikes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (5 weeks): design and copy finalization (weeks 1-2), Next.js component build with YAML config and Zod validation (weeks 2-4), server-side reCAPTCHA and Salesforce Lead API integration, Lighthouse and axe-core CI, Vercel production launch for Series A campaign. Phase 2 (4 weeks after Series A close): password-protected investor data room via Next.js middleware auth, multilingual support (Japanese, Korean) via next-i18next, construction progress photo gallery with S3 source.
reference.development_guidelines: reCAPTCHA secret key and Salesforce access token must never appear in client-side code or console logs; enforced via pre-commit secret scan hook. YAML config changes require Zod schema validation to pass in CI before merge. Lighthouse CI gate blocks merge if performance score drops below 95. axe-core accessibility checks block merge on WCAG 2.1 AA violations. All user-visible strings in en.json locale file for future i18n compatibility.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 score thresholds may block legitimate investors submitting from corporate proxy networks with atypically low behavioral scores. Mitigation: log borderline scores (0.4-0.5) without blocking during first week; adjust threshold based on observed investor traffic patterns. Risk: Salesforce API rate limits at 100 calls/minute on standard edition may cause inquiry drops during campaign launch spike. Mitigation: silent 200 response with Vercel log drain backup allows manual CRM entry for any missed leads. Tradeoff: returning 200 on all form outcomes prevents bot feedback but requires active monitoring of Vercel logs for genuine Salesforce integration failures.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel KV over Redis for rate limiting due to Vercel-native integration simplicity for landing page traffic. Selected reCAPTCHA v3 over hCaptcha for Salesforce form tracking compatibility. Salesforce Lead API chosen over Web-to-Lead form to enable custom field mapping and campaign source tagging for Series A attribution.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for the marketplace reseller repricer. The remaining gap is token expiry lifecycle management — the plan needs to describe how the app detects expiring tokens (24-hour warning banner), attempts automatic refresh before each poll cycle, and surfaces failure if refresh fails. This is a local desktop app so no server-side monitoring is needed; Sentry is sufficient for crash reporting. The token refresh flow needs to be documented per marketplace (Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth, Walmart API key) since refresh token behavior differs across their SDKs.

fix_report.action_taken: Added token_expiry_at field to marketplace_credentials table; added 24-hour warning banner in settings UI when expiry is within 24 hours; added automatic token refresh attempt before each poll cycle using marketplace SDK refresh token flow (Amazon LWA refresh token, eBay refresh token, Walmart API key rotation); added failure logging and UI surface for refresh failure
fix_report.expected_outcome: Resellers receive proactive warning before token expiry causes poll failures; automatic refresh attempts to prevent silent marketplace API failures

parsed.title: Marketplace Reseller Desktop Repricing Tool v3
parsed.executive_summary: An Electron 32 + Vue 3 desktop application for independent marketplace resellers that polls competitor prices every 15 minutes, evaluates margin rules against Amazon SP-API, eBay, and Walmart price feeds, and submits approved reprices in batches. A DuckDB local database stores listing snapshots, competitor price points, and reprice run logs. OAuth2 tokens for each marketplace are stored in the OS keychain via Electron safeStorage. This revision adds token expiry lifecycle management: a 24-hour warning banner in the settings UI, automatic refresh attempts before each poll cycle, and failure surface for refresh failures.
parsed.architecture_overview: Electron 32 main process manages app lifecycle, OS keychain access via safeStorage, and Electron Forge auto-update. Vue 3 renderer process handles all UI via Pinia state and Vue Query sidecar REST calls. Python FastAPI sidecar launched as subprocess by Electron main process on startup; modules: MarketplaceClient (SP-API, eBay API, Walmart API wrappers), Poller (APScheduler jobs per connected marketplace), RepricingEngine (margin rule evaluation), Analytics (DuckDB query layer), Settings (keychain read/write). DuckDB file stored in OS app data directory. No cloud data storage for v1. Self-hosted Electron auto-update server on low-cost VPS.
parsed.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 32, Vue 3 with TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Query, Tailwind CSS, PrimeVue, vue-virtual-scroller. Sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, sp-api-python (Amazon SP-API), ebaysdk (eBay), requests (Walmart). Local DB: DuckDB 0.10.0. Token storage: Electron safeStorage (OS keychain, AES-256). Build: Electron Forge, GitHub Actions matrix (macOS 13+ and Windows 11). Update server: Electron auto-update with Electron Forge. Crash reporting: Sentry Electron SDK. CI: GitHub Actions.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Marketplace OAuth2 Connection: per-marketplace connection wizard with LWA for Amazon, OAuth 2.0 for eBay, API key for Walmart; tokens stored in OS keychain via safeStorage; expiry_at field in marketplace_credentials table; 24-hour warning banner in settings UI. Competitor Price Polling: APScheduler job per connected marketplace at 15-minute interval; calls marketplace price API for tracked ASINs/item IDs; upserts competitor_price_points in DuckDB; token refresh attempted before each poll. Margin Rule Builder: floor price, target margin %, ceiling price per rule; applies_to scope (all, category, SKU list); saved as JSON in app config. Reprice Queue: diff table showing current price, proposed price, expected margin_pct, approve/override per row; approve-all button; approve-all button shows total expected margin gain/loss. Batch Reprice Submission: groups approved items by marketplace; calls pricing API in batches respecting rate limits; records submission result in reprice_run_logs. Analytics Dashboard: margin trend chart per ASIN, reprice action history, competitor price history.
parsed.system_components: Electron 32 main process: app lifecycle, OS keychain safeStorage access, auto-update server endpoint, subprocess manager for FastAPI sidecar. Vue 3 renderer: Pinia app state, Vue Query sidecar REST calls, vue-virtual-scroller for listings table. FastAPI sidecar (subprocess): MarketplaceClient modules (SP-API, eBay, Walmart), Poller APScheduler jobs, RepricingEngine, Analytics DuckDB queries, Settings keychain calls. DuckDB file: listing_snapshots (append-only), competitor_price_points (90-day retention), repricing_rules (JSON in app config), reprice_run_logs, channel_performance_reports. Electron auto-update server: self-hosted on VPS, serves signed binaries to app.
parsed.workflows: 15-Minute Poll Cycle: APScheduler job fires for each connected marketplace; Poller calls marketplace price API for tracked ASINs; for each price point, upserts competitor_price_points in DuckDB; checks token_expiry_at field; if expiry within 24 hours, shows warning banner in settings UI; attempts automatic token refresh using marketplace SDK refresh token flow (Amazon LWA refresh token, eBay refresh token, Walmart API key rotation); if refresh fails, logs failure and surfaces retryable item in UI; if token valid, proceeds to RepricingEngine evaluation. Margin Rule Evaluation: for each listing_snapshot, RepricingEngine queries active repricing_rules; for each rule, computes proposed_price = max(floor_price, min(current_price, ceiling_price)); if proposed_price differs from current_price by >threshold, adds to reprice_run_logs with status=pending; if rule applies_to=sku_list and SKU not in list, skips. Reprice Queue Approval: reseller reviews diff table; approves individual rows or all; for approved items, adds to batch submission queue; batchSubmitter groups by marketplace, calls pricing API in batches respecting rate limits, records submission result in reprice_run_logs.
parsed.data_model: marketplace_credentials (id, marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/walmart, token_ref VARCHAR stored as keychain key reference, connected_at, expiry_at TIMESTAMP, last_used_at). listing_snapshots (id, marketplace, seller_sku, asin_or_item_id, title, current_price_cents, cost_price_cents, polled_at). competitor_price_points (id, asin_or_item_id, marketplace, competitor_seller_id, price_cents, condition, polled_at; 90-day retention). repricing_rules (id, name, floor_price_cents, target_margin_pct DECIMAL, ceiling_price_cents, applies_to ENUM all/category/sku_list, sku_list JSON array). reprice_run_logs (id, run_at, marketplace, sku, old_price_cents, proposed_price_cents, expected_margin_pct, status ENUM pending/approved/submitted/failed, submitted_at). channel_performance_reports (id, marketplace, report_date, total_listings, repriced_count, avg_margin_pct, total_reprice_gain_cents).
parsed.api_design: Local REST on 127.0.0.1:8923 (FastAPI sidecar). GET /marketplaces (list connected channels with expiry status). POST /marketplaces/{id}/poll (trigger manual poll for debugging). GET /rules (list active margin profiles). POST /rules (create new rule). GET /queue (reprice queue with pending items). POST /queue/{id}/approve (approve single item). POST /queue/all (approve all pending items). POST /submit (trigger batch reprice submission). GET /analytics/margin-trend?asin={id} (DuckDB query result). GET /logs?marketplace={id} (reprice run log history).
parsed.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 tokens stored via Electron safeStorage (OS-level AES-256 encryption backed by OS keychain); token values never logged; Authorization headers redacted in sidecar debug logs. Token expiry_at field in marketplace_credentials; 24-hour warning banner in settings UI when expiry is within 24 hours; automatic token refresh attempted before each poll cycle using marketplace SDK refresh token flow (Amazon LWA refresh token, eBay refresh token, Walmart API key rotation); failure logged and surfaced as retryable item in UI. DuckDB file stored in OS app data directory with OS-level file permissions (user-only read/write); no credentials written to DuckDB. TLS 1.3 enforced for all outbound marketplace API calls. Rate limiter token bucket per marketplace in poller module; configurable poll frequency bounded by marketplace ToS minimum intervals; user warning if custom interval would breach SP-API throttle quota.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest for FastAPI sidecar (RepricingEngine, Poller, BatchSubmitter unit tests), Vitest for Vue 3 components, Electron Forge build on macOS 13+ and Windows 11 runners, auto-update server deploy on tag push. Electron Forge builds DMG (macOS) and NSIS installer (Windows) via GitHub Actions matrix; code-signed with Apple Developer ID (macOS notarization) and EV certificate (Windows). Distributed via self-hosted Electron auto-update endpoint on low-cost VPS (~10 USD/month). No cloud hosting for v1. Auto-update server serves signed binaries only; no binary preview builds served to beta testers.
parsed.observability: Sentry Electron SDK for renderer and main process crash reporting; Sentry project configured with marketplace reseller beta tester distribution list. Local rotating log file in app data directory (10 MB max, rotate on size). Log levels: DEBUG (sidecar debug), INFO (poll cycle start/complete, token refresh success/failure), WARNING (token expiry within 24 hours, rate limit breach), ERROR (marketplace API 5xx, reprice submission failure). No server-side monitoring for v1; Sentry crash reports are the primary observability signal.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost for v1 — all processing runs on local machine. Self-hosted Electron auto-update server on low-cost VPS (~10 USD/month). DuckDB file size growth bounded by 90-day competitor price retention and 2 GB warning threshold. For resellers with 10,000+ SKUs, consider DuckDB partitioning by date range to keep active price history within 2 GB.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Amazon SP-API connection with LWA, listing snapshot import, 15-minute competitor price polling, basic margin rule (floor price, target margin %), reprice queue with diff UX and approve-all, batch reprice submission to SP-API, token expiry warning and automatic refresh, DuckDB analytics dashboard for margin trend and reprice history. Phase 1.1 (8 weeks): eBay and Walmart Marketplace connections, multi-rule profiles, analytics export to CSV, archive/purge flow for large DuckDB files. Phase 2 (future): AI-driven margin rule suggestions, supplier cost import, multi-seat licensing with central sync server.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python sidecar: pytest for all modules; 85% coverage gate in CI. Vue 3: Vitest for component tests; Playwright for reprice queue approval flow. Electron Forge: code-signing step must pass in CI before release tag. Token refresh logic must be tested with mock marketplace SDK responses (Amazon LWA refresh token, eBay refresh token, Walmart API key rotation). DuckDB queries must be parameterized — no string interpolation in SQL. All marketplace API calls must use retry with exponential backoff with jitter.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Amazon SP-API rate limit changes (e.g., increased rate limits for LWA credentials) break poller without warning. Mitigation: token bucket rate limiter with configurable rate limit per marketplace; alert in settings UI if custom interval would breach throttle quota. Risk: DuckDB file corruption on app crash during write. Mitigation: append-only tables; weekly backup via export to Parquet; auto-recovery on next app start. Tradeoff: local DuckDB over SQLite — DuckDB columnar scan is 2-5x faster for price history queries but requires Python sidecar to execute; acceptable for v1 since sidecar is already required for marketplace API calls.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed DuckDB over SQLite for price history analytics — columnar scan handles millions of price points in sub-second latency. Confirmed Amazon SP-API LWA over AWS Developer Token for v1 — LWA provides refresh token for automatic re-authentication. Confirmed Electron Forge auto-update over manual binary distribution — auto-update reduces reseller support burden for version updates.

reference.thinking_summary: The key fix in round 3 is ISS-003: OAuth token expiry lifecycle management. I need to add a token expiry tracking field to marketplace_credentials, implement 24-hour pre-expiry warnings in the settings UI, and ensure the poller checks token freshness before each poll cycle. Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth 2.0, and Walmart each have different refresh token TTLs so the expiry monitoring must be per-marketplace. I will also document Walmart Marketplace rate limits which were flagged as missing.

fix_report.action_taken: Added token_expires_at field to marketplace_credentials; poller module now checks token freshness before each poll cycle and attempts refresh if within 2 hours of expiry; Settings page shows expiry countdown per marketplace with amber warning at 24 hours and red warning at 2 hours; Sentry alert raised if automatic refresh fails
fix_report.expected_outcome: Resellers will see proactive expiry warnings before marketplace API failures occur; automatic refresh will silently renew tokens in the happy path; manual re-authentication flow is clearly surfaced when refresh fails

reference.title: Marketplace Reseller Desktop Repricing Tool
reference.executive_summary: An Electron 32 desktop application with Vue 3 frontend and embedded Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar, backed by a local DuckDB database, that automates competitor price monitoring and bulk repricing across Amazon, eBay, and Walmart Marketplace. OAuth2 tokens for each marketplace are stored in the OS keychain via Electron safeStorage with proactive expiry monitoring and automatic token refresh. A 15-minute poller refreshes competitor price points; the RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against margin rules and populates a diff-style approval queue showing current price, proposed price, and expected margin before batch submission.
reference.architecture_overview: Electron 32 main process manages the app lifecycle, OS keychain access via safeStorage, and auto-updater. The Vue 3 renderer process handles all UI via a typed IPC bridge exposed through a preload script. A Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar is spawned as a child process on app start and listens on loopback 127.0.0.1:8923 — the Electron preload script proxies API calls to the sidecar via HTTP. DuckDB stores all listing snapshots and competitor price point history locally with no cloud dependency. The Poller module runs APScheduler jobs per connected marketplace on 15-minute intervals, checking token freshness before each cycle and refreshing tokens within 2 hours of expiry. The RepricingEngine processes each listing_snapshot against the active margin profile and pushes candidates to the reprice queue. The BatchSubmitter groups approved items by marketplace and submits reprices in rate-limit-compliant batches.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 32, Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PrimeVue, Pinia, Vue Query. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, APScheduler, sp-api-python, ebaysdk, DuckDB Python SDK, cryptography library for keychain IPC. Data: DuckDB (columnar, local file). Distribution: Electron Forge with NSIS (Windows) and DMG (macOS); code-signed; self-hosted auto-update server. Observability: Sentry Electron SDK; local rotating log files.
reference.functional_feature_map: Marketplace Connections: connect Amazon, eBay, and Walmart via OAuth2 flows; settings UI shows token expiry countdown per marketplace with amber/red warnings; automatic background token refresh at 2-hour threshold. Listing Snapshot Import: fetch active listings from each connected marketplace via API, upsert into DuckDB listing_snapshots table. Competitor Price Polling: APScheduler 15-minute jobs per marketplace fetch competitor prices for all tracked SKUs and upsert competitor_price_points. Reprice Queue: RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against active margin profile, computes proposed_price, and populates queue with current vs. proposed diff and expected_margin_pct. Batch Reprice Submission: user approves or overrides items in queue; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace and submits in rate-limit-compliant batches. Analytics Dashboard: DuckDB queries powering margin trend per ASIN, reprice action history, competitor price history charts in Vue renderer.
reference.system_components: Electron 32 main process (app lifecycle, safeStorage, auto-updater); Vue 3 renderer (SPA via preload IPC bridge); Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar child process on loopback:8923; DuckDB file in OS app data directory; APScheduler for marketplace pollers; Sentry Electron SDK; self-hosted Electron Forge auto-update server.
reference.workflows: Token Expiry Monitoring and Refresh: On each Poller tick, the sidecar checks marketplace_credentials.token_expires_at for the target marketplace; if within 2 hours of expiry, attempts token refresh using the stored refresh token via the marketplace OAuth2 token endpoint; on success, updates token and token_expires_at in keychain and database; on failure, sets token_status=refresh_failed and emits an IPC event to the renderer causing the Settings page to display a red re-authenticate banner. The poller aborts the current cycle and logs the failure to avoid sending requests with an expired token. Settings page polls token status every 60 seconds and shows per-marketplace countdown: green (>24 hours), amber (2-24 hours), red (<2 hours or refresh_failed). Reprice Queue Approval and Batch Submit: User opens Reprice Queue page, views diff table with columns: SKU, marketplace, current_price, proposed_price, expected_margin_pct; clicks approve on individual rows or 'Approve All' for bulk; approved items submitted to BatchSubmitter; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace, respects rate limits (Amazon SP-API: 1 req/sec with burst 5; eBay Sell API: 5 req/sec; Walmart Marketplace: 10 req/sec); submission results written to reprice_run_logs with status submitted or failed; failed items surfaced as retryable in queue.
reference.data_model: marketplace_credentials (marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/walmart, token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, refresh_token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_status ENUM active/expiring_soon/refresh_failed, connected_at). listing_snapshots (id, marketplace, seller_sku, asin_or_item_id, title, current_price_cents INT, cost_price_cents INT, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). competitor_price_points (id, asin_or_item_id, marketplace, competitor_seller_id, price_cents INT, condition, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE per polled_at). repricing_rules (id, name, floor_price_cents INT, target_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), ceiling_price_cents INT, applies_to ENUM all/category/sku_list, active BOOLEAN). reprice_run_logs (id, run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, marketplace, seller_sku, old_price_cents INT, proposed_price_cents INT, expected_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), status ENUM pending/approved/submitted/failed/overridden, submitted_at). channel_performance_reports (id, marketplace, report_date DATE, total_listings INT, repriced_count INT, avg_margin_pct DECIMAL).
reference.api_design: FastAPI sidecar on loopback:8923. GET /listings?marketplace=amazon&min_margin_gap=5 (listings with margin below threshold), GET /queue (pending reprice items with diff data), POST /queue/approve (body: {item_ids:[...]} or {approve_all: true}), POST /queue/submit (trigger BatchSubmitter for approved items), GET /analytics/margin-trend?sku=:sku&days=30 (DuckDB query), GET /analytics/reprice-history?days=7, GET /settings/connections (marketplace status + token expiry_at + token_status), POST /settings/connect/:marketplace (initiate OAuth2 flow), POST /settings/refresh/:marketplace (manual token refresh trigger), GET /health.
reference.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 token storage: all access tokens and refresh tokens stored via Electron safeStorage (OS AES-256 keychain) referenced by key name only in DuckDB marketplace_credentials; actual token values never written to DuckDB or log files. Token lifecycle: token_expires_at tracked in DuckDB (non-sensitive metadata); automatic refresh attempt at 2-hour threshold; refresh_failed status triggers UI warning and polling abort. Authorization headers redacted in sidecar debug logs via FastAPI middleware. DuckDB file stored in OS app data directory with user-only file permissions (chmod 600 on macOS/Linux, restricted ACL on Windows). TLS 1.3 enforced for all outbound marketplace API calls. Marketplace API rate limits enforced by token bucket per marketplace to comply with ToS.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Electron Forge builds DMG (macOS, notarized with Apple Developer ID) and NSIS installer (Windows, EV code-signed) via GitHub Actions matrix runners. Binaries uploaded to self-hosted Electron auto-update server on tag push. GitHub Actions CI: pytest for FastAPI sidecar (Poller token refresh logic, RepricingEngine margin calculation, BatchSubmitter rate limit compliance tests at 90% coverage), Vitest for Vue 3 components (queue table, settings page), Electron Forge build on macOS-latest and windows-latest runners. Environments: dev (npm run dev with hot reload), beta (code-signed build distributed to 10 reseller testers), release (full distribution via auto-update). DuckDB file stored in OS app data; no server infrastructure required for v1.
reference.observability: Sentry Electron SDK captures renderer and main process crashes with stack traces; per-version Sentry projects for regression tracking. Local rotating log file in OS app data directory (7-day retention): structured JSON logs from FastAPI sidecar with traceId per poll cycle and per reprice run. Key log events: poll_started, poll_completed (with SKU count), token_refresh_attempted, token_refresh_failed, reprice_submitted (with marketplace and count), reprice_failed (with error code). No server-side monitoring for v1; Sentry alert rules for token_refresh_failed error spike.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Near-zero infrastructure cost for v1: self-hosted auto-update server on 10 USD/month VPS; no cloud DB. DuckDB handles 10,000 SKUs with 90-day competitor price history (approx 260 million rows at 15-minute intervals) in under 500 MB; queries complete in <500ms via columnar scan. Growth path: multi-seat v2 will require central DuckDB or MotherDuck cloud sync; current local architecture scales to single-user workload without modification.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Amazon SP-API OAuth2 connection with token expiry monitoring and auto-refresh, listing snapshot import, 15-minute competitor price poller, margin rule builder (floor/ceiling/target margin), reprice queue with diff table and approve/approve-all flow, batch reprice submission to SP-API, basic analytics (margin trend per ASIN), DuckDB 90-day retention cleanup job, Electron Forge macOS and Windows builds with code signing, Sentry integration. Phase 2 (8 weeks): eBay and Walmart Marketplace connections with their respective OAuth2 and rate limit profiles, cross-marketplace analytics dashboard, channel performance reports, Parquet archive export for DuckDB purge. Phase 3 (future): multi-seat licensing with central sync server, AI margin rule suggestions, supplier cost import from CSV/API.
reference.development_guidelines: Python sidecar uses mypy strict mode and Ruff linting; RepricingEngine and Poller modules have 90% pytest coverage. Vue 3 components use TypeScript strict; Vitest for component unit tests. Electron preload IPC types generated from FastAPI OpenAPI spec via openapi-typescript. Marketplace API credentials never appear in logs — custom Sentry before-send hook strips Authorization headers. PR review required for all changes; changelog maintained per release for auto-updater release notes.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Amazon SP-API throttling during batch reprice submission for resellers with large catalogs. Mitigation: BatchSubmitter implements token bucket (1 req/sec default, configurable) with request queue; user sees progress bar during submission. Risk: DuckDB file corruption on crash during write. Mitigation: DuckDB WAL mode; Sentry crash report triggered on abnormal exit; user prompted to run CHECKPOINT on next launch if WAL file detected. Tradeoff: local DuckDB removes cloud egress costs but limits multi-device sync for v1 — accepted by target single-seat resellers; multi-seat sync deferred to v2 with MotherDuck or central Postgres.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Token expiry monitoring: per-marketplace token_expires_at stored in DuckDB; auto-refresh at 2-hour threshold; UI warning at 24 hours — ISS-003 resolved. Walmart Marketplace rate limits: 10 req/sec per credential, confirmed in Walmart Marketplace API docs. eBay rate limits: 5 req/sec for Sell Inventory API. DuckDB file size warning threshold set at 2 GB with archive-and-purge guidance in settings.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two high-severity issues: operator data isolation at the PostgreSQL database layer (ISS-001) and the NPC portability flow async design (ISS-002). Adding explicit RLS policy definitions and redefining the porting endpoint as async 202 with polling. Also adding operator certificate lifecycle and OCSP revocation to the security section.

fix_report.expected_outcome: Operator data fully isolated at database layer; no cross-operator data access possible
