parsed.title: MVNO SIM Lifecycle Management API v2
parsed.executive_summary: A carrier-grade Go 1.22 Gin REST API on AWS ECS Fargate enabling MVNO operators to programmatically manage SIM card lifecycle: activate, suspend, port, and assign data plans. This revision adds operator data isolation at the PostgreSQL database layer via RLS policies and redesigns the NPC portability flow as async with polling, resolving both high-severity issues from round 1.
parsed.architecture_overview: Go 1.22 Gin microservice deployed on ECS Fargate in us-east-1a/1c/1d behind an ALB with mTLS termination. All partner connections present valid operator certificates validated against a CA trust store; JWT claims carry operator_id and allowed_operations scope. PostgreSQL 16 stores subscriber records with row-level security policies enforcing operator_id scoping on all tables. Redis 7 handles idempotency key deduplication and rate limit counters per operator. HLRClient abstraction layer enables vendor swap and mock injection. Webhook delivery worker handles quota events with 72-hour retry. NPC porting uses async 202 pattern with GET /v1/port-requests/:id polling for regulatory validation status.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Web framework: Gin with custom middleware chain. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies. Cache: Redis 7. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager. Hosting: AWS ECS Fargate. Container registry: ECR. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM. Certificates: AWS ACM with private CA for operator mTLS.
parsed.functional_feature_map: SIM Activation: POST /v1/sims/activate with ICCID and operator_id; validates ICCID format, checks for existing active SIM, assigns MSISDN from pool, provisions HLR, returns 201 with subscriber_id. SIM Suspension: DELETE /v1/sims/:iccid/suspend; marks status suspended, triggers HLR update. SIM Reactivation: POST /v1/sims/:iccid/reactivate; re-enables MSISDN, re-provisions HLR. NPC Porting: POST /v1/sims/:iccid/port returns 202 Accepted; GET /v1/port-requests/:id polls NPC validation status (pending/validated/completed/rejected). Data Plan Assignment: POST /v1/sims/:iccid/data-plan with plan_id; assigns quota and rate_plan_id. Quota Webhooks: POST /v1/webhooks/quota with subscriber_id, used_data_mb, remaining_mb. Operator Audit Log: GET /v1/audit-log?operator_id=&start=&end=.
parsed.system_components: ALB with mTLS listener (ACM private CA), ECS Fargate task (Go Gin service), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (RLS policies per operator_id), Redis 7 ElastiCache (idempotency keys, rate limit counters), HLRClient abstraction (HLR vendor adapter), WebhookDeliveryWorker (sidecar), NPCAsyncPollingJob (sidecar), AWS Secrets Manager (HLR credentials, NPC API keys), ECR (Docker images), GitHub Actions (CI/CD), Datadog APM (Go tracer), PagerDuty (SLA alerts).
parsed.workflows: SIM Activation: Operator POSTs to /v1/sims/activate with ICCID and operator_id JWT claim; Gin middleware validates mTLS cert and JWT; HLRClient calls provision() with ICCID and operator_id; if HLR returns success, PostgreSQL INSERT sim_cards with RLS policy enforcement (only operator_id matches JWT), INSERT subscribers, INSERT activation_events; return 201 with subscriber_id and MSISDN; if HLR times out after 30 seconds, circuit breaker fires and returns 503 with retry-after. NPC Porting Async: Operator POSTs to /v1/sims/:iccid/port; Gin validates mTLS and JWT; HLRClient calls port_request_create() which calls NPC API POST /npc/port-requests; NPC returns port_request_id; PostgreSQL INSERT port_requests with status=pending; return 202 Accepted with Location /v1/port-requests/:id; Operator polls GET /v1/port-requests/:id; Gin returns {status: pending|validated|completed|rejected, npc_reference, completed_at}; if NPC returns rejected, HLRClient calls cancel_port_request(). Quota Webhook: HLR triggers POST /v1/webhooks/quota with subscriber_id and usage metrics; Gin validates mTLS and JWT; Redis INCR used_data_mb counter; if quota threshold reached, POST to operator billing system; if delivery fails, WebhookDeliveryWorker retries up to 72 hours.
parsed.data_model: sim_cards (iccid VARCHAR PK, msisdn VARCHAR UNIQUE, operator_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, status ENUM active/suspended/ported/cancelled, data_plan_id VARCHAR, hlr_provisioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- RLS policy: USING (operator_id = current_setting('app.current_operator_id')::VARCHAR). subscribers (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, operator_subscriber_id VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). port_requests (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, donor_msisdn VARCHAR, npc_reference VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/validated/completed/rejected, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- RLS policy: USING (operator_id = current_setting('app.current_operator_id')::VARCHAR). data_plans (id VARCHAR PK, operator_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, name VARCHAR, quota_mb INTEGER, rate_plan_id VARCHAR, validity_days INTEGER). activation_events (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, operator_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, event_type VARCHAR, payload_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- partitioned by month. subscriber_access_log (id UUID PK, operator_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, subscriber_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) -- append-only, no DELETE.
parsed.api_design: POST /v1/sims/activate -- body: {iccid, operator_id}; returns 201 {subscriber_id, msisdn, hlr_provisioned_at}. DELETE /v1/sims/:iccid/suspend -- returns 204. POST /v1/sims/:iccid/reactivate -- returns 200. POST /v1/sims/:iccid/port -- returns 202 Accepted {port_request_id, location: /v1/port-requests/:id}. GET /v1/port-requests/:id -- returns {status: pending|validated|completed|rejected, npc_reference, initiated_at, completed_at, error_code}. POST /v1/sims/:iccid/data-plan -- body: {plan_id}; returns 200. POST /v1/webhooks/quota -- body: {subscriber_id, used_data_mb, remaining_mb}; returns 202. GET /v1/audit-log?operator_id=&start=&end= -- returns array of access events. All endpoints require mTLS and JWT Bearer; operator_id and allowed_operations claims validated in Gin middleware.
parsed.security_and_compliance: mTLS enforced at ALB listener with ACM private CA; operator certificates rotated every 90 days; OCSP stapling required for all connections. JWT claims validated by Gin middleware: operator_id and allowed_operations scopes enforced. PostgreSQL RLS policies on all tables enforce operator_id scoping at database layer. AWS Secrets Manager for HLR credentials and NPC API keys with 90-day rotation. CPNI compliance: subscriber_access_log captures every subscriber data access by operator_id; forwarded to Datadog SIEM for retention. CTIA guidelines: subscriber records encrypted at rest; HLR credentials isolated in Secrets Manager. FCC Part 64: subscriber data access logged with timestamp and operator_id.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Go test with race detector, golangci-lint, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with 2-task minimum healthy. SLA gate: p99 latency check against Datadog metric sim_activation_latency_ms in staging before production promotion. Environments: dev (single AZ, no RDS Multi-AZ), staging (multi-AZ, synthetic load test), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c/1d, RDS Multi-AZ, Auto Scaling 4-20 tasks on CPU 60%). Rollback: ECS service rollback to previous task definition on health check failure within 5 minutes. Secrets Manager automatic rotation for HLR credentials and NPC keys.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM Go tracer on all Gin handlers. Custom metrics: sim_activation_latency_ms (p99), hlr_timeout_count (per operator), port_request_age_hours (avg), webhook_delivery_failures (per operator), rls_policy_violation_count. PagerDuty P1 alerts: HLR circuit breaker open, p99 activation latency >200ms, RLS policy violation detected, SLA breach (uptime <99.95% for 5 minutes).
parsed.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$180/month for 4 tasks baseline. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.r6g.large: ~$350/month. ElastiCache Redis cache.r6g.large: ~$150/month. ALB: ~$50/month. Total: ~$730/month at baseline. Scales to 20 tasks at peak activation periods; Auto Scaling handles capacity without pre-warming.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): mTLS + JWT auth, SIM activation with ICCID validation and MSISDN assignment, HLR provisioning via HLRClient abstraction, operator data isolation via RLS policies, PostgreSQL audit log, GitHub Actions CI with SLA gate, ECS Fargate deployment. Phase 2 (4 weeks): NPC porting async flow with polling, quota webhook callbacks, data plan assignment, operator audit log export to Datadog SIEM. Phase 3 (4 weeks): eSIM profile management, real-time charging integration, bulk SIM provisioning batch API.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforced in CI. All HLRClient calls must go through the HLRClient interface; no direct HLR vendor library calls in handlers. Idempotency keys must be checked before any state transition; duplicate activations rejected with 409. RLS policy enforcement must be verified in integration tests using a test operator_id. All porting status changes must be written to port_requests table before returning to operator. No cross-operator data access in any query; enforced by RLS policies and JWT middleware.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HLR vendor latency variance causes activation timeouts. Mitigation: circuit breaker with 10-second timeout; return 503 with retry-after to avoid misleading operators. Risk: NPC regulatory validation takes hours; async polling adds latency to porting. Mitigation: async 202 pattern with polling endpoint; operators can poll at their own cadence. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS policies add query overhead (~5ms per query) vs. application-layer filtering; accepted for CPNI compliance and operator isolation. Tradeoff: Redis idempotency keys add memory at scale; acceptable at carrier scale with 24-hour TTL.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed async 202 pattern for NPC porting over synchronous 200 response to reflect regulatory validation latency. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS policies over application-layer filtering for operator data isolation to enforce CPNI compliance at database layer. Confirmed 90-day certificate rotation for operator mTLS certificates to align with Secrets Manager rotation policy.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by adding explicit PostgreSQL RLS policy definitions ensuring operator_id-scoped query isolation at the database layer. Resolving ISS-002 by redesigning the number portability endpoint as async with 202 Accepted and a dedicated status polling resource, reflecting NPC regulatory processing latency. mTLS certificate lifecycle and CPNI audit logging get explicit treatment given the high risk level and CPNI compliance context.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit PostgreSQL RLS policy definitions to data_model section: CREATE POLICY operator_isolation ON sim_cards USING (operator_id = current_setting('app.operator_id')::uuid). All query paths now set app.operator_id from JWT claims before execution. api_design and security_and_compliance sections updated to describe enforcement mechanism.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auditor confirms database-layer operator isolation is demonstrably enforced with RLS policy syntax present

fix_report.action_taken: Redesigned portability endpoint: POST /v1/port-requests returns 202 Accepted with Location: /v1/port-requests/:id header. Added GET /v1/port-requests/:id returning status ENUM pending/validated/completed/rejected and NPC reference. NPC polling background job described. Workflow section updated with async portability state machine.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Portability flow correctly reflects NPC regulatory async processing; no more synchronous completion assumption

reference.title: MVNO SIM Lifecycle Management API — Carrier Grade
reference.executive_summary: A carrier-grade Go REST API enabling MVNO operators to manage full SIM card lifecycles including activation with HLR provisioning, suspension, async number portability via NPC, and data plan assignment. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with mTLS partner authentication, operator-scoped PostgreSQL RLS, Redis idempotency, and CPNI-compliant audit logging meeting FCC Part 64 requirements.
reference.architecture_overview: Go 1.22 Gin service on ECS Fargate (us-east-1, multi-AZ) sitting behind an ALB that terminates mTLS from MVNO operator systems. Service decomposes into sim_lifecycle, porting, data_plans, webhooks, hlr_client, and audit domain packages. HLR provisioning calls are wrapped by an HLRClient interface supporting circuit breaking and timeout handling. PostgreSQL 16 enforces operator data isolation via row-level security policies binding queries to operator_id from JWT claims. Redis 7 provides idempotency key deduplication and rate limit counters. Webhook worker delivers quota exhaustion callbacks to operator endpoints with exponential backoff retry.
reference.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, pgx/v5, go-redis/v9, otelgo for distributed tracing. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies, partitioned activation_events table. Cache: Redis 7 for idempotency TTL and rate counters. Infra: ECS Fargate, ALB with mTLS termination, AWS Secrets Manager, CloudWatch, Datadog APM. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, Go race detector, ECR, ECS rolling deploy.
reference.functional_feature_map: SIM Activation: ICCID validation, HLR provisioning, MSISDN assignment, idempotency enforcement. Suspension/Reactivation: atomic SIM state transitions with HLR sync. Number Portability: async NPC submission, regulatory validation polling, atomic subscriber migration on approval. Data Plan Assignment: quota configuration and rate plan binding with HLR update. Quota Webhook Delivery: operator endpoint callbacks with retry. Operator Audit Log: CPNI-compliant subscriber access logging.
reference.system_components: ALB with mTLS listener (operator certificate trust store); ECS Fargate cluster (4-20 tasks, CPU 60% target autoscale); Go API service with Gin router and operator_id middleware; HLRClient module with circuit breaker (10s timeout, 5-failure threshold); PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies and monthly activation_events partitions; Redis 7 for idempotency keys (24h TTL) and rate limit counters; SQS-backed webhook delivery worker with exponential backoff; Secrets Manager for HLR credentials and NPC API keys.
reference.workflows: SIM Activation: MVNO POST /v1/sims/activate with ICCID and operator JWT; idempotency key checked in Redis; ICCID validated against sim_cards table; HLRClient provisions SIM on HLR within 30s timeout; MSISDN assigned from operator pool; sim_cards row inserted with RLS operator_id binding; activation_event logged; 201 response with assigned MSISDN. Number Portability (Async): MVNO POST /v1/port-requests with ICCID and donor MSISDN; port_requests row created with status=pending; NPC API called to register portability request; 202 Accepted returned with Location: /v1/port-requests/:id; NPC polling job tracks status transitions pending->validated->completed; on completion subscriber record atomically migrated and HLR updated; webhook notifies operator. Quota Exhaustion Webhook: Policy enforcement node publishes quota event; webhook worker reads from SQS; operator endpoint called with event payload; exponential backoff on failure up to 72h.
reference.data_model: sim_cards (iccid VARCHAR PK, msisdn VARCHAR UNIQUE, operator_id UUID NOT NULL, status ENUM active/suspended/ported/cancelled, data_plan_id UUID FK, hlr_provisioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). PostgreSQL RLS: CREATE POLICY operator_isolation ON sim_cards USING (operator_id = current_setting('app.operator_id')::uuid); policies identical on port_requests and activation_events. subscribers (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, operator_subscriber_id VARCHAR, created_at). port_requests (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, donor_msisdn VARCHAR, npc_reference VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/validated/completed/rejected, initiated_at, completed_at). data_plans (id UUID PK, operator_id UUID, name VARCHAR, quota_mb INT, rate_plan_id VARCHAR, validity_days INT). activation_events (id UUID PK, iccid VARCHAR FK, operator_id UUID, event_type VARCHAR, payload_json JSONB, created_at) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at). rate_plans (id UUID PK, operator_id UUID, plan_code VARCHAR, per_mb_rate NUMERIC).
reference.api_design: RESTful /v1 with Idempotency-Key header on all mutations. POST /v1/sims/activate {iccid, operator_subscriber_id, data_plan_id} -> 201 {msisdn, iccid, status}. POST /v1/sims/:iccid/suspend -> 200 {status}. POST /v1/sims/:iccid/reactivate -> 200 {status}. POST /v1/port-requests {iccid, donor_msisdn, target_msisdn} -> 202 {id, status, location}. GET /v1/port-requests/:id -> 200 {id, status ENUM, npc_reference, completed_at}. PUT /v1/sims/:iccid/data-plan {data_plan_id} -> 200 {quota_mb, validity_days}. GET /v1/audit-log?operator_id=&from=&to= -> 200 paginated CPNI audit entries. All endpoints enforce operator_id from JWT claims against RLS context variable.
reference.security_and_compliance: mTLS: ALB listener configured with operator CA bundle; operator certificates rotated every 365 days with OCSP stapling; revocation via OCSP responder URL embedded in certs. JWT: RS256, operator_id and allowed_operations claims; 1-hour expiry; Go middleware sets app.operator_id session variable for RLS before each query. CPNI: subscriber_access_log captures operator_id, iccid, action, requesting_user, justification, timestamp; append-only with no UPDATE/DELETE privileges; forwarded to Datadog SIEM. IP allowlist: per-operator CIDR ranges enforced at ALB security group. Rate limiting: 1000 req/min per operator enforced via Redis sliding window. FCC Part 64: subscriber data access restricted to authorized operator scope; CPNI disclosure procedures documented in runbook.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate us-east-1a/1c/1d; ALB with mTLS, WAF (SQLi/XSS rules), and access logs to S3. GitHub Actions CI: golangci-lint, go test -race, 85% coverage gate, docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy (minimum 2 healthy tasks). Staging SLA gate: automated p99 latency check against 200ms threshold before production promotion. Secrets Manager: HLR credentials, NPC API keys, DB password — 90-day rotation with Lambda rotator. Blue/green rollback via ECS service rollback to previous task definition. Aurora Multi-AZ with automated failover for PostgreSQL. Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ with automatic failover.
reference.observability: Datadog APM with Go otel tracer; trace IDs propagated in all HLR and NPC calls. Custom metrics: sim_activation_duration_ms (p50/p95/p99), hlr_circuit_breaker_state, port_request_age_hours (histogram), webhook_delivery_attempt_count, operator_rate_limit_rejections. PagerDuty P1: HLR circuit breaker open >60s, activation p99 >200ms, ECS task count <2. PagerDuty P2: webhook delivery failure rate >5%, port request stuck in pending >4 hours. Structured JSON logs with correlation_id, operator_id, iccid fields for log-based alerting.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ~$800/month baseline for 4 Fargate tasks + Aurora Multi-AZ + Redis. HLR call volume drives primary scale; ECS autoscaling to 20 tasks at 1000 concurrent activations. Redis idempotency keys capped at 1M entries before LRU eviction. Activation_events monthly partitioning limits index bloat; S3 Glacier archival reduces hot storage cost at year 5.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (10 weeks): SIM activation with HLR integration, suspension/reactivation, operator mTLS auth, RLS enforcement, CPNI audit logging, CI/CD pipeline, Datadog integration. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Async number portability with NPC integration and polling endpoints, data plan assignment with quota sync. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Quota webhook delivery worker, operator audit log export API, bulk activation batch endpoint, eSIM groundwork.
reference.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 strict mode; golangci-lint with errcheck, staticcheck, gosec enabled in CI. HLRClient always mocked in unit tests; integration tests use containerized HLR simulator. RLS policies tested with operator_id cross-contamination test suite. All database migrations via golang-migrate with rollback scripts. ADR required for any HLR vendor change.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HLR vendor outage during activation surge. Mitigation: circuit breaker queues requests for retry; operators receive 503 with Retry-After header. Risk: NPC regulatory changes affecting portability SLA. Mitigation: NPC adapter abstracted behind interface for swap without API contract change. Tradeoff: RLS over application-layer operator filtering chosen for defense-in-depth at cost of session variable overhead per query. Risk: CPNI audit log growth exceeding PostgreSQL performance at high volume — mitigation: forward to Datadog SIEM and archive to S3 after 90 days.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Async portability confirmed: 202 + polling pattern selected over webhook-only to support operators without public callback endpoints. RLS selected over schema-per-operator: schema-per-operator rejected due to migration complexity at 50+ MVNO operator scale. Certificate rotation period set to 365 days with OCSP; shorter rotation deemed operationally disruptive for carrier-grade partners.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 must correct two high-severity contract violations: replace all React Native references with Flutter 3 and Dart throughout the plan, and add SQLCipher device encryption with iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore key derivation to the security_and_compliance and data_model sections. Additionally, change the sync conflict resolution from last-write-wins to keep-both-records-with-conflict-flag to honor the 'never silently discard health event records' constraint. The offline-first architecture is sound; the plan quality is weak per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all React Native references with Flutter 3, Dart, Drift ORM, and flutter_blue_plus throughout technology_stack, system_components, and functional_feature_map sections. Updated architecture_overview to describe Flutter 3 app with Drift offline storage.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan no longer violates contract frontend_stack value 'Flutter 3 with Dart'

fix_report.action_taken: Added SQLCipher device encryption with iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore key derivation to security_and_compliance and data_model sections. Updated data_model to describe SyncQueue table with SQLCipher encryption.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan now describes SQLCipher encryption as required by contract security_baseline

parsed.title: Livestock Health Tracking Mobile App v2
parsed.executive_summary: A Flutter 3 mobile app for livestock producers to log animal health events, vaccinations, and weight measurements offline in remote paddocks, with SQLCipher-encrypted local SQLite storage and a Cloud Run REST API for batch sync on connectivity restore. Firebase Auth with operation-scoped tenancy and PostgreSQL row-level security enforce data isolation across farm operations. This revision corrects the two high-severity contract violations from Round 1: replaces React Native with Flutter 3 throughout, and adds SQLCipher device encryption with iOS Secure Enclave and Android Keystore key derivation.
parsed.architecture_overview: Flutter 3 mobile app with Drift SQLite ORM and SQLCipher encryption stores all offline health event, vaccination, and weight data locally on the device. When connectivity is available, a batch sync endpoint on GCP Cloud Run uploads pending records to PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL. The sync endpoint uses a keep-both-records-with-conflict-flag policy: if a health event with the same animal_id and event_type exists on both client and server within a 60-second window, both records are retained with conflict_flag=true rather than discarding either. Firebase Auth JWT tokens carry an operation_id claim scoped to the user's assigned farm operation; PostgreSQL row-level security policies enforce operation_id scoping on all queries. A Bull queue on Redis 7 handles traceability report generation jobs triggered by market shipment events. The app uses flutter_blue_plus for BLE RFID ear tag scanning and Riverpod 2 for offline-first state management with optimistic local writes.
parsed.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3, Dart, Drift 2 ORM with SQLCipher, flutter_blue_plus for RFID BLE reader, Riverpod 2 for state management, firebase_auth for Firebase Auth integration. Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, Bull 3 for report queue, Sequelize ORM for PostgreSQL, jsonwebtoken for Firebase token validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL with RLS, Redis 7 Memorystore for Bull queue and session cache. Auth: Firebase Auth with custom token claims for operation_id scoping. Hosting: GCP Cloud Run (min 1 instance for cold start avoidance), Cloud SQL, Memorystore. CI/CD: Bitrise for Flutter, GitHub Actions for Node.js. Crash reporting: Firebase Crashlytics.
parsed.functional_feature_map: RFID Animal Scan: field worker opens animal detail screen, taps 'Scan RFID', app activates flutter_blue_plus BLE reader, reads ear tag number, resolves to animal record by tag number and operation_id. Health Event Logging: offline-capable screen for logging illness, treatment, vaccination, death, or observation events; event type and symptom multi-select; treatment drug picker with withdrawal period auto-calculation; notes text field; local SQLite write immediately with sync status indicator. Offline Sync Queue: Drift SyncQueue table stores pending uploads with local_id, entity_type, payload_json, and sync_status; batch uploaded to Cloud Run on connectivity restore. Lot and Pen Management: field worker assigns animals to lots and pens via lot_assignments table; lot summary counts in app home screen. Birth Record Entry: record birth date, sire, dam, sex, species, RFID tag number; auto-populates current_lot_id from operation assignment. Market Shipment Traceability: manager selects shipment date and buyer, app generates PDF report via Bull queue job showing all animals in lots shipped to buyer with vaccination and health event history.
parsed.system_components: Flutter 3 mobile app (iOS 16+, Android 12+): Drift SQLite ORM with SQLCipher, flutter_blue_plus RFID reader, Riverpod 2 state management, Firebase Auth SDK. GCP Cloud Run (Node.js Express sync API): min 1 instance for cold start avoidance, max 20 for burst sync load during morning and evening check times. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: operation_id RLS policies, vaccination_records withdrawal period indexes. Redis 7 Memorystore: Bull queue for report jobs, Firebase session cache. Firebase Auth: custom token issuance with operation_id claim. GCP Cloud Tasks: scheduled job for operation data deletion 30 days post-cancellation.
parsed.workflows: Offline Health Event Logging: field worker opens animal detail screen in remote paddock with no connectivity; taps 'Log Health Event'; selects event type (illness, treatment, vaccination, death, observation); enters symptom and notes; treatment drug picker auto-calculates withdrawal_days from vaccine product; local SQLite health_events record written with client_created_at and conflict_flag=false; SyncQueue row inserted with local_id, entity_type, payload_json, and sync_status=pending; app shows sync status badge in app bar. Connectivity Restore Sync: field worker returns to cellular coverage; app checks SyncQueue for pending uploads; batches records by operation_id; POSTs to Cloud Run /sync/health-events with array of {local_id, entity_type, payload_json, client_created_at}; server validates JWT operation_id claim; inserts health_events records with server_created_at; if server record exists within 60s window, sets conflict_flag=true on both records; returns array of {local_id, server_id, status}; app updates SyncQueue sync_status to synced and removes from queue. Traceability Report Generation: manager selects market shipment date and buyer; POSTs to /reports/traceability; server inserts report_job into Redis queue; Bull worker job queries health_events and vaccination_records for all animals in lots shipped to buyer, generates PDF via html2pdf, stores in Cloud Storage, returns download URL; field worker opens PDF in app browser showing birth-to-market traceability chain.
parsed.data_model: animals (id UUID PK, operation_id UUID FK, rfid_tag_number STRING UNIQUE per operation, species ENUM cattle/swine/sheep, birth_date DATE, sex ENUM, dam_id UUID nullable, sire_id UUID nullable, current_lot_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/sold/dead). health_events (id UUID PK, animal_id UUID FK, operation_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM illness/treatment/vaccination/death/observation, event_date DATE, notes TEXT, recorded_by UUID FK users, conflict_flag BOOL DEFAULT false, client_created_at TIMESTAMP, server_created_at TIMESTAMP). vaccination_records (id UUID PK, health_event_id UUID FK, vaccine_product STRING, dose_ml FLOAT, route ENUM subcutaneous/intramuscular/oral, withdrawal_days INT, withdrawal_date DATE COMPUTED). lot_assignments (animal_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, released_at TIMESTAMP nullable). market_shipments (id UUID PK, operation_id UUID FK, shipment_date DATE, destination_buyer STRING, lot_ids UUID[]). SyncQueue (local_id STRING PK, entity_type STRING, payload_json JSONB, sync_status ENUM pending/uploading/synced/failed, created_at TIMESTAMP) — local SQLite table encrypted with SQLCipher using key derived from Firebase UID via platform secure storage (iOS Keychain/Secure Enclave, Android Keystore).
parsed.api_design: POST /sync/health-events: body {operation_id: string, records: [{local_id, entity_type, payload_json, client_created_at}]}; returns {records: [{local_id, server_id, status}]}. GET /animals/:id: returns animal record with vaccination and health event history scoped to operation_id. POST /animals: body {rfid_tag_number, species, birth_date, sex, dam_id, sire_id, current_lot_id}; returns created animal record. POST /reports/traceability: body {shipment_date, destination_buyer, lot_ids[]}; returns {report_id, status, download_url}. GET /reports/:id: returns report status. All endpoints require Firebase Auth JWT Bearer token; operation_id claim validated in Express middleware.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated in Express middleware; operation_id claim extracted from custom token claims and used for all database queries. PostgreSQL row-level security policies: all tables include operation_id column; RLS policy enforces operation_id = current_setting('app.operation_id') for all queries. SQLCipher encryption on local SQLite: key derived from Firebase UID via platform secure storage (iOS Keychain/Secure Enclave, Android Keystore) using SQLCipher 4.x key derivation API; database re-encryption handled via migration that re-encrypts existing records without re-opening the database. HTTPS TLS 1.3 enforced on Cloud Run. Cloud SQL access restricted to Cloud Run service account with least-privilege IAM binding. Cloud Run invocations logged to Cloud Logging with operation_id and user_id for audit trail.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GCP Cloud Run: Node.js 20 runtime, min 1 instance to prevent cold start latency on sync endpoint, max 20 instances for burst sync load during morning and evening check times. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: automated daily backups to Cloud Storage, point-in-time recovery enabled. Redis Memorystore: single-zone standard tier for Bull queue. Bitrise: Flutter build and test on every PR; TestFlight and Play Store internal track distribution on main merge; bi-weekly manual promotion to production track after field testing sign-off. GitHub Actions: Node.js lint, test (Jest with Cloud SQL emulator), docker build, Cloud Run deploy on merge to main. Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reporting with Flutter crashlytics plugin; sync error rate tracked as custom metric.
parsed.observability: Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reporting with Flutter crashlytics plugin; sync error rate tracked as custom metric. Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request count and latency, Cloud SQL connection pool utilization, Redis memory. Custom metrics: sync_success_rate (target >99%), conflict_rate (target <1%), report_generation_duration_p50. Alerts: Cloud Run error rate >5% triggers PagerDuty; sync_success_rate <95% over 15-minute window triggers Slack notification to ops.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run: ~$50/month at expected sync load (morning and evening bursts). Cloud SQL: ~$150/month for standard tier. Redis Memorystore: ~$30/month. Firebase Auth: free tier covers expected user count. Total estimated ~$250/month for 500 active users. Scaling: Cloud Run auto-scales to 20 instances during burst sync periods; Cloud SQL read replica added at 10,000 daily sync records.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Flutter 3 app with Drift SQLite and SQLCipher offline storage, RFID ear tag scan via flutter_blue_plus, health event logging with offline queue, Cloud Run sync endpoint with keep-both-records-with-conflict-flag policy, Firebase Auth with operation_id scoping, PostgreSQL Cloud SQL with RLS, TestFlight and Play Store internal track distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Weight measurement logging with Bluetooth scale integration, vaccination withdrawal period calendar view, lot and pen assignment management, basic animal detail view. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Market shipment traceability report generation via Bull queue, PDF download in app browser, operation data deletion Cloud Tasks job, herd analytics dashboard.
parsed.development_guidelines: Flutter: flutter analyze enforced in Bitrise; flutter test coverage >80% for sync and auth modules. Drift migrations must include SQLCipher re-encryption steps for any schema changes that add or remove columns from SyncQueue. Node.js: ESLint, Jest with Cloud SQL emulator, 80% test coverage gate. Sync endpoint must return conflict records with conflict_flag=true rather than discarding them. All database queries must include operation_id scoping; RLS policy must be tested in integration tests. SQLCipher key derivation must be tested with Firebase UID mock in unit tests.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: flutter_blue_plus BLE reader pairing reliability varies by device model and Android BLE stack; mitigation: test against Allflex RS420 reader in lab before field deployment. Risk: SQLCipher key derivation on iOS Secure Enclave requires iOS 15+; mitigation: fallback to iOS Keychain on iOS 14+ devices. Tradeoff: keep-both-records-with-conflict-flag policy adds storage overhead for duplicate records but is required to honor the 'never silently discard health event records' constraint. Tradeoff: Cloud Run cold start adds 2-4 second latency to first sync after idle period; min-instances=1 prevents cold start but adds ~$20/month baseline cost.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SQLCipher over Tresorit-style encrypted SQLite for simpler key management on device. Confirmed keep-both-records-with-conflict-flag over last-write-wins to honor health record completeness constraint. Confirmed Cloud Run over Cloud Functions for better request timeout handling during sync batch uploads. Confirmed Flutter 3 over React Native for native iOS and Android performance and BLE reader integration.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 plan for livestock mobile app must fix two critical contract violations from Round 1: (1) replace React Native with Flutter 3 throughout, and (2) add SQLCipher device encryption with platform-native key derivation. Also fixing the sync conflict resolution policy to keep-both-with-conflict-flag to honor the health record completeness constraint. The plan_quality is flawed because this round's target_output intentionally demonstrates a partially corrected plan where ISS-001 and ISS-002 are fixed but the data_model section still uses incorrect entity naming inconsistent with the contract.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all React Native and Expo references with Flutter 3, Dart, Drift ORM, Riverpod 2 state management, and flutter_blue_plus RFID BLE integration throughout technology_stack, system_components, and development_guidelines sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Contract violation ISS-001 resolved; plan now correctly reflects Flutter 3 as specified in frozen_requirement_contract.frontend_stack

fix_report.action_taken: Added SQLCipher device encryption with iOS Keychain/Secure Enclave key derivation and Android Keystore key derivation to security_and_compliance and data_model sections; specified SQLCipher version 4.x and key rotation on password change.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Contract violation ISS-002 resolved; device encryption now matches contract security_baseline requirement

reference.title: Livestock Health Tracking Mobile App
reference.executive_summary: Flutter 3 offline-first mobile app for livestock producers logging individual animal health events, vaccinations, and RFID ear tag scans in remote paddocks without cellular connectivity. Local Drift/SQLite storage with SQLCipher encryption queues records for batch sync to a Node.js Express Cloud Run API backed by PostgreSQL 16. Firebase Auth with operation_id row-level security enforces multi-operation tenancy. Conflict resolution keeps both concurrent records with conflict_flag rather than silently discarding, honoring the traceability completeness constraint.
reference.architecture_overview: Flutter 3 mobile app with Drift ORM managing local SQLite (SQLCipher-encrypted) for offline-first data entry. Riverpod 2 providers implement optimistic local write: health events, vaccination records, and RFID scan results written immediately to device SQLite SyncQueue, then batch-uploaded to Cloud Run Node.js Express API on connectivity restore. PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL on GCP stores the authoritative animal health record with operation_id row-level security for multi-operation tenancy. Sync conflict policy: server creates both records with conflict_flag=true when concurrent edits detected, never discarding either. Firebase Auth issues JWT with operation_id custom claim; Express middleware validates JWT and sets PostgreSQL session variable for RLS enforcement. Bull queue on Redis Memorystore handles background traceability PDF report generation.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22 with Dart 3, Drift 2.x SQLite ORM, SQLCipher 4.x for device database encryption, Riverpod 2 for state management, flutter_blue_plus for BLE RFID reader (Allflex RS420) pairing, Firebase Auth SDK for JWT token management. Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, firebase-admin for JWT validation, Bull 4 with Redis for report job queue, pg driver for PostgreSQL. Cloud: GCP Cloud Run (min 1 instance), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, Redis Memorystore, Firebase Auth, Google Cloud Monitoring, Firebase Crashlytics. CI/CD: Bitrise for Flutter iOS/Android builds and TestFlight/Play Store distribution; GitHub Actions for Node.js API.
reference.functional_feature_map: RFID Ear Tag Scan: flutter_blue_plus connects to Allflex RS420 BLE reader, receives tag number, looks up or creates animal record locally. Health Event Entry: offline-capable form with event_type selector (illness/treatment/vaccination/death/observation), symptom multi-select from species-specific symptom library, treatment drug picker with automatic withdrawal period calculation from drug database. Vaccination Record: records vaccine product, dose, route, and computes withdrawal_date from drug withdrawal_days. Lot and Pen Management: assign animals to lots, view lot summary with active animal count and pending health events. Sync Status: SyncNotifier watches connectivity, shows pending upload count in app bar, executes batch upload on connectivity restore. Market Shipment Traceability Report: links all animals in a lot to their complete health history and validates antibiotic withdrawal compliance before shipment (Phase 2).
reference.system_components: Flutter App: Drift database (SQLCipher key from platform secure storage), SyncQueue table (local_id, entity_type, payload_json, sync_status, attempt_count), AnimalRepository (Drift queries + cloud sync overlay), HealthEventRepository (offline write + queue), SyncCoordinator (connectivity watch via connectivity_plus, batch upload to /sync endpoint). Node.js Express API: authMiddleware (Firebase JWT validation, operation_id extraction), syncRouter (POST /sync/health-events batch upsert with conflict detection), animalsRouter (CRUD with RLS), reportRouter (Bull job enqueue for PDF generation). PostgreSQL 16: animals, health_events (with conflict_flag), vaccination_records, lot_assignments, market_shipments tables with operation_id RLS policies. Redis Memorystore: Bull job queue for report generation, Firebase session cache. Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reports with device model and OS version tags.
reference.workflows: Offline Health Event Logging Workflow: Field worker scans RFID tag with Allflex BLE reader via flutter_blue_plus. Flutter app looks up animal by rfid_tag_number in local Drift SQLite. Worker selects event type (e.g., vaccination), picks vaccine product from local drug list, enters dose and route. Drift writes health_event and vaccination_record to local SQLite with sync_status=pending. SyncNotifier detects no connectivity; event remains in SyncQueue. When paddock connectivity restores, SyncCoordinator batches pending events (up to 50 per request) and POSTs to /sync/health-events with idempotency key. Server inserts records scoped to operation_id, checks for concurrent edits within 60s window, creates conflict_flag=true duplicates if found. Returns local_id to server_id mapping; Drift updates sync_status to synced. Market Shipment Traceability Workflow (Phase 2): Ranch manager selects a lot for shipment, app generates traceability report request. Bull job fetches all animals in lot, joins health_event history and vaccination_records, validates withdrawal dates are cleared for all treatment records, generates PDF via PDFKit, uploads to Cloud Storage, pushes download link notification to manager.
reference.data_model: animals: {id UUID PK, operation_id UUID FK, rfid_tag_number VARCHAR, species VARCHAR, birth_date DATE, sex CHAR(1), dam_id UUID nullable FK, sire_id UUID nullable FK, current_lot_id UUID FK, status VARCHAR}. health_events: {id UUID PK, animal_id UUID FK, operation_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, event_date DATE, notes TEXT, recorded_by UUID FK, conflict_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, client_created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, server_created_at TIMESTAMPTZ}. vaccination_records: {id UUID PK, health_event_id UUID FK, vaccine_product VARCHAR, dose_ml NUMERIC, route VARCHAR, withdrawal_days INT, withdrawal_date DATE}. lot_assignments: {animal_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ}. Local SQLite SyncQueue (Drift): {local_id TEXT PK, entity_type TEXT, payload_json TEXT, sync_status TEXT, attempt_count INT, created_at INTEGER}. NOTE: data_model uses generic VARCHAR types rather than proper ENUM constraints — this is an intentional quality flaw in this revision demonstrating a plan that fixes the blocking contract violations but still has a data model quality gap.
reference.api_design: POST /sync/health-events — accepts {idempotency_key, events: [{local_id, animal_id, event_type, event_date, notes, client_created_at, vaccination_payload?}]}. Returns {synced: [{local_id, server_id}], conflicts: [{local_id, conflict_server_id}]}. GET /animals/:id — returns animal with latest health event summary. POST /animals — create animal with rfid_tag_number (unique per operation_id). GET /lots — list lots with animal counts for operation. POST /reports/traceability — enqueue PDF report Bull job for lot_id. All routes require Firebase JWT Authorization header; operation_id extracted from custom claim.
reference.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated in Express authMiddleware; operation_id custom claim used to set PostgreSQL session variable app.operation_id before each query, enabling RLS enforcement. PostgreSQL RLS: all tables include operation_id column with policy USING (operation_id = current_setting('app.operation_id')::uuid). Device encryption: SQLCipher 4.x on local Drift SQLite database. Key derivation: iOS uses SecKeyCreateRandomKey in Keychain with kSecAttrTokenIDSecureEnclave binding; Android uses Android Keystore AES-256-GCM key. Key stored in platform secure storage via flutter_secure_storage; database re-keyed on password change. USDA APHIS traceability: operation_id isolation ensures farm operations cannot access other producers' animal records. Firebase Crashlytics configured with data redaction to exclude animal health event content from crash reports.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GCP Cloud Run Node.js service: min-instances=1 (prevent cold start on morning sync burst), max-instances=20, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Redis Memorystore for Bull queue. GitHub Actions CI: ESLint, Jest tests with Cloud SQL emulator, docker build, Cloud Run deploy on main merge. Bitrise Flutter CI: flutter analyze, flutter test, flutter build ios (--release), flutter build appbundle, upload to TestFlight (iOS) and Play Store internal track (Android) on main merge. Bi-weekly field testing cycle: internal track promoted to production track after QA sign-off on offline sync and RFID BLE pairing. Firebase Crashlytics integrated in Flutter app for production crash visibility. Google Cloud Monitoring alerts: Cloud Run error rate >1%, sync endpoint P99 latency >3s.
reference.observability: Firebase Crashlytics: mobile crash reports with Flutter stack traces, device model, OS version, and Flutter version tags. Custom Crashlytics keys: sync_queue_depth (pending upload count at time of crash), last_sync_status. Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request count, error rate, P99 latency. Custom metrics logged via Cloud Logging: sync_batch_size (events per batch upload), sync_conflict_count (per-operation daily count), ble_connection_success_rate (RFID reader pairing success ratio). Alert: sync_conflict_count >10/day for any operation triggers investigation; high conflict rates indicate clock skew between worker devices.
reference.cost_and_scaling: GCP estimated $380/month: Cloud Run min-instances=1 $45, burst scaling to 20 instances during morning/evening sync $80, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-standard-2 $190, Redis Memorystore Basic 1GB $40, Firebase Auth free tier, Bitrise Team plan $25/month. Scales to 500 concurrent farm operations without architecture changes; Cloud Run auto-scales sync burst load; Cloud SQL read replica added when analytics queries degrade sync endpoint performance.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Flutter iOS and Android app with RFID ear tag scan, health event and vaccination logging, offline SQLite SyncQueue with SQLCipher, Firebase Auth, Node.js sync API, PostgreSQL with RLS, Bitrise CI/CD to TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Weight measurement logging with Bluetooth scale integration, lot management and pen assignment, market shipment traceability PDF report generation via Bull queue, antibiotic withdrawal compliance validation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Herd analytics dashboard (death rate, treatment frequency by lot), packer buyer portal integration for electronic traceability record submission, bulk import from legacy paper records via CSV.
reference.development_guidelines: Flutter: Dart null safety enforced, Riverpod 2 for all state (no setState in feature widgets), Drift migrations versioned and tested with in-memory SQLite in unit tests. SQLCipher key must never be logged or included in crash reports — Crashlytics redaction configured. Node.js: ESLint with no-console rule (use structured logging via pino), all PostgreSQL queries use parameterized statements (no string interpolation), operation_id extracted from JWT claim never from request body. Sync idempotency: SyncCoordinator includes idempotency_key (UUID v4 generated per batch) in POST /sync/health-events; server stores processed idempotency keys in Redis for 24 hours to handle retry deduplication.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Allflex RS420 BLE connection unreliable on certain Android device models due to manufacturer-specific BLE stack variations. Mitigation: manual RFID tag number entry fallback always available in UI; field test with target device hardware before GA release. Risk: SQLCipher key loss (device wipe without backup) results in unrecoverable local SQLite data. Mitigation: sync pending queue items before enabling device wipe; warn users if sync_queue_depth >0 when logout initiated. Tradeoff: keep-both-with-conflict-flag policy creates duplicate health event records requiring manual review by ranch manager for conflicting entries; accepted because data completeness for USDA APHIS traceability is more important than clean records. Risk: Cloud Run cold start (when min-instances not warm) causes 3-5s first-sync delay for early-morning field workers; mitigated by min-instances=1 baseline.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Conflict resolution confirmed as keep-both-with-conflict-flag (not last-write-wins) per explicit constraint. SQLCipher 4.x selected over Android-native Room encryption because Drift SQLite ORM has first-class SQLCipher support via sqlcipher_flutter_libs. RFID reader confirmed as Allflex RS420 BLE; flutter_blue_plus supports BLE central mode on both iOS and Android.
