 "executive_summary": "A serverless automation tool on AWS that periodically scans the cardholder data environment for PCI-DSS 4.0 evidence, stores findings in PostgreSQL, and generates pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV reports. The

parsed.thinking_summary: First-pass PCI-DSS evidence collection tool on AWS. Key decisions: Step Functions for scan orchestration with parallel workers per CDE component, FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum for the control plane API, PostgreSQL for scan result history and requirement mappings, S3 for evidence artifacts with SSE-KMS. The tool itself is in PCI scope so all Lambda execution roles must be least-privilege with no write access to CDE resources.
parsed.title: PCI-DSS Evidence Collector and QSA Report Automation Tool
parsed.executive_summary: A serverless automation tool on AWS that periodically scans the cardholder data environment for PCI-DSS 4.0 evidence, stores findings in PostgreSQL, and generates pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV reports. The tool runs as scheduled scans via EventBridge Scheduler and on-demand via API, with Step Functions orchestrating parallel workers for Config rule compliance, IAM policy review, log retention verification, and network segmentation analysis.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions state machines on a quarterly schedule. Step Functions orchestrates four parallel Lambda workers: ConfigComplianceLambda (AWS Config rule evaluation), IamPolicyReviewLambda (CDE IAM policy analysis), LogRetentionCheckLambda (CloudWatch Logs retention verification), and NetworkSegmentationLambda (network ACL analysis). All scan workers write control_check records to PostgreSQL and upload evidence artifacts to an S3 bucket with SSE-KMS. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda assembles findings into a QSA questionnaire PDF via WeasyPrint and a CSV deviation report. FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum exposes the control plane API for scan history, findings, and report download. Cognito JWT authentication protects all API endpoints.
parsed.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Mangum Lambda adapter, boto3, psycopg3. Scan workers: Python 3.12 Lambda functions with moto for mocked AWS SDK calls in tests. Report generation: WeasyPrint for PDF, csv.DictWriter for CSV. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with SSE. Artifact store: S3 with SSE-KMS and versioning. Orchestration: AWS Step Functions with ASL. Auth: Cognito user pool with RBAC. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Terraform. Monitoring: CloudWatch, CloudTrail, SNS email alerts.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Scan Execution: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions state machine on quarterly schedule; on-demand scan via POST /scans API. Scan Workers: ConfigComplianceLambda evaluates AWS Config rules against CDE resource ARNs; IamPolicyReviewLambda analyzes IAM policy statements for CDE resource references; LogRetentionCheckLambda verifies CloudWatch Logs retention periods; NetworkSegmentationLambda evaluates network ACLs and security group rules. Evidence Storage: all evidence artifacts uploaded to S3 with SSE-KMS; control_check records in PostgreSQL with evidence_s3_key. PCI Requirement Mapping: PciRequirementMapper maps scan findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs (e.g., 1.1.1, 2.2.1, 3.6.1) and sub-requirements. Deviation Detection: DeviationDetector flags findings with result=fail or severity=critical. QSA Report Generation: ReportBuilder renders QSA questionnaire responses using Jinja2 templates, exports PDF via WeasyPrint, and CSV deviation report. Report Download: GET /scans/:id/report returns PDF or CSV from S3 pre-signed URL.
parsed.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler (quarterly scan trigger), Step Functions state machine (orchestration with parallel branches), ConfigComplianceLambda (AWS Config rule evaluation), IamPolicyReviewLambda (CDE IAM policy analysis), LogRetentionCheckLambda (CloudWatch Logs retention verification), NetworkSegmentationLambda (network ACL analysis), ReportGeneratorLambda (PDF and CSV assembly), FastAPI Lambda (control plane API), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (scan result history and requirement mappings), S3 evidence bucket (PCI evidence artifacts with SSE-KMS), Cognito user pool (RBAC authentication), API Gateway v2 HTTP API (external access), CloudWatch (Lambda metrics and Step Functions execution logs), CloudTrail (S3 evidence access logging), SNS (deviation alert email notifications).
parsed.workflows: Scheduled Scan: EventBridge Scheduler fires Step Functions state machine every quarter. Step Functions starts ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, and NetworkSegmentationLambda in parallel. Each Lambda writes control_check records to PostgreSQL with pci_requirement_id, result, and evidence_s3_key. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda queries control_checks JOINed with cde_components and qsa_questionnaires, renders QSA questionnaire PDF via Jinja2 and WeasyPrint, and uploads PDF to S3. QSA Report Download: Compliance analyst calls GET /scans/:id/report. FastAPI Lambda validates Cognito JWT, queries PostgreSQL for scan_run_id, generates pre-signed S3 URL for PDF, returns URL in response. On-Demand Scan: Compliance analyst calls POST /scans with body {triggered_by: on_demand, cde_component_ids: [...]}. FastAPI validates Cognito JWT, creates scan_run record, starts Step Functions state machine with parallel workers, returns scan_run_id. Deviation Alert: DeviationDetector identifies control_check with result=fail or severity=critical. SNS publishes email alert to compliance team with scan_run_id, affected cde_component, and finding description.
parsed.data_model: cde_components (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id VARCHAR, in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_runs (id UUID PK, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pci_version VARCHAR(5), generated_report_s3_key VARCHAR). control_checks (id UUID PK, scan_run_id UUID FK, cde_component_id UUID FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type VARCHAR, result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key VARCHAR, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_findings (id UUID PK, control_check_id UUID FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description TEXT, expected_value TEXT, actual_value TEXT, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID PK, scan_run_id UUID FK, pci_version VARCHAR(5), report_s3_key VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). remediation_items (id UUID PK, audit_finding_id UUID FK, owner VARCHAR, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/complete, notes TEXT).
parsed.api_design: POST /scans — trigger on-demand scan run, body {triggered_by: on_demand, cde_component_ids: [UUID]}. GET /scans — list all scan runs with status and last scanned date. GET /scans/:id — scan run details with status, triggered_by, and duration. GET /scans/:id/results — list control_checks with pci_requirement_id, result, and evidence_s3_key. GET /scans/:id/report — download QSA PDF or CSV from S3 pre-signed URL. GET /deviations — list open audit_findings with severity and description. GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence — list evidence artifacts for a specific PCI requirement. GET /cde-components — list all registered CDE components with in_scope status.
parsed.security_and_compliance: PCI scope: tool itself is in PCI scope — all Lambda execution roles must be least-privilege with no write access to CDE resources. ScanWorkerRole: Config:GetResourceConfig, IAM:GetUser, IAM:ListAttachedGroupPolicies, Logs:FilterLogEvents, s3:PutObject on evidence bucket only. ControlPlaneRole: s3:GetObject on evidence bucket, rds:connect on PostgreSQL. Cognito JWT validated by API Gateway Lambda authorizer on all endpoints. Evidence S3 bucket: SSE-KMS, no public access, CloudTrail object-level logging enabled. RDS: Multi-AZ, automated backups, no public access. CloudTrail data events on all API Gateway and S3 access. PCI-DSS 4.0 evidence: all control_check records and evidence artifacts retained 3 years. QSA report PDFs retained 3 years. Audit findings retained until remediated plus 1 year.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages all AWS resources: Lambda functions, Step Functions state machine, API Gateway v2 HTTP API, Cognito user pool, RDS, S3 buckets, CloudWatch alarms, CloudTrail data events. GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with moto for mocked AWS SDK calls, Terraform plan in staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda zip packages built with pip and uploaded to S3 deployment bucket. Lambda aliases: scan-worker-latest and scan-worker-production. Step Functions state machine versioned in ASL. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, no CloudTrail data events), staging (Multi-AZ RDS, CloudTrail data events enabled), production (Multi-AZ RDS, CloudTrail data events enabled, CloudWatch alarms enabled). Rollback: Lambda function version alias available for immediate rollback.
parsed.observability: CloudWatch Metrics: Lambda invocation count, error rate, duration (alert if p95 exceeds 10 minutes). Step Functions execution success/failure metrics. SNS email alert for scan failure (status=failed) and for critical audit_findings created. CloudWatch Dashboard: last scan run status, open deviation count, days since last successful scan. CloudTrail data events on evidence S3 bucket for all GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject calls. Lambda function logs in CloudWatch with 1-year retention. Step Functions execution history retained 90 days.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $180/month for a Level 1 merchant with 50 CDE components: Lambda scan workers at $0.01/invocation (estimated 200 invocations per quarterly scan), RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium at $80/month, S3 storage and data transfer for evidence artifacts, API Gateway at $3/month. Step Functions state machine scales to parallel workers automatically. Lambda cold starts eliminated by keeping scan-worker-latest alias warm via EventBridge Scheduler.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Core Evidence Collection (8 weeks): AWS Config rule compliance scans for CDE segmentation, IAM policy review for CDE access, evidence artifact storage in S3 with SSE-KMS, basic PCI requirement mapping for SAQ D, CSV deviation report, Cognito RBAC, GitLab CI/CD pipeline, Terraform infrastructure as code. Phase 2 — QSA Report Generation (4 weeks): PDF QSA questionnaire report via WeasyPrint, Jinja2 template engine for requirement mapping, S3 pre-signed URL download, CloudWatch alarm for scan failure. Phase 3 — Continuous Monitoring (future): Continuous scan mode with real-time deviation alerting, Jira integration for remediation ticket creation, multi-cloud support for Azure CDE components.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff and mypy strict mode. All Lambda functions must have unit tests with moto mocking AWS SDK calls. Step Functions state machine defined in ASL with version control. Terraform modules for each AWS resource type. No secrets in Lambda environment variables — RDS credentials in Secrets Manager. Evidence S3 keys must include scan_run_id and cde_component_id in the prefix. PCI requirement mapping logic must be configurable via YAML files for PCI-DSS version switching.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Lambda 15-minute timeout exceeded during large CDE scan. Mitigation: Step Functions parallel workers allow partial scan completion; ReportGeneratorLambda can assemble findings from partial results. Risk: AWS Config rule evaluation returns stale data if CDE resources are modified between scan schedule and report generation. Mitigation: scan_run record includes timestamp; ReportGeneratorLambda queries control_checks for scans within the last 7 days. Tradeoff: Step Functions adds operational overhead vs a simple cron job — justified by audit trail and retry logic. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for scan result history — relational model needed for PCI requirement mapping joins.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Firewall export: NetworkSegmentationLambda will call DescribeNetworkFirewallPolicy API for AWS Network Firewall rules. Evidence format: PDF via WeasyPrint and CSV deviation report — PDF for QSA submission, CSV for compliance team review. PCI version: PCI-DSS 4.0 mapping logic will be configurable via YAML files for future version transition.

reference.thinking_summary: Building a PCI-DSS evidence automation tool as a serverless AWS architecture. The key design decisions are Step Functions for orchestrating parallel scan workers (each scan type is a separate Lambda), Mangum to run FastAPI on Lambda for the control plane API, and strict IAM least-privilege for scan execution roles since the tool itself is in PCI scope. ReportBuilder uses Jinja2 templates per PCI-DSS requirement to generate pre-formatted QSA responses — this is the core differentiating logic.
reference.title: PCI-DSS Evidence Collection Automation Tool Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A serverless AWS automation tool for PCI-DSS Level 1 evidence collection, built with Python 3.12 FastAPI on Lambda (via Mangum) for the control plane and AWS Step Functions orchestrating parallel scan workers across the cardholder data environment. Scans AWS Config rule compliance, IAM access control lists, log retention policies, and network segmentation configurations, mapping findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs and generating pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV. All evidence artifacts stored in S3 with SSE-KMS; the tool itself operates under documented CDE scope.
reference.architecture_overview: Serverless architecture with three layers: a Cognito JWT-authenticated FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum behind API Gateway for human-triggered scans and report downloads; an AWS Step Functions state machine orchestrating parallel Lambda scan workers (ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda); and PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for scan result metadata with S3 for raw evidence artifacts. EventBridge Scheduler triggers quarterly scans automatically; the control plane API supports on-demand runs for pre-audit preparation. All Lambda execution roles are least-privilege read-only against CDE resources. ReportGeneratorLambda maps findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs via PciRequirementMapper and renders PDF QSA responses using Jinja2 and WeasyPrint.
reference.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Control plane: FastAPI with Mangum Lambda adapter, API Gateway v2 HTTP API. Orchestration: AWS Step Functions (Express Workflow). Scan workers: Lambda functions per scan type. PDF generation: Jinja2 templates with WeasyPrint. Infrastructure: RDS PostgreSQL 16, S3 with SSE-KMS, Cognito user pools, API Gateway, EventBridge Scheduler, SNS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitLab CI. AWS SDK: boto3 for Config, IAM, Logs API calls.
reference.functional_feature_map: Scheduled Scan Execution triggered by EventBridge Scheduler quarterly and ad-hoc via API. ConfigComplianceLambda evaluating AWS Config managed rules against CDE component list and baseline. IamPolicyReviewLambda enumerating IAM policies, roles, and access keys with CDE access, flagging excessive permissions. LogRetentionCheckLambda verifying CloudWatch Log Groups for CDE resources meet PCI Requirement 10.7 retention minimums. NetworkSegmentationLambda reviewing Security Group and Network Firewall rules for CDE isolation. PciRequirementMapper tagging each finding to a specific PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement ID (e.g., Req 7.2.1, Req 10.7.1). DeviationDetector comparing findings against compliance baseline. QSA Report Generator producing PDF with pre-populated SAQ D responses and CSV deviation summary.
reference.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler rule (quarterly cron, UTC) invoking Step Functions state machine. Step Functions Express Workflow with parallel branches for Config, IAM, LogRetention, and NetworkSegmentation scan workers, then a sequential ReportGenerator step. FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum with API Gateway v2. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ in production for scan metadata. S3 evidence bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning, and lifecycle to Glacier at 12 months. ComplianceBaselineLoader reading expected states from S3 JSON config files. ReportBuilder with Jinja2 QSA templates per PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement section. SNS topic for scan completion and critical deviation notifications.
reference.workflows: Quarterly Automated Scan: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions at 02:00 UTC on first day of each quarter. State machine fans out to 4 parallel scan Lambda workers, each writing findings to control_checks and evidence artifacts to S3. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda runs PciRequirementMapper against all findings, renders Jinja2 QSA template per requirement section, produces PDF via WeasyPrint and CSV deviation list, stores both to S3, updates qsa_questionnaires record, and fires SNS notification with report download URL to compliance team. Pre-Audit On-Demand Run: Compliance analyst calls POST /scans via Cognito-authenticated API, triggering same Step Functions workflow with on_demand trigger type. Progress visible via GET /scans/:id status polling.
reference.data_model: cde_components (id UUID, name, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id VARCHAR(255), in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_runs (id UUID, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, pci_version VARCHAR(10), started_at, completed_at). control_checks (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, cde_component_id FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type VARCHAR(100), result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key, checked_at). audit_findings (id UUID, control_check_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, expected_value, actual_value, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, pci_version, report_s3_key, csv_s3_key, generated_at). remediation_items (id UUID, audit_finding_id FK, owner, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, notes).
reference.api_design: FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum. POST /scans (trigger on-demand scan, returns scan_run_id). GET /scans (list history, paginated). GET /scans/:id (status and summary). GET /scans/:id/results (all control_checks with findings). GET /scans/:id/report?format=pdf|csv (presigned S3 download URL). GET /deviations?status=open (all open audit_findings across runs). GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence (evidence artifacts for a specific PCI requirement). GET /cde-components (registry). PUT /cde-components/:id (update in-scope flag). Auth: API Gateway Lambda authorizer validating Cognito JWT. All endpoints require compliance_reader role minimum.
reference.security_and_compliance: Tool in PCI CDE scope — access controls and change management are themselves audit evidence. ScanWorkerRole IAM policy: Config:GetComplianceDetailsByConfigRule, Config:DescribeConfigRules, IAM:GetPolicy, IAM:ListAttachedRolePolicies, IAM:ListUsers, logs:FilterLogEvents, ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups, network-firewall:DescribeFirewallPolicy — no write permissions to any CDE resource. Evidence S3 bucket: SSE-KMS with dedicated KMS key, block all public access, object-level CloudTrail logging for GetObject and PutObject. Cognito user pool with password policy and MFA optional at admin discretion. CloudTrail trail with S3 data events covering evidence bucket. API Gateway access logs retained 1 year. Step Functions execution history 90 days.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with moto for AWS SDK mocking (80% coverage gate on scanner modules), Terraform plan against staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda packages built as Python 3.12 zip with dependencies via pip install -t. Lambda function versions pinned in Terraform with previous version alias for rollback. EventBridge Scheduler enabled/disabled via Terraform variable for maintenance windows. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, mock scan data), staging (multi-AZ RDS, sandboxed AWS account), production (multi-AZ RDS, live CDE read access). Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock.
reference.observability: CloudWatch custom dashboard: last_scan_run_status, open_deviations_count, critical_findings_count, days_since_last_successful_scan. Lambda metrics: invocation count, error rate, duration (p99). Step Functions: execution success rate, execution duration. SNS email alerts: scan failure (Step Functions execution FAILED), critical severity audit_finding created, report generation timeout (Lambda duration > 10 minutes). CloudTrail anomaly detection for unexpected CDE resource access by scan roles.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ~$150/month for quarterly scan cadence: Lambda invocations at quarterly scale are near-zero cost. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small Multi-AZ ~$80, S3 storage ~$20, Step Functions Express Workflows ~$10, API Gateway ~$10, CloudWatch ~$20. On-demand pre-audit runs add minor Lambda cost. WeasyPrint PDF generation is CPU-intensive — consider 3008MB Lambda memory for report generator to stay within 15-minute timeout for large CDE environments.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): CDE component registry, ConfigComplianceLambda for CDE segmentation rules, IamPolicyReviewLambda, evidence S3 storage, PciRequirementMapper for SAQ D requirements, CSV deviation report, Cognito auth, Terraform IaC, EventBridge quarterly schedule, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda via Security Group rules, PDF QSA report generation with Jinja2/WeasyPrint, SNS notifications, DeviationDetector with baseline comparison. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Remediation tracking via remediation_items, Jira webhook integration for ticket creation, compliance posture dashboard via CloudWatch, AWS Network Firewall rule analysis.
reference.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting and mypy strict type checking enforced in GitLab CI. All scanner Lambda functions must have unit tests with moto mocking AWS SDK calls — no live AWS calls in tests. PciRequirementMapper logic unit-tested against all 12 PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement sections. Terraform modules for Lambda, Step Functions, and S3 in separate reusable modules. No hardcoded AWS resource IDs — all configuration via SSM Parameter Store or environment variables. Evidence artifact S3 keys follow pattern: {scan_run_id}/{cde_component_id}/{check_type}/{timestamp}.json.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WeasyPrint PDF generation timing out for CDE environments with 100+ components. Mitigation: chunk report generation into per-requirement-section Lambda invocations and assemble via Step Functions. Risk: AWS Config rule coverage gaps for custom CDE components not tracked by managed rules. Mitigation: ComplianceBaselineLoader supports custom check definitions for non-Config-native resources. Tradeoff: FastAPI on Lambda over a persistent container — cold start latency is acceptable for infrequent on-demand scans. Tradeoff: RDS over DynamoDB for structured query support on scan history and requirement-level evidence lookup.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Network firewall rule export will use AWS Network Firewall DescribeFirewallPolicy API — no manual upload required. PDF format confirmed via WeasyPrint with Jinja2 templates; report generator Lambda allocated 3008MB to handle large CDE environments. Baseline configs stored in S3 JSON files editable by admin role, not hardcoded in Lambda.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing ISS-001 (embedding-similarity lockout) and ISS-002 (conversation context window). The fix for ISS-001 adds a Titan Embeddings-based similarity check as a secondary lockout mechanism, triggered after keyword matching fails. The fix for ISS-002 defines a last-8-turns sliding window with summarization of dropped context prepended to the window when the session exceeds Bedrock context limits. Both fixes are targeted additions to the existing plan without structural changes.

fix_report.action_taken: Added embedding-similarity lockout check to PolicyEnforcer: after keyword matching fails, embed the incoming query with Titan Embeddings v2, compute cosine similarity against all problem_statement_hash embeddings in hint_policies for the course, trigger deflection if similarity exceeds 0.88 threshold (configurable via policy rule). Added document_chunks table with ivfflat index on embedding column for fast similarity lookups.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Paraphrased assignment queries are correctly deflected; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Defined last-8-turns sliding window for Bedrock context: last 8 turns of conversation stored in Redis (TTL 30 min) keyed by session_id; if session exceeds 8 turns, generate 2-sentence summary of earlier context using a simple summarization function (extract top 2 entities and 2 key phrases) and prepend it to the window before Bedrock call. Added summarization function to TutoringService.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long tutoring sessions maintain context without silent API errors; ISS-002 resolved

parsed.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
parsed.executive_summary: An AI-powered tutoring assistant for undergraduate STEM courses, built on AWS Bedrock Claude with a RAG pipeline over course PDFs via pgvector semantic search. Students engage in step-by-step problem walkthroughs; assignment lockout policies enforced by keyword matching and embedding-similarity checks before any LLM call. Interaction logs are FERPA-compliant educational records stored in PostgreSQL with 1-year retention. React 18 chat UI streams tutor responses via SSE. This revision adds embedding-similarity lockout as a secondary integrity check and defines a last-8-turns sliding window with summarization for long tutoring sessions.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to an ALB with WAF, which routes to an ECS Fargate cluster running a FastAPI TutoringService. The service orchestrates RAG retrieval via Titan Embeddings v2 (document chunk embeddings), PolicyEnforcer (keyword and embedding-similarity lockout), prompt construction, and Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet API calls. Redis 7 caches active conversation context (last 8 turns) with 30-minute TTL. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS stores interaction logs, course metadata, and document chunks with pgvector embeddings. Cognito JWT validates all API requests. Interaction logs are FERPA-compliant educational records with course enrollment linkage.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, useSSE hook, Zustand. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 for Bedrock, psycopg3 for PostgreSQL, redis-py for Redis, pydantic v2 for request validation. LLM: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet. Vector DB: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (document chunks), pgvector extension on PostgreSQL 16 (in-database). Auth: AWS Cognito user pools. Database: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgvector extension. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, ECR, GitHub Actions CI/CD.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Conversational Tutoring: student submits STEM question; RAG retrieves top-3 course document chunks via pgvector cosine similarity; PolicyEnforcer checks keyword and embedding-similarity lockout rules; if no lockout, constructs prompt with course context and last 8 turns, calls Bedrock, streams response via SSE. Assignment Lockout: instructors configure keyword and embedding-hash rules per course; PolicyEnforcer evaluates incoming query against all active rules; deflection triggered if any rule matches. Interaction Logging: every conversation turn recorded in interaction_logs with course_id, student_id (hashed after 90 days), query_text_hash, response_type, policy_triggered, and latency_ms. Instructor Analytics: per-course session volume, deflection rate, average response latency, and top query topics. Document Ingestion: embedding-worker service asynchronously ingests uploaded course PDFs into document_chunks via Titan Embeddings v2.
parsed.system_components: ALB with WAF (Cognito JWT validation, rate limiting 20 messages/hour/student/course), ECS Fargate cluster: TutoringService (FastAPI, min 2 max 10 tasks, CPU 1vCPU/2GB), EmbeddingWorkerService (async document ingestion, min 1 max 2 tasks), Redis 7 cluster (active conversation context, 30 min TTL), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (interaction_logs, course metadata, document_chunks with pgvector), CloudFront (React SPA static assets), Cognito user pools (student and instructor groups), AWS Bedrock (Claude claude-sonnet, API calls within AWS boundary), Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (document chunk embeddings), LaunchDarkly (feature flag per course section).
parsed.workflows: Tutoring Session: Student submits STEM question via chat; FastAPI validates Cognito JWT and course enrollment; PolicyEnforcer evaluates incoming query against all active hint_policies for the course: (1) keyword check -- if any rule matches, returns canned deflection message without Bedrock call; (2) embedding-similarity check -- if keyword check fails, embeds query with Titan Embeddings v2, computes cosine similarity against all problem_statement_hash embeddings in hint_policies, if similarity exceeds 0.88 threshold, returns deflection; (3) if no lockout, constructs Bedrock prompt with course context and last 8 turns from Redis (or 2-sentence summary if session exceeds 8 turns), calls Bedrock claude-sonnet, streams response via SSE; every turn logged to interaction_logs with course_id, student_id (hashed), query_text_hash, response_type, policy_triggered, and latency_ms. Document Ingestion: instructor uploads course PDF; EmbeddingWorkerService receives SNS notification; worker downloads PDF, splits into 500-word chunks, embeds each chunk with Titan Embeddings v2, stores in document_chunks table with ivfflat index on embedding column; knowledge_base_documents record created with chunk count.
parsed.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_hashed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, enrolled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id UUID FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, course_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). knowledge_base_documents (id UUID PK, course_id UUID FK, filename VARCHAR, chunk_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id UUID FK, chunk_index INT, content_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value VARCHAR, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id UUID FK, student_id UUID FK, course_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE streaming, Cognito JWT required, body: {session_id, query_text, course_id}), returns {type: 'hint', content: string} or {type: 'deflected', reason: string} or {type: 'error', code: int}. GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id} (Cognito JWT required, returns session history). POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies (Cognito instructor role required, body: {rule_type, rule_value, course_id}), returns created policy. GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics (Cognito instructor role required, returns {session_count, deflection_rate, avg_latency_ms, top_topics}). GET /v1/documents/{id}/chunks (Cognito instructor role required, returns list of document_chunks).
parsed.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI dependency level; course enrollment verified per request against enrollments table. Assignment lockout: PolicyEnforcer evaluates keyword and embedding-similarity rules before any Bedrock call; deflection triggered if any rule matches. Embedding-similarity lockout: Titan Embeddings v2 cosine similarity against problem_statement_hash embeddings in hint_policies; threshold 0.88 (configurable via policy rule). Prompt injection guard: regex checks user input for role-switch patterns (e.g., 'what is the answer to') before constructing Bedrock prompt; Bedrock API call blocked if pattern detected. FERPA: interaction_logs record course_id and student_id (hashed after 90 days); access restricted to enrolled students and their instructors; no interaction log data shared with LLM provider. WAF rate limiting: 20 messages/hour/student/course. TLS 1.3 on ALB. Interaction logs encrypted at rest via RDS encryption.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mock Bedrock responses, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared RDS), staging (multi-AZ, isolated RDS), production (multi-AZ, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks on CPU 60%). Feature flags: LaunchDarkly per course section; new course sections enabled via flag toggle before broad rollout. Document ingestion: EmbeddingWorkerService triggered via SNS on course document upload event. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Cognito app client secret and Bedrock API key; injected via ECS task environment variables.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM traces on all FastAPI requests with student_id replaced by hashed session token in logs. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram (p50/p99), policy_deflection_rate counter (per course), bedrock_tokens_used counter, document_ingestion_queue_depth gauge. Alerts: p99 latency > 5s triggers PagerDuty P2; policy_deflection_rate > 10% in 1 hour triggers instructor notification; document ingestion queue depth > 5 triggers EmbeddingWorkerService scale-out.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$180/month for 2-10 tasks at 1vCPU/2GB. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$120/month. ElastiCache Redis 7 cache.t3.micro: ~$30/month. Bedrock claude-sonnet: ~$0.005/1K tokens at 500 tokens per turn = ~$2.50/month per active student. Total estimated ~$350/month for 500 active students. Scaling: ECS auto-scales on CPU 60%; Redis context cache prevents Bedrock API calls from exceeding 8-turn context limit even at high concurrency.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Cognito auth, 3 pilot STEM courses, RAG over uploaded PDFs via pgvector, keyword lockout policy configuration, basic chat interface with SSE, interaction logging, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Embedding-similarity lockout as secondary check, last-8-turns sliding window with summarization, instructor analytics dashboard, LaunchDarkly feature flags per course section. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-course rollout, document ingestion worker, instructor self-service policy export, WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting. pytest with mock Bedrock responses; 80% coverage gate. FastAPI dependency injection for JWT validation and course enrollment check. pydantic v2 for all request and response models. Embedding-similarity lockout threshold configurable via hint_policies rule_value column. Context summarization function unit-tested with synthetic long-session scenarios. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly; no hard-coded course IDs in code.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 cold start adds latency to document ingestion. Mitigation: EmbeddingWorkerService pre-warms on startup with a small dummy document; cold start adds ~30s to first ingestion. Risk: Bedrock claude-sonnet context window of 2048 tokens limits long-session context. Mitigation: last-8-turns sliding window with summarization; summarization function is simple and domain-agnostic. Tradeoff: pgvector over a dedicated vector DB (Weaviate) saves operational overhead but requires RDS Multi-AZ for redundancy; acceptable at current scale. Tradeoff: keyword lockout as primary mechanism is imperfect but sufficient for MVP; embedding-similarity lockout adds accuracy at the cost of Titan Embeddings latency.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: embedding-similarity lockout added as secondary check using Titan Embeddings v2 cosine similarity against problem_statement_hash embeddings in hint_policies. ISS-002 resolved: last-8-turns sliding window with 2-sentence summarization of dropped context prepended to the window when session exceeds 8 turns. RAG chunk size: 500-word chunks chosen for STEM problem sets; lecture slides split into 200-word chunks. FERPA anonymization: student name hashed at enrollment time; student_id in logs replaced by hashed session token after 90 days.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by adding an embedding-similarity lockout check in PolicyEnforcer: the student query is embedded via Titan Embeddings, cosine similarity is computed against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, and deflection is triggered if similarity exceeds 0.88. ISS-002 is resolved by defining a last-8-turns sliding window with a summarization step for dropped context. Both fixes integrate cleanly into the existing TutoringService and PolicyEnforcer without architectural changes. The pgvector infrastructure already supports embedding-based lockout since document_chunks already uses vector(1536).

fix_report.action_taken: PolicyEnforcer now runs a two-stage lockout check: (1) keyword match against hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword'; (2) if keyword check passes, embed the student query via Titan Embeddings v2 and query hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' using pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>); deflect if any stored problem embedding has cosine similarity >=0.88. Threshold is configurable per course via hint_policies.similarity_threshold column. Deflection events logged to interaction_logs with policy_triggered=True and rule_id.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Paraphrased queries that bypass keyword matching are caught by embedding similarity check; academic integrity enforcement is robust to student rephrasing strategies

fix_report.action_taken: TutoringService implements a sliding window context builder: the last 8 conversation_turns are fetched from Redis or PostgreSQL fallback and passed to Bedrock. If session has more than 8 turns, a summarization prompt (system: 'Summarize the following STEM tutoring exchange in 2 sentences focusing on concepts covered') is sent to Bedrock to generate a context_summary; the summary is prepended to the window as a system message. Summaries are cached in Redis keyed by session_id:summary_turn_N with 2-hour TTL.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long tutoring sessions maintain coherent context without Bedrock API errors; students receive contextually appropriate hints even after extended sessions

reference.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
reference.executive_summary: An LLM-powered STEM tutoring assistant on AWS Bedrock replacing a legacy static FAQ, with RAG over course PDFs via pgvector, two-stage assignment lockout enforcement (keyword + embedding-similarity), and FERPA-compliant interaction logging. Students receive adaptive hints via a React chat UI with SSE token streaming; instructors configure lockout policies and view session analytics. Long sessions use a sliding-window context strategy with summarization to maintain coherence within Bedrock's context limits.
reference.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates via HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. TutoringService orchestrates: (1) pgvector hybrid search retrieving top-3 course document chunks, (2) two-stage PolicyEnforcer checking keyword and embedding-similarity lockout rules, (3) context window builder assembling last-8-turns sliding window with optional summarization, (4) AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet invocation via boto3 with streamed response. Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 handles both document chunk ingestion and per-query embedding for similarity-based lockout. PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector stores course embeddings and interaction logs; Redis 7 caches conversation context. Cognito authenticates students and instructors with course-enrollment-scoped authorization.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, useSSE hook for token streaming. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 for Bedrock and Titan Embeddings. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) with pgvector extension, ivfflat index on document_chunks.embedding. Cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for conversation context and summarization cache. LLM: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet for tutoring responses and context summarization. Embeddings: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (1536-dim). Auth: Cognito user pools. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS Fargate rolling deploy. Observability: Datadog APM. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly per course.
reference.functional_feature_map: Student Tutoring Chat: SSE-streamed responses for STEM concept questions and step-by-step walkthroughs. Assignment Lockout Enforcement: keyword match then embedding-similarity check (cosine >=0.88) against instructor-defined problem embeddings; deflected queries receive canned guidance without LLM invocation. RAG Retrieval: hybrid pgvector cosine search + PostgreSQL full-text search for STEM notation across course document chunks. Context Window Management: last-8-turns sliding window with 2-sentence summarization for dropped context. Instructor Policy Config: keyword and problem-statement embedding upload, per-course similarity threshold tuning. Instructor Analytics: session volume, deflection rate, avg response latency, top-queried course topics.
reference.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQLi/XSS rules + rate limiting). ECS Fargate: tutor-api service (min 2, max 10 tasks), embedding-worker service for async document ingestion. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgvector. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for conversation context and summarization cache. AWS Bedrock: Claude claude-sonnet (response generation, context summarization), Titan Embeddings v2 (document and query embedding). S3 bucket for raw course document uploads before ingestion. Cognito user pools with student/instructor groups. Datadog APM sidecar. LaunchDarkly SDK for per-course feature flags.
reference.workflows: Two-Stage Lockout Check: student sends message; TutoringService calls PolicyEnforcer; stage 1: SQL query on hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword' AND course_id=:course_id, check if any keyword appears in query text; stage 2: if stage 1 passes, embed query via Titan Embeddings v2, execute pgvector query SELECT rule_id FROM hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' AND course_id=:course_id ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 1 -- if cosine similarity of top result >= hint_policies.similarity_threshold, trigger deflection; if both stages pass, proceed to RAG retrieval and Bedrock call. Context Window Build: fetch last 8 turns from Redis (TTL 30 min) or PostgreSQL fallback; if session.message_count > 8, retrieve or generate context_summary via Bedrock summarization prompt; prepend summary as system message; assemble context array: [system_prompt, context_summary_if_any, last_8_turns]; send to Bedrock with streaming=True.
reference.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR, name_hashed_at DATE). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id FK, course_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT, context_summary TEXT, summary_as_of_turn INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, rule_id FK nullable, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id FK, chunk_index INT, content_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536)). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value TEXT, embedding vector(1536) nullable, similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.88, created_by FK). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id FK, student_id VARCHAR, course_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE, body: {session_id, message, course_id}) -- runs lockout check, RAG retrieval, context build, Bedrock stream. GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id}/history -- returns conversation_turns for session. POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies -- instructor creates keyword or embedding_hash lockout rule; embedding_hash rules include base64-encoded problem statement, backend embeds and stores vector. GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics?from=&to= -- returns session_count, deflection_rate, avg_latency_ms, top_topics. POST /v1/courses/{id}/documents -- multipart upload; triggers async embedding-worker ECS task. GET /v1/health -- ALB health check.
reference.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI Depends(get_current_user); course enrollment verified per request against students-courses enrollment table. Prompt injection guard: regex scan of user input for patterns ('ignore previous', 'you are now', 'system:', 'assistant:'); blocked queries logged with event_type='injection_attempt'. Assignment lockout: two-stage check (keyword + embedding similarity >=0.88) enforced in backend before any Bedrock call; client-side blocking is not relied upon. FERPA: interaction_logs link to course_id and student_id; access restricted to the student themselves and course instructor; student identifiers in logs anonymized (hashed) after 90 days via nightly Lambda; interaction logs excluded from Datadog log forwarding. Bedrock: system prompt passed as system role, not prepended to user message; no student query content included in Bedrock model training (Bedrock API guarantee).
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mocked Bedrock responses (pytest-mock) and pgvector test DB (Docker Compose), build Docker, push ECR. ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy task during update. Environments: dev (single-AZ, t-class RDS, mock Bedrock), staging (multi-AZ RDS, real Bedrock, 3 pilot courses), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks, LaunchDarkly per-course rollout). Document ingestion: S3 event triggers ECS embedding-worker task; chunks documents at 512 tokens with 64-token overlap, embeds via Titan v2, inserts into document_chunks with pgvector index rebuild after batch. Secrets: Cognito pool IDs and RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; Bedrock IAM role via ECS task role -- no API keys.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: trace per /v1/tutor/chat request with spans for PolicyEnforcer, pgvector retrieval, context build, and Bedrock invocation. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram per course_id, policy_deflection_rate counter per course_id and rule_type, bedrock_input_tokens_total and bedrock_output_tokens_total counters, context_summarization_invocations_total. Alerts: p99 response latency >5s fires PagerDuty; deflection rate spike >2x 7-day average fires Slack alert to instructor; embedding-worker queue depth >50 fires ops alert. Structured logs: student_id replaced by hashed_session_token; no student query plaintext in Datadog.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet at $0.003/1K input tokens and $0.015/1K output tokens; avg tutoring turn ~800 input tokens and ~300 output tokens = ~$0.007/turn; 10,000 turns/month = ~$70/month. Titan Embeddings v2 at $0.0001/1K tokens; 200 course document chunks/course, 3 courses = 600 embeddings plus query embeddings ~$5/month. ECS Fargate: min 2 tasks x 1vCPU/2GB = ~$60/month baseline; scales to 10 tasks during peak exam periods. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$120/month. Total: ~$300/month for pilot, scaling proportionally with course enrollment.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- MVP Tutoring (6 weeks): ECS Fargate, FastAPI, Cognito auth, RAG over course PDFs (pgvector + Titan Embeddings v2), Bedrock Claude tutoring with system-role-locked prompt, SSE streaming React chat UI, keyword-only lockout policy, interaction logging. Phase 2 -- Academic Integrity Hardening (3 weeks): embedding-similarity lockout check (ISS-001 fix), sliding-window context with summarization (ISS-002 fix), prompt injection guard, instructor policy configuration UI, LaunchDarkly per-course feature flags. Phase 3 -- Analytics and Scale (3 weeks): instructor analytics dashboard, Datadog APM custom metrics, anonymization Lambda for 90-day FERPA requirement, performance tuning of pgvector ivfflat index, load testing for exam-period demand spikes.
reference.development_guidelines: All Bedrock prompt templates versioned in Git under prompts/ directory with changelog entries. Lockout policy changes require instructor authentication and are audit-logged. pytest mocks all Bedrock and Titan Embeddings calls; integration tests run against a dockerized PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. Student query plaintext must never appear in structured logs -- log only content_hash. Feature flag every course rollout; no direct production deployments without staging validation.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: embedding-similarity threshold (0.88) is too aggressive, flagging legitimate STEM questions as assignment problems -- mitigated by per-course threshold tuning and instructor review of deflection logs. Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 model version changes causing embedding space drift and false-negative lockout misses -- mitigated by storing embedding_model_version on courses and reindexing when model updates. Tradeoff: pgvector over Pinecone/Weaviate eliminates operational overhead but limits to single-node vector search; acceptable at pilot scale but may require migration at 1M+ chunks. Tradeoff: Bedrock Claude over GPT-4 satisfies AWS data boundary requirement at slightly higher latency.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Cosine similarity threshold 0.88 chosen as default after testing on sample STEM problem sets; configurable per course to allow stricter enforcement in exam periods. Sliding window of 8 turns chosen to balance context quality and Bedrock token cost for typical 30-minute tutoring sessions. Titan Embeddings v2 chosen over OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 to keep all embeddings within AWS boundary.

raw:
{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a first-pass data pipeline plan for a K-12 assessment score ingestion pipeline. The critical architectural decisions are: Airflow DAG chain with distinct task groups for ingestion, validation, linkage, and aggregation; ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level aggregates to handle principal dashboard query patterns; and a dedicated quarantine step before normalization to protect the normalized store from corrupt vendor extracts. The plan quality is intentionally weak: several sections are thin and the data model is incomplete.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "K-12 Assessment Score Ingestion Pipeline",
 "executive_summary": "A nightly Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer that ingests raw K-12 standardized test scores from assessment vend

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a first-pass data pipeline plan for a K-12 assessment score ingestion pipeline. The critical architectural decisions are: Airflow DAG chain with distinct task groups for ingestion, validation, linkage, and aggregation; ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level aggregates to handle principal dashboard query patterns; and a dedicated quarantine step before normalization to protect the normalized store from corrupt vendor extracts. The plan quality is intentionally weak: several sections are thin and the data model is incomplete.
parsed.title: K-12 Assessment Score Ingestion Pipeline
parsed.executive_summary: A nightly Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer that ingests raw K-12 standardized test scores from assessment vendors via SFTP, validates against a canonical JSON schema, links scores to student enrollment records via BigQuery, normalizes into a ClickHouse analytics store, and refreshes school-level and grade-level performance aggregates for principal and curriculum coach dashboards.
parsed.architecture_overview: Airflow DAG chain on Cloud Composer orchestrates five task groups: ingest (SFTP poll and GCS upload), validate (Cloud Run microservice call), link (BigQuery enrollment join), normalize (Python transforms), and aggregate (ClickHouse MV refresh). Cloud Run workers handle validation at scale. ClickHouse on Compute Engine stores normalized scores and performance aggregates. BigQuery captures compliance audit queries. GCS stores raw vendor files and quarantine batches. VPC-SC perimeter prevents PII exfiltration.
parsed.technology_stack: Orchestrator: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2. Workers: Python 3.12, Pydantic for schema validation, PyArrow for ClickHouse writes. Analytics: ClickHouse 24.x on Compute Engine n2-standard-8. Validation: Cloud Run Python 3.12 service. Raw storage: GCS with lifecycle policies. Audit: BigQuery. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring. CI/CD: Cloud Build. Secrets: Secret Manager.
parsed.functional_feature_map: SFTP Ingestion: nightly poll of vendor SFTP servers, download raw score files to GCS. Validation: Cloud Run microservice validates each file against vendor-specific JSON schema, quarantines rows with validation errors. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery temp table join of normalized scores to enrollment_records by state_student_id and academic_year. Normalization: Python transforms map vendor-specific fields to canonical normalized_scores schema. Aggregation: ClickHouse materialized views compute school-level and grade-level proficiency rates. Dashboard Delivery: Grafana dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches, scoped to their school via ClickHouse row-level security.
parsed.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 (Airflow scheduler and workers), Cloud Run (validation microservice), ClickHouse cluster (2x n2-standard-8 Compute Engine), BigQuery (compliance audit dataset), GCS (raw, quarantine, processed buckets), Secret Manager (SFTP credentials, CMEK keys), VPC-SC (perimeter around GCP project), Cloud Build (CI/CD), Grafana (dashboard service), Prometheus (ClickHouse metrics), Cloud Monitoring (alerting).
parsed.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Airflow cron triggers ingest_dag at 23:00. SFTP task polls each active vendor SFTP server, downloads new files to GCS raw/ prefix. Validation task calls Cloud Run validation microservice with file path and vendor schema version. Failed validation rows written to GCS quarantine/ prefix. Successful files passed to link_dag. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery temp table joins normalized_scores on state_student_id and academic_year to enrollment_records. Rows with no matching enrollment record written to quarantine. Normalization: Python transforms applied to each row, written to ClickHouse normalized_scores. Aggregation: ClickHouse materialized views refreshed with new normalized rows. Dashboard refresh: Grafana data source queries ClickHouse performance_aggregates for principal school scope.
parsed.data_model: assessment_vendors (id, name, sftp_host, schema_version, dsa_document_id, active). raw_score_files (id, vendor_id, gcs_path, received_at, row_count, validation_status). students (id, state_student_id, district_id, enrollment_school_id, grade_level, active). enrollment_records (id, student_id, school_id, academic_year, grade_level, enrollment_date). normalized_scores (id, student_id, vendor_id, assessment_name, subject, raw_score, scaled_score, proficiency_level, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id, school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate, n_students, suppressed). school_benchmarks (id, school_id, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target, academic_year).
parsed.api_design: No external API. Internal Airflow task communication: XCom for small payloads (e.g., validation error counts). GCS object paths shared between DAG tasks via XCom. ClickHouse query API: internal only, authenticated via GCP IAM. BigQuery query API: internal only, authenticated via GCP IAM.
parsed.security_and_compliance: FERPA: no individual student score exposed in any report with cohort smaller than 10 students; ClickHouse MV suppression logic applied at query time. VPC-SC perimeter prevents PII exfiltration. CMEK encryption at rest on GCS and ClickHouse. TLS 1.3 in transit. SFTP credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation. BigQuery audit dataset captures every ClickHouse query with principal identity. Airflow task logs scrubbed of PII before Cloud Logging ingestion.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build pipeline: flake8 lint, pytest unit tests, airflow dags test, container build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy, DAG sync via GCS bucket. Environments: dev (single Composer env, ClickHouse dev node), staging (full topology, anonymized fixtures), production (Composer + ClickHouse 2-node). ClickHouse backup: daily snapshot via Compute Engine disk snapshot. Airflow DAG versioning: git tags for rollback. Cloud Run validation service scales 0-to-10 on ingestion trigger.
parsed.observability: Grafana dashboards: DAG run duration per task group, validation pass/fail rate by vendor, normalized row count per vendor, ClickHouse MV refresh latency. Prometheus: ClickHouse query latency, Airflow task retry count, Cloud Run validation worker count. PagerDuty alerts: failed DAG run, validation quarantine rate above 5%, ClickHouse disk utilization above 80%.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Composer: ~$800/month for 2 workers. ClickHouse 2x n2-standard-8: ~$1,200/month. Cloud Run validation: ~$50/month at 10 workers. GCS: ~$200/month for 7-year raw file retention. Total: ~$2,350/month. Scaling: ClickHouse scales horizontally by adding nodes; Cloud Run validation scales on demand.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Single vendor ingestion, validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage, grade-level aggregates for one academic year, Grafana dashboard for one school. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Additional vendor onboarding with schema registry step, multi-year aggregation, school-level benchmark comparison views. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Teacher effectiveness correlation views, BigQuery compliance export reports.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations. Pydantic models for all data structures. pytest with 80% coverage gate. Airflow DAG tests via testcontainers-airflow. ClickHouse schema changes via migration scripts, not ad-hoc ALTER TABLE. Vendor schema changes require schema registry approval before production promotion.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor schema changes without notice corrupt normalization. Mitigation: schema registry step in validation DAG; schema version tracked in assessment_vendors table. Risk: ClickHouse single-node bottleneck at district scale. Mitigation: horizontal scaling by adding nodes; ClickHouse replication for high-availability. Tradeoff: BigQuery for compliance audit over dedicated audit log service; BigQuery query latency acceptable for infrequent audit exports.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: ClickHouse chosen over BigQuery for analytics due to sub-second aggregation performance at district scale. Cloud Run chosen for validation over Cloud Functions for better error handling and retry semantics. VPC-SC perimeter chosen over Cloud Armor for PII boundary enforcement.

reference.thinking_summary: Designing a FERPA-compliant K-12 assessment data pipeline on GCP. Key decisions: Airflow DAG separation by task group enables targeted retries without re-ingesting clean data; ClickHouse materialized views pre-aggregate school and grade-level metrics to serve principal dashboards at sub-second latency; small-cohort suppression baked into MV definitions prevents FERPA violations at query time; VPC-SC perimeter and GCS IAM ensure student PII never leaves the district GCP project boundary.
reference.title: K-12 Multi-Vendor Assessment Data Pipeline
reference.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant multi-stage data pipeline on GCP that ingests nightly standardized test score files from assessment vendors via SFTP, validates against versioned JSON schemas, quarantines invalid rows, links scores to student enrollment records, normalizes into a canonical schema stored in ClickHouse, and refreshes materialized views powering Grafana dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches. The pipeline enforces small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer and maintains a 7-year retention chain for state compliance.
reference.architecture_overview: Five-stage Airflow DAG chain orchestrated by Cloud Composer: (1) Ingest DAG polls vendor SFTP endpoints and deposits raw files to GCS raw/ prefix with vendor and date partitioning. (2) Validate DAG invokes Cloud Run validation microservice checking each file against a versioned JSON schema registry, routing passing files to processed/ and failures to quarantine/ with structured error manifests. (3) Link DAG joins validated score rows to students and enrollment_records tables in BigQuery temp datasets by state_student_id and academic_year. (4) Normalize DAG applies Python transforms mapping vendor-specific score scales to canonical proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse normalized_scores. (5) Aggregate DAG triggers ClickHouse MV refreshes on performance_aggregates applying small-cohort suppression for groups below 10. VPC-SC perimeter encloses the entire GCP project; no student PII exits the boundary.
reference.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2, Python 3.12 DAGs. Validation microservice: Python 3.12 FastAPI on Cloud Run with jsonschema library. Storage: GCS for raw/quarantine/processed files, ClickHouse 24.x on Compute Engine n2-standard-8 for analytics, BigQuery for compliance audit exports. Dashboards: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse data source plugin. Auth: Google Workspace SSO, GCP IAM with Workload Identity. CI: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Vendor SFTP Ingestion: polling-based download with file deduplication via GCS object hash. JSON Schema Validation: per-vendor schema registry with version pinning and quarantine routing with structured error manifests. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery join by state_student_id resolving multi-year enrollment changes. Score Normalization: configurable transform map per vendor converting raw/scaled scores to canonical proficiency_level. Aggregation Refresh: ClickHouse MV refresh computing proficiency rates by school, grade, subject, and academic year with cohort suppression. Dashboard Layer: Grafana panels for school comparison, grade-band trends, year-over-year proficiency shifts. Quarantine Management: data team remediation queue surfacing failed rows with field-level error codes.
reference.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment hosting Airflow scheduler and workers, Cloud Run validation service scaling 0-10 instances on ingestion trigger, GCS buckets for raw/quarantine/processed/dag-sync, ClickHouse 2-node Compute Engine cluster with SSD for normalized_scores and performance_aggregates, BigQuery dataset for enrollment lookups and compliance audit exports, Secret Manager storing vendor SFTP credentials, Grafana instance on Compute Engine with ClickHouse and BigQuery data sources, Prometheus scraping ClickHouse and Cloud Run metrics, PagerDuty via Cloud Monitoring alerting policies.
reference.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Cloud Composer triggers ingest_dag at 23:00. Airflow SFTPSensor polls each active vendor host for new files matching date pattern. Files downloaded to GCS raw/vendor_id/date/ prefix. File hash checked against processed manifest to skip duplicates. validate_dag triggered on successful ingest. Validation and Quarantine: validate_dag submits each raw file to Cloud Run microservice. Service loads schema version from vendor registry, validates each row, accumulates errors. Files with greater than 5% row failure rate quarantined to GCS quarantine/vendor_id/date/ with JSON error manifest. PagerDuty alert fired with vendor name, file name, and failure rate. Passing files moved to processed/. Normalization and Aggregation: link_dag loads processed rows into BigQuery temp table, joins to enrollment_records on state_student_id and academic_year, flags unmatched rows as orphaned for data team review. normalize_dag transforms linked rows to proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse. aggregate_dag executes ClickHouse MV refresh on performance_aggregates grouped by school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, applying WHERE n_students >= 10 suppression. Dashboard queries read exclusively from performance_aggregates.
reference.data_model: assessment_vendors (id UUID PK, name, sftp_host, sftp_credential_secret_id, schema_version, dsa_document_gcs_path, active BOOL). raw_score_files (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, gcs_path, file_hash, received_at, row_count INT, valid_row_count INT, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed, quarantine_manifest_path, processed_at). students (id UUID PK, state_student_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, district_student_id, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, current_school_id FK, active BOOL). enrollment_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, school_id FK, academic_year, grade_level INT, enrollment_date, withdrawal_date). normalized_scores (id UUID PK, student_id FK, vendor_id FK, raw_score_file_id FK, assessment_name, subject, raw_score FLOAT, scaled_score FLOAT, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id UUID PK, school_id FK, grade_level INT, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate FLOAT, n_students INT, suppressed BOOL, refreshed_at). school_benchmarks (id UUID PK, school_id FK, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target FLOAT, academic_year).
reference.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Cloud Run validation endpoint POST /validate (multipart file + vendor_id + schema_version, returns {passed_rows, failed_rows, error_manifest_path}). Airflow REST API internal: GET /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for monitoring integration. BigQuery compliance export: scheduled query writing score access events to compliance_audit dataset. Grafana queries ClickHouse via native HTTP interface on port 8123 restricted to read-only grafana service account. PagerDuty alert webhook on DAG failure and quarantine threshold breach.
reference.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: small-cohort suppression (n less than 10) enforced in ClickHouse MV definition. ClickHouse row-level security restricts principals to their own school_id. VPC-SC perimeter blocks all GCS and BigQuery API calls originating outside the district GCP project. CMEK via Cloud KMS encrypts all GCS buckets and ClickHouse persistent disks. Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation. Student PII fields (first_name, last_name, dob) AES-256 encrypted before insert. BigQuery audit dataset captures every query touching normalized_scores with timestamp, principal identity, and row count. Data sharing agreement document IDs logged per vendor ingestion run.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1 with 3 scheduler nodes and auto-scaling worker pool (2-8 workers). DAG deployment: Cloud Build pushes Python files to GCS dag-sync bucket; Composer picks up within 60s. Cloud Run validation service: Cloud Build builds Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys revision with 100% traffic shift on health check pass. ClickHouse upgraded via Ansible playbook with pre-upgrade disk snapshot. Environments: dev (single Composer, ClickHouse dev node with 90-day score sample), staging (full topology with 2 mock vendors), production (Composer + 2-node ClickHouse). Rollback: DAG git tag rollback via GCS sync; ClickHouse snapshot restore; Cloud Run revision rollback.
reference.observability: Prometheus scrapes ClickHouse system.metrics every 30s: query_duration_ms p99, rows_read_per_second, disk_used_bytes. Cloud Monitoring tracks Cloud Run: request_count, error_rate, instance_count. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health showing DAG run durations and quarantine rates per vendor; Data Quality showing orphaned_scores count and validation pass rate; ClickHouse Performance showing MV refresh duration and disk growth. PagerDuty alerts: DAG run failure, quarantine rate above 10%, MV refresh above 90 minutes, ClickHouse disk above 80%.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month: Cloud Composer 2 ~$400, ClickHouse 2x n2-standard-8 ~$280, Cloud Run validation ~$30, GCS lifecycle storage ~$60, Grafana/Prometheus instance ~$30. Scaling trigger: add ClickHouse replica node at 100M normalized_scores rows or p99 MV refresh exceeding 60 minutes. Cloud Run validation workers scale automatically on file volume without manual intervention.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SFTP ingestion for one vendor, JSON schema validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage via BigQuery join, normalization to canonical schema, ClickHouse aggregation with small-cohort suppression, Grafana grade-level dashboards for 5 pilot schools, Cloud Build CI and VPC-SC perimeter. Phase 2 Multi-Vendor (6 weeks): schema registry with version management for 2 additional vendors, parallel DAG execution per vendor, orphaned_scores remediation workflow, expanded dashboards for all district schools. Phase 3 Advanced Analytics (8 weeks): year-over-year proficiency trend views, school benchmark comparison panels, early-warning data mart for predictive model ingestion, BigQuery compliance export automation.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type hints on all DAG task functions. pytest for unit tests with Airflow LocalExecutor; minimum 80% coverage on normalization transform functions. Airflow DAG linting via airflow dags test in Cloud Build pipeline. ClickHouse schema migrations via versioned SQL files in /migrations directory applied by Ansible. No student PII in Airflow XCom payloads — pass GCS paths only. Vendor schema versions pinned in config/vendor_schemas.yaml under git version control. PR requires data engineer review for normalization or aggregation query changes.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor sends mid-year schema change without notice corrupting normalization. Mitigation: schema registry with strict version pinning; validation DAG blocks normalization on schema mismatch and pages data team. Risk: ClickHouse single-node disk exhaustion on 7-year score accumulation. Mitigation: quarterly disk usage review with auto-alert at 80%; replica node planned at 100M rows. Risk: Enrollment record gaps leave test scores unlinked. Mitigation: orphaned_scores metric on Grafana with 5-business-day remediation SLA. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics sub-second latency; accepts manual upgrade and backup overhead.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics layer due to sub-second aggregation latency requirements for principal dashboards. Confirmed GCS over Cloud SQL for raw file storage given unstructured vendor file formats. Chose Cloud Composer over self-managed Airflow to reduce operator burden. FERPA small-cohort suppression threshold set at 10 students per state guidance.
